title: chore: upgrade to Typescript v6
body:
## Describe your changes

- Upgrades our monorepo to TypeScript v6, making changes as needed to align to [the migration guide](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0/).

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

- Covered by existing tests

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Upgrades the frontend toolchain to TypeScript v6 and adjusts related lint/build configuration across the Streamlit frontend workspaces.

**Changes:**
- Bump TypeScript to `^6.0.2` across frontend workspaces and update related tooling (`typescript-eslint`, `@vitest/eslint-plugin`, lockfile).
- Adjust `frontend/lib` build/type-generation setup (separate `tsconfig.build.json`, Vite DTS plugin config, tsconfig path mappings).
- Small TS6-compatibility code tweaks (e.g., `matchMedia` usage, websocket send payload typing).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 18 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/yarn.lock | Updates lockfile for TS6 + related dependency bumps. |
| frontend/typescript-config/package.json | Bumps TypeScript devDependency for shared tsconfig package. |
| frontend/typescript-config/base.tsconfig.json | Removes `baseUrl` from shared compiler options. |
| frontend/package.json | Bumps TypeScript + typescript-eslint and updates some resolutions. |
| frontend/lib/vite.config.ts | Points `vite-plugin-dts` at a build-specific tsconfig. |
| frontend/lib/tsconfig.json | Removes emit options; adjusts path mappings. |
| frontend/lib/tsconfig.build.json | New tsconfig intended for declaration-only emit. |
| frontend/lib/src/theme/utils.ts | Updates `matchMedia` access pattern. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DateInput/useIntlLocale.tsx | Type adjustments for Intl weekInfo + date-fns Day typing. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/DataFrame.tsx | Safer `matchMedia` usage with fallback. |
| frontend/eslint.config.mjs | Globally ignores all `vite.config.ts` files. |
| frontend/eslint-plugin-streamlit-custom/package.json | Bumps TS + `@typescript-eslint/*` dependencies/peer range. |
| frontend/connection/src/WebsocketConnection.tsx | Sends encoded protobuf payload

## Summary

This PR upgrades the frontend toolchain from TypeScript v5 to TypeScript v6. The changes include bumping `typescript` to `^6.0.2` across all workspace packages, updating `typescript-eslint` to `^8.57.2` and `@vitest/eslint-plugin` to `^1.6.13`, restructuring `lib/tsconfig.json` with a new `tsconfig.build.json` for declaration emit, removing `baseUrl` and updating `paths` to use relative prefixes, wrapping the protobuf `finish()` result with `Uint8Array.from()` for WebSocket send compatibility, converting `matchMedia` guards to optional chaining, adapting `useIntlLocale` types for TS6's stricter narrowing, adding `**/vite.config.ts` to ESLint `globalIgnores`, and updating documentation to reflect v6.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews.

## Code Quality

**Consensus**: All three reviewers agree the changes are clean, minimal, and well-targeted. Each modification addresses a specific TS6 compatibility issue:

- The `tsconfig.build.json` split is architecturally sound — it isolates declaration emit from type-checking so sibling workspace sources aren't pulled into the build root under TS6's stricter `rootDir` inference.
- The `matchMedia` optional chaining conversions align with the `@typescript-eslint/prefer-optional-chain` rule enforced by the codebase.
- The `interface` → `type` intersection change for `IntlLocaleWithWeekInfo` and the simplified `IntlWeekInfo` types are pragmatic adaptations to TS6's stricter type system.

**Key discussion point**: The `Uint8Array.from(buffer)` in `WebsocketConnection.tsx` was flagged by all three reviewers. Gemini raised it as blocking (requesting changes), while Claude and GPT acknowledged it as non-blocking. The concern is that `Uint8Array.from()` copies the entire buffer on every outbound WebSocket message. Since `BackMsg` payloads (client→server) are typically small, this is not a practical performance issue, but a zero-copy view (`new Uint
LGTM 👍

commits.message: chore: upgrade to Typescript v6
fix: package.json pathing
fix: address PR comments
fix: tsconfig path change

title: Bump the vitest group in /frontend with 3 updates
body:
Bumps the vitest group in /frontend with 3 updates: [@vitest/coverage-v8](https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/tree/HEAD/packages/coverage-v8), [vitest](https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest/tree/HEAD/packages/vitest) and [vitest-canvas-mock](https://github.com/wobsoriano/vitest-canvas-mock). Updates `@vitest/coverage-v8` from 4.1.0 to 4.1.1
Release notes

Sourced from @​vitest/coverage-v8's releases.

v4.1.1

   🚀 Features

  • experimental:
    • Expose matchesTags to test if the current filter matches tags  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9913 (eec53)
    • Introduce experimental.vcsProvider  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9928 (56115)

   🐞 Bug Fixes

  • Mark TestProject.testFilesList internal properly  -  by @​sapphi-red in vitest-dev/vitest#9867 (54f26)
  • Detect fixture that returns without calling use  -  by @​oilater in vitest-dev/vitest#9831 and vitest-dev/vitest#9861 (633ae)
  • Drop vite 8.beta support  -  by @​AriPerkkio in vitest-dev/vitest#9862 (b78f5)
  • Type regression in vi.mocked() static class methods  -  by @​purepear and @​hi-ogawa in vitest-dev/vitest#9857 (90926)
  • Properly re-evaluate actual modules of mocked external  -  by @​hi-ogawa in vitest-dev/vitest#9898 (ae5ec)
  • Preserve coverage report when html reporter overlaps  -  by @​hi-ogawa in vitest-dev/vitest#9889 (2d81a)
  • Provide vi.advanceTimers to the preview provider  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9891 (1bc3e)
  • Don't leak event listener in playwright provider  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9910 (d9355)
  • Open browser in --standalone mode without running tests  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9911 (e78ad)
  • Guard disposable and optional body  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9912 (6fdb2)
  • Resolve retry.condition RegExp serialization issue  -  by @​nstepien and @​hi-ogawa in vitest-dev/vitest#9942 (7b605)
  • collect:
    • Don't treat extra props on test return as tests  -  by @​sheremet-va in vitest-dev/vitest#9871 (141e7)
  • coverage:
    • Simplify provider types  -  by @​AriPerkkio in vitest-dev/vitest#9931 (aaf9f)
    • Load built-in provider without module runner  -  by @​AriPerkkio in vitest-dev/vitest#9939 (bf892)
  • expect:
    • Soft assertions continue after .resolves/.rejects promise errors  -  by @​mixelburg, Maks Pikov, Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) and @​hi-ogawa in vitest-dev/vitest#9843 (6d74b)

  • pretty-format:
    • Limit output for large object  -  by @​hi-ogawa and Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) in vitest-dev/vitest#9949 (0d5f9)

title: Add key-value list example for `st.table`
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a docstring example and e2e demo showing `st.table` with key-value data using:
- Material Icons as key prefixes (`:material/folder:`, `:material/code:`, etc.)
- Markdown badges with colors (`:green-badge[...]`, `:blue-badge[...]`, etc.)
- Horizontal borders (`border="horizontal"`)
- Content-fit width (`width="content"`)

This provides users with a practical example of displaying rich key-value metadata in tables.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Related #13185

## Testing Plan

- [x] Python unit tests pass (`make check`)
- [x] Demo verified with `make debug`

title: [Fix] Make `_stcore/metrics` OpenMetrics-compliant
body:
## Describe your changes
- Canonicalize counter metric families in `/_stcore/metrics` so metadata uses OpenMetrics-compliant family names while emitted counter samples still use the `_total` suffix.
- Tighten text serialization to omit empty `# UNIT` lines and emit `# HELP <family_name> <help>`.
- Update runtime, server, and e2e metrics tests for the `session_events` family rename and the canonical family/sample split.

Follow-up to #14476 

### API-visible change
- `families=session_events_total` no longer works.
- Consumers should now request `families=session_events`.
- The emitted sample line remains `session_events_total{...}`.

## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
- E2E Tests
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates Streamlit’s `/_stcore/metrics` OpenMetrics text output to be more spec-compliant by using canonical counter *family* names (without `_total`) in metadata while continuing to emit counter *samples* with the `_total` suffix, and by tightening comment serialization.

**Changes:**
- Canonicalize the `session_events` counter family name (metadata uses `session_events`; sample remains `session_events_total{...}`), and remove the counter `sample_name` override mechanism.
- Update text serialization to omit `# UNIT` lines for unitless metrics and to emit `# HELP <family_name> <help>`.
- Update unit, server, and e2e tests to reflect the family rename and the metadata/sample split behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/stats.py | Renames `SESSION_EVENTS_FAMILY` and makes counter samples consistently render as `<family>_total`. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/websocket_session_manager.py | Aligns emitted session metrics with canonical counter family naming (no explicit sample name override). |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/stats_request_handler.py | Tightens OpenMetrics text comment rendering (`# HELP` includes family name; omits empty `# UNIT`). |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/stats_test.py | Updates CounterStat expectations to match `_total` sample rendering. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/websocket_session_manager_test.py | Updates runtime stats assertions for `session_events` family and `_total` sample naming. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/stats_handler_test.py | Updates Tornado metrics endpoint tests for `session_events` family and `# HELP/# UNIT` formatting. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_app_test.py | Updates Starlette metrics endpoint tests for the same OpenMetrics output changes. |
| e2e_play

## Summary

This PR makes the `/_stcore/metrics` endpoint OpenMetrics-compliant by canonicalizing counter metric family names. The key changes:

1. **Family name fix**: `SESSION_EVENTS_FAMILY` changes from `"session_events_total"` to `"session_events"` — the `_total` suffix is reserved for counter sample names per the OpenMetrics spec, not the metric family name.
2. **`sample_name` field removed**: `CounterStat.sample_name` is eliminated; `to_metric_str()` now derives the sample name by appending `_total` to the family name automatically.
3. **`# HELP` line fix**: Now emits `# HELP <family_name> <help>` instead of `# HELP <help>`, matching the OpenMetrics specification.
4. **`# UNIT` line fix**: Empty unit lines are no longer emitted, matching the OpenMetrics specification.
5. **Test updates**: All unit and E2E tests are updated for the new family name and serialization format.

This is a follow-up to #14476 and introduces an intentional API-visible breaking change: consumers must now use `families=session_events` instead of `families=session_events_total`.

*All three expected reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews.*

## Code Quality

**Unanimous agreement across all reviewers.** The code is clean, well-structured, and follows existing patterns. The removal of the `sample_name` field in favor of deriving the sample name from the family name (`f"{self.family_name}_total"`) is a good simplification that eliminates a source of inconsistency where callers had to manually keep the sample name and family name in sync. The text serialization logic in `stats_request_handler.py` is improved — conditionally emitting `# UNIT` and including the family name in `# HELP` both align with the [OpenMetrics exposition format](https://github.com/OpenObservability/OpenMetrics/blob/main/specification/OpenMetrics.md).

## Test Coverage

**Unanimous agreement across all reviewers.** Test coverage is thorough and well-updated a
LGTM 👍

title: [fix] Restrict deploy button to localhost only
body:
## Describe your changes

- Add `isLocalhost()` check to `showDeployButton()` to ensure the deploy button only shows when the app is running on localhost
- Remove the now-redundant `isInCloudEnvironment()` method since the localhost check makes it unnecessary (cloud environments don't run on localhost)

This change ensures that even when `toolbarMode === DEVELOPER`, the deploy button will only show on localhost, preventing it from appearing in non-local environments.

## Pull request overview

Restricts visibility of the in-app Deploy button so it only appears when the app is running on localhost, preventing the button from showing up in non-local environments even if `toolbarMode === DEVELOPER`.

**Changes:**
- Gate `showDeployButton()` behind `isLocalhost()`.
- Remove the now-unused `isInCloudEnvironment()` helper.
- Delete the obsolete unit test that asserted Deploy button behavior based on host menu items.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/app/src/App.tsx` | Adds a localhost-only condition for showing the Deploy button and removes the cloud-environment helper. |
| `frontend/app/src/App.test.tsx` | Removes a test that depended on prior “cloud environment” detection via host menu items. |

## Summary

This PR restricts the deploy button to only appear when the app is running on localhost. It replaces the indirect `isInCloudEnvironment()` heuristic — which inferred a cloud context from the presence of `hostMenuItems` — with a direct `isLocalhost()` hostname check in `showDeployButton()`. The now-unused `isInCloudEnvironment()` method and its corresponding test are removed. Two files are changed: `frontend/app/src/App.tsx` and `frontend/app/src/App.test.tsx`.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed that the change is clean, well-scoped, low-risk, and improves the codebase. The sole point of disagreement was whether a missing negative integration test should block approval.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the code quality is high. Replacing the `isInCloudEnvironment()` method with `isLocalhost()` is a clear improvement:

- **Simpler:** One function call instead of a method that inspects component state.
- **More direct:** Checking the hostname is a stronger, synchronous signal compared to relying on `hostMenuItems` arriving via `postMessage`.
- **No dead code:** `isInCloudEnvironment()` was only used by `showDeployButton()`, so its removal is appropriate.

Minor observation (noted by claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking): In `AUTO` toolbar mode, `isLocalhost()` is checked twice — once in `showDeployButton()` and once inside `showDevelopmentOptions()`. This is harmless due to short-circuit evaluation and the check being cheap and synchronous.

## Test Coverage

All three reviewers identified the same minor gap: the removed test ("Deploy button should be hidden for cloud environment") tested the negative path that no longer applies, but no replacement test verifies the deploy button is hidden when `isLocalhost()` returns `false`. Since jsdom defaults `window.location.hostname` to `"localhost"`, existing tests only exercise the positive path.

**Mitigating factors:**
- The `isLo

## Summary

This PR restricts the deploy button to only appear when the app is running on `localhost`. It replaces the previous `isInCloudEnvironment()` heuristic (which relied on the presence of `hostMenuItems` as a proxy for cloud environments) with a direct `isLocalhost()` check in `showDeployButton()`. The old cloud-environment test is removed and a new test verifying the deploy button is hidden on non-localhost hostnames is added.

**Reviewer consensus**: All three models (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews and reached the same verdict — APPROVED. No expected models failed to complete their review.

## Code Quality

**All reviewers agree** the change is clean, minimal, and improves maintainability. Only two files are modified. The logic is straightforward: `isLocalhost()` from `@streamlit/utils` is already well-tested with its own unit tests covering `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, null, and undefined hostnames. Removing `isInCloudEnvironment()` is appropriate since `hostMenuItems` is still used elsewhere (e.g., `shouldShowToolbar`, `MainMenu` props) and only the deploy-button check is affected.

One reviewer (claude) noted a minor redundancy where `isLocalhost()` is called both in `showDeployButton()` and inside `showDevelopmentOptions()` for the default/AUTO toolbar mode, but agreed this is acceptable for clarity.

## Test Coverage

Test coverage has been appropriately updated. The obsolete cloud-environment test was removed and a new negative-path test was added for non-localhost hostnames.

**All three reviewers agree** no E2E tests are needed since this is a narrow frontend condition on `window.location.hostname` with no backend or protocol changes.

**Two reviewers** (claude, gemini) independently flagged the same test robustness issue: the new test manually saves/restores `window.location` inline rather than using `beforeEach`/`afterEach` (or `try...finally`), which could leave the mock in place if the as
LGTM! Also thanks for cleaning up `isInCloudEnvironment` 👍

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR incorporates user-testing feedback for Streamlit’s experimental Starlette/ASGI support by hardening internal route safety, improving runtime lifecycle behavior when mounted as a sub-app, and adjusting uvicorn logging behavior to restore expected CLI logs.

**Changes:**
- Reserve `/static/` as a non-overridable route prefix to prevent user routes from breaking frontend asset loading.
- Auto-start the Streamlit runtime on the first HTTP/WebSocket request when an `st.App` is mounted without using `lifespan()`.
- Improve Starlette health endpoint 503 messaging based on runtime state and adjust uvicorn config to avoid suppressing logs (while disabling access logs).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_app_test.py | Extends reserved-prefix assertions to include `/static/`. |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_server.py | Updates uvicorn kwargs to avoid overriding logging config and disables access logs. |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_routes.py | Adds `BASE_ROUTE_STATIC` constant and improves 503 health messaging with runtime-state hints. |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_app.py | Reserves `/static/` and adds lazy runtime auto-start for mounted apps without lifespan. |

## Summary

This PR addresses user feedback from the experimental Starlette/ASGI support (issue #13600) with four improvements:

1. **Reserved `/static/` route prefix** — Adds `/static/` to `_RESERVED_ROUTE_PREFIXES` to prevent users from accidentally overriding frontend asset serving.
2. **Auto-start runtime for mounted apps** — Enables `Mount("/st", App("main.py"))` without requiring explicit `lifespan()` by lazily starting the runtime on the first HTTP/WebSocket request.
3. **Improved 503 health messages** — Replaces the generic "unavailable" message with state-specific guidance (e.g., "Runtime not started").
4. **Fix missing uvicorn logs** — Replaces `log_config=None` with `access_log=False` so error logs still appear.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed on the overall scope and direction. No expected models failed to complete their review.

## Code Quality

**Consensus across all reviewers**: The code is well-structured and follows existing patterns in the Starlette server module. The auto-start logic is sound — using an asyncio lock to serialize startup is appropriate.

**Unanimous agreement on one issue**: The comment on line 572 ("handle the edge case where two requests create separate locks") is technically inaccurate for a single asyncio event loop, since cooperative multitasking prevents preemption between the `is None` check and assignment. All three reviewers flagged this — either the comment should be corrected or the lock should be initialized eagerly in `__init__`.

**One reviewer (gpt-5.3-codex-high) raised a lifecycle concern**: The `_cleanup` function registered via `atexit` calls `Runtime.stop()`, which internally uses `call_soon_threadsafe` on the event loop. During process exit, the event loop may already be closed, which would raise `RuntimeError`. This is a valid concern — verified by inspecting `Runtime.stop()` at `runtime.py:351`. A `try/except RuntimeError` guard would make cleanu

This PR addresses user feedback from the experimental Starlette/ASGI support (issue #13600) with four targeted improvements scoped entirely to `lib/streamlit/web/server/starlette/`:

Key implementation details validated:

title: [feature] Add `st.iframe` command
body:
## Describe your changes Implements `st.iframe` as specified in [#14373](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/14373), consolidating `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a single, discoverable command in the main Streamlit namespace. **Features:** - Auto-detects input type: URL, Path object, existing file, or HTML string - Supports absolute URLs (`http://`, `https://`, `data:`), relative URLs (`/app/static/...`) - Embeds local HTML files via `srcdoc`, non-HTML files via media storage - `width`/`height` parameters with `"stretch"`, `"content"`, and pixel values - `height="content"` auto-sizes to content for srcdoc (falls back to 400px for URLs) - `width="content"` auto-sizes to content for srcdoc (falls back to stretch for URLs) - Uses standard `widthConfig`/`heightConfig` layout system (no custom proto fields) - `tab_index` parameter for accessibility/keyboard navigation **Usage:** ```python import streamlit as st from pathlib import Path # Embed a URL st.iframe("https://docs.streamlit.io", height=600) # Embed HTML content with auto-sizing st.iframe("

Hello World

") # height="content" by default # Embed a local HTML file st.iframe(Path("reports/dashboard.html"), height=800) # Embed a PDF st.iframe(Path("documents/manual.pdf"), height=600) # Auto-size both width and height st.iframe("
Content
", width="content", height="content") ``` ## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable) - Closes #12977 ## Testing Plan - [x] Unit Tests (Python) - 42 tests covering URL handling, HTML string detection, local file handling, dimension parameters, tab_index validation, width/height content sizing - [x] Unit Tests (Frontend) - 27 tests covering rendering, auto-sizing behavior, message validation - [x] E2E Tests (Playwright) - 10 tests covering rendering, content verification, sandbox policy, auto-sizing - [x] Typing Tests (mypy) - Parameter and return type validation

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new public `st.iframe` command to the main Streamlit namespace, consolidating existing iframe embedding behavior into a more discoverable API and extending it to handle URLs, HTML strings, and local files.

**Changes:**
- Implement `st.iframe` in `IframeMixin`, including tab index validation and local file handling (HTML via `srcdoc`, non-HTML via media storage).
- Export `iframe` from `streamlit.__init__` so it’s available as `st.iframe`.
- Add/extend Python unit tests and new Playwright E2E coverage for `st.iframe`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/elements/iframe.py | Adds `st.iframe` implementation, shared `tab_index` validation, and local file processing logic. |
| lib/streamlit/__init__.py | Exposes `st.iframe` in the public Streamlit namespace. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/iframe_test.py | Refactors/extends unit tests for tab index validation and `st.iframe` behaviors (URL/HTML/local HTML). |
| e2e_playwright/st_iframe.py | Adds an E2E example app exercising key `st.iframe` scenarios. |
| e2e_playwright/st_iframe_test.py | Adds E2E tests for rendering, sandboxing, scrolling, and tab index behavior. |
</details>

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).

## Summary

This PR introduces a new public `st.iframe` API that consolidates iframe embedding into the main Streamlit namespace. It auto-detects input types (URLs, HTML strings, `Path` objects, local files) and supports `width`/`height` parameters with `"stretch"`, `"content"`, and pixel values. The `height="content"` mode injects JavaScript into `srcdoc` content to measure and report height via `postMessage`, with a frontend `useEffect` listener that applies the measured height as an inline style.

Key changes span proto (`use_content_height` field), backend (`IframeMixin.iframe()` with input-type detection and auto-height script injection), frontend (`postMessage` listener in `IFrame.tsx`), and tests (40 Python unit tests, 10 E2E tests with snapshots).

## Reviewer Consensus

**Available reviews**: claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking (APPROVED), gpt-5.3-codex-high (CHANGES_REQUESTED).
**Missing reviews**: gemini-3.1-pro failed to complete its review.

### Agreement

All reviewers agreed on:
- The feature is well-designed with clear input-type detection logic and good separation of concerns.
- **Missing frontend unit tests** for the `useContentHeight` / `postMessage` auto-sizing behavior is a significant coverage gap.
- **Missing typing tests** (`lib/tests/streamlit/typing/iframe_types.py`) for the new public API surface.
- The `contentHeight ? ...` truthy check on line 82 of `IFrame.tsx` is a latent correctness issue (height of 0 treated as falsy).
- External test coverage (`@pytest.mark.external_test`) should be added for iframe embedding and `postMessage`-based sizing in nested/hosted iframe scenarios.
- Backwards compatibility is clean: `st.iframe` is a new API, existing `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` are untouched, and the proto change is purely additive.
- Security risk is low to moderate; the `postMessage` with `'*'` target is acceptable given the `srcdoc` context and the frontend source validation.

### Disagreement

| Topic | claude-4.6-o

## Summary

This PR introduces a new `st.iframe` command that consolidates `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a single, discoverable API in the main Streamlit namespace. Key features include auto-detection of input type (URL, Path, file, or HTML string), flexible `width`/`height` parameters (supporting `"stretch"`, `"content"`, and pixel values), content-based auto-height sizing via injected JavaScript and `postMessage`, and a `tab_index` parameter for keyboard accessibility. The legacy `components.v1.html` and `components.v1.iframe` are deprecated with console-only warnings pointing to `st.iframe`.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed this is a well-implemented, well-tested feature addition. No critical or blocking issues were identified.

## Code Quality

**Consensus: Strong.** All reviewers agreed the code is well-structured and follows existing Streamlit patterns across all layers:

- **Backend (`iframe.py`)**: Clean separation of concerns with `_is_file`, `_validate_tab_index`, `_inject_auto_height_script` helpers appropriately extracted. The `_AUTO_HEIGHT_SCRIPT` correctly uses `Math.ceil` on `getBoundingClientRect` to handle fractional pixels. NumPy-style docstrings are comprehensive with examples.
- **Frontend (`IFrame.tsx`)**: The `useEffect` for `postMessage` listening has proper cleanup, source validation, and correct dependency array. The `heightStyle` is correctly derived during render rather than in an effect.
- **Deprecation (`deprecation_util.py`)**: The new `include_st_prefix`, `show_in_browser`, and `show_once` parameters maintain backward compatibility with sensible defaults.
- **Protobuf (`IFrame.proto`)**: The `use_content_height` field (field 9) is additive with implicit `false` default, ensuring backward compatibility.

## Test Coverage

**Consensus: Comprehensive.** All reviewers rated coverage as strong/excellent:

- **Python unit tests (40 tests)**: Cover URL

## Summary

This PR introduces `st.iframe` as a new public API that consolidates `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a single, discoverable command in the main `st` namespace. Key capabilities include auto-detection of input type (URL, `Path` object, existing file, relative URL, or HTML string), auto-sizing height via `height="content"` using injected JavaScript and `postMessage`, local file embedding (HTML via `srcdoc`, non-HTML via media storage), and graceful deprecation of the legacy components APIs.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews and unanimously approved the PR.

## Code Quality

**Consensus: Well-structured, follows Streamlit patterns.** All reviewers agree the implementation is clean and well-organized:

- The backend `IframeMixin` cleanly separates input detection, proto construction, dimension handling, and local file processing.
- The frontend `useEffect` for `postMessage` listening is properly scoped with cleanup, source validation, and height validation.
- The deprecation utility additions (`include_st_prefix`, `show_in_browser`, `show_once`) are backward-compatible with sensible defaults.
- The auto-height measurement script is comprehensive, using multiple measurement strategies (`getBoundingClientRect`, `scrollHeight`, `offsetHeight`) with `MutationObserver` for dynamic content and `lastHeight` deduplication.

One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) noted a minor robustness concern with `_is_file()` — it should guard against very long strings and `ValueError` from null bytes. This is covered in the inline comments.

- **Python unit tests** (40+ tests): Cover URL handling, HTML detection, local files (HTML, non-HTML, `Path`, `.htm`), dimensions, `tab_index` validation, `height="content"` fallback, and error paths.
- **Frontend unit tests**: Cover `useContent

This PR introduces `st.iframe`, a new public Streamlit command that consolidates `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a single, discoverable API in the main `st.` namespace. Key capabilities include auto-detection of input type (URLs, `Path` objects, local files, raw HTML strings), content-based auto-sizing via injected JavaScript that measures and reports dimensions through `postMessage`, graceful fallbacks for URLs where cross-origin restrictions prevent measurement, and deprecation of the legacy APIs with console-only warnings shown once per message.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews successfully. No expected models failed.

## Code Quality

- **Backend** (`lib/streamlit/elements/iframe.py`): The `IframeMixin.iframe()` method cleanly separates input detection, proto population, and layout configuration. Helper functions (`_is_file`, `_validate_tab_index`, `_inject_auto_size_script`) are appropriately extracted. Local file processing handles both HTML and non-HTML files with proper error handling and uses the existing media file manager.
- **Frontend** (`frontend/lib/src/components/elements/IFrame/IFrame.tsx`): The `postMessage` listener is correctly implemented as a `useEffect` (an external system subscription) with proper cleanup. The `setContentDimensions` updater uses referential equality checks to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- **Deprecation utilities**: The additions to `deprecate_func_name` (`include_st_prefix`, `show_in_browser`, `show_once`) are backwards-compatible with well-chosen defaults. The `show_in_browser=False, show_once=True` configuration is a thoughtful choice for soft deprecation.
- **Protobuf**: Only adds field 9 to the reserved list — safe and follows proto guidance for released messages.

## Summary

This PR introduces a new `st.iframe` command in the main Streamlit namespace, consolidating `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a unified, more discoverable API. Key capabilities include auto-detection of input type (URL, Path object, local file, or HTML string), content-based auto-sizing via injected JavaScript that measures dimensions and reports them via `postMessage`, and local file handling (HTML embedded via srcdoc, non-HTML uploaded to media storage). The old APIs are preserved with gentle, log-only deprecation warnings.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews. Two approved; one requested changes citing implementation-level robustness concerns and missing external test coverage.

## Code Quality

All three reviewers agreed the implementation is well-structured and follows established Streamlit patterns across backend, frontend, proto, and test layers. Specific points of agreement:

- **Backend**: Clean separation of concerns with well-named module-level helpers (`_is_file`, `_validate_tab_index`, `_inject_auto_size_script`, `_process_local_file`). The input detection order is logical and documented.
- **Frontend**: The `useEffect` for message handling follows React best practices with proper cleanup, source validation via `event.source === iframeRef.current?.contentWindow`, and a functional `setState` updater. The `shouldMeasureContent` derived value correctly gates both the effect and dimension style application.
- **Deprecation**: The new `show_in_browser=False` and `show_once=True` options to `deprecate_func_name` provide a less intrusive deprecation path.

commits.message:
feat: Add st.iframe command for embedding content

Implements st.iframe as specified in #14373, consolidating
st.components.v1.iframe and st.components.v1.html into a single,
discoverable command in the main namespace.

Features:
- Auto-detects input type: URL, Path object, existing file, or HTML string
- Supports absolute URLs (http/https/data:), relative URLs (/app/static/...)
- Embeds local HTML files via srcdoc, non-HTML files via media storage
- Width/height parameters with "stretch", "content", and pixel values
- tab_index parameter for accessibility/keyboard navigation

Closes #12977

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add auto-sizing for st.iframe with height='content'

When using height='content' with HTML strings or local HTML files,
the iframe now automatically sizes to fit its content:

- Injects JavaScript into srcdoc content to measure and report height
- Frontend listens for postMessage and applies height dynamically
- URLs still fall back to 400px due to cross-origin restrictions

This enables a more natural user experience where iframe content
displays without unnecessary scrollbars or extra whitespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use Math.ceil on getBoundingClientRect to avoid iframe scrollbar

The previous height measurement used scrollHeight which rounds to
integers, but content can have fractional pixel heights. This caused
a tiny scrollbar to appear when content height was e.g. 194.4375px
but the iframe was sized to 194px.

Now using getBoundingClientRect().height with Math.ceil() to properly
round up and fully contain the content without scrollbars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: Simplify st.iframe code and fix e2e test reliability

- Add Final type annotations to module constants
- Consolidate duplicated frontend test cases for sandbox/feature policies
- Replace wait_for_timeout with proper expect assertion for height updates
- Fix misleading comments about iframe index and scrolling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Address CI failures and PR review comments

- Add iframe to element_mocks.py for public API tests

- Add save_media_data call for caching compatibility

on the iframe element, not as inline style properties. Updated the tests to
check the correct attributes.

  frontend IFrame component (single source of truth)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix st.iframe to always treat /-prefixed strings as relative URLs

Strings starting with `/` are now always treated as relative URLs and
never checked against the filesystem. This prevents accidentally reading
filesystem data when the user intends a relative URL (e.g., for static
file serving). To read an absolute path from disk, use Path() explicitly.

and width="content" when the source is a URL (not srcdoc):

- width="content" falls back to "stretch" for URLs

Also fixes the detection order so that existing Unix file paths (starting
with /) are correctly identified as files before being treated as relative
URLs.

## Pull request overview

Adds frontend unit tests across shared utils, chart data helpers, dialogs, and form clearing to incrementally improve frontend line coverage.

**Changes:**
- Expanded `utils.test.ts` coverage for core utility helpers (debounce/hash/id parsing/proto enum conversions/element builders).
- Added ArrowVegaLiteChart `arrowUtils` tests for inline/datasets data extraction helpers.
- Added Dialog tests for width variants and non-dismissible keyboard behavior; introduced new FormClearHelper tests for the helper class + hook lifecycle.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/util/utils.test.ts | Adds broad unit coverage for utility functions and proto-to-enum conversion helpers. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/Form/FormClearHelper.test.ts | Introduces tests for `FormClearHelper` subscription/disconnect behavior and the `useFormClearHelper` hook. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Dialog/Dialog.test.tsx | Adds tests intended to cover dialog width mapping and keyboard handling in non-dismissible dialogs. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/arrowUtils.test.ts | Adds tests for extracting inline data, datasets, and dataset arrays from Quiver-backed chart inputs. |

## Summary

This PR adds frontend unit tests to improve line coverage from 87.66% to 87.91% (+0.25%). All changes are strictly in test files — no production code is modified. Four test files are changed or added:

- `arrowUtils.test.ts` — Tests for `getInlineData`, `getDataSets`, `getDataArrays` in VegaLite chart utilities
- `Dialog.test.tsx` — Tests for dialog width mapping and keyboard handling
- `FormClearHelper.test.ts` — New test file for `FormClearHelper` class and `useFormClearHelper` hook
- `utils.test.ts` — Tests for `debounce`, `hashString`, `notUndefined`, platform detection, element/form ID validation, proto enum conversions, and Element creation helpers

**Reviewer consensus**: All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved this PR unanimously. Reviewers agreed on all major points — well-structured tests, good coverage, no production risk, and adherence to frontend testing conventions. Minor differences were limited to the level of detail in inline suggestions (see below).

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the tests are well-structured and follow repository conventions documented in `frontend/AGENTS.md`:

- **Vitest-only syntax**: No Jest patterns; correctly imports from `vitest`.
- **Proper timer handling**: `debounce` tests use `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.useRealTimers()` with correct lifecycle hooks.
- **RTL best practices**: Dialog tests use accessible queries (`getByRole`, `getByText`, `getByLabelText`) and `userEvent` for interactions.
- **Hook testing**: `FormClearHelper.test.ts` uses `renderHook` from `@testing-library/react` and tests unmount cleanup.
- **Positive + negative assertions**: Tests include both expected and unexpected behavior checks (e.g., valid vs. invalid form IDs, subscribe vs. skip).
- **License headers**: All files have the correct Apache 2.0 header.

Two minor inline findings were noted regarding assertion depth in the Dialog tests — the width variant tests only check vis

title: [chore] Add basic server load testing framework
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a load testing framework to measure Streamlit server performance under concurrent user load. The framework uses Python's multiprocessing with Playwright browser sessions to simulate realistic user interactions while collecting server-side metrics (memory, CPU, threads) via psutil.

- Framework in `e2e_playwright/load_testing/` with configurable scenarios, metrics collection, and markdown report generation
- Five test scenarios covering common patterns: simple app, dataframe, widgets, caching, and fragments
- Server metrics collection via psutil (memory, CPU, threads) at configurable intervals
- Markdown report generation with performance summaries
- Properly excluded from regular CI E2E test runs

[Tech spec
](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/a8909766b30069c8363c998655cf7fa2f21c1452/specs/2026-03-20-load-testing/tech-spec.md)


## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A - Infrastructure improvement

## Testing Plan

- The framework is self-testing through its scenarios
- Excluded from CI via `--ignore ./load_testing` in playwright.yml and playwright-starlette.yml
- Manual testing via `make run-e2e-test e2e_playwright/load_testing/test_load.py::test_scenario[simple_app]`

---

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds an opt-in load-testing harness under `e2e_playwright/` to stress the Streamlit server with concurrent real-browser sessions, collect server-side metrics via `psutil`, and publish results via a dedicated manually triggered GitHub Actions workflow.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `e2e_playwright/load_testing/` with multiprocessing-based Playwright workers, scenario apps, metrics sampling/aggregation, and result/report generation.
- Adds a new manual `Load Testing` GitHub Actions workflow and updates existing Playwright workflows to ignore the new load-testing directory during regular CI E2E runs.
- Documents the design/usage via a new tech spec and updates workflow documentation indexes.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 21 out of 21 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| specs/2026-03-20-load-testing/tech-spec.md | Tech spec describing goals, architecture, metrics, scenarios, and CI approach for load testing. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/__init__.py | Declares load-testing package. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/.gitignore | Ignores generated load test outputs and caches in the load-testing subdir. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md | Local usage docs, scenarios, metrics, and results format explanation. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/conftest.py | Pytest options/fixtures for starting/stopping servers, selecting ports, and writing results. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/metrics_collector.py | `psutil`-based server sampling + aggregation and percentile utilities. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/worker.py | Per-user Playwright worker session logic and scenario-specific interactions. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/test_load.py | Orchestrates scenarios, spawns worker pools, asserts pass/fail thresholds, writes results. |
| e2e_playwright/load_testing/generate_report.py | Aggregates JSON results

## Summary

This PR adds a load testing framework under `e2e_playwright/load_testing/` to measure Streamlit server performance under concurrent user load. The implementation uses Python's `multiprocessing` to spawn N separate Playwright browser sessions against a single Streamlit server, collecting server-side metrics (memory, CPU, threads) via psutil alongside client-side timing (load times, rerun durations). Results are written as JSON and rendered as markdown summaries.

Key components:
- Five test scenarios (`simple_app`, `dataframe_app`, `widget_heavy_app`, `caching_app`, `fragment_app`) exercising different server stress patterns
- A `MetricsCollector` that samples process metrics from a background thread
- A `generate_report.py` script for markdown/HTML summary generation
- A `workflow_dispatch`-only GitHub Actions workflow (`load-testing.yml`) for CI execution on 64-core runners
- Proper exclusion from regular CI E2E runs via `--ignore ./load_testing`

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved this PR unanimously.

## Code Quality

**Consensus: Well-structured and maintainable.** All three reviewers agreed the code is cleanly modularized with clear separation of concerns across `conftest.py` (fixtures/server lifecycle), `worker.py` (browser session logic), `metrics_collector.py` (psutil collection), `test_load.py` (test orchestration), and `generate_report.py` (reporting). Good use of dataclasses, type annotations, `from __future__ import annotations`, and private-prefixed module-level names following Streamlit conventions.

The `conftest.py` correctly overrides the parent `app_server` autouse fixture with a no-op since load tests manage their own server lifecycle. The deliberate localhost URL construction in `test_load.py` is properly annotated with a comment explaining why it diverges from e2e conventions.

One cross-cutting concern raised by one reviewer: the `playwright-changed-files.yml` workflow is

## Summary

This PR adds a server load testing framework under `e2e_playwright/load_testing/` that measures Streamlit server performance under concurrent user load. The framework uses Python's `multiprocessing` module to spawn N Playwright browser workers, each in its own process, against a single Streamlit server, while a background `MetricsCollector` thread samples server-side metrics (memory RSS, CPU, threads) via psutil. Five test scenarios cover common usage patterns (simple app, dataframes, widget-heavy, caching, fragments). A new GitHub Actions workflow (`load-testing.yml`) runs these tests on demand via a `run-load-testing` label or manual dispatch on a 64-core runner. Existing CI workflows are updated to exclude load tests from regular E2E suites.

**Reviewer agreement:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed on the overall quality, safety, and scope of this PR. Two approved outright; one requested changes based on inline implementation concerns detailed below.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the code is well-structured with clean separation of concerns across modules (`conftest.py`, `metrics_collector.py`, `worker.py`, `test_load.py`, `generate_report.py`). The implementation follows codebase conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, `from __future__ import annotations`, type annotations throughout, correct use of `dataclass`, `Final`, and `TYPE_CHECKING` guards. The `app_server` no-op override in the local `conftest.py` is the correct pattern to prevent the parent e2e conftest from managing the server lifecycle. Thread safety in `MetricsCollector` is handled correctly — the sampling thread is joined before computing the summary.

## Test Coverage

This PR is testing infrastructure — the five scenarios serve as the tests themselves, asserting success rates and P95 load times. Coverage spans simple baseline measurement, large payload serialization, 90+ widgets with state management, concurrent `@st.cach

widget-heavy, caching, fragment), and a GitHub Actions workflow for CI.

title: [feature] Add filter mode to select widgets
body:
## Describe your changes

- Adds `filter_mode` to `st.selectbox` and `st.multiselect` with `"fuzzy"`, `"contains"`, `"prefix"`, and `None` modes.
- Keeps fuzzy matching as the default while letting apps use substring or prefix matching, or disable typing entirely for short option lists.
- Wires the setting through protobuf and shared frontend filtering so widget identity, bulk-select behavior, and docs stay consistent across both widgets.
- Adds focused Python, typing, frontend, and E2E coverage for the new filtering modes.

## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a new `filter_mode` option for `st.selectbox` and `st.multiselect`, wiring it end-to-end (Python API → protobuf → frontend filtering) to support `"fuzzy"` (default), `"contains"`, `"prefix"`, and disabling filtering/typing (`None`).

**Changes:**
- Add `filter_mode` to Selectbox/MultiSelect protos and Python widget implementations (including validation + docs).
- Add shared frontend filtering utilities + hook support to apply the selected filter mode consistently.
- Add/extend typing, unit, frontend, and Playwright E2E coverage for the new modes.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 21 out of 21 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

commits.message:
Add configurable filter mode for selectbox and multiselect

Implement configurable dropdown filtering so selectbox and multiselect can use fuzzy, contains, prefix, or disabled matching. This gives apps more precise search behavior for IDs and codes, and lets them turn off typing when filtering is unnecessary.

Refactor filter mode validation and docs

Centralize select widget filter_mode validation in shared option utilities and sync the related spec and feature-guide docs. This keeps the selectbox and multiselect code paths aligned while preserving the shipped behavior.

Restore Knip proto ignoreUnresolved; use toLowerCase for filter matching

- Align knip.json with develop so generated protobuf imports do not fail Knip on a clean tree.
- Use locale-invariant toLowerCase() for select option filter normalization (review feedback).

Handle review follow-ups for filter mode

Tighten filter_mode validation to return a Streamlit API error for unhashable inputs and strengthen the shared Selectbox tests with explicit negative assertions. This addresses the remaining actionable review feedback without changing widget behavior.

Remove filter_mode examples from spec docs

The filter_mode feature is now implemented, so remove it as an example
of a hypothetical feature addition from the spec documentation.

reviews.body:
Really love this spec, great work! Overall I agree with the direction here, left a few detail comments. 

Few more generic questions:
1. I think I agree that going with `st.App` makes more sense, but I know in the past we also considered adding something like `st.mount`, which would work similar to Gradio's `mount_gradio_app` (see [here](https://www.gradio.app/docs/gradio/mount_gradio_app)). Did you consider that option? I guess the main advantage of `st.App` is that I can also directly modify the Starlette app object, without mounting it in a FastAPI app, right?
2. API-wise, instead of `st.App` we could also consider calling the app/server object `streamlit.Streamlit`, similar to `fastapi.FastAPI`. But I think I agree that `st.App` sounds a bit nicer and more intuitive. 
3. When do you plan to ship this? Do you first want to wait until we fully migrated to Starlette, or already ship this before? If we do ship it before, is there any risk we need to revert the Starlette migration?

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a product specification for a new `st.App` class that will provide an ASGI-compatible entry point for Streamlit applications. The specification follows up on the ongoing Starlette support implementation and aims to address numerous community feature requests totaling over 400 upvotes.

Key changes:
- Proposes an ASGI application entry point with Starlette-compatible API
- Enables custom HTTP routes, middleware configuration, and lifecycle hooks
- Supports integration with popular Python web frameworks (Django, FastAPI)
Sounds great, thanks for the answers! Approving.

title: [test] AI-assisted flaky E2E test fixes
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to fix flaky E2E tests identified from recent CI runs.

This PR was created by the `ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests` workflow using `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.

## Changes

# Flaky E2E Test Fixes Summary

**Date:** 2026-03-27
**Lookback period:** 4 days
**Top flaky tests analyzed:** 10

## Tests Analyzed

| # | Test | Reruns | Browsers | Result |
|---|------|--------|----------|--------|
| 1 | `st_file_uploader_test.py::test_uploads_directory_with_multiple_files` | 9 | firefox, webkit | **Skipped** - already marked `@pytest.mark.flaky` |
| 2 | `st_slider_test.py::test_slider_with_float_formatting` | 7 | firefox | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 3 | `bidi_components/basics_test.py::test_trigger_interactions` | 6 | chromium, webkit | **Fixed** |
| 4 | `st_spinner_test.py::test_spinner_time` | 6 | firefox | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 5 | `st_altair_chart_basic_select_test.py::test_point_bar_chart_displays_selection_text` | 4 | webkit | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 6 | `st_altair_chart_basic_select_test.py::test_interval_bar_chart_displays_selection_text` | 4 | webkit | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 7 | `st_chat_input_test.py::test_file_upload_error_message_file_too_large` | 3 | webkit | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 8 | `forward_msg_cache_test.py::test_check_total_websocket_message_number_and_size` | 3 | webkit | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 9 | `hello_app_test.py::test_mapping_demo_page` | 3 | chromium, webkit | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |
| 10 | `mega_tester_app_test.py::test_mega_tester_app_interactions_validate_behavior` | 3 | chromium | Not reproducible (25/25 passed) |

## Fixes Applied

### 1. `e2e_playwright/bidi_components/basics_test.py` - `test_trigger_interactions`

**Root cause:** The bidi component (custom component v2) loads JavaScript and HTML in an iframe, which can be slow to render on webkit. The first assertion `expect(trigger.get_by_text("Foo count: 0")).to_be_visible()` was using the default 5-second timeout, which was sometimes insufficient for the component to fully load.

**Fix:** Increased the timeout on the first visibility assertion from the default 5s to 10s to allow the bidi component iframe sufficient time to load and render on slower browsers.

**Verification:** 25/25 runs passed on webkit after the fix (previously failed on run 17/25 before the fix).

**File changed:** `e2e_playwright/bidi_components/basics_test.py` (line 94)

```diff
-    expect(trigger.get_by_text("Foo count: 0")).to_be_visible()
+    # Bidi components load JS/HTML in an iframe; allow extra time on slower browsers
+    expect(trigger.get_by_text("Foo count: 0")).to_be_visible(timeout=10000)
```

## Notes

- 8 out of 9 non-skipped tests could not be reproduced locally after 25 runs each. These flaky failures are likely caused by CI environment factors (resource contention, slower I/O, network latency) that cannot be addressed locally.
- 1 test was already marked with `@pytest.mark.flaky` and was skipped per the skill instructions.

---
*This is an automated flaky test fix by `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.*

title: Fix programmatic dataframe selections to return AttributeDictionary
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes programmatic dataframe selection returning a plain `dict` instead of `AttributeDictionary`, which caused `AttributeError` when accessing selection attributes (e.g., `event.selection.rows`).

Changes:
- Wraps validated programmatic selection state in `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` to match the behavior of user-driven selections
- `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` extends `AttributeDictionary` so `isinstance` checks still work
- Selection state is read-only to prevent in-place modifications that wouldn't trigger UI updates (users get a clear error message guiding them to use full assignment instead)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #14454

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py::test_programmatic_selection_returns_attribute_dictionary` — Verifies programmatic selection returns `AttributeDictionary` with working attribute access
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py::test_selection_state_is_read_only` — Verifies selection state is read-only
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/util_test.py::TestReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` — Comprehensive tests for the new `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` class
- [x] E2E Tests  
  - `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_selections_test.py` — Verifies attribute access works via `selection.selection.rows`

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a regression where programmatic `st.dataframe(..., on_select=...)` selection state returned a plain `dict` instead of an `AttributeDictionary`, breaking attribute-style access like `event.selection.rows`.

**Changes:**
- Wrap programmatically-validated dataframe selection state in `AttributeDictionary` to match user-driven selection return behavior.
- Add a Python unit test covering attribute-style access for programmatic selections.
- Extend the existing Playwright E2E coverage to assert attribute access works in the programmatic selection scenario.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py` | Ensures programmatic selection return values use `AttributeDictionary` for `event.selection.*` attribute access. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py` | Adds regression unit test verifying programmatic selection supports attribute-style access. |
| `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_selections.py` | Emits `selection.selection.rows` to validate attribute access in the E2E app. |
| `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_selections_test.py` | Adds E2E assertions for the new attribute-access output. |

## Summary

This PR fixes a bug (#14454) where programmatic dataframe selection via `st.session_state` returned a plain `dict` instead of an `AttributeDictionary`, causing `AttributeError` when users accessed selection attributes with dot notation (e.g., `result.selection.rows`). The fix is a one-line change in `lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py` (line 999) wrapping `validated_state` in `AttributeDictionary(...)` before returning, consistent with the `deserialize` path at line 231. The PR includes a unit test and E2E test additions.

**Reviewer consensus**: 3/3 expected models completed their reviews. Two reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved; one (gemini-3.1-pro) requested changes citing a potential edge case on subsequent reruns. After independent verification, the edge case does not manifest in practice (see Code Quality section).

## Code Quality

The fix is minimal, correct, and follows existing codebase patterns. The `DataframeSelectionSerde.deserialize` method (line 231) already wraps its return in `AttributeDictionary`; this change makes the programmatic-set path consistent.

**Resolved disagreement**: gemini-3.1-pro raised a concern that on subsequent reruns (where `widget_state.value_changed` is `False`), `widget_state.value` at line 1002 would be a plain `dict`, causing the bug to reappear. After tracing the full widget lifecycle through `SessionState.register_widget` → `_getitem` → `WStates.__getitem__`, this concern is not valid:

1. On every rerun (both in production and AppTest), widget states are sent back from the frontend (or simulated via `ElementTree.get_widget_states()`), populating `_new_widget_state` with serialized proto values.
2. `WStates.__getitem__` deserializes these through `serde.deserialize` (line 231), which wraps the result in `AttributeDictionary`.
3. Therefore `widget_state.value` at line 1002 is already an `AttributeDictionary` on subsequent reruns through the normal deserialization path.

## Summary

This PR fixes a bug (#14454) where programmatic dataframe selection via `st.session_state` returned a plain `dict` instead of an `AttributeDictionary`, causing `AttributeError` when users accessed selection attributes with dot notation (e.g., `event.selection.rows`). The fix wraps the return value in `AttributeDictionary` on both code paths in `st.dataframe()`: the `value_changed` path (the actual bug) and the normal widget-state path (defensive hardening).

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) are in full agreement on every aspect of this PR.

## Code Quality

The fix is minimal, well-targeted, and follows existing patterns. Two lines changed in `lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py`, each wrapping a return value in `AttributeDictionary(...)` with an appropriate `cast`. The pattern is consistent with other chart widgets (`plotly_chart`, `vega_charts`, `deck_gl_json_chart`) that all wrap selections in `AttributeDictionary` via their deserializers. This PR closes the gap where `st.dataframe` was the only widget that could return a plain dict on the programmatic-selection path. No structural or maintainability concerns were raised by any reviewer.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers agree coverage is thorough and appropriately layered:

- **Unit test** (`test_programmatic_selection_returns_attribute_dictionary`): Uses `AppTest.from_function` to exercise both the `value_changed` path (second run sets selection via session state) and the normal path (third run verifies persistence). Asserts attribute-style access works, which would fail with `AttributeError` on the old code.
- **E2E test**: Adds `expect_prefixed_markdown` assertions in the existing `test_programmatic_row_selection_via_session_state` test to verify attribute access in the full stack, covering both the initial programmatic selection and after changing selection via a button click.
- Updated snapshots for `st_dataframe-programmatic_col_cell_selection`

## Summary

This PR fixes [#14454](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/14454) where programmatically setting dataframe selection state via `st.session_state` returned a plain `dict` instead of an `AttributeDictionary`, causing `AttributeError` when users accessed selection attributes (e.g., `event.selection.rows`).

The fix introduces a `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` class in `lib/streamlit/util.py` that extends `AttributeDictionary` with read-only enforcement, and wraps all return paths in `lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py` to consistently produce this type. Comprehensive unit tests and an E2E regression test are included.

**Reviewed by:** claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking (APPROVED), gemini-3.1-pro (CHANGES_REQUESTED), gpt-5.3-codex-high (CHANGES_REQUESTED). All three expected reviewers completed their reviews.

## Code Quality

The implementation is clean and follows existing patterns in `streamlit.util`. The `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` class properly extends `AttributeDictionary`, maintaining `isinstance` compatibility, and comprehensively blocks mutation methods (`__setitem__`, `__delitem__`, `clear`, `pop`, `popitem`, `setdefault`, `update`). The `__copy__`/`__deepcopy__` implementations are correct.

**All three reviewers flagged a gap in read-only enforcement for dict-style nested access.** Currently, `__getattr__` wraps nested dicts dynamically (line 132 of `util.py`), but `__getitem__` and `get()` inherit from `dict` and return the raw inner dict. This means `d["selection"]["rows"] = [3, 4]` silently succeeds, bypassing read-only protection. Two reviewers (gemini, gpt) considered this blocking; one (claude) acknowledged it as an acceptable trade-off. The fix — wrapping nested dicts at init time — is small, clean, and closes the gap (see inline comment for the suggested approach).

Additionally, the error message string `_READ_ONLY_ERROR_MSG` uses `{{` and `}}` in a plain string literal (not an f-string), causing these to display literally as doubled

## Summary

This PR fixes [#14454](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/14454) where programmatic dataframe selections via `st.session_state` returned a plain `dict` instead of an `AttributeDictionary`, causing `AttributeError` when users accessed selection attributes (e.g., `event.selection.rows`).

The fix introduces a `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` class (extending `AttributeDictionary`) and uses it in all return paths of `st.dataframe` when `on_select` is enabled. Beyond restoring attribute-style access, the read-only enforcement prevents users from modifying widget state in-place — mutations that previously silently succeeded but never triggered UI updates — and provides a clear error message guiding them toward correct usage patterns.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed this is a focused, well-implemented bug fix. Two approved outright; one requested changes based on a mutable-leaf limitation assessed here as non-blocking (see Recommendations).

## Code Quality

The code is well-structured and follows existing Streamlit patterns. All three reviewers confirmed high code quality:

- `ReadOnlyAttributeDictionary` properly inherits from `AttributeDictionary`, preserving backward compatibility for `isinstance` checks.
- All dict mutation operations are comprehensively blocked (`__setitem__`, `__delitem__`, `clear`, `pop`, `popitem`, `setdefault`, `update`, `__ior__`, `__setattr__`).
- Custom `__copy__` and `__deepcopy__` ensure the read-only wrapper survives copy operations.
- The `_READ_ONLY_ERROR_MSG` constant is clear, actionable, and follows project conventions for private module-level declarations.
- The three return paths in `arrow.py` (`deserialize`, programmatic selection, normal widget state) are consistently wrapped.

One minor optimization opportunity exists in `__getattr__` (covered in inline comments): the current implementation creates unnecessary intermediate
LGTM

fix: make dataframe selection state read-only to prevent silent failures

When users tried to modify selections via nested assignment like

Adds/extends Python unit tests to raise coverage across several Streamlit utility modules, supporting the repo’s 90%+ coverage goal without changing production behavior.

**Changes:**

<details>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/write_test.py` | Adds coverage for `st.write` handling `io.StringIO`. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/url_util_test.py` | Adds a regression test ensuring GitHub `.../raw/...` URLs remain unchanged. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/string_util_test.py` | Adds edge-case tests for emoji/material icon validation, binary detection, and `from_number` error paths. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/errors_test.py` | New tests covering exception/warning classes and key message formatting behaviors in `streamlit.errors`. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/lib/image_utils_test.py` | New tests covering internal image utilities: format selection, numpy shape validation, and clipping behavior. |
</details>

## Summary

This PR adds new unit tests and extends existing ones to improve Python code coverage for `errors.py`, `image_utils.py`, `string_util.py`, `url_util.py`, and `st.write` (StringIO handling). Additionally, the PR removes the Windows-specific file-watcher stability check from `event_based_path_watcher.py` along with its corresponding tests. While the test additions are well-structured, both individual reviewers characterized this as a "test-only" PR, overlooking the production code removal in the watcher module.

**Reviewer consensus:** 2 of 3 expected models completed reviews (gemini-3.1-pro and gpt-5.3-codex-high). claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking failed to complete its review. gemini-3.1-pro requested changes (local import nit); gpt-5.3-codex-high approved. Both reviewers agreed on code quality, no security risk, no external-test need, and no accessibility impact — but both missed the production code change.

## Code Quality

The test additions are well-structured, following project conventions with clear docstrings, appropriate assertions, and good edge-case coverage. Minor style issue: `from io import StringIO` is imported locally inside two test functions in `write_test.py` (lines 779, 791) rather than at the top of the file, which contradicts `lib/tests/AGENTS.md` guidelines. This is a low-severity nit covered by the inline comment.

**Production code change (missed by both reviewers):** The diff removes ~68 lines of production code from `lib/streamlit/watcher/event_based_path_watcher.py`, specifically the Windows-specific stability check that mitigated spurious file-change events from Windows Defender, Search Indexer, and OneDrive (linked to issue #13954). The corresponding `_WINDOWS_STABILITY_DELAY_SECS` constant and the module-level docstring describing the Windows considerations are also removed, along with ~145 lines of related tests. This is a functional behavior change on Windows, not a test-only change.

## Test Coverage

title: [Fix] OpenMetrics compliance for `session_duration` metric
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes the Prometheus scraping error:
> Error scraping target: unit "seconds" not a suffix of metrics "session_duration_seconds_total"

The [OpenMetrics spec](https://github.com/OpenObservability/OpenMetrics/blob/main/specification/OpenMetrics.md) requires the UNIT value to be a suffix of the MetricFamily name. The session duration counter was using `session_duration_seconds_total` as both the family name and the sample name, so the declared unit `seconds` wasn't a valid suffix (it's buried before `_total`).

### What changed
- Renamed the `SESSION_DURATION_FAMILY` constant from `"session_duration_seconds_total"` to `"session_duration_seconds"`.
- Added an optional `sample_name` field to `CounterStat` so counter samples can use a name distinct from the family metadata name. When unset, behavior is unchanged.
- The session duration producer now sets `sample_name="session_duration_seconds_total"` so the emitted sample line keeps the required `_total` suffix while the family metadata uses the canonical name.
- No changes to the serializers — they already separate metadata (`family_name`) from sample rendering (`to_metric_str()`).
### Scope notes
- Only the broken unitful counter (`session_duration`) is renamed. `session_events_total` is technically non-canonical per OpenMetrics but does not cause a Prometheus failure, so it is left unchanged to limit the API-visible break.
- The pre-existing issue of emitting `# UNIT` lines for unitless metrics is tracked separately.


## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)
Closes #14432 

## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python): ✅ Added/Updated
- Manual testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes OpenMetrics unit compliance for the `session_duration` counter by separating the metric *family name* (used for `# TYPE`/`# UNIT` metadata and protobuf `MetricFamily.name`) from the emitted counter *sample name* (which must keep the `_total` suffix).

**Changes:**
- Rename `SESSION_DURATION_FAMILY` from `session_duration_seconds_total` → `session_duration_seconds` to make the declared unit (`seconds`) a valid suffix of the family name.
- Extend `CounterStat` with an optional `sample_name` override so emitted samples can differ from family metadata while preserving existing behavior when unset.
- Update runtime producer and tests to emit `session_duration_seconds_total` samples under the `session_duration_seconds` family for both text and protobuf outputs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/stats.py | Renames `SESSION_DURATION_FAMILY` and adds `CounterStat.sample_name` to decouple family metadata name from emitted sample name. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/websocket_session_manager.py | Emits the session duration counter with canonical family name + `_total` sample name override. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/stats_test.py | Adds coverage for `CounterStat.sample_name` and verifies default remains `None`. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/websocket_session_manager_test.py | Updates assertions to the new family name and validates the `_total` sample name is still emitted. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/stats_handler_test.py | Adds/updates endpoint tests to verify canonical family metadata vs `_total` sample name in both text and protobuf formats. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_app_test.py | Mirrors the same assertions for the Starlette server implementation (text + protobuf). |
</details>

## Summary

This PR fixes an OpenMetrics compliance error where Prometheus scrapers reject the `session_duration_seconds_total` metric with: `unit "seconds" not a suffix of metrics "session_duration_seconds_total"`. Per the OpenMetrics spec, the declared UNIT must be a suffix of the MetricFamily name.

The fix renames `SESSION_DURATION_FAMILY` from `"session_duration_seconds_total"` to `"session_duration_seconds"` and introduces an optional `sample_name` field on `CounterStat` so the emitted sample line can still use the required `_total` suffix while the family metadata uses the canonical (unit-suffixed) name.

**Reviewer consensus**: All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved the PR unanimously. There were no disagreements on the approach, correctness, or scope of the change.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agree the implementation is clean, minimal, and well-structured:

- Adding `sample_name` as an optional field on a `NamedTuple` with a `None` default preserves full backward compatibility. All existing call sites use keyword arguments, so the new field at the end is safe.
- `to_metric_str()` cleanly falls back to `family_name` when `sample_name` is `None`, preserving existing behavior for all other counters.
- `marshall_metric_proto()` correctly does **not** use `sample_name` — in protobuf format, the family name already identifies the metric family with no separate "sample name" concept.
- The scope is well-contained: only the broken unitful counter is renamed, and the PR description explains why `session_events_total` is intentionally left unchanged.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers rated test coverage as excellent/strong. The PR adds and updates tests across all layers:

- **`stats_test.py`**: New unit tests for `CounterStat.to_metric_str()` with `sample_name` (with and without labels), plus assertion that `sample_name is None` by default.
- **`websocket_session_manager_test.py`**: All references to the o

title: [feature] Add `autoscroll` parameter to `st.container`
body:
## Describe your changes

Add an `autoscroll: bool | None = None` parameter to `st.container()` that allows users to explicitly control the scroll-to-bottom behavior for fixed-height containers.

**Changes:**
- `autoscroll=True`: Force enable auto-scroll for any content (not just chat messages)
- `autoscroll=False`: Force disable auto-scroll even when chat messages are present
- `autoscroll=None` (default): Preserve existing behavior - auto-scroll only when container has chat messages

**Implementation:**
- Backend: Added `autoscroll` parameter to `st.container()` in `layouts.py`
- Protobuf: Added `optional bool autoscroll` field to `Block.proto`
- Frontend: Updated `shouldActivateScrollToBottom()` in `utils.ts` to check explicit autoscroll setting

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #8836

## Testing Plan

- [x] Frontend Unit Tests - Parameterized tests for all autoscroll scenarios
