reviews.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an `autoscroll: bool | None = None` parameter to `st.container()`, giving users explicit control over scroll-to-bottom behavior in fixed-height containers. The implementation spans all layers: a new `optional bool autoscroll = 16` protobuf field in `Block.proto`, backend parameter handling in `layouts.py`, updated frontend logic in `shouldActivateScrollToBottom()`, and comprehensive tests (Python unit, frontend unit, and E2E). The default (`None`) preserves existing behavior — auto-scroll only activates when chat messages are present.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) completed their reviews successfully.

## Code Quality

**Consensus: Clean and well-structured.** All three reviewers agreed the implementation is cleanly scoped and follows existing Streamlit patterns.

Specific strengths noted across reviews:
- The `autoscroll` parameter is correctly keyword-only with a `None` default.
- The protobuf field uses the correct next available ID (16) and the "Next ID" comment is properly updated from the stale value of 14 to 17.
- The frontend refactor of `shouldActivateScrollToBottom()` improves readability with an early return for the no-pixel-height case, and correctly handles `null`/`undefined` for protobuf optional field semantics.
- The backend only sets the proto field when explicitly provided (`if autoscroll is not None`), preserving clean wire format.

**One documentation issue identified** (unanimous across all reviewers): the RST substitution `|st.chat_message|_` in the docstring is missing its corresponding replacement definition. See inline comment for details.

## Test Coverage

**Consensus: Solid and adequate.** All reviewers agreed the test coverage is comprehensive across all three layers:

- **Python unit tests** (3 tests): Cover `autoscroll=True`, `autoscroll=False`, and `autoscroll=None` (default), including proto field presence/absence checks and the without-height edge ca

## Pull request overview

Adds an `autoscroll: bool | None = None` parameter to `st.container()` to let users explicitly enable/disable the “scroll to bottom” behavior for fixed-height containers, while preserving the existing default behavior when unset.

**Changes:**
- Adds `optional bool autoscroll` to the `Block` protobuf.
- Plumbs the new `autoscroll` parameter through the Python backend `st.container()` API into the block proto.
- Updates frontend scroll-activation logic + unit/E2E coverage for explicit autoscroll settings.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Block.proto | Adds `autoscroll` field to the `Block` message to transport the setting to the frontend. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/layouts.py | Adds `autoscroll` parameter to `st.container()` and serializes it into the block proto when explicitly set. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py | Adds unit tests validating proto serialization behavior for `autoscroll`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/utils.ts | Updates `shouldActivateScrollToBottom` to respect explicit `autoscroll` when present. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/utils.test.ts | Adds parameterized tests covering explicit `autoscroll` scenarios and defaults. |
| e2e_playwright/st_container.py | Adds app script cases to exercise explicit `autoscroll=True/False`. |
| e2e_playwright/st_container_test.py | Adds an E2E test asserting the rendered scroll behavior attribute for explicit `autoscroll`. |
</details>

## Summary

This PR adds an `autoscroll` parameter (`bool | None = None`) to `st.container()`, giving users explicit control over scroll-to-bottom behavior in fixed-height containers. Previously, auto-scroll was hard-coded to activate only when a container had both a fixed pixel height and `st.chat_message` children. Now:

- `autoscroll=True` forces auto-scroll for any fixed-height container.
- `autoscroll=False` disables auto-scroll even when chat messages are present.
- `autoscroll=None` (default) preserves existing behavior.

Changes span all layers: protobuf (`Block.proto`), Python backend (`layouts.py`), frontend logic (`utils.ts`), with coverage from Python unit tests, frontend unit tests, and E2E Playwright tests.

**Reviewer consensus: 3/3 reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved this PR unanimously. No critical or blocking issues were raised.**

## Code Quality

All three reviewers agreed the implementation is clean, minimal, and follows existing Streamlit codebase patterns:

- **Protobuf**: The new `optional bool autoscroll = 16` field on `Block` is correctly placed. The "Next ID" comment is properly updated.
- **Backend**: The Python code correctly uses protobuf optionality — only setting the field when `autoscroll is not None`, so the default case sends no value over the wire.
- **Frontend**: The `shouldActivateScrollToBottom` refactor cleanly handles the precedence of the explicit `autoscroll` setting over the implicit chat message check. The `autoscroll !== null && autoscroll !== undefined` guard properly handles protobuf optionality semantics.
- **Docstring**: Parameter documentation follows the existing NumPy-style convention with proper RST cross-references.

No maintainability concerns were identified by any reviewer.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers confirmed adequate test coverage across all layers:

- **Python unit tests** (`layouts_test.py`): 3 test cases covering `True`/`False` with `HasField` asse
LGTM, I like the test artifacts!

Add an `autoscroll: bool | None = None` parameter to `st.container()` that
allows users to explicitly control the scroll-to-bottom behavior for
fixed-height containers.

- `autoscroll=True`: Force enable auto-scroll for any content
- `autoscroll=False`: Force disable auto-scroll even with chat messages
- `autoscroll=None` (default): Preserve existing behavior

Closes #8836

**Changes:**
- Import and use `getPopoverContainerStyle` for the main menu `StatefulPopover` Body override.

## Summary

This PR applies the shared `getPopoverContainerStyle` utility to the MainMenu popover in `MainMenu.tsx`, replacing a hardcoded `boxShadow: theme.shadows.popover` style. This aligns the main menu's visual treatment with other popover/dropdown menus across the codebase (Selectbox, Multiselect, DateInput, DataFrame menus, st.popover, TopNavSection, etc.). The practical effect is adding a visible border around the main menu in dark mode, where the shadow alone was insufficient to visually distinguish the popover from the background.

The diff is a 2-line change in a single file: one new import and one style-object swap.

## Code Quality

All three reviewers unanimously agree the change is minimal, clean, and well-targeted. It follows established patterns — at least 10 other components in the codebase already use `getPopoverContainerStyle`. The import is placed in the correct alphabetical position, and the spread syntax `...getPopoverContainerStyle(theme)` is idiomatic and consistent with how `TopNavSection.tsx` and `Popover.tsx` use the same function in their BaseWeb `Body` style overrides. No issues identified.

- **Unit tests**: The existing 108 `MainMenu.test.tsx` tests pass. No unit tests assert on inline style values for the popover Body override, so no changes are needed.

One reviewer (gpt-5.3-codex-high) suggested adding a focused unit test assertion for dark-mode main menu popover styling (e.g., border presence) to guard against future visual regressions. This is a reasonable but non-blocking suggestion — the existing E2E snapshot tests already cover visual appearance.

## Summary

This PR standardizes the main menu popover styling by replacing a component-specific `boxShadow` override with the shared `getPopoverContainerStyle(theme)` helper in `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/MainMenu.tsx`. This aligns the main menu with all other popover-based menus in the codebase (dropdowns, dataframe menus, color picker, navigation popover, etc.). The practical visual effect is a visible border around the menu in dark mode; light mode is visually unchanged. The PR also updates 9 E2E snapshot images across 3 browsers covering both themes and the custom-fonts test.

**Reviewer consensus:** All 3 of 3 reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved with no changes requested and no inline comments.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the code quality is excellent. The change is small, focused, and follows the established pattern precisely — `TopNavSection.tsx` already uses the identical `...getPopoverContainerStyle(theme)` spread. The import comes from the existing `@streamlit/lib` barrel export. No new code or abstractions are introduced; this is purely a consistency alignment that improves maintainability by removing a one-off style override.

## Test Coverage

Coverage is adequate for this UI-only change:

- **Unit tests**: The existing 108 `MainMenu.test.tsx` unit tests continue to pass. They don't assert specific CSS styles on the popover body, so no unit test changes are needed.

No breaking changes. All reviewers agreed this is a purely cosmetic CSS change to an internal UI element. No public API, protobuf, or behavioral changes are invol

title: [spec] Configurable filter mode for selectbox & multiselect
body:
## Describe your changes

Spec renderer: https://issues.streamlit.app/spec_renderer?pr=14507

Product spec for adding a `filter_mode` parameter to `st.selectbox` and `st.multiselect` to control how user input filters dropdown options.

- Proposes `filter_mode` parameter with values: `"fuzzy"` (default), `"contains"`, `"prefix"`, `"exact"`, or `None`
- Addresses user requests for exact/strict matching when working with IDs, part numbers, and codes
- Allows disabling filtering entirely for short lists (Yes/No selections)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Adresses #7238
- Adresses #6160

## Testing Plan

- Spec document only, no code changes

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 1 |
| skill | writing-spec | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a product spec proposing a new `filter_mode` parameter for `st.selectbox` and `st.multiselect` to control how typed input filters dropdown options (including an option to disable filtering).

**Changes:**
- Defines the proposed `filter_mode` API surface and enumerated modes.
- Specifies intended matching/sorting behavior per mode plus interactions with `accept_new_options`.
- Documents rationale, alternatives considered, and rollout checklist items.

## Summary

This PR adds a product spec for a new `filter_mode` parameter on `st.selectbox` and `st.multiselect`. The parameter allows developers to control how user-typed text filters dropdown options, supporting modes: `"fuzzy"` (default/current behavior), `"contains"`, `"prefix"`, `"exact"`, and `None` (disable filtering). The spec addresses real user pain points documented in GitHub issues #7238 and #6160. No code changes are included — this is a spec-only PR.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved this PR. The reviewers agreed on all major aspects: the spec is well-structured, the API is backwards-compatible, there are no security concerns, and no external tests are needed. The only divergence was in depth of feedback — one reviewer provided detailed improvement suggestions while the other two found no issues requiring changes.

## Code Quality

The spec is well-structured and follows the established product spec template in `specs/AGENTS.md`. It includes a clear problem statement with linked GitHub issues, concrete API examples with progressive complexity, a comprehensive behavior matrix, thorough alternatives-considered section with rationale, and a complete checklist. All three reviewers agreed on the high quality of the document.

**Minor gaps identified (by one reviewer, not raised by others):**

1. **`format_func` interaction is unspecified.** The spec does not state whether filtering operates on the `format_func`-transformed display label or the raw option value. The current frontend implementation filters on the label, and this should be documented explicitly — especially important for `"exact"` mode.

2. **`"exact"` mode partial-typing UX.** With `"exact"` requiring full string equality, typing a partial string shows zero results. A brief note explaining that this mode targets paste-and-verify workflows (not incremental typing) would set expectations.

3. **`st.radio` not mentioned

commits.message:
Add product spec for selectbox/multiselect filter_mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use emoji checkboxes in product spec checklist

fix: address AI review feedback on filter_mode spec

- Correct fuzzy mode description to "in-order subsequence" instead of "any order"
- Change "exact" mode from substring match to full string equality match
- Update matching behavior table to reflect exact mode as equality
- Clarify accept_new_options interaction with exact string matching
- Update case sensitivity decision note for clarity
- Fix Mobile UX pain point to acknowledge existing read-only behavior
- Align checklist format with template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change exact mode to case-sensitive prefix match

Update "exact" filter_mode from full string equality to case-sensitive
prefix matching, which better supports ID/code workflows where users
progressively narrow results while preserving case sensitivity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove unnecessary dividers from filter_mode spec

The initial implementation will include fuzzy, contains, prefix, and None.

title: Fix #11308: update server.port after binding to port 0
body:
## Describe your changes
This PR fixes an issue where Streamlit displays incorrect URLs when `server.port=0`.

When port 0 is used, Tornado binds the server to an ephemeral port chosen by the operating system. However, the configuration value `server.port` remained 0, which caused the CLI to display URLs such as `http://localhost:0`.

This patch updates `server.port` with the actual bound port after the TCP socket is created so that the displayed URLs reflect the real listening port.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

Not applicable.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes #11308

## Testing Plan

The bug occurs specifically when `server.port` is set to 0 and the OS assigns an ephemeral port. A unit test was added to verify that the configuration is updated with the real port once the socket is bound. 

### Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)

Added the test:

`test_updates_configured_port_when_binding_to_zero`

in:

`lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/server_test.py`

This test verifies that when `server.port=0`, the configuration value is updated to the actual assigned port.

### E2E Tests

No E2E tests are required because this change only affects the internal server configuration logic and CLI output.



---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: joanaarnauth

reviews.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes [#11308](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/11308) where Streamlit displays `http://localhost:0` in the CLI when `server.port=0` is used to request an OS-assigned ephemeral port. The fix replaces `http_server.listen(port, address)` with the two-step `tornado.netutil.bind_sockets(port, address)` + `http_server.add_sockets(sockets)` pattern, which allows inspecting the actual bound port and updating `server.port` in the config so that displayed URLs reflect the real listening port. Existing port rotation tests are refactored to mock `bind_sockets` instead of `http_server.listen`, and a new `DynamicPortTest` validates the port-0 behavior with a real socket.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved the PR unanimously. No critical or blocking issues were raised.

## Code Quality

The code is clean and follows Streamlit's existing patterns. All three reviewers agreed that the `bind_sockets()` + `add_sockets()` pattern is the idiomatic Tornado approach for port discovery — Tornado's `HTTPServer.listen()` internally calls exactly these two functions, so the refactor is safe and functionally equivalent.

The test refactoring is a net improvement: the old `PortRotateOneTest` had a stale class-level `which_port` mock that was never exercised by the code under test. The new tests are more focused, correctly assert against `bind_sockets` call arguments, and verify that `add_sockets` is not called when binding fails.

One minor cleanup opportunity was identified by one reviewer: `start_listening_unix_socket` (around line 241 of `server.py`) still contains a local `import tornado.netutil` that is now redundant since the module-level import was added on line 24. This is harmless but could be cleaned up for consistency.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers agreed that test coverage is adequate:

- **`DynamicPortTest.test_updates_configured_port_when_binding_to_zero`** —
Looks good to me, will approve once the two small changes are made, thanks!
LGTM, thank you for the contribution @joanaarnauth

commits.message:
Fix #11308: update server.port after binding to port 0

When Streamlit is started with server.port=0, the OS assigns an
ephemeral port. However, the configured server.port value remained 0,
which caused the CLI to display URLs such as http://localhost:0.

This patch updates server.port with the actual bound port after the
socket is created, so the displayed URLs reflect the real listening
port.

A unit test was added to verify that server.port is updated when
binding to port 0.
Use Tornado bind_sockets API and update server port rotation tests
Merge branch 'develop' into fix/11308-port-0-url
Address review feedback: remove redundant import and simplify test cleanup

## Pull request overview

Updates the internal “sharing-pr-agent-artifacts” skill documentation to use the new agent wiki explorer URL format when linking uploaded artifacts from `streamlit.wiki`.

**Changes:**
- Replace the raw GitHub wiki URL pattern with `https://issues.streamlit.app/agent_wiki_explorer?file=...`
- Update the example “Added Agent Docs” PR comment links to the new format

## Summary

This PR updates `.claude/skills/sharing-pr-agent-artifacts/SKILL.md` to replace the old raw GitHub wiki URL format (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wiki/streamlit/streamlit/<path>`) with the new `https://issues.streamlit.app/agent_wiki_explorer?file=<relative-path>` format. Four URL references are updated: the canonical public URL pattern, the inline example, and two links in the example PR comment template. The change is documentation-only and scoped to internal agent guidance.

## Code Quality

The edits are clean, concise, and internally consistent — all four URL references in the file are updated to the new format. The new URL pattern uses `<relative-path>` instead of `<path>`, which is clearer since the path is relative to the wiki root.

All three reviewers independently identified that `AGENTS.md` (line 86) still references the old URL pattern, and since `.github/copilot-instructions.md` and `.cursor/rules/overview.mdc` are auto-generated from `AGENTS.md`, those files are also stale. This is the only consistency concern with this PR.

## Test Coverage

No tests are needed — this is a documentation-only change to an internal AI agent skill file with no behavioral or runtime impact. All reviewers agreed on this assessment.

## Backwards Compatibility

No breaking changes. This affects internal contributor/agent documentation only and does not touch any user-facing APIs, features, or behavior.

## Security & Risk

No security concerns or regression risks. The change swaps one public URL pattern for another in a markdown file. No code execution, authentication, data handling, or dependency changes are involved.

## External test recommendation

- Recommend external_test: No
- Triggered categories: None
- Evidence:
  - `.claude/skills/sharing-pr-agent-artifacts/SKILL.md`: Documentation-only URL text replacement; no runtime code, network stack, auth, embedding, storage, or header logic changes.
- Suggested external_test focus areas: None
- Confidence

## Summary

This PR updates the wiki URL pattern used in internal agent documentation from the raw GitHub wiki URL format (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wiki/streamlit/streamlit/<path>`) to the new `issues.streamlit.app/agent_wiki_explorer` format (`https://issues.streamlit.app/agent_wiki_explorer?file=<relative-path>`). The change is applied consistently across four documentation/configuration files:

- `.claude/skills/sharing-pr-agent-artifacts/SKILL.md` (the canonical skill definition)
- `.cursor/rules/overview.mdc`
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- `AGENTS.md`

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved with no issues raised.

## Code Quality

The changes are clean, minimal, and consistent across all affected files. The placeholder variable was improved from `<path>` to `<relative-path>`, which better communicates that the query parameter expects a path relative to the wiki root (e.g., `pull-requests/12345/implementation-plan.md`). Synchronization between `AGENTS.md` and generated files (`.cursor/rules/overview.mdc`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`) is preserved, avoiding drift.

**Agreement:** All reviewers agreed the edits are focused, correct, and well-synchronized.

## Test Coverage

No tests were added or modified. All reviewers agreed this is appropriate — the change is purely documentation with no executable runtime behavior to validate.

## Backwards Compatibility

No backwards compatibility concerns. These files are internal agent instructions that do not affect end-user APIs, protobufs, or library behavior. All reviewers agreed unanimously.

## Security & Risk

No security concerns. The change points to `issues.streamlit.app`, a Streamlit-owned domain. No code execution, credential handling, or external dependency changes are involved. Regression risk is negligible, limited to documentation correctness.

**Agreement:** All reviewers agreed there is no s
LGTM

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a mypy typing regression test for `st.color_picker` so the type checker continues to infer the correct return type (`str`) across common parameter combinations. This fits into the existing `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/` suite that validates Streamlit’s public API typing behavior via `assert_type`.

**Changes:**
- Add a new mypy `assert_type` test module for `st.color_picker`.
- Cover return type inference for common parameters (value/help/disabled/label_visibility/width/bind/on_change and callback args/kwargs).

## Summary

This PR adds a new mypy `assert_type` typing test file for `st.color_picker` at `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/color_picker_types.py`. The test verifies that the return type of `color_picker` is correctly inferred as `str` across all parameter combinations, including basic usage, `value`, `help`, `disabled`, `label_visibility`, `width`, `bind`, and callback parameters.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) independently approved this PR with no issues identified.

## Code Quality

**Unanimous agreement**: The new file closely follows established conventions from other typing test files in `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/` (e.g., `checkbox_types.py`, `text_input_types.py`). All reviewers confirmed it correctly:

- Uses the `if TYPE_CHECKING:` guard so assertions are never executed at runtime.
- Imports from the `ColorPickerMixin` class directly.
- Includes `from __future__ import annotations`.
- Covers all parameters in the `ColorPickerMixin.color_picker` signature.

Structure and readability are good, and the test intent is clear.

## Test Coverage

**Unanimous agreement**: Coverage is thorough and appropriate for the scope. All parameters from the `color_picker` API are covered: `label`, `value`, `key`, `help`, `on_change`, `args`, `kwargs`, `disabled`, `label_visibility`, `width`, and `bind`, plus a full-parameter combination test. Since `color_picker` always returns `str` regardless of input, the typing surface is straightforward and adequately covered. No additional runtime unit/e2e tests are required for this test-only change.

## Backwards Compatibility

**Unanimous agreement**: No backwards compatibility concerns. This is a purely additive test file with no changes to library code, public APIs, or protobuf definitions.

## Security & Risk

**Unanimous agreement**: No security concerns. The file is a static type-checking artifact that is never executed at runtime. No new dependencies, network calls, or ru

## Summary

This PR adds a new mypy `assert_type` typing test file for `st.color_picker` at `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/color_picker_types.py`. The test verifies that all parameter combinations of `ColorPickerMixin.color_picker` correctly resolve to a `str` return type. No runtime code, frontend code, or protobuf definitions are changed.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved unanimously with no concerns raised.

## Code Quality

**Reviewer consensus: Strong.** All reviewers agree the file is well-structured and follows established patterns from existing typing tests (e.g., `checkbox_types.py`, `text_input_types.py`). Specifically noted:

- Uses `from __future__ import annotations` at the top.
- All assertions and imports are correctly placed inside a `TYPE_CHECKING` block.
- Imports the Mixin class directly (`ColorPickerMixin`) rather than `import streamlit as st`.
- Does not use `def test_*()` functions (mypy-checked, not pytest-executed).
- Section headers with comment separators provide clear organization.
- Parameter coverage is comprehensive: `label`, `value`, `key`, `help`, `disabled`, `label_visibility`, `width`, `bind`, `on_change`/`args`/`kwargs`, and a full-combination test.
- All `assert_type(..., str)` assertions are correct — `color_picker` always returns `str`.

## Test Coverage

This PR adds typing tests only. The `st.color_picker` widget already has runtime unit tests and e2e coverage elsewhere. The new typing tests add a valuable layer of verification for the public API's type annotations, catching potential regressions in parameter or return type signatures.

No additional runtime unit tests or e2e tests are needed for this change. All reviewers concur.

## Backwards Compatibility

No backwards compatibility concerns. This PR adds a new test file and does not modify any library code, public API, protobuf definitions, or frontend components. All reviewers agree.

## Security & Risk

No secu

title: Bump knip from 5.88.1 to 6.0.0 in /frontend
body:
Bumps [knip](https://github.com/webpro-nl/knip/tree/HEAD/packages/knip) from 5.88.1 to 6.0.0.
Release notes

Sourced from knip's releases.

Release 6.0.0

Blog post: Announcing Knip v6

See the pre-releases for details:

  • https://github.com/webpro-nl/knip/releases/tag/knip%406.0.0-0
  • https://github.com/webpro-nl/knip/releases/tag/knip%406.0.0-1
  • https://github.com/webpro-nl/knip/releases/tag/knip%406.0.0-2
  • https://github.com/webpro-nl/knip/releases/tag/knip%406.0.0-3

Breaking changes:

  • Dropped support for Node.js v18 → Knip v6 requires Node.js v20.19.0 or newer
  • Dropped issue type classMembers
  • Dropped --include-libs → this is now the default and only behavior
  • Dropped --isolate-workspaces → this is now the default and only behavior
  • Dropped --experimental-tags → use --tags
  • In reporter functions, issues.files is consistent with other issue shapes. Removed issues._files.
  • In the JSON reporter, issues are consistently arrays for any issue type. Removed root files.

Release 6.0.0-3

  • Improve Nuxt plugin (staticExports → ExportNamedDeclaration) (6554251a8)
  • Improve isReferencedInUsedExport items (d4d996758)

Release 6.0.0-2

  • Bump GitHub actions to their latest versions (#1624) (bf2ffca61)
  • Use resolveFromAST to skip jiti loading for JS/TS configs (91cb6f662)
  • Use resolveFromAST for flat configs, skip jiti loading (5e5f63c45)

  • Dedupe plugin visitor registration across workspaces (5b9df9f9f)
  • Consistent object shapes in JSON reporter (b10d87efd)

title: [fix] Component template E2E tests failing due to build 1.4.1
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes the component template E2E tests that started failing after `build` 1.4.1 was released on March 24, 2026.

**Root cause:** Build 1.4.1 introduced a regression ([pypa/build#1004](https://github.com/pypa/build/issues/1004)) where it incorrectly detects pip as usable in uv-created virtualenvs, then fails when trying to use `python -m pip` because pip isn't actually installed as a module.

**Fix:** Install pip in the venv before running the component-template actions.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Related upstream: https://github.com/pypa/build/issues/1004

## Testing Plan

- CI workflow should pass after this change
- No additional unit/E2E tests needed — this is a CI workflow fix

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes CI failures in the component-template E2E workflow caused by a `build` 1.4.1 regression when running inside uv-created virtualenvs that lack `pip`.

**Changes:**
- Install `pip` into the activated uv virtualenv before running the component-template build/E2E composite actions.

## Summary

This PR fixes the component-template E2E CI workflow (`.github/workflows/component-template-e2e-tests.yml`) that broke after `build` 1.4.1 was released. The upstream regression ([pypa/build#1004](https://github.com/pypa/build/issues/1004)) causes `python -m build` to incorrectly assume pip is available in uv-created virtualenvs, then fail when invoking `python -m pip`. The fix adds a single workflow step — `python -m ensurepip --upgrade` — to install pip into the venv before the component-template build actions run.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) independently approved this PR with no blocking issues identified.

## Code Quality

**Unanimous agreement across all reviewers.** The change is minimal, well-placed, and clearly documented:

- The inline workflow comments explain *why* pip is needed and link to the upstream issue for future traceability.
- Step placement is correct: after `make package` (which runs `uv sync` and would remove pip) but before `build_component_wheels` which invokes `python -m build`.
- `python -m ensurepip --upgrade` is the right tool — it's part of the Python standard library, doesn't depend on external packages, and is idempotent.
- No maintainability issues identified.

## Test Coverage

**All reviewers agree:** No additional unit or E2E tests are needed. This is a CI workflow fix — the workflow itself is the test infrastructure, and the component-template E2E test suite passing in CI validates this fix.

## Backwards Compatibility

**All reviewers agree:** No backwards compatibility concerns. The change is entirely internal to CI and does not affect the Streamlit library, public API, runtime, protobuf definitions, or any user-facing behavior.

## Security & Risk

**All reviewers agree:** No security concerns:

- The change is confined to a CI workflow file.
- `ensurepip` is a standard library module — no new external dependencies or third-party actions are introduced.
-

commits.message:
Fix component template E2E tests for build 1.4.1

Build 1.4.1 introduced a regression where it incorrectly detects pip
as usable in uv venvs. Install pip to work around this issue.

See: https://github.com/pypa/build/issues/1004

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use ensurepip instead of uv pip install for build compatibility

The previous approach using `uv pip install pip` did not properly make pip
available via `python -m pip`. The `build` package (v1.4.1+) requires pip
to be accessible this way for isolated builds. Using `python -m ensurepip`
reliably bootstraps pip into the venv.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Move pip installation after make package to prevent uv sync removal

The `make package` step runs `uv sync` which removes packages not in the
lockfile, including pip. Moving the ensurepip step to after make package
ensures pip remains available for the component-template build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [chore] Add skill for uploading intermediate files to wiki
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds infrastructure for agents to share intermediate artifacts (specs, plans, learnings) via the [streamlit.wiki](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit.wiki.git) repo, making them accessible in PR comments and descriptions.

### Changes

- **New `sharing-pr-agent-artifacts` skill**: Workflow for uploading agent-generated files to `pull-requests/<pr-number>/` in the wiki, with automatic PR commenting
- **`finalizing-pr` integration**: Automatically uploads intermediate files after PR creation
- **`make all-dev` enhancement**: Auto-clones wiki repo into `agent-wiki/` on first setup
- **Documentation updates**: Added `agent-wiki/` usage guidance to AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and IDE instruction files
- **`.gitignore` additions**: `agent-wiki/` directory for local wiki checkout

### How it works

1. Wiki is cloned to `agent-wiki/` (gitignored, persistent across sessions)
2. Agent artifacts are copied to `agent-wiki/pull-requests/<pr-number>/`
3. Files are committed and pushed to wiki master branch
4. PR comment is posted with links to uploaded docs

**Public URL pattern:**
```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wiki/streamlit/streamlit/pull-requests/<pr-number>/<filename>
```

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

Documentation and workflow changes only. Verified skill follows existing patterns and conventions.

title: Docs for URL pages and navigation
body:
## Describe your changes
* Use bullet list to better distinguish distinct items.
* Clarify limitations of URL pages (hidden or external considerations can be simplified to just: defined by URL or not)

There is a known bug #10572. In actuality, `st.switch_page` and `st.page_link` care more about the `url_path` than the page source. You can switch to the wrong page, or get rerouted to the homepage silently if you get a conflict in the URL pathname. If you use automatic URL paths inherited from the file and callable names, you probably won't notice, but as it stands, matching the `url_path` takes precedence. I just stated that you need to match both the source and path to avoid describing the current (unexpected) behavior of concocting a page with any source as long the the `url_path` matches where you want to go.

---
**Contribution License Agreement**
By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: MathCatsAnd

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates Streamlit’s public API documentation to better explain URL-defined pages and multipage navigation behavior, mainly by restructuring parameter descriptions into clearer bullet lists and refining the stated limitations around internal vs external pages.

**Changes:**
- Rewrites `st.Page` docstring sections to clarify what constitutes a page source (callable, Python file, or external URL) and how visibility affects access.
- Expands `st.page_link` and `st.switch_page` docstrings to more explicitly describe acceptable `page` inputs for `st.navigation` vs `pages/` directory apps.
- Minor doc polish (e.g., capitalization of “URL”) and reformats the `st.Page` example into an RST `code-block`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/navigation/page.py` | Refines `st.Page` docstring for page source types, visibility behavior, URL access note, and example formatting. |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/button.py` | Updates `st.link_button` and `st.page_link` docs, especially the `page` parameter semantics and URL-page behavior. |
| `lib/streamlit/commands/execution_control.py` | Updates `st.switch_page` docstring to clarify supported targets and `st.navigation` vs `pages/` behavior. |
LGTM 👍

title: feat: add generating-changelog skill
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR adds a new Claude skill for generating polished website changelog entries between git tags. The skill automates the process of creating release notes for docs.streamlit.io by:

- Fetching PR data between two git tags using GitHub's GraphQL API
- Filtering out bot PRs, release noise, and internal-only changes
- Categorizing PRs by labels (`change:breaking`, `change:feature`, `change:bugfix`, etc.)
- Identifying external contributors automatically
- Generating formatted changelog entries with proper emojis, API links, and attribution

The skill includes two Python scripts:
- `changelog_fetch_prs.py` - Extracts PR numbers from git log and batch-fetches metadata via GitHub GraphQL
- `changelog_categorize_prs.py` - Filters noise, categorizes by labels, and identifies external contributors

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- Manually tested locally

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new AI skill (`generating-changelog`) to help produce docs.streamlit.io-style website changelog entries between two git tags, backed by helper scripts that fetch and categorize PR metadata.

**Changes:**
- Added a script to extract PR numbers from `git log` between tags and batch-fetch PR metadata via GitHub GraphQL (`gh api graphql`).
- Added a script to filter “noise” PRs, categorize remaining PRs by `change:*` / `impact:*` labels, and flag external contributors.
- Registered the new skill in `CONTRIBUTING.md` and ensured it’s not excluded by `.claude/.gitignore`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `scripts/changelog_fetch_prs.py` | New script to validate tags, extract PR numbers, and fetch PR metadata via GitHub GraphQL. |
| `scripts/changelog_categorize_prs.py` | New script to exclude bots/release/internal-only PRs, categorize by labels, and summarize results. |
| `.claude/skills/generating-changelog/SKILL.md` | New skill instructions describing the end-to-end changelog workflow and formatting rules. |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Adds `generating-changelog` to the skills table. |
| `.claude/.gitignore` | Whitelists the new skill directory so it’s committed. |
</details>

## Summary

This PR adds a new Claude skill (`generating-changelog`) that automates the generation of website release notes for docs.streamlit.io. It introduces two Python helper scripts — `scripts/changelog_fetch_prs.py` (extracts PR numbers from `git log` and batch-fetches metadata via the GitHub GraphQL API) and `scripts/changelog_categorize_prs.py` (filters noise and categorizes PRs by labels) — along with a detailed `SKILL.md` with step-by-step instructions, and minor updates to `.claude/.gitignore` and `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## Code Quality

The code is well-structured, cleanly typed, and follows the repo's Python conventions. Responsibilities are clearly separated between the fetch and categorization scripts. Error handling is thoughtful — the fetch script gracefully handles GraphQL failures by falling back to individual queries, and properly skips non-PR numbers that get picked up from commit messages. The SKILL.md is thorough and well-organized, with detailed formatting rules that will produce consistent changelogs.

## Reviewer Consensus

**2 of 3 expected models completed reviews.** `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking` failed to complete its review.

| Reviewer | Verdict |
|---|---|
| gemini-3.1-pro | APPROVED |
| gpt-5.3-codex-high | CHANGES REQUESTED |

**Agreement:** Both reviewers agreed on: (1) the overall value of the feature, (2) no security concerns, (3) no backwards compatibility risk, (4) no need for external e2e tests, and (5) the code is well-organized and readable.

**Disagreement:** The reviewers differed on whether two issues in `changelog_fetch_prs.py` are blocking. gpt-5.3-codex-high flagged the `...` (three-dot) git range and the broad `#(\d+)` regex as "blocking correctness concerns." gemini-3.1-pro did not flag these and approved without reservations.

**Resolution:** After independent verification, neither issue is blocking:

1. **Three-dot range (`...`):** For Streamlit's release model where tags are on the same branch, `..` and `...` produc
LGTM 👍

commits.message: feat: add generating-chagelog skill
fix: address PR feedback

## Summary

Automated PR to update internal documentation based on AI review.

This PR was created by the `ai-update-docs` workflow using `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.

## Changes

# Documentation Review Summary

Reviewed all internal documentation files against the current codebase state.

## Scope

Reviewed 40+ markdown files across:
- `AGENTS.md` files (11 files)
- `README.md` files (10 files)
- `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (14 files)
- `.claude/agents/*.md` (3 files)
- `wiki/**/*.md` (5 files)

## Issues Found and Fixed

### 1. [BROKEN_LINK] `agent-knowledge/README.md`

- **Line 16**: Referenced `../wiki/creating-prs.md` which does not exist.
- **Fix**: Changed to `../wiki/pull-requests.md` (the actual file name).

### 2. [INCORRECT] `e2e_playwright/AGENTS.md`

- **Lines 14-17**: Used `test_results` (underscore) for the test results directory path in 4 places.
- **Actual**: The Playwright output directory is `test-results` (hyphen), as configured in `e2e_playwright/pytest.ini` (`--output ./test-results/`).

### 3. [INCORRECT] `wiki/running-e2e-tests.md`

- **Line 59**: Used `snapshot_updates` (underscore) for the snapshot updates subfolder.
- **Fix**: Changed to `snapshot-updates` (hyphen) to match actual directory name from `conftest.py`.

### 4. [OUTDATED] `CONTRIBUTING.md`

- **Line 88**: Referenced [Prettier](https://prettier.io) as a code formatter. Prettier is no longer a dependency in the project — it has been replaced by [oxfmt](https://github.com/nicolo-ribaudo/oxfmt) for frontend formatting.

- All 3 subagents listed in `CONTRIBUTING.md` match `.claude/agents/*.md` files
- Cross-references between wiki, AGENTS.md files, and skill files are consistent
- Node.js version in `.nvmrc` (v24) is used correctly in documentation

title: [fix] Add Windows stability check to file watcher to reduce false positives
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes #13954

On Windows, background processes (Windows Defender, Search Indexer, OneDrive) can trigger spurious file change events through the `ReadDirectoryChangesW` API used by the watchdog library. This can cause false "File change" notifications in Streamlit even when no actual modifications have been made.

This PR adds a Windows-specific stability check to the event-based file watcher:

- After detecting a potential file change (MD5 differs), wait 50ms on Windows
- Re-read the file's MD5 to verify the change is real
- If the MD5 reverts to the original value, treat it as a spurious event and ignore it
- If the MD5 stays changed, proceed with the normal change notification

The fix only affects Windows (no latency impact on macOS/Linux) and only runs when an MD5 difference is detected (not on every event).

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests - Added 3 new tests:
  - `test_windows_stability_check_filters_transient_changes` - verifies transient changes are ignored
  - `test_windows_stability_check_allows_real_changes` - verifies real changes are still detected
  - `test_non_windows_skips_stability_check` - verifies no impact on non-Windows platforms
- [ ] Manual testing on Windows (not available to author - requesting community verification)
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a Windows-only “stability” verification step to the event-based file watcher to reduce false-positive change notifications caused by spurious filesystem events on Windows.

**Changes:**
- After an MD5 mismatch on Windows, sleep briefly and re-hash to confirm the change is stable before notifying.
- Expands module documentation with Windows-specific rationale and behavior.
- Adds unit tests covering transient vs real Windows changes and the non-Windows behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/watcher/event_based_path_watcher.py` | Implements the Windows stability re-check and documents the Windows-specific motivation. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/watcher/event_based_path_watcher_test.py` | Adds unit tests validating Windows stability filtering and ensuring non-Windows behavior is unchanged. |
LGTM

## Summary

This PR adds a Windows-specific stability check to the event-based file watcher (`EventBasedPathWatcher`) to reduce false-positive "file changed" notifications caused by background processes (Windows Defender, Search Indexer, OneDrive). When an MD5 content change is detected on Windows, the watcher waits 50ms and re-reads the file hash. If the hash reverts to the original value, the event is treated as spurious and suppressed. No behavioral changes on macOS/Linux.

Changes span two files:
- `lib/streamlit/watcher/event_based_path_watcher.py` — stability check logic + docstring update
- `lib/tests/streamlit/watcher/event_based_path_watcher_test.py` — three new unit tests

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved the PR.

## Code Quality

The implementation is clean, well-scoped, and follows existing patterns in the file watcher module. All reviewers agreed on the high quality of the code:

- The stability check is correctly positioned in the event handling pipeline — after the initial MD5 change is detected but before the callback fires.
- Error handling is appropriate: if the verification MD5 calculation fails (`StreamlitMaxRetriesError`), the code gracefully falls back to using the initially computed MD5 rather than dropping the event.
- The `_WINDOWS_STABILITY_DELAY_SECS` constant is properly typed as `Final` and prefixed with `_`.
- Logging uses the existing `_LOGGER` at appropriate debug levels.
- The `new_md5 = verification_md5` update on line 548 correctly tracks the most recent known file state.

One reviewer (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking) noted that `time.sleep(0.05)` blocks the watchdog observer's event dispatch thread, which could compound during bulk file operations (e.g., `git checkout` switching many files). For typical use cases this is negligible; this is a minor behavioral consideration, not a blocker.

## Test Coverage

Three unit tests are added covering the primary sc

commits.message:
Add Windows stability check to file watcher to reduce false positives

On Windows, background processes (Windows Defender, Search Indexer,
OneDrive) can trigger spurious file change events through the
ReadDirectoryChangesW API used by watchdog. This causes false "File
change" notifications even when no actual modifications were made.

This adds a Windows-specific stability check:
- After detecting an MD5 change, wait 50ms on Windows
- Re-read the file's MD5 to verify the change is real
- If MD5 reverts to original, treat as spurious event and ignore
- If MD5 stays changed, proceed with normal change notification

The fix only affects Windows (no latency impact on macOS/Linux) and
only runs when an MD5 difference is detected (not on every event).

Fixes #13954

- Extract stability delay to _WINDOWS_STABILITY_DELAY_SECS constant

title: [feature] Add `single-row-required` selection mode to `st.dataframe`
body:
## Describe your changes Adds `single-row-required` selection mode to `st.dataframe` that ensures exactly one row is always selected (radio-like behavior): - Auto-selects the first row when no default is provided - Prevents clearing the selection (only allows changing to a different row) - Uses circle checkbox style for visual differentiation from square checkboxes - Hides the "Clear selection" toolbar button - [Demo](https://dataframe-single-row-required.streamlit.app/) - [Product spec](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/3a45f237403ad8de03ab3fdd14d2fc07fe228ed0/specs/2026-03-10-single-row-required-selection/product-spec.md) # Open Questions: - Circular vs Checkbox (everything else requires changes in the underlying library): ## Github Issues - Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9253 ## Testing Plan - [x] Unit Tests (Python): `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py` - [x] Unit Tests (TypeScript): `useSelectionHandler.test.ts`, `useWidgetState.test.ts` - [x] E2E Tests: `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_selections_test.py` --- **Contribution License Agreement** By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
Agent metrics | Type | Name | Count | |------|------|------:| | skill | addressing-pr-review-comments | 1 | | skill | checking-changes | 1 | | skill | fixing-flaky-e2e-tests | 1 | | skill | updating-internal-docs | 2 | | skill | writing-product-specs | 1 | | subagent | Explore | 1 | | subagent | fixing-pr | 5 | | subagent | general-purpose | 6 | | subagent | qa-testing-feature | 1 | | subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 2 | | subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 2 |
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.



<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_BEGIN -->
<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_END -->

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a new `st.dataframe` selection mode, `"single-row-required"`, to enforce radio-like behavior where exactly one row is always selected (defaulting to the first row) and clearing the selection is disallowed.

**Changes:**
- Extended the backend selection mode parsing/validation and selection-state serde to support `"single-row-required"` and auto-select row 0 when needed.
- Updated the frontend DataFrame selection handling/state initialization to prevent row deselection and render circle-style row markers.
- Added/updated unit and e2e coverage plus a product spec for the new mode.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| specs/2026-03-10-single-row-required-selection/product-spec.md | Product/behavior spec for required single-row selection mode. |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Dataframe.proto | Adds `SINGLE_ROW_REQUIRED` enum value to the Dataframe selection mode proto. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py | Backend support: new mode literals, validation, proto mapping, and auto-select row 0 in required mode. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py | Backend unit tests updated to include the new selection mode and required-mode validation behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useWidgetState.ts | Frontend widget initialization: auto-select row 0 for required mode when no stored/default selection exists. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useWidgetState.test.ts | Updates existing tests for new function signature (but missing coverage for required-mode branch). |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useSelectionHandler.ts | Frontend selection processing updated to prevent clearing row selection in required mode. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useSelect

## Summary

This PR introduces a new `single-row-required` selection mode for `st.dataframe` that ensures exactly one row is always selected, providing radio-button-like behavior. The implementation spans the full stack: a new protobuf enum value (`SINGLE_ROW_REQUIRED = 6`), Python backend validation and auto-selection logic in `arrow.py`, frontend selection handling in `useSelectionHandler.ts` and `useWidgetState.ts`, visual differentiation via circular checkbox markers in `DataFrame.tsx`, and comprehensive tests at all layers (Python unit, frontend unit, and E2E).

## Code Quality

The code is well-structured and follows existing patterns for selection handling throughout. The new mode integrates cleanly into the existing selection infrastructure with clear separation of concerns between the hook layer (`useSelectionHandler`, `useWidgetState`) and the component layer (`DataFrame`).

One correctness issue was identified in the sort/remap path in `DataFrame.tsx`. Both sort handlers (column-header click and column-menu sort) use `clearSelection(true, false)`, which unnecessarily clears column selections and triggers a redundant backend sync with incorrect intermediate state before `performRowSelectionRemap` restores them. Additionally, `gridSelection.current` (cell selection) is captured and restored after sorting without remapping its row index, meaning a stale cell selection could point to different data post-sort. All three reviewers that completed their review agreed this is a blocking issue. See inline comments for specific remediation.

## Test Coverage

Test coverage is excellent across all layers:

- **Python unit tests** (`arrow_dataframe_test.py`): Cover the new selection mode parsing, validation, auto-selection of the first row, and edge cases (empty dataframes, invalid row indices).
- **Frontend unit tests** (`useSelectionHandler.test.ts`, `useWidgetState.test.ts`): Thoroughly test the new mode including required-row detection, prevention of row clearing,

## Summary

This PR adds a new `"single-row-required"` selection mode to `st.dataframe` that enforces exactly one row is always selected (radio-like behavior). The changes span the full stack: a new protobuf enum value (`SINGLE_ROW_REQUIRED = 6`), Python backend auto-selection/validation logic, frontend selection handling (prevention of clearing, sort-remap preservation, circle checkbox style, toolbar button hiding), and comprehensive tests across all layers.

**Reviewer consensus**: All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved this PR unanimously. There were no disagreements on any findings.

## Code Quality

The implementation is well-structured and follows existing Streamlit codebase patterns. All reviewers agreed on the high quality of the code. Specific positives noted:

- Good use of `_ROW_SELECTION_MODES` set to consolidate row mode checks and avoid scattered string comparisons.
- The `useTimeout` + `callbackRef` pattern for sort-remap correctly avoids stale closure issues — praised by multiple reviewers as "pragmatic and well-documented."
- `isRequiredRowSelectionActivated` is derived during render (not via effect), following React best practices.
- The `WidgetInfo` type assertion cleanup (`as WidgetInfo` → `?? undefined`) is a welcome incidental improvement.

One reviewer (claude) noted that the sort-remap logic in `DataFrame.tsx` is duplicated between `onHeaderClicked` (~line 979) and the `ColumnMenu`'s `onSortColumn` callback (~line 1193). While this follows the existing pattern for these two code paths, extracting a shared helper would improve maintainability. This is captured as an inline comment and is non-blocking.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers rated test coverage as strong to excellent. Coverage spans:

- **Python unit tests**: Auto-selection, valid selection preservation, empty dataframe edge case, single-row limit, combined modes, and invalid mode combinations.
- **TypeScript unit tests**: Mode

title: Add support for pandas 3.x
body:
## Describe your changes

Update pandas dependency upper bound from `<3` to `<4` to support pandas 3.0 and later versions. Streamlit's codebase is already fully compatible with pandas 3.x, including all breaking changes such as Copy-on-Write semantics, string dtype inference, and datetime resolution changes.

## Github Issues

- Closes #13211 

## Testing Plan

- All 8072 Python unit tests pass with pandas 3.0.0
- Type checking (mypy and ty) passes
- All lint and format checks pass
- No code changes were needed; the existing codebase handles all pandas 3.x breaking changes

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the pandas dependency to support pandas 3.x by changing the upper bound from `<3` to `<4`. The existing Streamlit codebase is already fully compatible with all pandas 3.x breaking changes, including Copy-on-Write semantics, string dtype inference, and datetime resolution changes.

**Changes:**
- Updated pandas dependency upper bound from `<3` to `<4` in `lib/pyproject.toml`

commits.message:
Support pandas 3.x in dependency constraints

Update pandas upper bound from <3 to <4 to support pandas 3.0 and later versions. Streamlit's codebase is already fully compatible with pandas 3.x breaking changes including Copy-on-Write semantics, string dtype inference, and datetime resolution changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-haiku-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pandas 3.x compatibility issues

This commit addresses various compatibility issues with pandas 3.x:

- Update hashing.py: Use regex patterns to match both pandas 2.x
  (`pandas.core.frame.DataFrame`) and pandas 3.x (`pandas.DataFrame`)
  type paths for DataFrame and Series hashing.

- Update metrics_util.py: Add pandas 3.x type paths to the object
  name mapping since pandas 3.x changed __module__ from `pandas.core.*`
  to `pandas.*`.

- Update column_config_utils.py: Handle `large_string` PyArrow type
  which pandas 3.x uses for string columns instead of `string`.

- Update map.py: Convert string columns to object dtype before
  mapping color values to tuples, since pandas 3.x StringDtype
  cannot hold tuple values.

- Update test files: Handle pandas 3.x behavior where string columns
  use NA instead of None, and use flexible type checks for PyArrow
  arrays which may be StringArray or LargeStringArray.

- Update CI workflow: Add step to upgrade pandas to latest version
  for Python >= 3.11 (pandas 3.x requires Python >= 3.11), with
  UV_NO_SYNC=1 to prevent downgrade during uv run commands.

- Add pydeck test skip: Skip pydeck-related tests on pandas 3.x due
  to upstream pydeck incompatibility (vars() on DataFrame issue).

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pydeck compatibility with pandas 3.x

Add _prepare_pydeck_for_json() function that converts pandas DataFrames
in pydeck layers to lists of dicts before JSON serialization. This works
around a pandas 3.x issue where DataFrames no longer have a __dict__
attribute that vars() can access, which breaks pydeck's default_serialize
function in json_tools.py.

This removes the need to skip pydeck tests on pandas 3.x.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use uv override for pandas 3.x on Python 3.11+

Replace the CI workaround that manually upgraded pandas with a uv
override-dependencies setting. This tells uv to automatically use
pandas 3.x for Python 3.11+ while keeping pandas 2.x for Python 3.10.

Also refine the pydeck compatibility fix to only apply for pandas >= 3.0.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add upstream issue reference for pydeck pandas 3.x workaround

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert uv override approach for pandas 3.x

The override-dependencies approach caused pandas to not be installed
on Python 3.10 due to unexpected resolution behavior. Reverting to
the explicit CI upgrade step which works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use explicit pandas overrides for each Python version

Define separate override-dependencies for Python < 3.11 (pandas 2.x)
and Python >= 3.11 (pandas 3.x) to help uv resolve correctly for
both cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip Ray integration test for pandas 3.x

Ray is not yet compatible with pandas 3.x (SettingWithCopyWarning
was removed in pandas 3.0). Skip the test until Ray releases a
compatible version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Ray integration test and dependency

Ray is not compatible with pandas 3.x and has had recurring CI issues
with initialization hangs. Remove the integration test and dependency
entirely - Ray dataset support is still tested via mocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move pandas version check to pydeck workaround call site

Move the version check outside of _prepare_pydeck_for_json so the
conditional nature of the workaround is clear at the call site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Suppress FutureWarning in data_editor row insertion

Suppress the pandas FutureWarning about DataFrame concatenation with
empty/NA entries that appears when users add rows in st.data_editor.
The warning is triggered by pandas 2.1-2.x when using .loc[] to assign
a new row, and is not actionable by users. The warning was removed in
pandas 3.x where the behavior change has been implemented.

Fixes #14321

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add upper bound to pandas override-dependency

Add `<4` upper bound to the pandas override-dependency for Python 3.11+
to align with lib/pyproject.toml and prevent future CI breakage when
pandas 4.x is released.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/pandas-3-support

Increase websocket size threshold for pandas 3.x

Pandas 3.x uses large_string (64-bit offsets) by default instead of
string (32-bit), which produces ~18% larger Arrow IPC serialization.
This is a pandas ecosystem change, not triggered by Streamlit code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add unit tests for _prepare_pydeck_for_json and improve docs

Address code review feedback:
- Add 8 unit tests covering DataFrame conversion, weakref handling,
  None input, multiple layers, and edge cases
- Enhance docstring to document in-place mutation behavior and
  multi-call pattern implications
- Add reference to upstream pydeck issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Ray integration from codebase

Ray Dataset support was rarely used and adds maintenance burden.
This removes all Ray-related code including type detection, conversion
functions, metrics tracking, and associated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [chore] Enforce uv run for git commit/push in agent hooks
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds `git commit` and `git push` to the list of commands that require `uv run` in agent hooks. Pre-commit hooks need the uv environment to access linters and formatters.

- Added `git commit` and `git push` to `UV_RUN_COMMANDS` in `.claude/hooks/pre_bash_redirect.py`
- Updated check logic to handle multi-word command prefixes
- Added matching hook in `.cursor/hooks.json` for Cursor compatibility

## Testing Plan

- No tests needed - configuration-only changes to agent hooks
- Validated with `make check`

---

| subagent | general-purpose | 3 |

author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Summary

This PR enforces `uv run` for `git commit` and `git push` commands in AI agent hooks, ensuring pre-commit and pre-push hooks have access to the `uv` environment (linters, formatters). The functional change is in `.claude/hooks/pre_bash_redirect.py`, which generalizes command-prefix matching from single-word to multi-word prefixes. A corresponding Cursor hook is added in `.cursor/hooks.json`, and documentation is updated consistently across `AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/rules/overview.mdc`, and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved this PR with no blocking issues.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the code is clean, concise, and well-structured. The refactored matching logic in `pre_bash_redirect.py` — checking `norm == cmd_prefix or norm.startswith(cmd_prefix + " ")` — correctly handles both single-word and multi-word prefixes while avoiding false positives (e.g., `git commit-graph` is not matched due to the space delimiter). The error message is clear and actionable. The regex in `.cursor/hooks.json` is accurate. Documentation changes are consistent across all three updated files.

## Test Coverage

No automated tests were added, which all reviewers found acceptable given this is internal dev-tooling configuration (not runtime product behavior) and no tests existed for the hook script previously. Two of three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gpt-5.3-codex-high) noted that adding lightweight unit tests for `pre_bash_redirect.py` command matching would be a worthwhile future hardening step but is not blocking.

## Backwards Compatibility

No backwards compatibility concerns. All reviewers agreed this change only affects AI agent tooling hooks and documentation — no Streamlit library code, API, or user-facing behavior is modified. The refactored matching logic preserves all existing behavior for single-word commands.

##

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the agent shell-command policy to require `uv run` not only for Python tooling but also for hook-triggering Git commands, ensuring pre-commit / pre-push hooks run inside the uv environment.

**Changes:**
- Documented the new requirement to run `git commit` / `git push` via `uv run`.
- Expanded the Claude Bash hook to recognize multi-word command prefixes (e.g. `git commit`).
- Added a corresponding Cursor `beforeShellExecution` hook to block non-`uv run` git commit/push.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| AGENTS.md | Documents `uv run` requirement for hook-triggering Git commands. |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | Mirrors the updated policy text (generated from AGENTS.md). |
| .cursor/rules/overview.mdc | Mirrors the updated policy text (generated from AGENTS.md). |
| .cursor/hooks.json | Adds Cursor hook prompt to block `git commit` / `git push` without `uv run`. |
| .claude/hooks/pre_bash_redirect.py | Updates enforcement logic to handle multi-word command prefixes and includes git commit/push. |
</details>

## Summary

This PR enforces `uv run` for `git commit` and `git push` commands in AI agent hooks (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) and updates the corresponding documentation. Pre-commit and pre-push hooks require the `uv` environment for linters and formatters, so bare `git commit`/`git push` commands issued by agents would fail or skip those checks.

Key changes:
- **`.claude/hooks/pre_bash_redirect.py`**: Added `"git commit"` and `"git push"` to `UV_RUN_COMMANDS`; refactored matching from single-word lookup to multi-word prefix matching via iteration.
- **`.cursor/hooks.json`**: Added a prompt-based hook rule for Cursor targeting these commands.
- **`AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/rules/overview.mdc`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`**: Added documentation about the `uv run` requirement for git commands that trigger hooks.

**Reviewer consensus**: All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved this PR unanimously with no blocking issues.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the code is clean, well-structured, and maintainable. The refactoring from single-token first-word matching to prefix-based iteration is a sound approach for supporting multi-word command prefixes (`git commit`, `git push`) while preserving existing behavior for single-word commands (`python`, `pytest`, etc.). The space-delimited check (`cmd_prefix + " "`) correctly prevents false positives (e.g., `git commit-tree` would not be blocked). The error message preserves the user's original command formatting for easier debugging. Documentation and policy text are consistent across all three agent configuration files.

## Test Coverage

No new tests were added. Two of three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gpt-5.3-codex-high) noted this as an optional follow-up opportunity, observing that `pre_bash_redirect.py` had no tests before this PR either. One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) stated no tests are needed given the scope. Given this is internal agent too

commits.message:
[docs] Add uv run guidance for git commands with hooks

Pre-commit hooks require the uv environment to run linters and
formatters. This documents that git commit/push should also be
run via uv run to ensure hooks work correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce uv run for git commit/push in agent hooks

Pre-commit hooks require the uv environment to access linters and
formatters. Add git commit and git push to UV_RUN_COMMANDS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use original command in error message for better copy/paste

Address PR review feedback to print the original `cmd` instead of
normalized `norm` in error messages, preserving original whitespace
for accurate copy/paste remediation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use wildcard patterns in ask rules to catch uv run git commands

The previous patterns like `Bash(git push *)` only matched direct git
commands. Updated to `Bash(*git push*)` to also match `uv run git push`
variants, ensuring destructive git operations prompt for confirmation
regardless of how they're invoked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Code Quality

title: [feature] Add `required` parameter to `st.pills` and `st.segmented_control`

## Describe your changes

Adds `required: bool = False` parameter to `st.pills` and `st.segmented_control`:

- Prevents deselection in single-select mode when `required=True` (clicking already-selected option does nothing)
- Raises `StreamlitAPIException` when `required=True` with `selection_mode="multi"`

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes an artifact (`(streamdown:incomplete-link)`) that appears during `st.write_stream` markdown streaming when Streamlit color/size directives (e.g. `:red[...]`, `:small[...]`) are mid-token, by adding a custom `remend` handler that completes unterminated directive bracket syntax before link completion runs.

**Changes:**
- Add a `remend` handler that appends missing `]` for incomplete directive syntax during streaming markdown parsing.
- Add frontend unit tests covering incomplete + nested directives under streaming (`unterminatedParsing`) conditions.
- Add an E2E regression test and app path to stream directive-containing text via `st.write_stream`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.tsx | Adds a custom `remend` handler to close incomplete directive bracket syntax during streaming. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.test.tsx | Adds unit tests to ensure incomplete/nested directives don’t surface the streamdown artifact while streaming. |
| e2e_playwright/st_write_stream_test.py | Adds an E2E regression check asserting the artifact does not appear for streamed directive content. |
| e2e_playwright/st_write_stream.py | Adds a new stream generator that emits directive syntax via a UI button for the E2E test. |

## Summary

This PR fixes a streaming markdown bug where Streamlit color directives (`:red[text]`, `:blue[text]`, etc.) display the artifact `(streamdown:incomplete-link)` during streaming. The root cause is that the `remend` library's built-in link handler (priority 20) misinterprets incomplete directive bracket syntax (e.g., `:red[text` mid-stream) as an incomplete markdown link.

The fix adds a custom `RemendHandler` (`directiveCompletionHandler`) at priority 10 that runs before the link handler and closes any unclosed brackets, allowing `remark-directive` to parse them correctly.

**Changed files:**
- `frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.tsx` — Core fix: custom remend handler
- `frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.test.tsx` — Frontend unit tests
- `e2e_playwright/st_write_stream.py` — E2E app script with color directive streaming
- `e2e_playwright/st_write_stream_test.py` — E2E regression test

All three reviewers agreed on the core assessment: the fix is well-scoped, follows codebase patterns, has good test coverage, and correctly addresses the reported issue (#14460). The sole disagreement was on whether the bracket-counting algorithm's handling of non-directive brackets constitutes a blocking issue (see Recommendations).

## Code Quality

The implementation is clean and follows existing patterns in the codebase:

- The handler is correctly defined as a module-level constant (per frontend AGENTS.md guidance on static data structures), avoiding re-creation on each render.
- Uses JSDoc documentation and proper TypeScript typing with the `RemendHandler` type import.
- The priority choice (10) is intentional and well-documented — it must run before remend's link handler (priority 20). Priority 10 coincides with remend's built-in `htmlTags` handler, but they handle orthogonal patterns.

**Bracket-counting edge case (2/3 reviewers flagged):** One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) identified that the brack

## Summary

This PR fixes a streaming markdown bug ([#14460](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/14460)) where Streamlit color directives like `:red[text]` produce a `(streamdown:incomplete-link)` artifact during `st.write_stream`. The root cause is that `remend`'s built-in link handler misinterprets the `[` in directive syntax (e.g., `:red[text`) as an incomplete markdown link.

The fix adds a custom `RemendHandler` (`directiveCompletionHandler`) with priority 10 that runs before `remend`'s link handler (priority 20). It detects unclosed Streamlit directive brackets and appends the necessary closing `]` characters, preventing the link handler from ever seeing them as incomplete links.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) unanimously approved this PR.

## Code Quality

The implementation is clean and well-structured. The handler is correctly defined at module level as a static constant, following the "Static Data Structures" guideline from `frontend/AGENTS.md`. The regex `/:[a-z][a-z0-9-]*\[/g` correctly matches Streamlit directive patterns while avoiding false positives on non-directive colons (URLs, time formats, emoji shortcodes). JSDoc clearly explains the purpose, priority rationale, and links to the issue.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers agreed the code is well-organized and maintainable. One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) identified an edge case where non-directive `[` characters inside an open directive (e.g., `:red[text [link`) are ignored by the depth tracker, which could cause the artifact to reappear in that specific scenario. This edge case was verified against the code and is valid — see the inline comment on `StreamlitMarkdown.tsx` line 953 for details and a suggested fix. The other two reviewers did not flag this, but the analysis holds upon inspection.

## Test Coverage

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers agreed that test coverage is strong.

- **Frontend unit tests** (`S

syntax (e.g., :red[text) during streaming. This prevents remend's

links, which was causing "(streamdown:incomplete-link)" artifacts.

title: added server.FolderWatchList to cli + LocalSourcesWatcher
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR adds a new configuration option `server.folderWatchList` to allow users to specify a custom path to watch for file changes. Currently, Streamlit only supports watching the directory of the main script or excluding files via a blacklist, but this change provides the flexibility to monitor external directories or specific folders. Additionally, logic has been added to ensure the new path is recursively watched for file changes, and tests have been updated to cover this feature.

Note: This is my first time doing a PR to open source, so any feedback would be appreciated.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Issue Number: #9655 

## Testing Plan

- Unit tests were added to `local_sources_watcher_test.py` to ensure the custom watch path feature behaves as expected.

### MANUAL TEST
`streamlit run app/hello.py --server.runOnSave=true --server.folderWatchList=<BASE_PATH>/test_folder --logger.level=debug`

```
...
...
├── app/
│   ├── hello.py
└── test_folder
    └── test_file.txt
```

**app/hello.py**
```app/hello.py
import streamlit as st

st.write("Hello, World!")

with open("test_folder_1/test_file.txt", "r") as f:
    st.write("test_folder_1/test_file.txt")
    st.write(f.read())

with open("test_folder_2/test_file.txt", "r") as f:
    st.write("test_folder_2/test_file.txt")
    st.write(f.read())
```

**.streamlit/config.toml**
```.streamlit/config.toml
[server]
folderWatchList = [
    "test_folder_1",
    "test_folder_2"
]
runOnSave = true
```

**Note**: the list of folders should be _absolute paths_ currently, thought this is a good first step and then we can add regex search or relative paths in another PR if possible, since smaller PR's are more manageable/




**Contribution License Agreement**
