reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR replaces the frontend formatting toolchain (Prettier) with `oxfmt`, updating local scripts, pre-commit hooks, and developer documentation/config to reduce formatting time across the repo.

**Changes:**
- Swap formatting commands from `prettier` to `oxfmt` across `make check`, workspace `format/formatCheck` scripts, and the VSCode/devcontainer sync script.
- Remove Prettier-related ESLint integration and dependencies; add `oxfmt` dependency + config (`frontend/.oxfmtrc.json`).
- Update docs/instructions to reflect `oxfmt` as the formatter.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 23 out of 24 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `scripts/sync_vscode_devcontainer.py` | Switches JSON formatting from Prettier to `oxfmt` during VSCode/devcontainer sync. |
| `Makefile` | Updates `make check` formatting step and pre-commit skip list to use `oxfmt`. |
| `frontend/yarn.lock` | Adds `oxfmt` and removes Prettier-related dependencies. |

| `frontend/eslint-plugin-streamlit-custom/package.json` | Updates workspace format scripts to run `oxfmt`. |
| `frontend/eslint.config.mjs` | Removes `eslint-plugin-prettier` recommended config and applies formatter-driven reformatting. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/audio/cor

## Summary

This PR replaces Prettier with [oxfmt](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/formatter.html) (v0.41) as the frontend code formatter, achieving a reported ~70% reduction in formatting time. The migration touches all formatter integration points: pre-commit hooks, ESLint config, Makefile targets, `package.json` scripts, VSCode/devcontainer settings, and documentation. Formatting options (`semi: false`, `trailingComma: "es5"`, `arrowParens: "avoid"`) are preserved, and ignore patterns from `.prettierignore` are migrated to `ignorePatterns` in the new `.oxfmtrc.json`.

**Consolidated from 2 of 3 expected reviews.** `gemini-3.1-pro` failed to complete its review.

## Reviewer Agreement

Both available reviewers (`gpt-5.3-codex-high` and `opus-4.6-thinking`) agree on:

- The tooling migration is cohesive, thorough, and well-structured
- No runtime or user-facing behavior changes; no API impact
- No security concerns — purely a developer tooling change
- No additional unit/e2e tests are needed
- No accessibility regressions
- No external test coverage is required
- ESLint cleanup (removing `eslint-plugin-prettier`, `eslint-config-prettier`) is correct
- Dependency hygiene is clean (Prettier and transitive deps removed, only `oxfmt` added)
- Pre-commit hook architecture (using `run_in_subdirectory.py` vs `yarn --cwd` depending on path context) is correctly handled

## Reviewer Disagreement

| Topic | gpt-5.3-codex-high | opus-4.6-thinking |
|-------|-------------------|------------------|
| **Verdict** | APPROVED | CHANGES REQUESTED |
| **Documentation updates** | Not flagged | Flagged 5 files with stale "Prettier" references |

**Resolution:** I verified the stale documentation references against the codebase and confirmed all of them exist. These are legitimate issues that should be addressed as part of this migration for consistency:

1. **`AGENTS.md` (line 63):** `frontend-format` description still says "prettier" and "~10s" — should say "oxfmt" and update the tim

## Summary

This PR replaces **prettier** with **oxfmt** (from the [OXC project](https://oxc.rs/)) as the frontend code formatter, delivering a reported **70% reduction in formatting time** (~5s → ~1.5s). The migration is comprehensive, covering configuration files (`.oxfmtrc.json`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `package.json`), IDE/devcontainer settings, Makefile targets, ESLint config, documentation, and test files. No runtime or user-facing code is modified.

## Reviewer Consensus

All three reviewers (**gemini-3.1-pro**, **gpt-5.3-codex-high**, **opus-4.6-thinking**) **unanimously approved** this PR. There were no disagreements or conflicting assessments.

| Area | Consensus |
|---|---|
| Code quality | Excellent — thorough, consistent migration across all touchpoints |
| Test coverage | Appropriate — no new tests needed for a tooling-only change |
| Backwards compatibility | No impact on end users or runtime behavior |
| Security & risk | No concerns — oxfmt is from the OXC ecosystem already used in this repo |
| External test recommendation | Not needed (high confidence, all reviewers agree) |
| Accessibility | N/A — no UI changes |

## Key Observations (Cross-Cutting)

1. **Configuration parity**: The `.oxfmtrc.json` accurately mirrors the previous `.prettierrc` settings (`semi: false`, `trailingComma: "es5"`, `arrowParens: "avoid"`), with the appropriate addition of `sortPackageJson: false` and `ignorePatterns`.

2. **Pre-commit hook design**: The asymmetric approach for `oxfmt-frontend` (using `run_in_subdirectory.py`) vs. `oxfmt-yaml`/`oxfmt-vscode-devcontainer-json` (using `yarn --cwd frontend exec`) is well-motivated and clearly commented — oxfmt rejects `..` paths, so files outside `frontend/` need the `--cwd` workaround.

3. **ESLint integration cleanup**: Removing `eslint-plugin-prettier` and `eslint-config-prettier` is safe since the ESLint config doesn't enable stylistic/formatting rules that would conflict with oxfmt.

4. **Documentation**: All releva

## Summary

This PR replaces [Prettier](https://prettier.io) with [oxfmt](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/formatter.html) (from the oxc toolchain) as the frontend code formatter, achieving a **~70% reduction in formatting time** (~5s → ~1.5s). The change is purely internal tooling — no runtime or user-facing code is affected.

Key changes:
- Swaps `prettier` (+ `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`) for `oxfmt ^0.41.0` in `frontend/package.json`.
- Adds `.oxfmtrc.json` (mirroring previous `.prettierrc` options), with `sortPackageJson: false` and ignore patterns for proto files.
- Removes `.prettierrc`, `.prettierignore`, and `eslint-plugin-prettier/recommended` from the ESLint config.
- Updates all workspace `format`/`formatCheck` scripts, pre-commit hooks, Makefile targets, VSCode/devcontainer settings, and documentation.
- Applies minor reformatting in a handful of test files where oxfmt's output differs from Prettier's.

**Reviewer consensus**: All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved unanimously with no blocking issues.

## Code Quality

All reviewers agreed the PR is well-structured and thorough. Prettier references have been systematically replaced across all relevant files: 7 workspace `package.json` files, ESLint config, pre-commit hooks, Makefile, VSCode/devcontainer settings, documentation, and utility scripts.

Specific quality highlights noted across reviews:
- The `oxfmt-yaml` and `oxfmt-vscode-devcontainer-json` pre-commit hooks were thoughtfully redesigned to use `yarn --cwd frontend exec` with clear comments explaining that oxfmt rejects paths containing `..`.
- `sortPackageJson: false` in `.oxfmtrc.json` is a good preventive measure against unexpected key reordering.
- One remaining `prettier` reference in `frontend/vitest.config.ts` is a default Vitest exclusion glob and is fine to leave.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers agreed no new tests are needed. This is a tooling-only chang

## Summary

This PR replaces Prettier with [oxfmt](https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/formatter.html) as the frontend code formatter across the Streamlit repository, claiming a ~70% reduction in formatting time (~5s → ~1.5s). The migration scope includes:

- Swapping `prettier` for `oxfmt` in `package.json` dependencies and workspace scripts
- Replacing `.prettierrc` / `.prettierignore` with `.oxfmtrc.json`
- Updating pre-commit hooks, Makefile targets, and CI workflows
- Updating VSCode/devcontainer settings to use the `oxc.oxc-vscode` extension
- Removing `eslint-plugin-prettier` and `eslint-config-prettier` from ESLint configuration
- Updating internal documentation (`AGENTS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, Cursor/Copilot rules)
- Converting `// prettier-ignore` comments to `// oxfmt-ignore`
- Minor reformatting in source/test files where oxfmt style differs from Prettier

## Reviewer Consensus (3/3 models reviewed)

| Model | Verdict |
|---|---|
| claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking | APPROVED |
| gemini-3.1-pro | APPROVED |
| gpt-5.3-codex-high | APPROVED |

**All three reviewers unanimously approved** this PR. There is strong agreement across all dimensions:

### Areas of Agreement

- **Code quality**: All reviewers found the migration clean, thorough, and well-organized. Every reference to Prettier in config files, scripts, documentation, and code comments has been systematically updated.
- **Configuration correctness**: The `.oxfmtrc.json` faithfully preserves the previous Prettier style settings (`semi: false`, `trailingComma: "es5"`, `arrowParens: "avoid"`), minimizing formatting churn. The `ignorePatterns` correctly replaces `.prettierignore`.
- **Pre-commit hook handling**: The approach for root-level files (using `yarn --cwd frontend exec oxfmt` to work around oxfmt's path resolution rejecting `..` segments) is correct and well-handled.
- **ESLint cleanup**: Removal of `eslint-config-prettier` and `eslint-plugin-prettier` is appropriate since oxfmt handles formatting ind
LGTM 👍

title: fix: update font urls to a durable reference
body:
## Describe your changes

- Pin Monaspace Argon font URLs to a specific commit hash instead of using the main branch reference since they are returning a 404 before this change.
- This change updates all font URLs from `refs/heads/main` to commit `052b3c4eb409e7f026edf5f0609de4ff54db7e23` and adjusts the file paths to match the new repository structure (`Web%20Fonts/Static%20Web%20Fonts/Monaspace%20Argon/` instead of `webfonts/`).

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

N/A

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- **E2E Tests**: The updated theming tests (`snowflake_dark_theme_test.py`, `snowflake_light_theme_test.py`, `theme_from_window_test.py`) will validate font loading with the new URLs

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Pins Monaspace Argon font URLs used by theming-related tests to a specific `githubnext/monaspace` commit and updates paths to match the repo’s new font layout, addressing recent 404s in test runs.

**Changes:**
- Update Monaspace Argon font URLs from `refs/heads/main` to commit `052b3c4e…` across Python unit tests and Playwright theming tests.
- Adjust Monaspace font path to `fonts/Web%20Fonts/Static%20Web%20Fonts/Monaspace%20Argon/…`.
- Update the Linux Playwright snapshot for the snowflake light theme.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/app_session_test.py` | Updates expected theme `fontFaces` Monaspace URL to a pinned commit/path. |
| `e2e_playwright/theming/theme_from_window_test.py` | Updates injected theme config to use the pinned Monaspace URL. |
| `e2e_playwright/theming/snowflake_light_theme_test.py` | Updates env-configured theme fontFaces to use the pinned Monaspace URL. |
| `e2e_playwright/theming/snowflake_dark_theme_test.py` | Updates env-configured theme fontFaces to use the pinned Monaspace URL. |
| `e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/snowflake_light_theme_test/snowflake_light_theme[chromium].png` | Snapshot update reflecting the font loading/rendering change. |
</details>






---

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).

## Summary

This PR fixes broken Monaspace Argon font URLs used in theming E2E tests and a Python unit test. The upstream `githubnext/monaspace` repository reorganized its directory structure, causing the old `refs/heads/main/fonts/webfonts/` paths to return 404. The fix pins all URLs to a specific commit hash (`052b3c4`) and updates the file paths to match the new repository layout (`fonts/Web%20Fonts/Static%20Web%20Fonts/Monaspace%20Argon/`).

**Changed files:** 3 E2E test files, 1 Python unit test, and 9 snapshot images. No production code is modified.

## Reviewer Agreement

All three reviewers (**claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking**, **gemini-3.1-pro**, **gpt-5.3-codex-high**) unanimously approved this PR and agreed on the following:

- The change is minimal, mechanical, and correctly applied across all affected files.
- Pinning to an immutable commit hash is the right approach for durability.
- No production code, APIs, or user-facing behavior is modified.
- No backwards compatibility, security, or accessibility concerns.
- Existing E2E tests and the updated snapshot images provide sufficient validation.

## Reviewer Disagreement: External Test Recommendation

- **claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking** and **gemini-3.1-pro** recommended **No** external tests (High confidence).
- **gpt-5.3-codex-high** recommended **Yes** (Medium confidence), citing cross-origin behavior categories.

**Resolution:** External tests are **not needed**. The gpt-5.3-codex-high recommendation is overly conservative — these changes are confined to test fixtures and snapshot images. No production code that handles font loading, cross-origin behavior, CSP, or resource discovery is modified. The tests already used external URLs before this change; only the path and ref are updated. The medium confidence and diff-only caveat in gpt-5.3's own assessment further supports discounting this recommendation.

## Recommendations (Follow-up, Non-blocking)

1. **Bundle Monaspace Argon font files locally.** Tw
LGTM 👍

title: [fix] Remove heading padding in horizontal containers for proper alignment
body: ## Describe your changes Fixes #12434 Headings (`st.title`, `st.header`, `st.subheader`) have vertical padding that causes misalignment when placed in horizontal containers. The padding inflates the heading's box height, making it taller than sibling elements like buttons, so `vertical_alignment="center"` doesn't visually align the text. - Removes heading padding (h1–h6) when rendered inside a horizontal layout via `FlexContext` - Removes the compensating negative `marginBottom` on `StyledStreamlitMarkdown` in horizontal layouts ## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes) ## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable) Fixes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12434 ## Testing Plan - Unit Tests (JS): Added 2 tests to `Heading.test.tsx` verifying padding is removed in horizontal layout and preserved in vertical layout - E2E Tests: N/A - Manual testing: Verified with `verify.py` app showing title, header, and subheader alignment in horizontal containers with center/top/bottom alignment **Contribution License Agreement** By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes heading (`st.title`, `st.header`, `st.subheader`) vertical misalignment inside horizontal containers by removing the extra vertical padding that inflates heading height and by disabling the related negative margin compensation in that layout context.

**Changes:**
- Add a horizontal-layout flag to `StyledStreamlitMarkdown` to remove h1–h6 padding when used in horizontal layouts.
- Remove `StyledStreamlitMarkdown`’s compensating negative `marginBottom` in horizontal layouts.
- Add unit tests to verify heading padding behavior differs between horizontal vs vertical layouts.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/styled-components.ts | Adds horizontal-layout conditional heading padding + disables negative margin compensation in that mode. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/Heading.tsx | Reads `FlexContext` and passes horizontal-layout state to `StyledStreamlitMarkdown`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/Heading.test.tsx | Adds tests asserting padding is removed in horizontal layout and preserved in vertical layout. |

commits.message:
Remove heading padding in horizontal containers

Fixes #12434 — headings (h1–h6) had vertical padding that caused
misalignment with sibling elements in horizontal containers. Zero out
the padding and compensating negative marginBottom when FlexContext
indicates a horizontal layout.

title: [fix] st.exception links overflow at small widths
body: ## Describe your changes `st.exception` footer links (Copy, Ask Google, Ask ChatGPT) overflow their container at narrow widths because the flex container lacks wrapping. - Add `flexWrap: "wrap"` to `StyledExceptionLinks` so links wrap to new lines when space is tight - Remove invalid `underline: true` CSS property (not a valid CSS value; had no effect) - Add dedicated E2E test for link wrapping at narrow widths (250px), verifying all three footer links remain visible - Update existing `st_exception-fixed_width` snapshots (taller due to wrapped links) Fixes #12870 ## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes) **Desktop (narrow column)** — links wrap gracefully: **Mobile (375px):** ## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable) https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12870 ## Testing Plan - [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python) - [x] E2E Tests - [ ] Any manual testing needed? Existing ExceptionElement unit tests pass (13/13). Added a dedicated E2E test (`st_exception-narrow_width_link_wrap`) that renders an exception at 250px width and asserts all footer links (Copy, Ask Google, Ask ChatGPT) remain visible when they wrap. Updated `st_exception-fixed_width` snapshots to reflect the corrected wrapped-links rendering. **Contribution License Agreement** By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
LGTM! Clean fix — `flexWrap: "wrap"` is exactly the right approach for handling narrow container widths. Good catch also removing the unused `underline: true` property (it's not a valid CSS/Emotion property on a div anyway).

One minor note: the existing e2e test for `fixed_width` (200px) should now capture the wrapped link state in its snapshot, which will serve as regression coverage for this fix.

## Pull request overview

Fixes `st.exception` footer links (Copy / Ask Google / Ask ChatGPT) overflowing at narrow widths by allowing the link row to wrap, and updates E2E coverage/snapshots to validate the narrow-width rendering (Issue #12870).

**Changes:**
- Add `flexWrap: "wrap"` to the exception footer link container and remove an invalid CSS property.
- Add a new narrow-width `st.exception` scenario in the E2E app.
- Extend the `st_exception` Playwright test with an additional snapshot + visibility assertions and update expected element counts/snapshots.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ExceptionElement/styled-components.ts | Enables wrapping for exception footer links and removes invalid CSS. |
| e2e_playwright/st_exception.py | Adds a narrow-width `st.exception` example used by E2E snapshots. |
| e2e_playwright/st_exception_test.py | Adds a new snapshot assertion for narrow-width link wrapping and updates indices/counts. |
| e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/st_exception_test/st_exception-fixed_width[light_theme-webkit].png | Snapshot update for fixed-width exception rendering. |
| e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/st_exception_test/st_exception-fixed_width[light_theme-firefox].png | Snapshot update for fixed-width exception rendering. |
| e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/st_exception_test/st_exception-fixed_width[light_theme-chromium].png | Snapshot update for fixed-width exception rendering. |
| e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/st_exception_test/st_exception-fixed_width[dark_theme-webkit].png | Snapshot update for fixed-width exception rendering. |
| e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/linux/st_exception_test/st_exception-fixed_width[dark_theme-firefox].png | Snapshot update for fixed-width exception rendering. |
| e2e_playwright/_

commits.message:
Fix st.exception link overflow at small widths

Add flexWrap to StyledExceptionLinks so links wrap instead of
overflowing when the container is narrow.

Fixes #12870

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Add E2E test for exception link wrapping at narrow widths

Delete stale fixed_width snapshots (height changed due to flexWrap)
and add a dedicated 250px-width test that verifies footer links
(Copy, Ask Google, Ask ChatGPT) remain visible when they wrap.

title: [test] AI-assisted flaky E2E test fixes
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to fix flaky E2E tests identified from recent CI runs.

This PR was created by the `ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests` workflow using `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.

## Changes

# Flaky E2E Test Fixes

## Summary

Investigated the top 10 flaky E2E tests from the last 4 days. Each test was run up to 25 times on the affected browser(s) to attempt local reproduction. Only tests that reproduced locally were fixed.

## Changes Made

### Fixed: `e2e_playwright/st_link_button_test.py`

**Tests affected:**
- `test_link_button_click_returns_true_for_rerun` (7 CI reruns, chromium+firefox)
- `test_link_button_click_calls_callback` (6 CI reruns, chromium+firefox)

**Root cause:** The shared `_click_link_and_wait_for_rerun` helper used Playwright's `expect_popup()` to handle the new tab opened by the link button click. On Chromium and Firefox, the popup event sometimes never fires (30s timeout), likely because the Streamlit rerun triggered by `on_click` interferes with the browser's popup creation. This is consistent with the existing comment on `test_link_button_shortcut_triggers`: "Firefox and Chromium are a bit flaky on the expect_popup."

**Fix:** Replaced `expect_popup()` with `preventDefault()` on the click event. The link button's `on_click`/rerun callback fires independently of the browser's link navigation, so preventing the default action (opening a new tab) avoids the flaky popup handling entirely. A `{once: true}` listener ensures no interference with subsequent interactions.

**Reproduction:** Failed on run 3 of 25 on chromium (before fix).
**Verification:** 15/15 passes on chromium for both tests (after fix).

## Not Reproducible Locally (25/25 passes, skipped per policy)

| Test | CI Reruns | Browser(s) |
|------|-----------|------------|
| `st_slider_test.py::test_dynamic_slider_props` | 9 | chromium, webkit |
| `forward_msg_cache_test.py::test_check_total_websocket_message_number_and_size` | 7 | webkit |
| `st_audio_input_test.py::test_high_quality_sample_rate` | 5 | firefox |
| `st_slider_test.py::test_slider_with_float_formatting` | 5 | firefox |
| `multipage_apps_v2/mpa_v2_basics_test.py::test_widget_state_reset_on_page_switch` | 4 | webkit |
| `bidi_components/basics_test.py::test_trigger_interactions` | 3 | chromium, webkit |
| `theming/custom_theme_fonts_test.py::test_custom_theme` | 3 | chromium, firefox, webkit |
| `st_image_test.py::test_width_stretch_fullscreen` | 3 | firefox |

These tests may be flaky due to CI-specific factors (resource contention, network latency for font loading, browser sandbox differences) that cannot be reproduced locally.

---
*This is an automated flaky test fix by `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.*
author: github-actions

## Pull request overview

**Changes:**
- Removed `pytest` import from `e2e_playwright/config_static_serving_test.py`.

**e2e_playwright/config_static_serving_test.py:129**

Consider either re-adding the marker (and, if needed, configuring it appropriately for external assets) or renaming/merging this test so it reflects what’s actually executed.
```
def test_static_urls_in_external_app(app_target: AppTarget):
    """Test /app/static/ URL rendering in externally hosted or iframe-embedded apps.

    This test validates that media elements (image, audio, video, avatar, logo)
```
</details>

## Summary

This PR removes the `@pytest.mark.external_test` marker from `test_static_urls_in_external_app` in `e2e_playwright/config_static_serving_test.py` and cleans up the now-unused `import pytest`. The test will now run as part of the regular (local) E2E test suite instead of being gated behind external-only execution mode.

## Code Quality

The change is clean and minimal — only the two lines that needed to change were touched. The unused `import pytest` is correctly removed alongside the marker, and no other references to `pytest` remain in the file.

All three reviewers agreed the diff is well-scoped and easy to follow.

## Test Coverage

The net effect is that `test_static_urls_in_external_app` now runs in regular CI (increasing local coverage) but will no longer run when `--external-app-url` or `--external-host-url` is used (since the `skip_non_external_tests_in_external_mode` autouse fixture skips any test without the marker).

**Reviewer disagreement on coverage impact:**
- **claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking** and **gemini-3.1-pro** view this as a net positive — the test now runs in the standard suite, and the remaining `@pytest.mark.external_test` tests in `mega_tester_app_test.py` still provide broad external-mode coverage.
- **gpt-5.3-codex-high** flagged this as a coverage regression risk for external/iframe static-serving scenarios, recommending the marker be kept or replaced.

**Consolidated assessment:** This is a low-risk change. The author likely made a deliberate decision to move this test out of external-only mode. The `mega_tester_app_test.py` external tests continue to provide broad external coverage, and the static-serving assertions are still exercised locally. The concern is valid in principle but does not rise to a blocking issue.

## Backwards Compatibility

No backwards compatibility concerns. This is a test-infrastructure-only change with no impact on the Streamlit library, public API, or user-facing behavior. All three reviewers agreed

## Summary

This PR removes the `test_static_urls_in_external_app` test function from `e2e_playwright/config_static_serving_test.py` and cleans up the now-unused `pytest` and `AppTarget` imports. The removed test was a `@pytest.mark.external_test` that validated `/app/static/` URL rendering in externally hosted or iframe-embedded apps. Its assertions are fully covered by the existing `test_static_urls_in_media_elements` test, which additionally tests `st.set_page_config(page_icon=...)`.

**Reviewer agreement:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed that the code change is mechanically clean, the import cleanup is correct, and there are no security or backwards-compatibility concerns. Two of three reviewers approved; one requested changes over reduced external-test coverage (see Test Coverage below).

## Code Quality

The change is clean and minimal. The `import pytest` removal is correct (only used for `@pytest.mark.external_test`), and the `AppTarget` import removal is correct (only used as the type hint for the deleted test's `app_target` parameter). No trailing whitespace or formatting issues remain.

One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) noted that the PR description states the `@pytest.mark.external_test` marker is being *removed* from the test (implying it still runs as a regular test), when in fact the test was *deleted entirely* with coverage provided by the pre-existing `test_static_urls_in_media_elements`. The PR description should be corrected for accuracy.

## Test Coverage

The remaining tests in `config_static_serving_test.py` provide solid coverage for static file serving: endpoint 200/404 responses, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` header, markdown-embedded images, and comprehensive media-element assertions via `test_static_urls_in_media_elements`.

**Point of disagreement:** The key divide among reviewers was whether removing dedicated `@pytest.mark.external_test` coverage for static URL rendering is

title: [feature] `st.expander` & `st.popover` state persistence and CSS key class
body:
## Summary

Third PR in the layout container state persistence stack (after #14332 and #14354).

Adds frontend state persistence for keyed `st.expander` and `st.popover` elements via `elementStates`. When a `key` is provided in passive mode (`on_change="ignore"`), the expanded/open state survives component remounts caused by delta path shifts.

### Changes

**Frontend (Expander.tsx)**
- Reads stored `expanded` state from `elementStates` on mount when `shouldPersist` is true
- Writes `expanded` state to `elementStates` on toggle via `handlePersistToggle`
- Widget mode (`on_change="rerun"`) is explicitly protected — `elementStates` is never read/written; server state always takes precedence

**Frontend (Popover.tsx)**
- Same pattern: reads/writes `open` state to `elementStates` in passive mode
- Widget mode protection identical to Expander

**Frontend (Block.tsx)**
- `ContainerContentsWrapper` suppresses the `st-key-*` CSS class on the inner `StyledFlexContainerBlock` when it detects the block is inside an expander or popover, avoiding duplication with the container's own outermost element

**CSS Key Class**
- `st-key-*` class applied to the outermost element of both `st.expander` and `st.popover`

### Test Plan

- [x] Unit tests: passive state persistence (read on mount, write on toggle)
- [x] Unit tests: widget-mode guards (no elementStates read/write, server value wins over stale stored state)
- [x] Unit tests: CSS key class presence/absence
- [x] E2E tests: `test_keyed_expander_css_key_class` and `test_keyed_expander_persist_expanded_across_remount`
- [x] E2E tests: `test_keyed_popover_css_key_class` and `test_keyed_popover_persist_closed_across_remount`

**Depends on:** #14354
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds passive (non-widget) state persistence for keyed layout containers (`st.expander`, `st.popover`) by storing UI state in `WidgetStateManager.elementStates`, and relocates `st-key-*` to the outermost wrapper element for these containers to avoid duplicate key classes.

**Changes:**
- Persist expander `expanded` and popover `open` state across remounts in passive keyed mode via `elementStates`, while keeping widget-mode controlled by server state.
- Move `st-key-*` CSS key class placement to `StyledLayoutWrapper` for expander/popover blocks and remove it from inner vertical block wrappers.
- Add/adjust frontend unit tests and Playwright E2E tests for persistence + CSS key class behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Popover/Popover.tsx | Adds elementStates-backed persistence for passive keyed popovers and widget-mode guarding. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Popover/Popover.test.tsx | Adds unit tests for popover persistence + widget-mode behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Expander/Expander.tsx | Adds elementStates-backed persistence for passive keyed expanders and widget-mode guarding. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Expander/Expander.test.tsx | Adds unit tests for expander persistence + widget-mode behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/Block.tsx | Moves `st-key-*` to `StyledLayoutWrapper` for expander/popover and removes it from inner container wrapper. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/Block.test.tsx | Tests key class placement on wrapper vs inner block for expander/popover. |
| e2e_playwright/st_popover.py | Adds a keyed “Persist popover” example to the E2E app. |
| e2e_playwright/st_popover_test.py | Adds E2E checks for popover CSS key class + pers
LGTM 👍

commits.message:
feat: st.expander & st.popover state persistence and CSS key class

Add frontend state persistence for keyed expanders and popovers via
elementStates. When a key is provided (passive mode), the open/expanded
state survives component remounts caused by delta path shifts.

- Expander reads/writes "expanded" state to elementStates on toggle
- Popover reads/writes "open" state to elementStates on toggle
- CSS key class (st-key-*) applied to outermost element only
- Block.tsx suppresses duplicate CSS key class on inner vertical blocks
  for expander/popover containers
- Widget mode (on_change="rerun") is explicitly protected: elementStates
  is never read/written, server state always takes precedence
- Unit tests cover persistence, CSS class, and widget-mode guards
- E2E tests for CSS key class and persistence for both elements

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

fix: resolve lint errors in Expander, Popover, and Popover tests

- Remove non-null assertions on blockId (already guarded by shouldPersist)
- Remove unnecessary async and unused variable in Popover test

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

fix: use nullish coalescing for blockId to satisfy TypeScript

blockId is typed as string | undefined. The shouldPersist guard ensures
it's truthy at runtime, but TypeScript can't narrow through the ternary.
Use blockId ?? "" to satisfy the string parameter type.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

fix: remove no-op popover persistence E2E test

The test only verified the default (closed) state after remount, which
would pass regardless of persistence. The underlying elementStates
mechanism is already covered by expander and tabs E2E tests. The CSS
key class test is kept.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Refactor persistence to use useWidgetManagerElementState hook

- Expander/Popover: replace manual getElementState/setElementState with
  useWidgetManagerElementState hook for cleaner state persistence
- CSS key class: apply st-key-* on StyledLayoutWrapper (per spec) for
  expander/popover only; suppress duplicate on ContainerContentsWrapper
- Fix Popover bug: only persist state when isPassivelyKeyed (not on
  every non-widget toggle)
- Unit tests: rewrite persistence tests to verify behavior through
  public WidgetStateManager API rather than spying on internal calls
- Remove CSS class tests from Expander/Popover (now tested via Block)
- Add BlockNodeRenderer unit test verifying CSS class placement

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Simplify ContainerContentsWrapper: accept userKey prop instead of suppressKeyClass

Pass the computed CSS key from outside rather than having the component
derive and conditionally suppress it. Expander/popover children simply
omit userKey; all other callers pass it.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Clean up Block.tsx and st_popover: remove dead code

Remove unused userKey prop from ContainerContentsWrapper (no container
that passes through it sets Block.id). Remove leftover persistence test
fixtures from st_popover.py after test removal. Update keyClassOnWrapper
comment to reflect per-container gating rationale.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Simplify Block.tsx, reuse child element, fix Expander TS error

- Reuse {child} in expander/popover instead of duplicating
  ContainerContentsWrapper (now identical after userKey removal).
- Remove dead userKey prop from ContainerContentsWrapper.
- Fix TS2322: add ?? false for element.expanded defaultValue.
- Update keyClassOnWrapper comment.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Add E2E test for popover state persistence across rerun

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Fix ESLint no-non-null-assertion: make widgetMgr non-optional in Expander/Popover

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Fix Block.test: add vertical flag for realistic container rendering path

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Clarify widget mode comments in expander/popover tests

Make it explicit that element.id being set is what triggers widget mode
(on_change="rerun"), so the server value takes precedence.

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Improve popover E2E persistence test to exercise delta-path shift

The previous test only triggered a rerun via the "r" shortcut, which
doesn't cause a remount. The new test places a checkbox inside the
keyed popover that conditionally inserts an element above it, shifting
the popover's delta path and forcing a true remount — validating that
elementStates persistence works across identity changes.

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

reviews.body:
## Summary

This PR makes three small prompt-engineering changes to `.github/workflows/ai-pr-review.yml`, the AI-powered code review workflow:

1. **Individual review finalize step (line 203):** Removes the instruction "Do not include findings already covered by inline comments — the summary should only contain high-level and cross-cutting concerns not addressed inline." from the inline finalize directive. This instruction is redundant here because the same guidance already exists in the shared markdown files included via `$(cat ...)` (`code-review-instructions.md` and `code-review-inline-comments-instructions.md`).

2. **Consolidation prompt (lines 343–344):** Removes the directive "Do NOT attach or embed the individual reviews in the output. The consolidated review should stand on its own."

3. **Consolidation finalize step (line 347):** Removes the same dedup instruction as (1) from the consolidation finalize step.

## Reviewer Agreement

All three reviewers (**gemini-3.1-pro**, **gpt-5.3-codex-high**, **opus-4.6-thinking**) unanimously **APPROVED** this PR and agreed on the following:

- The change is low-risk and narrowly scoped to an internal CI/CD workflow.
- No security concerns, no backwards compatibility issues, no accessibility impact.
- No unit tests, E2E tests, or external tests are required.
- The `impact:internal` label is appropriate.

## Key Insight (opus-4.6-thinking)

One reviewer (opus-4.6-thinking) provided a deeper analysis that the other two did not surface:

- For **individual reviews**, removing the dedup instruction from the finalize step is purely removing redundancy — the same instruction exists in the shared markdown files included via `$(cat ...)`. This is a clean improvement.
- For the **consolidation** prompt, however, the removed dedup instruction was the *only* place telling the consolidation agent not to duplicate findings between `review.md` and `inline_comments.json`. The consolidation prompt includes `code-review-consolidation-i

## Summary

This PR refines the AI PR review workflow prompts and consolidation instructions across two files (`.github/workflows/ai-pr-review.yml` and `scripts/assets/code-review-consolidation-instructions.md`). The key changes are:

1. **Removes redundant "no-duplicate" instructions** from the `Finalize` steps in the workflow YAML. The guidance to not repeat inline-comment findings in the PR-level review body already exists in the shared instruction files (`scripts/assets/code-review-instructions.md` and `scripts/assets/code-review-inline-comments-instructions.md`), so the duplicated lines in the YAML heredocs were unnecessary prompt bloat.
2. **Adds explicit format guidance for the consolidation review** — the consolidation agent is now directed to follow the exact section structure and ordering from `scripts/assets/code-review-instructions.md` and to avoid adding extra top-level sections.
3. **Adds a "standalone review" rule** to the consolidation instructions file, preventing the consolidation model from embedding or quoting full individual reviews in its output.
4. **Minor formatting fixes** — adds a blank line before a markdown list (needed for correct list rendering) and normalizes "Do NOT" to "Do not" for style consistency.

**Reviewer consensus: 3/3 models reviewed; all 3 approved unanimously.** No disagreements were found across any review dimension.

## Code Quality

All three reviewers agreed that the changes are clear, minimal, and well-structured. The prompt instructions are non-ambiguous and the YAML heredoc correctly handles literal backticks since the delimiter is quoted (no shell expansion issues). The reference to `scripts/assets/code-review-instructions.md` in the consolidation prompt is valid because the consolidation job checks out the repository before running.

## Test Coverage

All reviewers agreed that no tests are needed. The changes are limited to CI workflow prompts and markdown instruction files — these are validated by running the wor

## Pull request overview

This PR adjusts the AI PR review workflow/prompting so the consolidated review output is more consistent (section structure) and avoids including full embedded individual model reviews.

**Changes:**
- Update consolidation triage instructions to explicitly require `review.md` to be standalone (not embedding `all_reviews.md`).
- Refine the consolidation prompt to enforce the exact section structure/order defined in `scripts/assets/code-review-instructions.md`.
- Simplify “Finalize” prompt instructions in the workflow for both individual and consolidated reviews.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `scripts/assets/code-review-consolidation-instructions.md` | Adds a rule intended to prevent embedding individual reviews into the consolidated `review.md`. |
| `.github/workflows/ai-pr-review.yml` | Tightens consolidation output formatting instructions and adjusts review writing guidance in the agent prompts. |
LGTM 👍

title: [spec] `st.iframe` - Embed content in an iframe
body:
## Describe your changes

Spec renderer: https://issues.streamlit.app/spec_renderer?pr=14373

Product spec for `st.iframe` — a new command to embed URLs, HTML content, or local HTML files in an iframe. Consolidates `st.components.v1.iframe` and `st.components.v1.html` into a single, discoverable API.

Key design decisions:
- Single `src` parameter with auto-detection (URL vs HTML vs file path)
- `height="content"` default with fallback to 400px for cross-origin URLs
- Permissive sandbox policy matching existing component behavior
- `fullscreen` permission enabled by default

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12977
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/4659


**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a product spec for a proposed new `st.iframe` command to embed URLs, HTML strings, or local HTML files via iframes, consolidating legacy `st.components.v1.iframe` + `st.components.v1.html` into a single API.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a full product spec describing the proposed `st.iframe` API surface and behavior (input auto-detection, sizing, sandbox/permissions policy).
- Documents migration guidance from `st.components.v1.*` and lists alternatives/future work.
- Adds examples and an implementation checklist.

<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->
✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

Generally LGTM! Agree that `st.iframe` is better than `st.embed` (I think with `embed` I'd think of embedding the app itself somewhere else). 

My only point is whether we need the `scrolling` parameter – shouldn't this just work like `st.container`, where if it has fixed height and the content is longer, we automatically enable scrolling, otherwise not? I can't really see a use case for showing a very long page in a smaller iframe without enabling scrolling, or is there one? (If there is a use case for it, I'm also fine with including the `scrolling` parameter, but I think we'd need to think through the name so we make it congruent with possible vertical/horizontal scrolling options on `st.container` in the future).

> to be fair, st.html doesn't even have height

Haha, might be good to add at some point. Anyway, approved.

Add st.iframe product spec

title: feat: add inline comments using GH review flow in ai-review
body:
## Describe your changes

Enable the AI review workflow to post inline-level comments on specific lines of code, in addition to the existing PR-level review body using the GitHub review flow.

Key changes:
- Individual review step: Each model now produces an inline_comments.json alongside review.md, containing file-path/line-targeted comments using Conventional Comments syntax.
- Consolidation step: A new triage phase deduplicates, ranks, and caps inline comments from all models before the judge consolidates the final review.
- Post review step: Switched from gh pr comment to the GitHub Reviews API (POST /pulls/{id}/reviews) so inline comments appear as review annotations on the diff. Includes a fallback that posts inline comments as a formatted summary in a plain comment if the API rejects any comment (e.g. invalid path/line).
- Hardening: Moved GitHub expression interpolations (${{ }}) into env vars to avoid script injection; added timeout-minutes: 10 to the post job; always post as COMMENT event (verdict is only used for labeling, not formal approval).
- New instruction docs: Added code-review-inline-comments-instructions.md (for individual reviewers) and code-review-consolidation-instructions.md (for the judge's triage step).

## Testing Plan

- Manually tested by running the workflow on this PR

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Summary

This PR enhances the AI PR review workflow (`ai-pr-review.yml`) to support inline code comments alongside the existing PR-level review body. It adds inline comment generation instructions to the review agent prompt, introduces a consolidation step with deduplication/ranking/capping rules (`code-review-consolidation-instructions.md`), and replaces `gh pr comment` with the GitHub Pull Request Reviews API to post structured reviews with inline annotations. The PR also hardens shell injection safety by migrating remaining `${{ }}` expressions in `run:` blocks to step-level `env:` variables.

## Code Quality

The workflow code is well-structured and follows existing patterns. The env-variable migration for shell injection prevention is thorough and consistent. The `jq` commands for merging and filtering inline comments are well-constructed with proper defensive checks (`jq -e` validation, `// []` fallback). The fallback from the Reviews API to a plain `gh pr comment` is a sensible safety net.

**Key issue:** The PR description explicitly mentions a "validation script to filter inline comments against the actual PR diff to ensure they target modified lines," but no such script is included. This is the primary gap identified by all three reviewers (see inline comments for details).

## Test Coverage

No automated test files are included, which is expected for a GitHub Actions workflow change. The PR description mentions unit tests for validation logic, but these are absent from the diff. The changes are partially self-validating through defensive `jq` filters, `continue-on-error: true`, and the API-to-comment fallback path. Manual end-to-end testing on a real PR (both single-model and multi-model modes) is the recommended validation approach.

## Backwards Compatibility

No concerns. Changes are limited to internal CI/CD automation. The existing review artifact flow (`review.md` + `verdict.txt`) is preserved, and no Streamlit library, runtime, frontend, or user

## Summary

This PR enhances the AI PR review workflow (`ai-pr-review.yml`) to support inline comments posted directly on specific lines of code via the GitHub Reviews API. Key changes include:

- **Inline comment generation**: Updated agent instructions (`reviewing-local-changes.md`, `code-review-instructions.md`) to produce `inline_comments.json` alongside `review.md` and `verdict.txt`.
- **Consolidation triage**: New `code-review-consolidation-instructions.md` with deduplication, ranking, and a 20-comment cap for the judge model.
- **Reviews API integration**: Replaced `gh pr comment` with the GitHub Pull Request Reviews API to post both the review body and inline comments in a single review, with proper `APPROVE`/`REQUEST_CHANGES` events.
- **Fallback handling**: When the Reviews API fails (e.g., 422 from invalid line references), falls back to posting a plain comment with inline comments appended in a `<details>` block.
- **Shell injection hardening**: Migrated several `${{ }}` expressions from `run:` blocks into step-level `env:` variables for improved security posture.

## Code Quality

**Strengths:**
- The `env:` variable migration is consistent and follows shell injection prevention guidelines. While some original expressions (`github.repository`, `github.run_id`) were already safe, the uniform approach improves readability and auditability.
- The jq-based inline comment validation (line 480) is well-constructed with safe iteration (`[]?`), field-presence checks, numeric validation, and a fallback to empty array.
- The inline comment merging logic using `jq -s` with null-coalescing (`// []`) correctly handles edge cases.
- The `timeout-minutes: 10` addition and `if [ ! -f inline_comments.json ]` guards are good hardening measures.

**Issues (all three reviewers concur):**
- **Verdict loss on fallback** (critical): When the Reviews API fails and the workflow falls back to `gh pr comment`, the `APPROVE`/`REQUEST_CHANGES` event is silently lost. All three revi

## Summary

This PR enhances the AI PR review workflow to post inline comments on specific lines of code via GitHub's Pull Request Reviews API. It adds inline comment generation instructions for individual reviewers, consolidation/triage logic for merging comments across models, robust fallback when the Reviews API rejects comments, and a systematic migration of `${{ }}` expressions to step-level `env:` variables for shell injection prevention.

**Changed files:**
- `.github/workflows/ai-pr-review.yml` — Core workflow: Reviews API posting, inline comment merging, fallback mechanism, env-variable migration
- `scripts/assets/code-review-inline-comments-instructions.md` (new) — Instructions for individual AI reviewers to generate inline comments
- `scripts/assets/code-review-consolidation-instructions.md` (new) — Instructions for the consolidation agent to triage inline comments

## Reviewer Consensus

| Reviewer | Verdict | Key Concern |
|---|---|---|
| `gemini-3.1-pro` | APPROVED | None — implementation is robust and secure |
| `gpt-5.3-codex-high` | CHANGES_REQUESTED | All-or-nothing inline comment failure drops valid comments |
| `opus-4.6-thinking` | APPROVED | Minor: `awk gsub` fragility, HEAD_SHA timing, PR description accuracy |

**Agreement areas (all 3 reviewers):**
- Shell injection prevention improvements are well-executed and follow `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` guidelines
- Changes are purely internal CI/CD — no impact on Streamlit library, runtime, frontend, or end users
- Fallback mechanism (Reviews API → plain comment with inline summary) is pragmatic and well-structured
- No external tests, accessibility review, or backwards compatibility concerns apply
- `jq` validation filter and `jq -s` merge logic are well-crafted

**Key disagreement — inline comment robustness:**
- `gpt-5.3-codex-high` requests changes because a single invalid comment causes the Reviews API to return 422, falling back to a plain comment and losing line-level positioning for all c

## Pull request overview

This PR enhances the `ai-pr-review` GitHub Actions workflow to generate, triage, and post **inline review comments** (review annotations) via the GitHub Reviews API, alongside the existing PR-level review body. It adds dedicated instruction assets to standardize the inline comment JSON schema for both individual model reviews and the consolidation/judge phase.

**Changes:**
- Add new instruction assets for producing (`inline_comments.json`) and triaging inline comments (`all_inline_comments.json` → `inline_comments.json`).
- Update the workflow to upload/download inline comment artifacts, aggregate per-model inline comments, and inject triage instructions into the consolidation prompt.
- Switch posting from `gh pr comment` to **Create a review** (Reviews API) with inline annotations, with a fallback to a plain PR comment if the API rejects any inline comment.

### Reviewed changes

| `.github/workflows/ai-pr-review.yml` | Produces/collects inline comment artifacts, triages candidates, and posts via Reviews API with fallback and injection hardening. |

Or, maybe on the converse, can we highlight things in the summary that are not addressed in inline comments?

## Summary

This PR enhances the AI code review workflow (`ai-pr-review.yml`) to support inline comments posted on specific lines of code via the GitHub Reviews API, in addition to the existing PR-level review body. It also introduces instruction files for inline comment generation and consolidation, applies security hardening by moving `${{ }}` interpolations to environment variables, and adds operational improvements like `timeout-minutes` and defensive defaults.

Key changes:
- **Individual review step**: Each model now produces `inline_comments.json` alongside `review.md` with file-path/line-targeted comments.
- **Consolidation step**: Merges inline comments from all models; the judge model triages, deduplicates, and caps at 20.
- **Post review step**: Switches from `gh pr comment` to the GitHub Reviews API (`POST /pulls/{id}/reviews`) with inline comments, plus a well-designed fallback that renders inline comments in a collapsible details block if the API rejects them.
- **Security hardening**: Moves `${{ }}` interpolations to `env:` variables in `run:` blocks, following shell injection prevention guidelines.
- **New instruction files**: `code-review-inline-comments-instructions.md` and `code-review-consolidation-instructions.md` keep the workflow YAML readable and instructions maintainable.
- **Deduplication of findings**: Instructions now explicitly direct models to separate inline findings from the PR-level summary to avoid redundancy.

## Code Quality

All three reviewers agreed the implementation is well-structured and follows existing patterns. The bash scripts in the workflow are written defensively with proper jq validation, fallback handling, and error recovery. Responsibilities are cleanly separated across individual-review, consolidation, and posting steps.

title: [Fix] Restore bound query params in URL with MPA
body:
## Describe your changes
When a widget uses `bind="query-params"` in a multi-page app, navigating away and back causes the URL parameter to be lost even though the widget's value persists in session state. This breaks URL shareability — the widget displays the correct value but the URL no longer reflects it.

The root cause is two-fold:
1. `_remove_stale_widgets` deletes the widget's value from `_old_state` during page transitions (stored under the widget ID, which is an element ID that gets filtered as stale), with no mechanism to preserve it for later remount.
2. `register_widget` has no code path to restore a missing URL parameter from a persisted non-default widget value.

**Changes:**
- **Value preservation** (`_remove_stale_widgets`): Before the stale filter runs, save bound keyed widget values under their user keys in `_old_state`. User keys are not element IDs, so they survive the filter. A durable `_query_param_bound_widget_ids` set tracks bound intent across page transitions where bindings and metadata may already be cleaned up.
- **URL sync** (`register_widget`): After value resolution, restore the URL parameter when `bind="query-params"` is active, the param is missing, the value is non-default, and no programmatic `st.session_state` set occurred this run. Default-value collapsing is preserved (defaults never appear in URL).
- **Bool normalization** (`_set_corrected_value`): Write `true`/`false` instead of `True`/`False` for bool query params to match URL conventions.

### GitHub Issue
Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/14350

## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python): ✅ 
- E2E Tests: ✅ 
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a multipage-app regression where widgets bound via `bind="query-params"` could keep their persisted value in `st.session_state` after page navigation, but lose the corresponding URL query parameter—breaking URL shareability and consistency.

**Changes:**
- Preserve stale *bound keyed* widget values across MPA page transitions by saving them under user keys during `_remove_stale_widgets`.
- Restore missing URL query params in `register_widget` for bound widgets when a persisted non-default value exists (while keeping default collapsing behavior).
- Normalize boolean query param serialization to `true`/`false` to match common URL conventions.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Preserves bound values across stale cleanup and re-syncs missing URL params on remount; tracks bound intent across page transitions. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/query_params.py | Ensures `_set_corrected_value` serializes bools as lowercase `true`/`false`. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state_test.py | Adds unit tests covering preservation + remount URL sync behavior, plus required context mocking. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/query_params_test.py | Extends scalar correction tests to include boolean normalization. |
| e2e_playwright/multipage_apps/mpa_basics_test.py | Adds E2E regression test verifying query param restoration after navigating away and back. |
</details>

title: [feature] `st.tabs` state persistence and CSS key class
body:
## Summary

Implements state persistence and CSS key class application for `st.tabs` containers, building on the block identity infrastructure from the parent PR.

### Changes

- **`Tabs.tsx`**: When a `st.tabs` has a `key` and is passive (non-dynamic), persists the active tab label to `elementStates` via `widgetMgr.setElementState/getElementState`. On mount, restores the previously active tab if it's still valid. Applies the `st-key-*` CSS class to the outermost `StyledTabContainer`.
- **`RenderNodeVisitor.tsx`**: Uses `node.deltaBlock.id` as the React `key` for block nodes when available, so keyed containers maintain component identity across positional shifts (e.g. when a conditional element above them causes a delta path change). Falls back to positional index for unkeyed blocks.
- **Frontend unit tests**: Added tests for CSS key class application, state persistence on mount, storage on tab click, fallback behavior, and non-persistence for unkeyed/dynamic tabs. Added test for `RenderNodeVisitor` block keying.
- **E2E tests**: Added `st_tabs_state_persistence.py` app and test suite verifying:
  - Keyed tabs persist the active tab across remounts caused by layout shifts
  - Unkeyed tabs reset to the default tab on remount
  - The `st-key-*` CSS class is correctly applied
  - Persistence works across multiple sequential tab switches

## Test plan

- [x] Frontend unit tests pass (`Tabs.test.tsx`, `RenderNodeVisitor.test.tsx`)
- [x] E2E tests pass (`st_tabs_state_persistence_test.py`)
- [x] `make check` passes
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

title: [feature] Stable Block.id for passive keyed layout containers
body:
## Describe your changes

Sets a stable `Block.id` via `compute_and_register_element_id` (with `key_as_main_identity=True`) for `st.tabs`, `st.expander`, and `st.popover` when `key` is provided and `on_change="ignore"` (the default). This gives these containers a position-independent identity — the foundation for frontend state persistence across remounts.

- Only `Block.id` is set, not the element-level ID (`tabContainer.id` / `expandable.id` / `popover.id`), so `session_state[key]` is not populated and the element is not registered as a widget
- Frontend `ElementsSetVisitor` now collects `Block.id` values and includes them in the active ID set passed to `removeInactive`, preventing `elementStates` entries from being garbage-collected

Follow-up PRs will wire up `useWidgetManagerElementState` in each component to persist active tab / expanded / open state across remounts, and apply `st-key-*` CSS classes.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Addresses https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8239

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
  - Python: `block_proto.id` set for passive keyed containers, absent without key, element-level ID not set
  - Frontend: `ElementsSetVisitor` collects block IDs from nested trees, ignores blocks without IDs

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces stable, key-based `Block.id` values for passive keyed layout containers (`st.tabs`, `st.expander`, `st.popover`) to enable frontend state persistence across remounts without registering these containers as widgets. On the frontend, active `Block.id` values are added to the “active IDs” set so `elementStates` keyed by `Block.id` are not garbage-collected.

**Changes:**
- Backend: Compute and set `Block.id` for keyed passive containers (and align stateful container IDs to be key-based when `key` is provided).
- Frontend: Extend `ElementsSetVisitor` to collect `Block.id` values and pass them to `WidgetStateManager.removeInactive`.
- Tests/spec: Add Python and frontend unit tests plus update the tech spec to reflect the new ID semantics.

### Reviewed changes

| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py | Adds regression tests for `Block.id` being set/stable for keyed passive containers and set for keyed stateful paths. |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/visitors/ElementsSetVisitor.ts | Collects `deltaBlock.id` values while traversing the render tree. |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/visitors/ElementsSetVisitor.test.ts | Adds tests validating `Block.id` collection behavior (including nested trees). |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/test-utils.ts | Adds `blockWithId` helper for visitor tests. |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/AppRoot.ts | Adds `getActiveIds()` to expose both active elements and collected block I
LGTM 👍

commits.message:
feat: set stable Block.id for passive keyed layout containers

When `key` is provided and `on_change="ignore"` (the default) for
st.tabs, st.expander, and st.popover, compute and set `block_proto.id`
via `compute_and_register_element_id` with `key_as_main_identity=True`.

This gives these containers a stable identity independent of their
position in the render tree, which is the foundation for frontend
state persistence across remounts (coming in follow-up PRs).

Only `Block.id` is set — NOT the element-level ID (e.g.
`tabContainer.id`), so these elements are not registered as widgets
and `session_state[key]` is not populated.

On the frontend, extend `ElementsSetVisitor` to collect `Block.id`
values and include them in the active ID set passed to
`removeInactive`, preventing elementStates entries from being
garbage-collected prematurely.

Addresses #8239

empty when on_change="rerun" or a callback is used (expander already

is a false positive (callback runs post-commit) and that converting

Change the stateful path (on_change="rerun" / callback) for st.tabs,
st.expander, and st.popover to use key_as_main_identity=True, matching

Previously these used key_as_main_identity=False, which caused the

title: [chore] Enable `noImplicitReturns` TypeScript compiler option
body:
## Describe your changes

Enables the `noImplicitReturns` TypeScript compiler option, which ensures all code paths in functions explicitly return a value. This improves type safety by catching potential bugs at compile time.

- Enables `noImplicitReturns: true` in `frontend/typescript-config/base.tsconfig.json`
- Fixes ~12 code locations to satisfy the stricter rule by adding explicit returns

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A - Code quality improvement

## Testing Plan

- No runtime behavior changes; this is a compile-time check only
- Existing unit tests pass (541 tests)
- All fixes either add explicit `return undefined` where undefined was already returned implicitly, or add `return` before `assertNever()` calls

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 2 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 2 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 3 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 1 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Enables the TypeScript `noImplicitReturns` compiler option for the frontend to enforce explicit returns on all code paths, and updates affected callsites to satisfy the stricter compile-time check.

**Changes:**
- Turn on `noImplicitReturns: true` in the shared frontend base tsconfig.
- Add explicit `return undefined` in several hooks/effects/utilities where some branches already returned a value/cleanup.
- Update `assertNever` switch defaults to explicitly `return assertNever(...)` to satisfy return-path checking.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/typescript-config/base.tsconfig.json | Enables `noImplicitReturns` in the shared TS compiler settings. |
| frontend/lib/src/util/uploadFiles.ts | Adds an explicit return in an async `map` callback used with `Promise.all`. |
| frontend/lib/src/theme/utils.ts | Makes `parseFontSize` explicitly return `undefined` on invalid input. |
| frontend/lib/src/hooks/useScrollToBottom.ts | Adds explicit `return undefined` from `useEffect` when no cleanup is registered. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/NumberInput/NumberInput.tsx | Adds explicit `return undefined` in an effect when the ref is missing. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/styled-components.ts | Ensures the style callback returns an object on all paths. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useColumnLoader.ts | Adds explicit `return undefined` in a `mergeWith` customizer. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Tabs/Tabs.tsx | Adds explicit `return undefined` from an effect when `tabListRef` is null. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Dialog/Dialog.tsx | Adds explicit `return undefined` from an effect when not attaching listeners. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/DeckGlJsonChart/useDeckGl.tsx

commits.message:
Enable noImplicitReturns TypeScript compiler option

Add noImplicitReturns: true to base tsconfig to ensure all code paths
explicitly return a value. Fix ~12 locations by adding explicit returns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: record failed uploads when no upload URL found

This addresses a bug where files that fail due to missing upload URLs
were not being recorded in failedUploads array. The return value from
the Promise.all callback was never read, so the failure was silently
dropped. Now we properly push to failedUploads when file is defined.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add unit tests for uploadFiles utility

Add test coverage for the uploadFiles function, verifying:
- Failed uploads when fetchFileURLs throws
- Failed uploads when upload URL is missing
- Failed uploads when file ID is missing
- Successful uploads with valid URLs
- Mixed success/failure scenarios
- Failed uploads when uploadFile throws

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/no-implicit-returns

Preview features

  • Add support for lazy import parsing (#23755)

title: [chore] Add AI skill and workflow for fixing flaky E2E tests
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds AI-powered tooling for diagnosing and fixing flaky E2E tests:

- New `/fixing-flaky-e2e-tests` skill with comprehensive instructions for reproducing, diagnosing, and fixing flaky Playwright tests
- Python script (`scripts/fetch_flaky_tests.py`) to identify top flaky tests from CI artifacts
- Weekly GitHub Actions workflow (`ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests.yml`) that automatically creates PRs to fix flaky tests

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- Documentation and tooling changes only, no runtime behavior modifications
- Python script follows repository conventions and passes all linting/type checks
- Workflow follows established patterns from `ai-update-docs.yml`

<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|

| skill | finalizing-pr | 1 |
| skill | fixing-flaky-e2e-tests | 1 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 2 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 1 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 1 |

</details>

author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds AI-driven tooling to identify and automatically propose fixes for flaky Playwright E2E tests in Streamlit, combining a new “fixing flaky E2E tests” skill, a helper script to mine CI rerun stats, and a scheduled workflow that generates PRs with suggested fixes.

**Changes:**
- Added `scripts/fetch_flaky_tests.py` to aggregate rerun-heavy tests from recent successful `playwright.yml` runs.
- Added a new `.claude/skills/fixing-flaky-e2e-tests` skill describing a reproducible workflow for diagnosing/fixing flakiness.
- Added a weekly `ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests.yml` workflow to run the skill, produce a patch + summary, and open an automated PR.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `scripts/fetch_flaky_tests.py` | New CLI utility to download `playwright_test_stats` artifacts and aggregate rerun counts. |
| `.github/workflows/ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests.yml` | New scheduled + manual workflow to run the agent, generate/apply a patch, and create a PR. |
| `.claude/skills/fixing-flaky-e2e-tests/SKILL.md` | New skill documentation for reproducing and fixing flaky Playwright tests. |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Adds the new skill to the skills list. |
| `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` | Documents the new workflow in the workflow catalog. |
| `.github/instructions/workflows.instructions.md` | Mirrors the workflow catalog update. |
| `.cursor/rules/workflows.mdc` | Mirrors the workflow catalog update for Cursor rules. |
| `.claude/.gitignore` | Allows the new skill directory to be tracked. |
</details>

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).

LGTM

One thing I was considering making a command/skill for in this area is comparing snapshots diffs to determine cause of the failure. It could be useful here, but also for non-flaky failures. I ran this with the agent once and it was really helpful. What do you think?

commits.message:
Add AI skill and workflow for fixing flaky E2E tests

- Add `/fixing-flaky-e2e-tests` skill with instructions for diagnosing and fixing flaky Playwright tests
- Add `fetch_flaky_tests.py` script to identify top flaky tests from CI artifacts
- Add `ai-fix-flaky-e2e-tests.yml` workflow for weekly automated flaky test fixing
- Update documentation (CONTRIBUTING.md, workflow AGENTS.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address PR review comments

- Reconcile contradictory guidance about skip_browser vs "don't disable tests"
- Add binary support for patch files to preserve snapshot updates
- Fix shell injection by using env vars instead of ${{ }} interpolation
- Add shebang and make script executable for consistency
- Rename runs_with_reruns to browser_rerun_count for accuracy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Firefox subpixel rendering workaround to flaky e2e skill

Document a workaround for Firefox snapshot flakiness caused by subpixel
rendering variations. Adding a one-liner markdown element above the
tested element can shift the subpixel position to a more stable value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove escapes from bash variable interpolation in workflow

The backslash-escaped variables \${TOP_N} and \${DAYS} were not being
interpolated by bash, resulting in literal text being sent to the AI
agent instead of actual values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve fetch_flaky_tests.py reliability

- Harmonize rerun_count default to 0 in both filter and output dict
  for consistency (previously 0 in filter, 1 in output)
- Filter gh run list to develop branch to exclude feature branch runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert: remove branch filter from fetch_flaky_tests.py

Include all successful workflow runs, not just develop branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: increase default top_n flaky tests to 10

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add snapshot comparison guidance to flaky e2e skill

Added details about comparing actual vs expected snapshots in
the test-results/snapshot-updates folder to help diagnose
persistent snapshot flakiness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Bump ty from 0.0.21 to 0.0.22
body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.21 to 0.0.22.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.22

Release Notes

Released on 2026-03-12.

Bug fixes

  • Fix issue where variables could be inferred as Divergent if they were assigned using tuple unpacking in loops (#23812)
  • Allow error = "all" in a root pyproject.toml file to be overridden using tool.ty.overrides in a subdirectory's pyproject.toml file (#23712)
  • Only unsoundly upcast type[] types to their constructor Callable type during assignability checks, not during redundancy/subtyping checks (#23834, #23901)
  • Fix stack overflow that could occur with certain recursive protocols (#23870)

LSP server

  • Improve syntax highlighting by fixing semantic token classification for generic class members in annotations (#23811)

CLI

  • Add ty explain <RULE> CLI command (#23766)

Core type checking

  • Add validation for type parameters with defaults after TypeVarTuple parameters (#23807)
  • Allow subtypes of LiteralString to be narrowed using equality checks (#23794)
  • Detect invalid partially stringified PEP-604 unions (#23285)
  • Disambiguate duplicate-looking overloaded callables in union display (#23907)
  • Don't promote module-literal types to types.ModuleType (#23786)
  • Improve type context support for __setitem__ dunder calls (#23800)
  • Infer t | {"foo": int} as TD if t is an instance of a TypedDict td with a foo: int key (#23806)
  • Narrow T to T & str rather than str if T is a constrained TypeVar with str as one of its constraints (#23850)
  • Promote None to None | Unknown in invariant contexts (#23790)
  • Reject type[Callable] in type annotations (#23753)
  • Support enum member access through enum instances and members (#23772)
  • Eagerly narrow the type associated with keys of heterogeneous dict literals even when a dict literal appears as a subexpression inside a list or tuple literals (#23569)
