title: [Fix] Duplicate `GUEST_READY` messages on bypass
body:
## Describe your changes
Fixes duplicate `GUEST_READY` message sent during initialization when the host-config bypass (window variable fast-path) is enabled.
When bypass is enabled, `setAllowedOrigins` is called twice during init:
1. From `applyInitialHostConfig()` — using window variables for the fast-path
2. From the `onHostConfigResp` callback — when background `doInitPings` resolves
Each call triggered `openHostCommunication()`, which unconditionally added a `receiveHostMessage` listener and sent `GUEST_READY`. This caused:
- Two `GUEST_READY` messages (which SiS interprets as connection issues)
- Duplicate `receiveHostMessage` listeners on `window`

This PR makes `openHostCommunication` idempotent by adding an `isHostCommOpen` flag. The first call opens communication (registers the listener, sends `GUEST_READY`). Subsequent calls are no-ops. The flag resets in `closeHostCommunication` for clean teardown.

This makes both bypass and non-bypass paths consistent: exactly one `GUEST_READY` per app lifecycle. Connection health continues to be communicated via the existing `WEBSOCKET_CONNECTED` / `WEBSOCKET_DISCONNECTED` messages, which are unaffected.

Addresses [SNOW-3225743](https://snowflakecomputing.atlassian.net/browse/SNOW-3225743)

### Behavior clarification
When `window.__streamlit.HOST_CONFIG` provides valid values, those values take precedence over endpoint values during reconciliation.
For example:
- window `allowedOrigins = [A]`
- endpoint `allowedOrigins = [A, B]`
- effective reconciled `allowedOrigins = [A]`
So this case has deterministic behavior and does not reopen host comms or emit extra `GUEST_READY` messages.

## Testing Plan
- JS Unit Tests: ✅ Added
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes duplicate `GUEST_READY` messages (and duplicate `message` listeners) during host-comm initialization when the host-config bypass fast-path is enabled by making host communication opening idempotent.

**Changes:**
- Add an `isHostCommOpen` flag to make `openHostCommunication()` idempotent and reset it in `closeHostCommunication()`.
- Add unit tests to ensure `GUEST_READY` is only sent once across multiple `setAllowedOrigins()` calls, and is re-sent after teardown + re-init.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/hostComm/HostCommunicationManager.tsx | Makes host communication setup idempotent to prevent duplicate listeners and duplicate `GUEST_READY`. |
| frontend/lib/src/hostComm/HostCommunicationManager.test.tsx | Adds regression tests covering repeated initialization and teardown/re-init behavior. |





---

## Pull request overview

Completes the `st.file_uploader` frontend redesign to align its UX and styling with `st.chat_input`, including updated file chip styling, updated dropzone behavior, and refreshed unit/E2E coverage.

**Changes:**
- Refactors FileUploader dropzone to render uploaded file chips inside the dropzone (with an “Add files” trailing action) and adds a drag-active overlay state.
- Updates shared uploaded-file chip styling + theme primitives to support the new layout and sizing.
- Updates unit tests, Playwright assertions, and a large set of E2E snapshots to match the new UI.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 230 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/theme/primitives/sizes.ts | Renames/introduces size tokens for uploaded-file chip UI and adds a min-width token for chip name layout. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/UploadedFiles.tsx | Extends UploadedFiles wrapper to pass optional trailing content into the chips row. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileUploader.tsx | Moves uploaded-files rendering into the dropzone, adds “Add files” affordance, and simplifies upload-type filtering/progress handling. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileUploader.test.tsx | Updates expectations for new button labeling and verifies “Add files” affordance behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileDropzoneInstructions.tsx | Simplifies instructions content (size/type info) to match redesigned UI. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileDropzoneInstructions.test.tsx | Updates instruction text expectations and adds a disabled render test. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileDropzone.tsx | Adds drag overlay + conditional rendering between instructions vs. uploaded files content. |
| frontend/

LGTM 👍  It looks like it might solve these two issues with the previous component?:
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/6454
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8151

The AI suggestion seems somewhat reasonable:

> Hide or relabel "Add files" button for single-file uploaders

Changing the aria label would be easy, but might still be a bit confusing UI-wise for single upload cases. Maybe we always hide the add button for single upload cases and require removing the existing file? This might probably need another design/product check on this aspect, but can be handled as a follow up.

title: [docs] AI-assisted documentation updates
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to update internal documentation based on AI review.

This PR was created by the `ai-update-docs` workflow using `opus-4.6-thinking`.

## Changes

# Documentation Review Changes

Review scope: All internal documentation files (AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, wiki/, CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md, .github/workflows/AGENTS.md)

## Changes Applied

### 1. [INCORRECT] CONTRIBUTING.md:98 — Homebrew install command

**File:** `CONTRIBUTING.md`

The Homebrew install command used `/usr/bin/ruby -e` (the old Ruby-based invocation) with the new `install.sh` URL. Ruby cannot execute a shell script, so this command would fail.

- **Before:** `/usr/bin/ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"`
- **After:** `/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"`

### 2. [MISSING] frontend/AGENTS.md:117-124 — Missing workspace package

**File:** `frontend/AGENTS.md`

The Yarn Workspaces packages list was missing `component-lib` (Library for building Streamlit custom components v1). The actual workspaces in `frontend/package.json` list 9 packages but only 8 were documented. Also reordered the list alphabetically to match `frontend/package.json`.

- **Added:** `component-lib` - Library for building Streamlit custom components v1.
- **Reordered** list alphabetically for consistency with `package.json`.

## Verification Summary

The following areas were verified with no issues found:

- **Make commands**: All documented `make` targets exist and descriptions match `make help` output
- **File/folder paths**: All referenced paths across all docs verified to exist
- **Tool versions**: TypeScript v5, React v18, Vite v8, Vitest v4, ESLint v9, oxlint v1, Prettier v3, Emotion v11, RTL v16, Yarn v4, Ruff 0.x, mypy 1.x, pytest 9.x, Python 3.10-3.14 — all match config files
- **Workflow documentation**: All 41 workflow files documented in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`; no missing or extra workflows

- **Code examples**: Referenced file patterns match actual codebase structure

frontend/AGENTS.md    | 11 ++++++-----

title: [feat] Complete incomplete markdown syntax during streaming
body:
## Describe your changes

- Add internal `_markdown` method with `unterminated_parsing` parameter for streaming support
- Add `unterminated_parsing` proto field to control frontend markdown completion behavior
- Integrates the `remend` package to automatically close unclosed markdown syntax (e.g., `**bold`, `` `code ``, ` ``` `code blocks) while content is being streamed via `st.write_stream`
- Updates `write_stream` to use `_markdown` with `unterminated_parsing=True` during streaming
- Updates frontend to conditionally apply remend only when `unterminatedParsing=true`
- Improves visual appearance during streaming by showing properly styled text instead of raw markdown syntax characters
- Skips processing for labels, HTML content, and when `unterminatedParsing` is not set

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python) - Tests for `_markdown` with `unterminated_parsing` parameter
- [x] Unit Tests (Frontend) - Tests for `unterminatedParsing` conditional behavior
- [x] E2E Tests - Basic write_stream functionality (note: mid-stream state can't be reliably tested)
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR integrates the `remend` package to automatically complete incomplete markdown syntax during streaming operations via `st.write_stream`. This improves the visual appearance by showing properly styled text instead of raw markdown characters while content is being streamed character-by-character.

**Changes:**
- Added `remend` npm package (v1.2.0) dependency for markdown syntax completion
- Integrated `remend` in `StreamlitMarkdown` component to auto-close unclosed markdown (bold, italic, code blocks, etc.) during rendering
- Added comprehensive unit tests for remend integration covering both positive cases (completion) and negative cases (when NOT to apply)
- Added E2E tests to verify the feature works end-to-end with actual streaming

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| ---- | ----------- |

| frontend/lib/package.json | Added `remend` package dependency |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.tsx | Integrated remend to complete incomplete markdown syntax, skipping for labels and HTML content; updated useMemo dependencies |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/StreamlitMarkdown/StreamlitMarkdown.test.tsx | Added comprehensive unit tests for remend integration including positive and negative test cases |
| e2e_playwright/st_write_stream.py | Added test cases for streaming incomplete bold and code block markdown |
| e2e_playwright/st_write_stream_test.py | Added E2E tests to verify incomplete markdown is properly styled during streaming |
| NOTICES | Added Apache 2.0 license notice for the remend package from Vercel |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

commits.message:
[feat] Complete incomplete markdown syntax during streaming

Integrates the remend package to automatically close unclosed markdown
syntax (e.g., **bold, *italic, `code, ```code blocks) while content is
being streamed via st.write_stream. This improves the visual appearance
during streaming by showing properly styled text instead of raw markdown
syntax characters.

[fix] Fix E2E tests for incomplete markdown streaming

- Pass generator directly to write_stream instead of wrapping in lambda
  (lambda returns a generator but is not itself a generator function)
- Add negative assertions to verify raw markdown syntax is not visible

[fix] Disable italic completion in remend to fix underscore handling

Underscores in identifiers (like Python repr strings `<bound method
Foo._bar>`) were incorrectly being treated as incomplete italic markdown
and getting extra underscores appended. This caused the st_components_v1
E2E test to fail.

Disabled italic completion in remend (both `*` and `_` styles) to prevent
this issue. Bold (`**`) completion still works for streaming scenarios.

CI workflow.

- Add `unterminated_parsing` proto field to control remend markdown completion
- Update `write_stream` to use `_markdown` with `unterminated_parsing=True` during streaming
- Update frontend to conditionally apply remend only when `unterminatedParsing=true`

title: [fix] Restore reactJsonViewCompat shim for local dev server
body:
## Describe your changes

Restores the `reactJsonViewCompat.ts` compatibility shim that was removed in #14380. The direct import from `@microlink/react-json-view` works in CI builds but fails when running locally with the dev server (e.g., `make debug` or `make frontend-dev`).

**Changes:**
- Restored `frontend/lib/src/util/reactJsonViewCompat.ts` with updated imports for `@microlink/react-json-view`
- Updated `Json.tsx`, `useJsonTooltip.ts`, and `JsonViewer.tsx` to import through the compat shim

**Why this is needed:**
- `@microlink/react-json-view` is published as CommonJS
- Vite 8 interop can yield different runtime module shapes (`module`, `module.default`, `module.default.default`) depending on the optimization path
- Without normalization, Json components fail with "Element type is invalid" errors in local dev

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Related to #14380

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (existing tests pass)
- [x] Manual testing with `make debug` to verify Json elements render correctly

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a Vite 8 compatibility shim for `@microlink/react-json-view` (CommonJS) to ensure the JSON viewer renders reliably across different Vite dependency-optimization module shapes, and updates internal consumers to use the shim.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `reactJsonViewCompat.ts` to normalize nested `default` exports via `resolveDefaultExport`.
- Switches DataFrame JSON cell viewer and the JSON element to import the ReactJson component (and related types) from the compat shim.
- Updates JSON tooltip hook to import `OnSelectProps` from the compat shim to keep type usage consistent.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/util/reactJsonViewCompat.ts | New shim to normalize `@microlink/react-json-view` default export shape under Vite 8. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/columns/cells/JsonViewer.tsx | Uses the compat ReactJson import for DataFrame JSON cell overlays. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Json/useJsonTooltip.ts | Imports `OnSelectProps` from the shim to align with the new import path. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Json/Json.tsx | Uses the compat ReactJson import and pulls `OnCopyProps` from the shim. |

commits.message:
[fix] Restore reactJsonViewCompat shim for local dev server

The direct import from @microlink/react-json-view works in CI but fails
when running locally with the hot-reload dev server due to Vite 8
CommonJS interop issues causing "Element type is invalid" errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[test] Add unit tests for resolveDefaultExport utility

Add comprehensive unit tests for the resolveDefaultExport function that
handles CommonJS module interop under Vite 8. Tests cover:
- Various module shapes (no wrapper, single default, double nested)
- Depth limiting behavior
- Edge cases (null, undefined, primitives, arrays)

This addresses the review feedback requesting test coverage for the
compatibility shim to prevent regressions during Vite/dependency upgrades.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Use shared `FileChips` in `st.file_uploader`
body:
## Describe your changes
Replace `st.file_uploader`'s old `UploadedFile` component and `withPagination` HOC with the shared `UploadedFileChips`. This is a structural change preparing for the visual redesign — the file uploader now renders the same chip components as `st.chat_input`.

**Note**:  This does not implement the full extent of file uploader re-design. See next PR

Key changes:
- `UploadedFiles.tsx` now delegates to shared `UploadedFileChips` instead of the old `UploadedFile` + `withPagination` pipeline
- Removed `newestToOldestFiles` reversal (files now render in insertion order)
- Added `disabled` prop to shared `UploadedFileChip` / `UploadedFileChips`
- Deleted old components: `UploadedFile.tsx`, `withPagination/` directory, and their associated styled-components and tests
- Migrated all consumers to barrel imports from `~lib/components/shared/UploadedFile`
- Updated unit and E2E test selectors (`stFileUploaderFile` → `stFileChip`, `stFileUploaderFileErrorMessage` → `role="alert"`, etc.)

## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python): ✅ Updated
- E2E Tests: ✅ Updated
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors uploaded-file rendering across the frontend to use the shared `UploadedFileChips` implementation (instead of the FileUploader-specific uploaded file list + pagination), and consolidates `UploadedFile` imports via the `~lib/components/shared/UploadedFile` barrel. It also updates unit/E2E tests and snapshots to match the new UI structure and test IDs.

**Changes:**
- Replace FileUploader’s uploaded-file list + pagination HOC with `UploadedFileChips` and remove related components/tests.
- Add `disabled` support to uploaded-file chips (delete button) and update widget/test selectors from `stFileUploader*` to `stFileChip*`.
- Update E2E assertions and snapshot baselines for the new chip-based UI and file ordering.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 18 out of 36 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/util/FileHelper.ts | Switch `UploadFileInfo` import to the UploadedFile barrel export. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/UploadedFiles.tsx | Replace paginated list rendering with shared `UploadedFileChips`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileUploader.tsx | Remove newest-first memo/pagination props; render chips directly from `files`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileUploader.test.tsx | Update selectors/counts/order expectations to chip UI and alert-based errors. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatInput/fileUpload/createFileUploadHandler.ts | Update `UploadFileInfo` import to barrel. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatInput/fileUpload/createDropHandler.ts | Update `UploadFileInfo` import to barrel. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatInput/ChatInput.tsx | Switch to barrel import for `UploadedFileChips` + status types. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/CameraInput/CameraInput.tsx | Updat
LGTM 👍

title: [feature] Add `alignment` parameter to `column_config` columns
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds an `alignment` parameter to `st.column_config.Column` and all typed column configs (TextColumn, NumberColumn, etc.) that controls horizontal text alignment (left, center, right) of cell content in `st.dataframe` and `st.data_editor`.

- Adds `ContentAlignment` type alias (`Literal["left", "center", "right"]`)
- Adds `alignment` parameter to all 19 column config functions
- Default is `None`, which preserves existing column-type-specific defaults

The frontend already supports this via Glide Data Grid's `contentAlign` property - this PR exposes it in the Python API.

The following column types do not support alignment: `SelectboxColumn`, `ListColumn`, `MultiselectColumn`, `BarChartColumn`, `LineChartColumn`, `AreaChartColumn`, `ProgressColumn`. 

- [Product spec](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/40213283ec93eea1778ad63c9ca14f033ffd2ec9/specs/2026-03-11-dataframe-column-alignment/product-spec.md)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #12106
- Related: #6706

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/lib/column_types_test.py` — Tests alignment parameter on all column types
- [x] E2E Tests
  - `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_config_test.py` — Visual regression test for alignment

## Product Spec

- See `specs/2026-03-11-dataframe-column-alignment/product-spec.md`

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 2 |
| skill | finalizing-pr | 1 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 2 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 2 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 7 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 2 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 2 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR exposes horizontal cell-content alignment in `st.dataframe` / `st.data_editor` by adding an `alignment` parameter to `st.column_config.Column` and all typed column config helpers, leveraging the frontend’s existing support for Glide Data Grid `contentAlign`.

**Changes:**
- Added `ContentAlignment` type alias and `alignment` to `ColumnConfig` plus all column config helper functions.
- Added Python unit tests to ensure `alignment` is propagated into the produced column config dict.
- Extended the Playwright dataframe configuration screenshot test app + assertions to cover alignment.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `specs/2026-03-11-dataframe-column-alignment/product-spec.md` | New product spec describing the alignment API and intended behavior. |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/lib/column_types.py` | Adds `ContentAlignment` + `alignment` to `Column()` and all typed column configs, and updates docstrings/types. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/lib/column_types_test.py` | Adds unit tests for alignment propagation and default omission behavior. |
| `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_config.py` | Adds a new “Column alignment” example dataframe for visual verification. |
| `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_config_test.py` | Increments expected dataframe count and adds a new snapshot assertion for the alignment example. |
</details>

commits.message:
Add alignment parameter to column config

Add alignment parameter to st.column_config.Column and all typed column
configs to control horizontal text alignment in dataframe cells. The
frontend already supports this via Glide Grid's contentAlign property.

- Add ContentAlignment type alias (Literal["left", "center", "right"])
- Add alignment parameter to all 19 column config functions
- Add unit tests and E2E test for alignment
- Add product spec for the feature

Closes #12106

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: clarify NumberColumn alignment docstring for number-like types

Updated the alignment parameter docstring for NumberColumn to note
that some number-like types (e.g., durations) may use a different
default alignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[snapshots] Update E2E snapshots for #14333 (#14335)

## Describe your changes

Automated snapshot updates for #14333 created via the snapshot autofix
CI workflow.

This workflow was triggered by adding the `update-snapshots` label to a
PR after Playwright E2E tests failed with snapshot mismatches.

**Updated snapshots:** 6 file(s)

⚠️ **Please review the snapshot changes carefully** - they could mask
visual bugs if accepted blindly.

This PR targets a feature branch and can be merged without review
approval.

Co-authored-by: Streamlit Bot <core+streamlitbot-github@streamlit.io>

fix: remove alignment param from column types that don't support it

Several column types (SelectboxColumn, chart columns, ProgressColumn,
ListColumn, MultiselectColumn) use third-party or custom renderers that
don't honor glide-data-grid's contentAlign property. This removes the
alignment parameter from these columns to avoid misleading users.

Also updates the E2E test to use right-aligned checkbox (instead of
center) to actually demonstrate the alignment feature works, since
checkboxes are center-aligned by default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[snapshots] Update E2E snapshots for column alignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: specify default alignment in column type docstrings

Updates alignment parameter docstrings to explicitly state the default
alignment for each column type instead of using generic wording. Also
removes the product spec as it's no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/column-alignment

Resolved conflict in column_types.py by keeping both the hidden column
note from develop and the alignment parameter docstring from this branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add frontend validation for invalid alignment values

Add parseAlignmentConfig() to validate column alignment configuration.
Invalid values now log a console warning and fall back to default
alignment instead of being silently ignored by the Canvas 2D API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [chore] Enable ruff DOC rules and fix docstring issues
body:
## Describe your changes

Enables ruff's DOC (documentation) rules for enhanced docstring linting and fixes docstring issues across the codebase.

- Selectively enables DOC rules while ignoring noisy completeness checks (DOC201, DOC402, DOC501, DOC502)
- Adds `ignore-one-line-docstrings = true` for pydoclint to reduce noise on simple docstrings
- Standardizes `Example` section headers to `Examples` (plural) per NumPy convention
- Removes unnecessary `Returns None` documentation sections
- Fixes parameter documentation to match actual function signatures

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- Documentation-only changes with no behavioral modifications
- All existing unit tests pass
- Linting and type checking pass with new rules enabled

## Pull request overview

This PR tightens Python docstring linting by enabling Ruff’s `DOC` (pydoclint) rules (while excluding the noisiest completeness checks) and updates docstrings across the repo to better conform to the NumPy docstring convention (notably standardizing `Examples` headers and removing redundant `Returns None` sections).

**Changes:**
- Enable Ruff `DOC` rules with selective ignores, and configure pydoclint to ignore one-line docstrings.
- Standardize docstring section headers (`Example` → `Examples`) and remove redundant `Returns None` sections.
- Adjust select docstrings to better match actual signatures/params (e.g., some parameter name corrections).

title: chore: upgrade vite v7 -> v8
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR upgrades the frontend workspace from Vite 7 to Vite 8 (and Vitest 4.0.x to 4.1.x), and applies the migration fixes needed for Streamlit’s build/dev/test flows.

One of the biggest benefits for this is perf.
1. `make frontend-fast` is now 72% faster (50s -> 14s)
2. Rolldown changes the way bundles are built and split, so now our entry file is 85% smaller
3. Wheel size is 1.5% smaller

- Upgraded Vite across frontend workspaces:
  - `vite`: `^7.3.1` -> `^8.0.0`
  - `@vitejs/plugin-react-swc`: `^4.2.3` -> `^4.3.0`
- Upgraded Vitest-related deps:
  - `vitest`: `^4.0.18` -> `^4.1.0`
  - `@vitest/coverage-v8`: `^4.0.18` -> `^4.1.0`
- Migrated Vite config keys from Rollup naming to Rolldown naming:
  - `build.rollupOptions` -> `build.rolldownOptions` in `app`, `lib`, `connection`, and `component-lib`
- Updated app asset handling to match Vite 8/Rolldown output behavior:
  - CSS/media routing now uses `assetInfo.names`-based checks
  - Added `renderBuiltUrl` handling for CSS font URLs so emitted font paths stay stable (`../media/...`)
- Added Vite 8 interop compatibility shims for mixed/CJS packages:
  - `react-uid` alias to local compat module
  - New compat wrappers for `react-json-view` and `react-plotly.js` to normalize nested default exports
  - Updated call sites (JSON, DataFrame JSON viewer, Plotly, related test imports) to consume compat modules
- Added runtime guard for DeckGL/Luma canvas resize edge case:
  - Patch for `CanvasContext.getMaxDrawingBufferSize()` to avoid crashes when `device.limits` is temporarily unavailable
- Adjusted BaseUI import pathing in theme creation to avoid interop/runtime issues under Vite 8
- Updated `knip` ignore config for newly introduced compat file
- Refreshed lockfile and updated theming E2E snapshots to reflect new build/render outputs

Per the Vite migration guidance, Vite 8 introduces Rolldown-based internals and changes CommonJS interop behavior. This branch applies the required config renames and adds targeted compatibility fixes for dependencies that surfaced runtime/import shape issues after the upgrade.

Reference: [Vite Migration from v7](https://vite.dev/guide/migration)

- The compatibility wrappers/patches are intentionally scoped and documented as temporary safeguards for Vite 8 migration stability.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

- E2E tests will catch a majority of this
- Manually tested dev mode, building in prod mode, running in prod mode, etc.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Upgrades the frontend toolchain from Vite 7 → Vite 8 (and Vitest 4.0.x → 4.1.x) across the monorepo, applying required migration changes (Rolldown config renames, CJS interop shims) and updating build output handling and snapshots to keep Streamlit’s build/dev/test flows working.

**Changes:**
- Bump Vite/Vitest (and related deps) across frontend workspaces and refresh `yarn.lock`.
- Migrate Vite configs from `build.rollupOptions` → `build.rolldownOptions`, and adjust app asset URL handling for Rolldown output.
- Add targeted runtime/interop shims: BaseUI theme import workaround, `react-json-view` / `react-plotly.js` compat modules, `react-uid` alias shim, and a DeckGL/Luma canvas resize guard.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 24 out of 46 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/yarn.lock | Lockfile refresh for Vite 8 / Vitest 4.1 dependency graph (Rolldown, lightningcss, etc.). |
| frontend/package.json | Bump root dev deps for Vitest + coverage. |
| frontend/utils/package.json | Bump workspace dev deps (vite/vitest). |
| frontend/app/package.json | Bump app dev deps (vite, vitest, plugin-react-swc). |
| frontend/app/vite.config.ts | Switch to `rolldownOptions`, add `react-uid` alias, adjust asset routing and `renderBuiltUrl` for fonts. |
| frontend/app/src/util/reactUidCompat.ts | New Vite-8 interop shim for `react-uid` (used via Vite alias). |
| frontend/lib/package.json | Bump lib dev deps (vite/vitest/plugin-react-swc). |
| frontend/lib/vite.config.ts | Switch to `build.rolldownOptions` for lib build. |
| frontend/lib/src/theme/createBaseUiTheme.ts | BaseUI import pathing adjusted for Vite 8 interop behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/util/reactJsonViewCompat.ts | New compat wrapper to normalize CJS default export shape for `react-json-view`. |

title: [Fix] Harden workflows against injection
body:
## Describe your changes
Ticket was filed to suggest areas for improvement in hardening our github actions against injection. See [SNOW-3230356](https://snowflakecomputing.atlassian.net/browse/SNOW-3230356). PR attempts to close some of these gaps.
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR hardens multiple GitHub Actions workflows against script-injection risks by reducing direct interpolation of `${{ ... }}` expressions inside `run:` blocks and instead passing values through step `env` variables with safer quoting.

**Changes:**
- Replace inline `${{ ... }}` usage in shell commands with step-level `env` variables (e.g., branch names, refs, PR/issue URLs).

title: [fix] Restore tooltip on st.multiselect selected options
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes a regression introduced in v1.55.0 where tooltips no longer appear when hovering over selected options in `st.multiselect`.

**Root cause:** PR #13796 added `pointerEvents: "none"` to the Tag Root element to allow clicks to pass through to the input field. This unintentionally disabled the browser's native `title` tooltip on the tag text.

**Fix:** Added `pointerEvents: "auto"` to the Text override of the Tag component, re-enabling hover events specifically on the text element so the native tooltip appears. This follows the same pattern already used for the Action (close button) override.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #14351

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests - All 44 existing Multiselect tests pass
- [x] E2E Tests - Added `test_multiselect_selected_tags_have_title_attribute` to verify tags have title attributes for tooltips

---

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

title: [fix] Python 3.14 PEP 649 annotation compatibility across `inspect.signature()` calls
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes Python 3.14 PEP 649 compatibility across multiple `inspect.signature()` call sites. On Python 3.14+, deferred annotation evaluation can raise `NameError` for functions using type annotations that reference types imported under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`.

**Implementation:**
- Added centralized `get_func_parameters()` helper in `type_util.py` with `annotation_format=Format.STRING` on Python 3.14+
- Added `_get_arg_keywords()` helper in `metrics_util.py` for PEP 649-safe argument name extraction

**Fixed locations:**
- `@st.cache_data` / `@st.cache_resource` — Uses `type_util.get_func_parameters()`
- `st.help()` — Added `NameError` handling in `_get_signature()`
- `@st.fragment` — Added `NameError` to `contextlib.suppress()`
- Internal metrics decorator — Uses `_get_arg_keywords()` and added `NameError` to `contextlib.suppress()`

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #14324

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python)
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_utils_test.py` — Tests `get_func_parameters()` via `_get_positional_arg_name()` with PEP 649 simulation
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/help_test.py` — Tests `_get_signature()` handling of PEP 649 annotations
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/fragment_test.py` — Tests fragment decorator handling of PEP 649 annotations
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/metrics_util_test.py` — Tests `_get_arg_keywords()` and `gather_metrics()` decorator handling

---

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a Python 3.14 (PEP 649) compatibility issue where `@st.cache_data` / `@st.cache_resource` could crash with `NameError` when introspecting decorated functions that use annotations referencing `TYPE_CHECKING`-only imports.

**Changes:**
- Added `_get_func_parameters()` to retrieve signature parameters without forcing annotation evaluation on Python 3.14+.
- Updated `_get_positional_arg_name()` to use `_get_func_parameters()` instead of calling `inspect.signature()` directly.
- Added unit tests for `_get_positional_arg_name()` behavior and for the PEP 649 regression scenario.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_utils.py` | Avoids PEP 649-triggered annotation evaluation during cache key construction by using `annotation_format=Format.STRING` on Python 3.14+. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_utils_test.py` | Adds coverage for positional-arg name resolution and a Python 3.14-specific regression test for deferred annotations. |

commits.message:
Fix cache decorators for Python 3.14 PEP 649 annotations

On Python 3.14+, PEP 649 causes annotation evaluation to be deferred.
When annotations reference types imported under TYPE_CHECKING,
inspect.signature() raises NameError. This fix uses annotation_format=STRING
to avoid evaluation since we only need parameter names and kinds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct PEP 649 test to properly simulate deferred annotations

The previous test used exec() which doesn't trigger PEP 649 deferred
annotation behavior. Instead, create a function with a custom
__annotate__ method that raises NameError when annotations are evaluated,
which properly simulates the real PEP 649 behavior for undefined types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address PR review comments

- Update docstring to reference Format.STRING enum value (copilot suggestion)
- Use Callable[..., Any] type annotation for func parameter instead of object,
  removing the need for type: ignore comment (copilot suggestion)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PEP 649 annotation handling in st.help, st.fragment, and metrics

On Python 3.14+, inspect.signature() can raise NameError for functions
with TYPE_CHECKING-only annotations due to PEP 649 deferred evaluation.

- st.help: Add NameError handling alongside ValueError
- st.fragment: Add NameError to contextlib.suppress
- metrics_util: Add NameError to contextlib.suppress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Consolidate PEP 649 handling into type_util and add tests

- Move get_func_parameters to type_util.py to avoid duplication
- Add _get_arg_keywords in metrics_util.py for PEP 649-safe arg parsing
- Add comprehensive tests for st.help, st.fragment, and metrics_util

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle TypeError from inspect.getfullargspec on Python 3.14

On Python 3.14, inspect.getfullargspec() catches NameError from
annotation evaluation and re-raises as TypeError("unsupported callable").
Update the test to accept both exception types.

Also consolidates the _get_func_parameters wrapper into type_util.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: include POSITIONAL_ONLY params in _get_arg_keywords and improve docs

Address PR review recommendations:
- Include POSITIONAL_ONLY parameters in _get_arg_keywords() to fully match
  getfullargspec().args behavior (fixes potential telemetry arg mapping bug)
- Update get_func_parameters() docstring to note that annotations are strings
  on Python 3.14+
- Move get_func_parameters PEP 649 test to type_util_test.py for better
  organization
- Add test for positional-only parameter handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: address PR review comments

- Move type_util import to top-level in metrics_util.py
- Fix docstring to use `annotation_format=Format.STRING`
- Create shared `create_pep649_function()` helper in testutil.py
- Refactor all PEP 649 tests to use the shared helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [feature] Add support for relative static file serving URLs in media elements
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds support for relative static URLs (`/app/static/`) in media elements, allowing users to reference files from Streamlit's static file serving endpoint using relative paths instead of requiring absolute URLs or local file paths.

- Adds `is_relative_static_url()` helper to `url_util.py` for detecting `/app/static/` paths
- Updates `image_to_url()` to pass through relative static URLs unchanged
- Updates `marshall_audio()` and `marshall_video()` to recognize relative static URLs
- Updates frontend `buildMediaURL()` to construct proper URLs for `/app/static/` paths

Supported commands: `st.image`, `st.audio`, `st.video`, `st.chat_message(avatar)`, `st.set_page_config(page_icon)`, `st.logo`

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #12104

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python and TypeScript)
- [x] E2E Tests (Playwright)

### Test files:
- `lib/tests/streamlit/url_util_test.py` — Tests `is_relative_static_url()` with valid/invalid inputs
- `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/image_test.py` — Tests `image_to_url()` with static URLs
- `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/media_test.py` — Tests `marshall_audio/video` with static URLs
- `frontend/connection/src/DefaultStreamlitEndpoints.test.ts` — Tests `buildMediaURL()` with static paths
- `e2e_playwright/config_static_serving_test.py` — E2E tests for all supported media elements

title: Add Streamlit API Design Principles documentation
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR adds comprehensive API design principles to guide the evolution of Streamlit's public API. The new `specs/AGENTS.md` document captures 40 principles covering simplicity, consistency, type safety, composability, and maintainability. These principles help maintain API coherence and provide guidance for future feature development and API reviews.

## Testing Plan

- Explanation of why no additional tests are needed: This is a documentation-only addition containing design principles and best practices, not code that requires testing. The principles are guidelines for future development and code review rather than functionality to validate.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new documentation guide under `specs/` that captures API design principles intended to guide future Streamlit public API evolution and reviews.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `specs/AGENTS.md` describing the specs process and a 40-point Streamlit API design principles list.
- Provides illustrative examples for API consistency, typing, composability, and evolution/deprecation practices.
Sounds great, thx for the work. Left a few small comments but overall good to merge and can iterate over time.

commits.message:
Add Streamlit API Design Principles (specs/AGENTS.md)

Document 40 principles guiding Streamlit's public API design, covering simplicity, consistency, type safety, and composability. These principles help maintain API coherence and guide future feature development.

Add specs/CLAUDE.md to reference API design principles

This allows agents working in the specs directory to automatically pick up the API design principles from specs/AGENTS.md.
Remove 'value (not default)' from vocabulary list
Update example: query_key='foo' -> bind='query-params'

Remove 3 principles and renumber remaining ones

Removed:
- Stateful by Default
- Visual Features Need Visual Defaults
- Protect LLM-Generated Code

Renumbered remaining principles 1-37.

Reorganize API principles with thematic sections and add Deterministic Output

- Group 38 principles into 9 thematic sections (Core Philosophy, Naming & Consistency,
  Type System, API Structure, Documentation & Errors, Evolution & Compatibility,
  Python Integration, Runtime Behavior, Platform & Architecture)
- Add new principle #33: Deterministic Output - same code and state should produce
  identical UI across reruns
- Renumber principles sequentially within their sections
Remove section headers, keep flat numbered list
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/specs-agents-md

title: feat: include pr-level comments in addressing-pr-review-comments skill
body:
## Describe your changes

Enhanced the addressing-pr-review-comments skill to handle both inline review comments and general PR (issue) comments. Previously, the skill only processed inline review comments attached to specific files and lines. Now it also considers general PR-level comments that don't have file/line associations.

Key improvements:
- Added fetching and processing of general PR comments from the `issues/{PR}/comments` endpoint
- Updated analysis logic to handle comments without file/line context by inferring scope from comment content
- Enhanced the presentation format to distinguish between inline and general comments
- Added logic to skip status-only automated comments (like Snyk checks or build notifications) while including substantive bot feedback
- Updated the action application process to handle general comments by inferring target files from comment text
- Clarified that PR author comments should be included as they can provide useful context

Since this section is instructing how to fetch *all* actionable feedback, add pagination (cursor-based) or an explicit note/mitigation (e.g., loop until no `pageInfo.hasNextPage`, or fall back to REST comments list when thread resolution state isn't critical).
```

```bash
gh api graphql -f query="
{
```

title: [feature] Add `st.file_uploader` support to AppTest
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds `st.file_uploader` support to the AppTest testing framework, addressing a long-standing feature request.

- Add `FileUploader` widget class to `element_tree.py` with `set_value()`, `upload()`, and `clear()` methods
- Add file registration logic to `app_test.py` that registers uploaded files with `MemoryUploadedFileManager` before script runs
- Add comprehensive unit tests covering single/multiple file uploads, incremental uploads, and clearing

**Example usage:**
```python
def test_file_upload():
    at = AppTest.from_file("app.py")
    at.run()
    
    # Upload a file
    at.file_uploader[0].set_value(("data.csv", b"col1,col2\n1,2", "text/csv"))
    at.run()
    
    # Or upload multiple files
    at.file_uploader[0].set_value([
        ("file1.txt", b"content1", "text/plain"),
        ("file2.txt", b"content2", "text/plain"),
    ])
    at.run()
```

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes #8093

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python)
  - Test single file upload
  - Test multiple file upload  
  - Test incremental upload via `upload()` method
  - Test clearing files via `clear()` method
  - Test key-based widget access

title: fix(testing): initialize BidiComponentManager in AppTest mock runtime
body:
## Summary

`AppTest._run()` creates a `MagicMock(spec=Runtime)` but does not configure `bidi_component_registry`, so any app that uses `st.components.v2` custom components crashes with:

```
TypeError: bad argument type for built-in operation
```

The crash occurs because `get_bidi_component_manager()` returns a MagicMock attribute instead of a real `BidiComponentManager`. Component registration silently no-ops on the mock, and later, when `_bidi_component()` tries to set protobuf string fields from MagicMock objects, the built-in protobuf C code raises the TypeError.

## Fix

Initialize a real `BidiComponentManager` instance on the mock runtime, alongside the existing `MediaFileManager` and `MemoryCacheStorageManager`:

```python
mock_runtime.bidi_component_registry = BidiComponentManager()
```

This ensures component registration and lookup work correctly during `AppTest` runs.

## Test

Added `test_v2_custom_component` that registers and mounts a v2 custom component inside `AppTest.from_function()`, verifying no exception is raised.

Fixes #14274
author: tysoncung

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a crash in `streamlit.testing.v1.AppTest` when running apps that use `st.components.v2` custom components by ensuring the mock `Runtime` includes a real v2 component manager.

**Changes:**
- Initialize `mock_runtime.bidi_component_registry` with a real `BidiComponentManager` during `AppTest._run()`.
- Add a regression test that registers and mounts a v2 custom component via `AppTest.from_function()` (issue #14274).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/testing/v1/app_test.py | Seeds the AppTest mock `Runtime` with a real `BidiComponentManager` so v2 component registration/lookup works correctly. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/testing/app_test_test.py | Adds a regression test ensuring v2 custom components can be registered and mounted in `AppTest` without exceptions. |
LGTM 👍  Thanks for the contribution

commits.message:
fix(testing): initialize BidiComponentManager in AppTest mock runtime

AppTest._run() creates a MagicMock runtime but does not set up
bidi_component_registry, causing apps that use st.components.v2
custom components to crash with TypeError during testing.

Initialize a real BidiComponentManager on the mock runtime so that
component registration and lookup work correctly in AppTest.

Fixes #14274

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds **oxlint** as a fast frontend linter to run ahead of eslint in local workflows (make check + pre-commit), plus applies a set of mechanical autofixes across the frontend codebase to satisfy the new linter.

**Changes:**
- Add `oxlint` dependency + scripts and introduce `frontend/.oxlintrc.json`.
- Run oxlint before eslint in `make check`, and add an oxlint frontend pre-commit hook (skipped in `make check` since it runs earlier).
- Apply linter-driven code cleanups (object spread simplifications, safer null checks, test fixes).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/yarn.lock | Adds oxlint package + platform bindings to the lockfile. |
| frontend/package.json | Adds oxlint scripts and runs oxlint before eslint in `lint` / `lint:fix`. |
| frontend/.oxlintrc.json | Introduces oxlint config with vendor exclusions. |
| Makefile | Runs oxlint (with fixes) before eslint during `make check` for changed frontend files; skips oxlint pre-commit hook in that flow. |
| .pre-commit-config.yaml | Adds `oxlint-frontend` hook; expands prettier hook scope to additional frontend packages. |
| frontend/AGENTS.md | Updates dev guide to document oxlint + eslint. |
| .github/instructions/typescript.instructions.md | Updates generated TypeScript instructions to mention oxlint + eslint. |
| .cursor/rules/typescript.mdc | Updates generated Cursor TypeScript rules to mention oxlint + eslint. |
| frontend/lib/src/dataframes/arrowConcatUtils.ts | Adds explicit error handling for missing range-index metadata. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DateInput/DateInput.test.tsx | Fixes mocked `Date` subclass to call `super()`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/columnConfigUtils.ts | Simplifies object spread patterns. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/

commits.message:
chore: add oxlint as fast frontend linter

Adds oxlint to complement eslint in the frontend tooling pipeline.
Runs before eslint in make check and pre-commit hooks (~20ms vs ~45s).

Applied auto-fixes to existing code:
- Simplified spread patterns (|| {} fallbacks)
- Added missing super() call in test
- Improved null checks in arrowConcatUtils

Cleaned up TypeScript ESLint warnings by removing unused variables in catch blocks and function parameters. Updated the ESLint configuration to allow variables prefixed with underscore to be unused, then replaced unused variables with either no variable binding (for catch blocks) or underscore-prefixed parameter names (for function parameters).

This PR cleans up the frontend codebase by removing `eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` suppressions and addressing the underlying unused bindings in a consistent way (optional catch bindings and underscore-prefixed unused identifiers), plus a small ESLint config tweak to support that pattern.

**Changes:**

title: Fix stale anchor preserved when clearing transient nodes
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes a regression introduced in #13849 where stale elements from previous script runs could persist after spinner completion.

**Root cause:** When a transient node (spinner) captured an element from a previous run as its anchor, and the transient was cleared, the anchor was restored directly without checking if it was stale.

**The fix:** Change `return node.anchor` to `return node.anchor.accept(this)` so the anchor is also checked for staleness before being restored.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes #14249

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit tests: Added test case for stale anchor scenario
- [x] Manual testing: Verified fix using reproduction steps from issue

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a frontend render-tree regression where clearing a transient node (e.g., spinner) could restore an anchor element from a previous script run without re-checking whether that anchor is stale, causing unexpected “old” UI content to reappear.

**Changes:**
- Update `ClearStaleNodeVisitor.visitTransientNode` to restore a cleared transient’s anchor via `anchor.accept(this)` so the anchor is also filtered for staleness.
- Adjust and extend unit tests to cover the “cleared transient with stale anchor” scenario.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/visitors/ClearStaleNodeVisitor.ts | Ensures restored transient anchors are also visited/filtered for staleness before being returned. |
| frontend/lib/src/render-tree/visitors/ClearStaleNodeVisitor.test.ts | Updates the existing “restore anchor” test and adds coverage for stale-anchor restoration returning `undefined`. |
The change feels reasonable. Perhaps we can confirm with the user. If there's anyway the AI could have caught this better, that would be great.

commits.message:
Fix stale anchor preserved when clearing transient nodes

When a transient node (spinner) captured an element from a previous run
as its anchor, and the transient was cleared, the anchor was restored
directly without checking if it was stale. This caused UI elements from
previous runs to incorrectly persist after spinner completion.

The fix checks if the anchor is stale before restoring it by calling
`node.anchor.accept(this)` instead of returning `node.anchor` directly.

Closes #14249

Add test for fragment-run scenario in ClearStaleNodeVisitor

Add unit test to verify that a stale anchor without fragmentId is
preserved during a fragment run when the transient node is cleared.
This addresses a review comment requesting explicit coverage for the
fragment-run edge case to prevent regressions.

title: [spec] Tech spec: layout container state persistence across remounts
body:
## Describe your changes

Spec viewer: https://issues.streamlit.app/spec_renderer?pr=14182

Tech spec for persisting active state of st.tabs, st.expander, and st.popover across  component remounts. Currently these elements reset to their default state on every remount; this spec proposes a frontend-only solution that preserves user state without registering these elements as widgets.

Key design decisions documented:

- Backend generates a stable Block.id per element (counter-based for unkeyed, $$ID-<hash>-<key> for keyed) to serve as a persistent identity across reruns
- Frontend stores active state in the existing WidgetStateManager.elementStates map, keyed by Block.id
- Element-level IDs (tabContainer.id, expandable.id, popover.id) are intentionally not used as store keys — they signal widget status and would trigger reruns
- Cleanup by extending the existing ElementsSetVisitor to also collect Block.ids in a single traversal; a new AppRoot.getActiveIds() method exposes both, so Block.ids are passed to removeInactive alongside active widget IDs
- Keyed elements receive st-key-<keyname> CSS class as a side effect

Related: https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8239

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

N/A

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

Explanation of why no additional tests are needed: spec-only PR, no code changes

Contribution License Agreement
By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a technical spec describing a frontend-driven approach to persist the active/open state of layout containers (`st.tabs`, `st.expander`, `st.popover`) across reruns and React remounts, using backend-provided stable `Block.id` values as the identity key.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a new tech spec outlining backend `Block.id` generation (keyed vs counter-based unkeyed).
- Proposes using `WidgetStateManager` element state (keyed by `Block.id`) to restore UI state without widget registration.
- Describes cleanup mechanics (`removeInactive`) and CSS `st-key-*` side effects for keyed containers.
Great spec, thank you for the write-up! I have a couple of questions inline.

I have a rather strong opinion on not introducing a new type of ID computation with this project since every element we have shares the same frontend identity semantics. 

My suggestion would be to reuse what's available, focus on stabilizing the identity for non-rerun tabs/expander/popover only when `key` is provided, which never runs into `StreamlitDuplicateElementId`.

And let's have a dedicated exploration on how to minimize `StreamlitDuplicateElementId` across all elements in a reliable way ([see related notion project](https://www.notion.so/snowflake-corp/Minimize-StreamlitDuplicateElementId-errors-13a42d4fcfc345369c6d44c0d6b2ba53)), which might allow us to stabilize the tabs/expander/popover identity even if no `key` is provided as a follow-up (if we can ensure we are not breaking existing uses).

The other aspects in this spec look good to me 👍
LGTM 👍  But the draft tech spec should be moved to a dedicated PR. And a question on the default handling.

Made-with: Cursor

Consolidate user requests to #8239 only

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Refine layout container state persistence tech spec

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Update specs/2026-02-26-layout-container-state-persistence/tech-spec.md

Co-authored-by: graphite-app[bot] <96075541+graphite-app[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Incorporate label params into Block.id and fix root cause framing

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Refine behavior tables and remove redundant checklist entry

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Refocus spec on key-based persistence and clarify Block.id vs element-level ID

Split into two specs: this one covers key-based persistence only (on_change="ignore" + key),
and a separate draft spec covers the non-key identity investigation.

Key changes:
- Problem statement clarified: resets happen on remount due to delta path shifts from
  conditional elements, not on every rerun.
- Pseudocode uses compute_and_register_element_id instead of manual hash.
- Block.id is always set when key is provided (both passive and stateful modes); the
  element-level ID (e.g. tabContainer.id) is what distinguishes a widget.
- Behavior tables scoped to keyed elements only.
- Removed counter-based unkeyed identity (deferred to follow-up investigation).

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Remove non-key container identity spec from this PR

The non-key identity investigation will be shared in a separate PR.

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

Drop lastDefault tracking — stored state always wins for keyed containers

Align with keyed widget behavior: when key= is provided, the default/expanded
parameter is only the initial seed. Stored elementStates always takes precedence,
consistent with how st.radio, st.number_input, st.select_slider etc. handle
default changes with key_as_main_identity=True.

Without key=, behavior is unchanged — the proto value is used directly on every
render, so changing the parameter still takes effect immediately.

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: lawilby <laura.wilby+oss@snowflake.com>

body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.20 to 0.0.21.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.21

Release Notes

Released on 2026-03-05.

Bug fixes

  • Avoid stack overflow with recursive typevar (#23652)
  • Fix panic on incomplete except handlers (#23708)
  • Allow unions of different-length iterables in *args unpacking into optional positional parameters (#23124)
  • Don't replace Any attributes with object after isinstance narrowing (#23725)

LSP server

  • Exclude decorators from class/def code folding (#23543)
  • Fix handling of non-Python text documents (#23704)

Configuration

  • Add all selector to ty.json schema (#23721)
  • Fix precedence of all selector in TOML configurations (#23723)
  • Make all selector case-sensitive (#23713)

Type checking

  • Add invalid-enum-member-annotation lint rule (#23648)
  • Add a diagnostic for an unused awaitable (#23650)
  • Add a diagnostic if a TypeVar is used to specialize a ParamSpec, or vice versa (#23738)
  • Add more type-variable default validation (#23639)
  • Add unbound type variable detection in annotations (#23641)
  • Apply narrowing to walrus values (#23687)
  • Do not union Unknown into unannotated container types (#23718)
  • Avoid inferring generics with negative intersections (#23750)
  • More precise types for name and value of an enum (#23683)
  • Better preserve user-provided union order in inferred specializations (#23715)
  • Support narrowing in ternary expressions (#23726)
  • Validate bare ParamSpec usage in type annotations, and support stringified ParamSpecs as the first argument to Callable (#23625)

  • Add a diagnostic if a TypeVar is used to specialize a ParamSpec, or vice versa (#23738)
  • Add more type-variable default validation (#23639)
  • Add unbound type variable detection in annotations (#23641)
  • Apply narrowing to walrus values (#23687)
  • Do not union Unknown into unannotated container types (#23718)
  • Avoid inferring generics with negative intersections (#23750)
  • More precise types for name and value of an enum (#23683)
  • Better preserve user-provided union order in inferred specializations (#23715)
  • Support narrowing in ternary expressions (#23726)
  • Validate bare ParamSpec usage in type annotations, and support stringified ParamSpecs as the first argument to Callable (#23625)

## Pull request overview

This PR is a broad cleanup/refactor to address unused exports/files reported by `knip` across the Streamlit frontend monorepo (shared `@streamlit/lib`, `@streamlit/app`, and `@streamlit/connection`). The changes primarily reduce public surface area by removing unused barrel exports, deleting unused mocks/vendor code, and converting exported-only-for-internals types/constants to module-private declarations.

**Changes:**
- Remove unused exports (types, constants, re-exports) and switch several `export interface/type/const` to module-private `interface/type/const`.
- Remove default exports in a few modules in favor of named exports (e.g., `useScrollToBottom`, `establishStaticConnection`).
- Delete unused vendor helper and large Arrow mock data files, and update mock export index accordingly.

title: chore: add knip to curtail frontend bloat
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR adds `knip` support for the frontend workspace to start surfacing unused files/exports and dead code paths.

- Adds a new `frontend/knip.json` config with:
  - rules to turn off dependency-related checks (`devDependencies`, `dependencies`, `unlisted`) because they were giving false positives.
  - a single workspace definition for the frontend root (`"."`)
- Adds a `knip` script to `frontend/package.json` (`"knip": "knip"`).
- Adds required dev dependencies in `frontend/package.json`

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

N/A (tooling/config-only change).

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- Explanation of why no additional tests are needed  
  This change is limited to frontend tooling/configuration and lockfile updates. It does not modify runtime product behavior.
- Any manual testing needed?  
  - Ran `cd frontend && yarn knip` to validate wiring of the new command and config.  
  - Result: command executes successfully and reports existing unused files/exports in the repo (expected for initial adoption), exiting non-zero when findings are present.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces [knip](https://knip.dev/) — a static analysis tool for detecting unused files, exports, and dependencies — to the frontend monorepo. The goal is to curtail frontend bloat over time.

**Changes:**
- New `frontend/knip.json` configuration file that sets up knip with dependency-checking rules disabled and only the root workspace configured
- `frontend/package.json` updated with the `knip` script, and new devDependencies: `knip@^5.86.0`, `typescript@^5.9.3`, and `@types/node@^25.4.0`
- `frontend/yarn.lock` updated with knip and all its transitive dependencies, plus bumped packages (`yaml`, `zod`, `@emnapi/*`)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/knip.json` | New configuration file defining knip rules and workspace scope |
| `frontend/package.json` | Adds `knip` script and new devDependencies (`knip`, `typescript`, `@types/node`) |
| `frontend/yarn.lock` | Adds knip and transitive deps; bumps `yaml`, `zod`, `@emnapi/*` to satisfy new ranges |

title: refactor: remove all barrel files
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR removes frontend barrel files (`index.ts` / `index.tsx` files that only re-export sibling modules) and updates imports to reference source modules directly.

- Adds a new frontend AGENTS rule to indicate no barrel files except designated package entry points.
- Deletes barrel files across `frontend/app` and `frontend/lib`.
- Rewrites imports throughout the codebase to direct module paths (for example, `.../Component/Component` or concrete utility files).
- Updates package-level/theme/type exports to import from concrete files instead of internal barrel paths.
- Keeps behavior unchanged. This is a structural refactor to reduce indirection and enforce consistent import style.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

- This is a refactor of module boundaries/import paths only, with no intended runtime logic changes.

---

Bumps [ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) from 0.15.4 to 0.15.5.
Release notes

Sourced from ruff's releases.

0.15.5

Release Notes

Released on 2026-03-05.

Preview features

  • Discover Markdown files by default in preview mode (#23434)
  • [perflint] Extend PERF102 to comprehensions and generators (#23473)
  • [refurb] Fix FURB101 and FURB103 false positives when I/O variable is used later (#23542)
  • [ruff] Add fix for none-not-at-end-of-union (RUF036) (#22829)
  • [ruff] Fix false positive for re.split with empty string pattern (RUF055) (#23634)

  • [pyflakes] Fix false positive for names shadowing re-exports (F811) (#23356)
  • [pyupgrade] Avoid inserting redundant None elements in UP045 (#23459)

title: [feature] Add `height` parameter to `st.chat_input`
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a `height` parameter to `st.chat_input()` that allows controlling the minimum height of the chat input widget.

- `None` or `"content"` (default): Uses default single-line height with auto-expand
- `"stretch"`: Fills available height of parent container
- Integer (pixels): Fixed minimum height with auto-expand behavior

## Github Issues

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10724

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python) - height config validation and protobuf field tests
- [x] Unit Tests (Frontend) - heightConfig rendering tests for stretch/content modes

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | addressing-pr-review-comments | 2 |
| skill | checking-changes | 2 |
| skill | debugging-streamlit | 2 |
| skill | finalizing-pr | 1 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 5 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 2 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 2 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new `height` parameter to `st.chat_input()` to control the widget’s minimum height via layout height config, with backend validation and frontend rendering support.

**Changes:**
- Backend: Add `height` param to `st.chat_input`, validate it, and propagate it via `LayoutConfig`.
- Backend tests: Add unit tests for `height_config` protobuf behavior and invalid height values.
- Frontend: Apply `heightConfig` to ChatInput layout/styling and add corresponding widget tests; adjust element container behavior for stretch height.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/chat_test.py` | Adds unit tests for default/explicit `height_config` and invalid `height` values. |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/chat.py` | Introduces `height` parameter, docstring docs, validation, and layout propagation. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatInput/ChatInput.tsx` | Implements height behavior (stretch/pixel/content) in ChatInput rendering. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatInput/ChatInput.test.tsx` | Adds frontend tests for stretch/content/undefined `heightConfig`. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/ElementNodeRenderer.tsx` | Adjusts container config for chat input when stretch height is enabled. |
</details>
This looks good to me, I am just wondering if some of the overrides are still more than necessary and if we might share some of the code with `TextArea`

commits.message:
[feat] Add height parameter to st.chat_input

Add a new `height` parameter to `st.chat_input` that allows controlling
the minimum height of the chat input widget:

- `None` or `"content"` (default): Uses default single-line height and
  auto-expands based on text content
- `"stretch"`: Height stretches to fill available parent container space
- Integer (pixels): Fixed minimum height that still auto-expands

This is useful for chat interfaces where the input should take up more
vertical space or fill a container.
Consolidate height config tests using parameterized

Address code review feedback

- Add frontend unit tests for heightConfig (stretch, content, undefined)
- Add clarifying constant for border calculation in ChatInput.tsx
- Enhance docstring to note that stretch requires parent container height

Address Copilot review feedback

- Add static STRETCH_HEIGHT config to ElementContainerConfig for referential stability
- Move inline styles to styled-components (isStretchHeight prop)
- Add height parameter typing tests to chat_input_types.py

Address AI review feedback for chat_input height parameter

- Fix pixel-height container behavior: Added AUTO_HEIGHT config to
  ElementContainerConfig so pixel height acts as minimum height with
  auto-expand, matching documented API behavior
- Fix isStacked + isStretchHeight flex conflict: Guard flex: 1 override
  with !isStacked to preserve stacked layout wrapping behavior
- Use theme border width: Replace hardcoded CONTAINER_BORDER with
  theme.sizes.borderWidth for maintainability
- Refactor height calculation: Convert getTextareaMinHeight to useMemo
  for better React patterns
- Consolidate tests: Use it.each for non-stretch height mode tests

Fix AUTO_HEIGHT config and address review feedback

- Fix AUTO_HEIGHT to also neutralize flex and overflow, following the
  textArea pattern for correct pixel-height auto-expand behavior
- Rename innerHeight to adjustedHeight to avoid variable shadowing with
  innerHeight from useWindowDimensionsContext()
- Strengthen pixel height test with better assertions and documentation

Remove chat_input-specific static configs from ElementContainerConfig

Address PR review feedback from @sfc-gh-lwilby:
- Remove STRETCH_HEIGHT and AUTO_HEIGHT static configs since they're
  too specific to be general element configs
- Create inline configs in chatInput case following the textArea pattern
- Fix flex reset to only apply in vertical layouts (flex-basis affects
  width in horizontal layouts, not height)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify ChatInput styled-components

- Remove redundant isStretchHeight logic from StyledTextareaWrapper
  (flex is already 1 when !isStacked)
- Remove unused isStretchHeight prop from ChatInput.tsx
- Remove redundant toBeInTheDocument() assertion in test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add minStretchWidth to chat_input for horizontal layout consistency

Match TextArea's behavior by adding minStretchWidth: MinStretchWidth.MEDIUM
to the chat_input container config. This ensures consistent minimum width
behavior when chat_input is placed in horizontal layouts (e.g., st.columns).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add E2E tests for chat_input height parameter

Add visual snapshot tests for the new height parameter:
- height=200 (pixel height)
- height="stretch" (fills parent container)

The stretch test uses a container with defined height to verify
the chat input properly fills the available space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: align chat_input submit button to bottom when height is configured

When height is set to stretch or a pixel value, the submit button should
stick to the bottom of the input rather than being vertically centered.

Introduces `hasExpandedHeight` which is true for both stretch and pixel
heights, and uses `alignItems: flex-end` on the input row to keep buttons
at the bottom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix submit button alignment for dynamically expanded chat input

Include autoExpand.isExtended in hasExpandedHeight calculation so the
submit button sticks to the bottom when the textarea expands due to
user-added newlines, not just when height is configured via props.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Separate toolbar row from text area in expanded chat input

When the chat input is expanded (multiline or configured height), use a
column layout that separates the textarea from the toolbar. This creates
a dedicated bottom row for buttons (file upload, mic, submit) that text
doesn't flow into.

- Add StyledToolbarRow component for the dedicated button row
- Change StyledInputRow to column layout when hasExpandedHeight is true
- Restructure JSX to conditionally wrap buttons in toolbar row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix textarea overflow in stretch/pixel height mode

When height is configured via props (stretch or pixel), disable the
autoExpand height constraints and use 100% height with no maxHeight.
This allows the textarea to fill the container and scroll properly
instead of overflowing.

- Add useFixedHeight parameter to createTextAreaOverrides
- Add hasConfiguredHeight to distinguish prop-based from dynamic height
- When hasConfiguredHeight is true, use height: 100% and maxHeight: none

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix + button position in single-line chat input mode

In non-expanded (single-line) mode, use CSS order: -1 on StyledLeftCluster
to position the file upload button before the textarea, which is now
rendered first in the DOM for the expanded mode layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move character counter to be inline with buttons in chat input

Moves the character count indicator (e.g. "4/200") from absolutely positioned
in the top-right corner to inline within StyledRightCluster. This prevents
the counter from overlapping with the submit button and places it next to
the audio/submit buttons in both normal and expanded height modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update E2E snapshots for chat input height feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: simplify chat_input height parameter API

Remove None as a valid value for the height parameter since it was
equivalent to "content". This makes the API clearer and more explicit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update test to match new height default behavior

The test was expecting no height_config field when using the default,
but with the API change (height="content" as explicit default), it
should now expect use_content: true.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [chore] Fix flaky spinner snapshot test on Firefox
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes flaky `test_spinner_width_300px_snapshot[firefox]` test that intermittently fails with "Element is not attached to the DOM" during screenshot.

- Increases spinner sleep duration from 2s to 5s for width-related spinner buttons
- Provides sufficient buffer for snapshot tests to complete before spinner disappears

**Root cause:** On slower browsers (Firefox, especially in CI), the time between the visibility check and screenshot capture could exceed the remaining sleep time, causing the spinner to detach.

## Testing Plan

- [x] E2E Tests
  - `e2e_playwright/st_spinner_test.py` — Verified 10/10 passes on Firefox

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 1 |
| skill | finalizing-pr | 1 |
| skill | fixing-flaky-e2e-tests | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a flaky E2E Playwright snapshot test (`test_spinner_width_300px_snapshot[firefox]`) that intermittently fails with "Element is not attached to the DOM" during screenshot capture. On slower browsers/CI environments, the time between Playwright's `expect(...).to_be_visible()` assertion and the actual `assert_snapshot()` screenshot call could exceed the remaining `time.sleep(2)` duration in the backend spinner script, causing the spinner to disappear before the screenshot was taken.

**Changes:**
- Increases `time.sleep` from 2s to 5s for the three width-related spinner buttons (`content`, `stretch`, and `300px`) to ensure the spinner remains visible long enough for snapshot capture on slower CI runners (e.g., Firefox)
- Adds an explanatory comment on each changed sleep line

commits.message:
Fix flaky spinner snapshot test on Firefox

Increase sleep duration from 2s to 5s for width-related spinner
buttons, providing sufficient buffer for snapshot tests to complete
before the spinner disappears from the DOM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
