title: [docs] AI-assisted documentation updates
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to update internal documentation based on AI review.

This PR was created by the `ai-update-docs` workflow using `opus-4.6-thinking`.

## Changes

# Documentation Review Summary

Reviewed all internal documentation files against the current codebase state.

## Scope

Files reviewed (32 total):
- `AGENTS.md` (root + 9 sub-directory files)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (11 skill files)
- `.claude/agents/*.md` (3 subagent files)
- `wiki/*.md` (5 files)
- `README.md` files (frontend, e2e_playwright, root)

## Verification performed

- All make commands verified against `make help` output
- All documented file/folder paths verified to exist
- Tool version numbers cross-checked against config files (`pyproject.toml`, `frontend/package.json`, `.nvmrc`)
- External URLs verified (Homebrew, nvm, uv install scripts)
- Skills and subagents table in `CONTRIBUTING.md` verified against actual `.claude/skills/` and `.claude/agents/` directories
- Workflow files in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` verified against actual `.yml` files
- Architecture skill file references verified

## Issues found and fixed: 2

### 1. [INCONSISTENT] `lib/tests/AGENTS.md`:13

- **Problem:** Recommended `@parameterized.expand` for parameterized tests, but the same file says "Prefer pytest or pytest plugins over unittest" (line 8). The `@parameterized.expand` pattern is from the `parameterized` package (unittest-style), inconsistent with the pytest-first principle and with the `simplifying-local-changes.md` agent which correctly uses `@pytest.mark.parametrize`.
- **Fix:** Changed recommendation to `@pytest.mark.parametrize` with a note that legacy tests may still use `@parameterized.expand`.

### 2. [OUTDATED] `frontend/README.md`

- **Problem:** Package list was missing `component-v2-lib` and `eslint-plugin-streamlit-custom`, both of which are listed as workspaces in `frontend/package.json` and documented in `frontend/AGENTS.md`.
- **Fix:** Added both packages to the README's package list in alphabetical order with brief descriptions.

## Files modified

```
lib/tests/AGENTS.md  | 2 +-
frontend/README.md   | 2 ++
```

## Items verified as correct (no changes needed)

- All make commands in AGENTS.md match `make help` output
- All folder structure paths exist as documented
- Python version range (3.10-3.14) matches `lib/pyproject.toml` classifiers
- Frontend versions (TypeScript v5, React v18, Vite v7, Vitest v4, ESLint v9, Prettier v3, Emotion v11, Yarn v4) all match `package.json`
- Node.js version (v24) matches `.nvmrc`
- Python tool versions (Ruff 0.x, mypy 1.x, pytest 9.x) match `pyproject.toml`
- All workflow files listed in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` exist
- All composite actions listed in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` exist
- Skills/subagents table in `CONTRIBUTING.md` matches actual files
- Architecture skill file paths and references all valid
- Proto file paths all valid
- External URLs reachable
- Wiki cross-references valid

title: [feature] Add `hide_index` and `hide_header` to `st.table`
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds `hide_index` and `hide_header` parameters to `st.table()` with intelligent auto-hide behavior:

- `hide_index`: Auto-hides default RangeIndex (0, 1, 2...) while showing custom indices
- `hide_header`: Auto-hides headers for data without user-defined column names (dict, list, numpy arrays)

Both parameters accept `True`/`False` to explicitly control visibility, or `None` (default) for auto-hide behavior.

## Github Issues

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9251
- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8235
- Related https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/13185

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/table_test.py` — Tests auto-hide logic, explicit values, Styler support
  - `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Table/Table.test.tsx` — Tests frontend rendering with hideIndex/hideHeader
- [x] E2E tests
  - `e2e_playwright/st_table_test.py` — Visual snapshot tests for all hide_index/hide_header scenarios
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds `hide_index` and `hide_header` parameters to `st.table()` with intelligent auto-hide behavior. When set to `None` (default), `hide_index` auto-hides default RangeIndex (0, 1, 2...) while showing custom indices, and `hide_header` auto-hides headers for data formats without user-defined column names (dict, list, numpy arrays). Both parameters accept explicit `True`/`False` values to override the auto-hide behavior.

**Changes:**
- Added two boolean protobuf fields to the Table message for hide_index and hide_header
- Implemented backend logic for auto-detection of RangeIndex and simple data formats
- Updated frontend rendering to conditionally hide index columns and header rows
- Added comprehensive unit tests (Python and TypeScript) and E2E visual snapshot tests

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/Table.proto` | Added `hide_index` and `hide_header` boolean fields (3 and 4) to Table message |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/table.py` | Added `_compute_hide_index` and `_compute_hide_header` helper functions, parameter handling, and auto-hide logic based on data format |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/table_test.py` | Added comprehensive unit tests for auto-hide logic, explicit values, Styler support, and combined scenarios |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Table/Table.tsx` | Implemented conditional rendering of index columns and headers, updated sticky positioning logic |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Table/Table.test.tsx` | Added unit tests verifying hideIndex and hideHeader rendering with positive and negative assertions |
| `e2e_playwright/st_table.py` | Added test app with 10 new table examples covering all hide_index/hide_header scenarios |
| `e2e_playwright/st_table_test.py` | Added 10 E2E tests with visua
LGTM

Address PR review recommendations for st.table hide_index/hide_header

- Add typing test for hide_index and hide_header parameter types
- Consolidate 10 E2E tests into 2 aggregated scenario tests to reduce browser loads
- Add .. output:: directives to docstring examples 4 and 5 for consistency

Simplify hide_index logic for unevaluated data objects

Unevaluated data objects (Snowpark, PySpark, generators, DB cursors, etc.)
always produce a default RangeIndex when converted to pandas DataFrame.
Instead of passing a pre-converted DataFrame to _compute_hide_index,
we now directly assume hide_index=True for these data types.

This removes the data_df parameter from _compute_hide_index and
simplifies the control flow in st.table.

Update lib/streamlit/elements/table.py

title: [feat] Add `st.menu_button` widget
body:
## Describe your changes

- Add new `st.menu_button` widget - a dropdown menu button that displays options when clicked and returns the selected value
- Implements trigger-based behavior (value resets to `None` after each script run, like `st.button`)
- Supports all button types (primary, secondary, tertiary), custom icons, `format_func`, width options, and help tooltips
- Extracts leading Material Icons from options (e.g., `:material/edit: Edit`) and renders them with optimized styling, similar to the navigation menu
- Includes comprehensive E2E tests, frontend unit tests, and Python typing tests
- Added ARIA accessibility attributes (`aria-haspopup`, `aria-expanded`, `role="menu"`, `role="menuitem"`)

## Github Issues

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/11409 

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and Python)
- [x] E2E Tests
- [x] `make check` passes locally

| skill | fixing-streamlit-ci | 1 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 4 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 1 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 2 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a new `st.menu_button` widget to Streamlit - a dropdown menu button that displays options when clicked and returns the selected value as a trigger-based widget (value resets to `None` after each script run, similar to `st.button`).

**Changes:**
- Adds protobuf support by re-purposing the deprecated `StringTriggerValue` message for trigger-based widgets that return string values
- Implements comprehensive Python backend with `MenuButtonMixin` class supporting all button types, icons, format_func, width options, and help tooltips
- Implements React frontend component with BaseUI Popover/StatefulMenu, proper ARIA attributes, and integration with WidgetStateManager
- Includes E2E tests with Playwright, frontend unit tests with Vitest, and Python typing tests

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/WidgetStates.proto | Un-deprecates StringTriggerValue and updates documentation for menu button use |
| proto/streamlit/proto/MenuButton.proto | New protobuf message definition for menu button widget |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Element.proto | Adds menu_button field to Element message |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Common.proto | Updates StringTriggerValue documentation |
| lib/tests/streamlit/typing/menu_button_types.py | Comprehensive typing tests for all menu_button parameters and return types |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state_test.py | Updates comment for json_trigger_value deserializer behavior |
| lib/tests/streamlit/element_mocks.py | Adds menu_button to widget elements list |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Adds string_trigger_value handling in session state deserialization and reset logic |
| lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/menu_button.py | Main implementation with MenuButtonSerde and MenuButtonMi

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
Some nits and would rather not add unrelated changes, but doesn't seem worth it to separate out the minor app test fix that made it in here.

commits.message:
[feat] Add st.menu_button widget

Add a new dropdown menu button widget that displays a button which,
when clicked, opens a dropdown menu with multiple options. Selecting
an option triggers a rerun and returns the selected value.

This is a trigger-based widget (like st.button) - the value resets to
None after each script run. Supports all button types (primary,
secondary, tertiary), custom icons, format_func, and width options.

Improve MenuButton component and tests

- Extract button type lookup map to module scope for better performance
- Explicitly pass event handlers to Option component (fixes test interaction)
- Add aria-hidden to expansion icon for screen reader accessibility
- Simplify test assertions using parameterized tests

Fix handle_deserialize to accept pre-parsed JSON values

Revert the json_trigger_value parsing changes since menu_button
uses string_trigger_value, not json_trigger_value. The json_trigger_value
parsing is only needed for bidi_component which has its own deserializer.

Add Python unit tests and address review feedback

- Add comprehensive Python unit tests for MenuButtonSerde
- Add proto marshalling tests for st.menu_button
- Simplify frontend tests using parameterized testing
- Remove redundant comments from E2E tests
- Add defensive element.disabled check in handleItemSelect

Simplify tests and improve code clarity

- Consolidate Python serde tests using pytest.mark.parametrize
- Simplify TypeScript test structure by combining related tests
- Improve comment clarity in styled-components and MenuButton component

Fix ChatInput testing to use chat_input_value proto field

The testing framework's ChatInput was incorrectly using string_trigger_value
to send widget state. This broke after adding special handling for
string_trigger_value in session_state.py. Update to use the correct
chat_input_value proto field that the chat_input widget expects.

Fix flaky MenuButton tests with waitFor

Use waitFor from testing-library to handle BaseUI Popover animation delays
when checking for menu close/open state changes.

Use findByText for async menu item queries

Use findByText instead of getByText to wait for menu items to appear
in the DOM before interacting with them. This makes the tests more
robust in slower CI environments.

Improve MenuButton tests and fix transient prop warnings

- Consolidate Python unit tests using @parameterized.expand
- Fix flaky frontend tests by using fireEvent for menu item clicks
- Filter out BaseUI internal props ($disabled, $isFocused, etc.) to
  prevent React warnings about unknown DOM attributes
- Use getByRole for more reliable menu item selection in tests

Fix MenuButtonSerde.serialize to always return valid proto

Changed serialize() to always return a StringTriggerValue proto object
instead of returning None for empty states. This matches the pattern
used by ChatInputSerde and prevents CopyFrom(None) crashes in
get_serialized().

Updated tests to verify the new behavior.

Add E2E test for menu button inside fragment

Verifies that menu_button works correctly when used inside st.fragment.
Update menu button E2E snapshots for firefox and webkit

Refactor menu button: use StreamlitValueError and styled component

- Replace StreamlitAPIException with StreamlitValueError for type validation
- Extract inline styles to StyledMenuItem styled component

Fix test_invalid_type assertion to match StreamlitValueError format

The test was asserting the old error message format from StreamlitAPIException,
but the implementation uses StreamlitValueError which has a different message.

[refactor] st.menu_button: reorder code and adjust menu styling

- Move buttonDisabled computation before handleItemSelect for clearer dependencies
- Adjust menu item spacing and padding for better visual appearance
- Use padding shorthand in styled-components

Add AppTest support for menu_button

Add exception handling for format_func in MenuButton._widget_state

Match the production code behavior in MenuButtonSerde.serialize() which
falls back to str() if format_func raises an exception.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ty type checker error for Exception in MenuButton

Use builtins.Exception explicitly to avoid confusion with the
Exception_pb2 proto import in TYPE_CHECKING block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add DynamicIcon component and improve MenuButton icon handling

- Create DynamicIcon component to dynamically load Material Symbol icons
- Add new styled component for loading spinner in menu button

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix E2E test to use exact=True for text matching in markdown options

The test_menu_button_markdown_options test was failing because get_by_text("Edit")
matched both the Material icon text "edit" and the option text "Edit", causing
a strict mode violation. Using exact=True ensures only the exact option text
is matched.

title: [chore] Add optional e2e test support to make check

Adds optional E2E test execution to `make check` for faster local validation of E2E-related changes:

- Add `E2E_CHECK=true` environment variable to run changed E2E tests in parallel with frontend checks

- Update skill docs and AGENTS.md to document the new option

- [x] Verified `E2E_CHECK=true make check` runs changed E2E tests in parallel

Adds an opt-in path to run Playwright E2E tests as part of `make check`, to speed up local validation when working on `e2e_playwright/` changes.

**Changes:**

- Update `make check` to optionally run changed E2E tests in parallel when `E2E_CHECK=true`.

Add overlay tests for audio and video columns

Verify that clicking audio/video cells opens an overlay containing
the appropriate HTML5 <audio> or <video> element with controls.
Remove product spec for audio/video columns

Consolidate AudioColumn/VideoColumn into unified MediaColumn

Refactor audio and video column implementations to reduce code
duplication (~40% less code). Both column types now share a single
MediaCell type with a mediaType discriminator and a BaseMediaColumn
factory function, following the established ChartColumn pattern.

Use rem units in MediaCell with convertRemToPx for canvas

consistency with the rest of the codebase. Use convertRemToPx
utility when pixel values are needed for canvas operations.

fix: add missing return type to createMockColumnProps in MediaColumn.test.ts

Fixes ESLint @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type warning

fix: use maxWidth instead of minWidth for audio player overlay

Prevents horizontal overflow on small viewports/narrow overlays by
allowing the audio player to shrink to fit available space.

This reverts the audio styling change - minWidth ensures the player
controls have enough space to be usable.
chore: add comment explaining minWidth for audio player

- Changed icons from music_video/hangout_video to audio_file/video_file

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/audio-video-columns

Update e2e tests to use local static assets instead of external URLs

Replace external URLs with relative static serving paths and data URIs
for audio, video, and image column tests to avoid network dependencies
in e2e tests.

Consolidate audio/video e2e tests and fix JSDoc comments

- Add `from __future__ import annotations` to e2e test app
- Consolidate test_audio_cell_overlay and test_video_cell_overlay into
  single test_media_cell_overlays function to reduce browser runs

fix(e2e): split media cell overlay tests to fix type and state issues

The test_media_cell_overlays test had two issues:
1. Type inference couldn't determine that dict["index"] was an int
2. State pollution between iterations caused the second media overlay
   to fail to open properly

Fixed by splitting into separate test_audio_cell_overlay and
test_video_cell_overlay tests with explicit type annotations.
Also updated the locator to look directly for media elements in the
portal instead of relying on .gdg-clip-region which varies by cell type.

fix(e2e): improve media cell overlay test stability

Add additional waits and longer timeouts to handle cross-browser
timing differences:
- Wait after scrolling to ensure canvas stability
- Increase wait_after_ms for click_on_cell
- Increase expect timeout from 5s to 10s

Address code review feedback for audio/video columns

- Add aria-label attributes to audio/video elements for accessibility
- Add negative assertions in E2E tests to verify opposite media type is not present
- Add MediaCellEditor unit tests using it.each pattern
- Fix off-by-one comment in E2E test (nth(32) is 32nd element)

Remove data URI from video column E2E test

The video data URI was several MB which is excessive for testing.
Using only local URLs is sufficient for coverage.

Use accessible RTL selectors in MediaCell tests

Replace `lodash-es` `uniqueId` with React's native `useId` hook in NumberInput, TextArea, and TextInput components. This change uses React's built-in solution for generating unique IDs.

author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Refactors widget ID generation in the frontend widget components by replacing `lodash-es`’s `uniqueId` with React 18’s native `useId`, keeping label-to-input associations intact while reducing reliance on an external utility.

**Changes:**
- Replace `uniqueId(...)`-based ID creation with `useId()` in TextInput, TextArea, and NumberInput.
- Remove `lodash-es` `uniqueId` imports where they’re no longer needed.
- Simplify TextArea’s prior `useRef(uniqueId(...)).current` pattern (and its associated lint suppression).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/TextInput/TextInput.tsx | Switches stable per-instance input/label ID generation from `uniqueId` to `useId`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/TextArea/TextArea.tsx | Replaces `useRef(uniqueId(...)).current` with `useId()`, removing the render-time ref access pattern. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/NumberInput/NumberInput.tsx | Updates NumberInput to use `useId()` for `htmlFor`/`id` wiring and drops the `uniqueId` import. |
LGTM 👍

refactor(ccv2): rename BidiComponentResult to ComponentResult

Promote `ComponentResult` as the canonical CCV2 result type across the
public API, internal type hints, and docstrings to align naming with the
rest of the v2 surface.

Keep `BidiComponentResult` as a backward-compatible alias to avoid breaking
existing imports and user code. Update tests and e2e typing annotations to
use `ComponentResult`, and add explicit compatibility coverage for the alias.

title: [docs] Add `implementing-feature` skill
body:
## Summary

Adds the `implementing-feature` skill for end-to-end feature implementation from specs to merge-ready PRs.

### New skill: `implementing-feature`

A guided workflow skill that takes a feature specification and implements it completely:

**Inputs supported:**
- Spec folder path (e.g., `specs/2025-12-12-menu-button`)
- Raw document URL
- GitHub issue URL (treats issue + comments as spec)

**Workflow phases:**
1. **Read spec** - Parse folder contents, fetch URL, or read GitHub issue via `gh`
2. **Implement** - Follow Streamlit patterns, add tests (unit + E2E)
3. **Verify** - Re-check spec requirements are met
4. **Finalize** - Run `/finalizing-pr` to create merge-ready PR

**Usage:**
```
/implementing-feature specs/2025-12-12-menu-button
/implementing-feature https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12345
```

### Supporting changes

- Moved `implementing-new-features` content to `wiki/new-feature-guide.md` for reference by the skill and broader discoverability
- Updated path references in AGENTS.md and architecture skill docs

## Test plan

- Documentation and skill definition only, no runtime behavior changes
- Verified `make check` passes

body:
## Describe your changes

Adds E2E tests for the `st.code` copy-to-clipboard functionality to verify expected behavior in response to GitHub Issue #12792, where users reported the copy button was not working properly.

**Changes Made:**

- Added `test_copy_to_clipboard_functionality`: Verifies single-line code copying works correctly
- Added `test_copy_to_clipboard_multiline_code`: Verifies multiline code blocks (with newlines and indentation) copy correctly
- Tests use Playwright's clipboard API to verify actual clipboard contents
- Tests verify copy button visibility on hover and state change after successful copy

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Related to GitHub Issue #12792

## Testing Plan

- [x] E2E Tests - Two new tests added to verify copy-to-clipboard behavior
- [x] Tests use clipboard permissions and navigator.clipboard.readText() to verify actual clipboard contents
- [x] Tests verify both single-line and multiline code preservation

**Additional Notes:**

These tests document the expected behavior of the copy-to-clipboard feature and can serve as regression tests to catch any future issues with this functionality.

---

## Pull Request Overview

This PR adds E2E tests to verify the copy-to-clipboard functionality for `st.code` elements, addressing reported issues in GitHub Issue #12792 where users experienced problems with the copy button.

**Key Changes:**

- Tests use Playwright's clipboard API with permission grants to verify actual clipboard contents

title: [fix] Add missing `contents:read` permission for nightly/release workflows
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds the missing `contents: read` permission to the `run-playwright-tests` job in nightly and release workflows.

The `check-e2e-test-count` job in `playwright.yml` requires `contents: read`, but the caller workflows only granted `actions: read` and `pull-requests: write`. GitHub validates reusable workflow permissions statically, so even though `check-e2e-test-count` only runs on PRs (not nightly/releases), the permission must be available.

## GitHub Issue(s)

Fixes the "Invalid workflow file" error in nightly.yml.

## Testing Plan

- [ ] CI validation (workflows will be validated by GitHub Actions)
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes an "Invalid workflow file" error in the nightly and release workflows by adding the missing `contents: read` permission to the `run-playwright-tests` job in both callers. GitHub Actions statically validates that a caller workflow grants every permission declared anywhere inside a reusable workflow — including jobs gated by conditions (like `if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'`) that will never run in the calling context. The `check-e2e-test-count` job inside `playwright.yml` declares `contents: read`, so both callers must also declare it.

**Changes:**
- Add `contents: read` permission to the `run-playwright-tests` job in `nightly.yml`
- Add `contents: read` permission to the `run-playwright-tests` job in `release.yml`

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.github/workflows/nightly.yml` | Adds `contents: read` to the `run-playwright-tests` job permissions block |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Adds `contents: read` to the `run-playwright-tests` job permissions block |

commits.message:
Add missing contents:read permission for playwright workflow calls

The check-e2e-test-count job in playwright.yml requires contents: read
permission. When reusable workflows are called via workflow_call, the
caller's permissions act as the maximum for all nested jobs. GitHub
validates this statically, even for jobs with conditional execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: [chore] Add `pragma: no cover` to defensive code paths
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds `# pragma: no cover` comments to exclude defensive code paths from test coverage metrics. This improves coverage accuracy without changing any runtime behavior.

- Excludes import fallbacks for optional dependencies (authlib, click, watchdog, pyarrow, sympy, matplotlib, streamlit_pdf)
- Excludes "should never happen" invariant checks in runtime, session management, and script execution
- Excludes platform-specific unreachable code (e.g., unsupported platform handling)
- Excludes backward compatibility paths (e.g., Pydantic v1 fallback)

## Testing Plan

- No additional tests needed - changes are comment-only metadata for coverage tools
- All existing unit tests pass (1655 passed)
- No functional code changes

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds `# pragma: no cover` markers across the Streamlit Python codebase to exclude “defensive” / invariant / optional-dependency paths from coverage reporting, aiming to keep coverage metrics focused on expected runtime behavior.

**Changes:**
- Annotates invariant checks (“should never happen”) in runtime/session/script execution paths with `# pragma: no cover`.
- Excludes optional-dependency import fallbacks (e.g., `watchdog`, `sympy`, `matplotlib`, `pyarrow`, `streamlit_pdf`, `authlib`) from coverage.
- Excludes a few platform-/compatibility-specific fallback branches from coverage reporting.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 27 out of 27 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/web/cli.py | Excludes defensive Click context invariants in `streamlit test` CLI path. |
| lib/streamlit/watcher/path_watcher.py | Excludes optional `watchdog` import fallback from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/type_util.py | Excludes optional `sympy` import fallback and Pydantic v1 fallback from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/time_util.py | Excludes “should never happen” date-invariant error raise from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/source_util.py | Excludes unreachable “non-Python file” invariant from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/websocket_session_manager.py | Excludes invariant checks around session id handling from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Excludes invariant validation in internal `_getitem` from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/scriptrunner_utils/script_requests.py | Excludes “unrecognized state” invariant from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/scriptrunner/script_runner.py | Excludes “unrecognized request type” invariants from coverage. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/runtime.py | Excludes invariant around mutually exclusive session id parameters from coverage. |
|
Fine with adding this, but also wondering if these obscure some areas where the code could be refactored to make testing easier.

commits.message:
Add pragma: no cover to defensive code paths

Exclude defensive code paths from test coverage metrics to improve
accuracy. Categories excluded:

- Import fallbacks for optional dependencies (authlib, click, watchdog,
  pyarrow, sympy, matplotlib, streamlit_pdf)
- "Should never happen" invariant checks in runtime, sessions, scripts
- Platform-specific unreachable code paths
- Backward compatibility paths (e.g., Pydantic v1 fallback)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pragma: no cover guidance to AGENTS.md

Document when to use coverage exclusion comments for code that
cannot reasonably be tested.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update pragma guidance to include brief reason

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add brief reasons to pragma: no cover comments

Categories:
- optional dep: Import fallbacks for optional dependencies
- defensive: "Should never happen" invariant checks
- unsupported platform: Platform-specific unreachable code
- pydantic v1 compat: Backward compatibility for Pydantic v1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Bump ty from 0.0.19 to 0.0.20
body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.19 to 0.0.20.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.20

Release Notes

Released on 2026-03-02.

Bug fixes

  • Disallow negative narrowing for isinstance() or issubclass() checks involving type[] types (#23598)
  • Fix binary operations between an instance of a NewType of float and an instance of Any/Unknown (#23620)
  • Fix bug where ty would think that a Callable with a variadic positional parameter could be a subtype of a Callable with a positional-or-keyword parameter (#23610)
  • Fix inference of t.__mro__ if t is an instance of type[Any] (#23632)
  • Fix overloaded callable assignability for unary Callable targets (#23277)
  • Limit recursion depth when displaying self-referential function types (#23647)
  • Ensure that python -m ty works even when ty was installed into an ephemeral virtual environment (#2852)

LSP server

  • Add support for the LSP protocol's "type hierarchy" feature (#23566)

Type checking

  • Add more ParamSpec validation for P.args and P.kwargs (#23640)
  • Ban nested Required/NotRequired, and ban them both outside of TypedDict fields (#23627)
  • Detect inconsistent generic base class specializations that appear in the same MRO (#23615)
  • Detect invalid uses of @final on non-methods (#23604)
  • Add partial support and validation for Unpack when used with tuple types (#23651)
  • Recurse into tuples and nested tuples when applying special-cased validation of arguments passed to isinstance() and issubclass() (#23607)
  • Reject ellipsis literals in odd places in type/annotation expressions (#23611)
  • Reject functions with PEP-695 type parameters that shadow type parameters from enclosing scopes (#23619)
  • Reject generic metaclasses parameterized by type variables (#23628)
  • Treat dataclass_transform dataclasses as neither frozen nor non-frozen (#23366)
  • Validate that type variable defaults don't reference later type parameters or type parameters out of scope (#23623)

Typeshed

  • Sync vendored typeshed stubs (#23642). Typeshed diff

fix: separate relative time tests from timezone-mock tests

timezone-mock 1.4.2 changed how it wraps the Date constructor,
which now interferes with vitest's vi.setSystemTime(). The fix
separates tests that need fake timers (distance, calendar formats)
from tests that need timezone mocking.

and don't need to run in withTimezones(), so they now run in their
own describe block with proper fake timer setup.

title: [Fix] Fix `st.text_area` `height="content"` not working
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes regression in `st.text_area` with `height="content"` introduced in v1.55.0 (#14222).

- Added `uiValue` to `useTextInputAutoExpand` dependencies so height recalculates when default value is set

**Root cause:** PR #13901 removed `element.default` fallback from `getStateFromWidgetMgr`. The textarea starts empty when `useLayoutEffect` calculates auto-expand height; the default value arrives later via `useEffect` but height was never recalculated.

## GitHub Issue

Fixes #14222

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (`frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/TextArea/TextArea.test.tsx` — 28 tests pass)
- [x] E2E Tests (`e2e_playwright/st_text_area_test.py::test_text_area_content_height_expansion` — passes)
- [x] E2E Tests (`e2e_playwright/st_text_area_test.py::test_text_area_content_height_default_value` — new regression test)

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| subagent | fixing-pr | 2 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a regression where `st.text_area(height="content")` fails to auto-expand when the widget’s default value is applied after the initial auto-expand measurement (introduced around v1.55.0).

**Changes:**
- Updates the `useTextInputAutoExpand` dependency list in `TextArea` to force an auto-height recalculation when the textarea value changes after mount (e.g., when default arrives).

<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.



<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_BEGIN -->
<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_END -->

commits.message:
Fix text_area height="content" not sizing to default value

Add uiValue to useTextInputAutoExpand dependencies so height
recalculates when the default value is set.

Add regression test for text_area height=content default value

Verifies the textarea auto-expands to fit its default value on initial
render, catching the regression from issue #14222.

fix: use committed value instead of uiValue in auto-expand dependencies

Address PR review feedback from Copilot and Cursor Bugbot. Using
`value` (the committed backend value) instead of `uiValue` prevents
unnecessary double DOM reflows on every keystroke while still
recalculating height when the default value is applied.
Merge branch 'develop' into fix/text-area-content-height

title: [chore] Use local static file in image column e2e test to fix flakiness
body:
## Describe your changes

- Replace external URL (`https://streamlit.io/images/brand/streamlit-mark-color.png`) with local static file (`./app/static/streamlit-mark.png`) in the Image column e2e test
- Fixes flaky test failures caused by network requests to external URLs

## Testing Plan

- [ ] E2E Tests
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the Image column e2e test to avoid network flakiness by using a local static asset instead of an external image URL.

**Changes:**
- Replaced the external Streamlit image URL with a local `./app/static/streamlit-mark.png` path in the dataframe config used by Playwright e2e.

commits.message:
Use local static file in image column e2e test

Replace external URL with local static file to fix flaky test
failures caused by network requests.

title: Fix stale query params in page URL during navigation
body:
## Describe your changes
**Fix:** Move `onUpdatePageUrl` call in `AppNavigation.handleNavigation` from the synchronous body into the `setState` callback. This fixes a race condition where `maybeUpdatePageUrl` reads stale `this.state.queryParams` when building the new page URL during `st.navigation` page switches.

**Root cause:** React 18 batches `setState` calls within the same synchronous task. When the frontend processes a batch of WebSocket messages, `handlePageInfoChanged` updates `queryParams` via `setState`, but the update hasn't been committed when `maybeUpdatePageUrl` runs moments later in the same batch. By deferring the URL update to the `setState` callback, we guarantee all batched state (including the cleaned query params from the backend) is committed before we read it.

**E2E tests:** Adds coverage for widget binding + page navigation scenarios that were previously untested:

| Scenario | Test file |
|---|---|
| 1. MPA v1 page switch clears params | `mpa_basics_test.py` |
| 2. MPA v2 page-specific params cleared | `st_navigation_test.py` |
| 3. MPA v2 entry file params persist | `st_navigation_test.py` |
| 4. Session state vs URL collision | `st_slider_test.py` |


## Testing Plan
- JS Unit Tests: ✅ Updated
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a race condition in the Streamlit frontend where page-bound widget query parameters were incorrectly carried over into the URL after navigating to a different page in multi-page apps. The root cause was that `onUpdatePageUrl` was called synchronously in `handleNavigation`, before React 18 had committed the batched `queryParams` state update (which comes in via `handlePageInfoChanged`). By moving the URL update into the `setState` callback, the fix guarantees the freshest `queryParams` value is used when the new page URL is constructed.

**Changes:**
- Deferred `onUpdatePageUrl` call in `AppNavigation.handleNavigation` from the synchronous body into the `setState` callback
- Updated the corresponding unit test to invoke the callback before asserting `onUpdatePageUrl`
- Added E2E test coverage for widget-bound query param behavior across page navigation (MPA v1, MPA v2/st.navigation, and session-state/URL collision)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/app/src/util/AppNavigation.ts` | Moves `onUpdatePageUrl` call into `setState` callback to fix stale `queryParams` reads during page navigation |
| `frontend/app/src/util/AppNavigation.test.ts` | Updates unit test to call the `setState` callback before asserting `onUpdatePageUrl` invocation |
| `e2e_playwright/st_slider.py` | Adds a slider widget with `bind="query-params"` and a pre-set `session_state` value, to test URL/session_state priority |
| `e2e_playwright/st_slider_test.py` | Adds E2E tests for session-state vs URL value collision scenarios; updates `NUM_SLIDER_WIDGETS` count |
| `e2e_playwright/st_navigation.py` | Adds page-specific bound widgets and a top-level entry-file bound widget for cross-page navigation testing |
| `e2e_playwright/st_navigation_test.py` | Adds E2E tests for

title: [chore] Fix nightly workflow permission validation error
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes the nightly workflow failure caused by permission validation error when calling `playwright.yml` via `workflow_call`:

> The nested job 'check-e2e-test-count' is requesting 'actions: read, pull-requests: write', but is only allowed 'actions: none, pull-requests: none'.

Adds `permissions` block to `run-playwright-tests` job in both `nightly.yml` and `release.yml`, matching the pattern already used for `run-python-tests` and `run-javascript-tests`.

The `check-e2e-test-count` job only runs on PRs (due to its `if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'` condition), but GitHub validates permissions at parse time regardless of runtime conditions.

## GitHub Issue

N/A (CI infrastructure fix)

## Testing Plan

- [x] Workflow YAML validated via `make check`
- [ ] Manual verification when nightly workflow runs

**Why no unit/E2E tests:** This is a CI workflow change, not a code change.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a GitHub Actions permission validation failure by moving a PR-commenting job out of the reusable Playwright workflow and into a standalone workflow triggered via `workflow_run`, so nightly/release invocations via `workflow_call` no longer hit nested-permissions constraints.

**Changes:**
- Removes the `check-e2e-test-count` job from `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
- Adds a new `.github/workflows/e2e-test-count-check.yml` workflow that runs after “Playwright E2E Tests” completes and posts/updates a PR comment when test count increases significantly.
- Adds a fallback PR-number resolution path when `workflow_run.pull_requests` is empty.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| .github/workflows/playwright.yml | Removes the permission-sensitive PR-commenting job from the reusable Playwright workflow. |
| .github/workflows/e2e-test-count-check.yml | Introduces a `workflow_run`-triggered workflow to compare E2E test counts and comment on PRs with elevated permissions. |

<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->

but GitHub validates permissions at parse time regardless.

title: feat: allow external URLs in st.Page for navigation sidebar
body:
Add support for external web links in st.navigation, allowing users to include external URLs alongside internal pages in both sidebar and top navigation modes.                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                 
  This implements the feature requested in #9025.                                                                                                

  Changes

  Backend (lib/streamlit/navigation/page.py)

  - Detect HTTP/HTTPS URLs using existing url_util.is_url()
  - Handle external URLs in StreamlitPage.__init__ with title validation and URL path sanitization
  - Add is_external and external_url properties
  - Skip code execution for external links in run()
  - Use explicit None check for url_path to correctly reject empty strings

  Backend (lib/streamlit/commands/execution_control.py, lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/button.py)

  - st.switch_page: raise StreamlitAPIException for external pages (internal navigation only)
  - st.page_link: render external StreamlitPage objects as external links

  Protobuf (proto/streamlit/proto/AppPage.proto)

  - Adds optional `external_url ` field

  Frontend

  - Update SidebarNavLink to handle external links with target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer"
  - Update SidebarNav, TopNav, TopNavSection to pass external link info
  - Pass props explicitly in TopNavSection instead of spreading proto fields

  Tests

  - Add unit tests for external URL support in page_test.py (URL detection, title validation, url_path sanitization, edge cases)
  - Add is_external=False to existing switch_page test mocks
  - Add test for st.switch_page rejecting external pages
  - Add tests for st.page_link with external StreamlitPage objects
  - Add unit tests for external link click behavior in SidebarNav.test.tsx and TopNav.test.tsx
  - Add 8 E2E tests for external links in sidebar and top navigation
  - Add proto compatibility test fields

  Example Usage

  import streamlit as st

  def home():
      st.title("Home")

  pages = [
      st.Page(home, title="Home", icon="🏠", default=True),
      st.Page("https://docs.streamlit.io", title="Docs", icon="📚"),
      st.Page("https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit", title="GitHub", icon="🐙"),
  ]
  pg = st.navigation(pages)
  pg.run()

  External links:
  - Require a title parameter (cannot be inferred from URL)
  - Cannot be set as the default page
  - Open in a new tab (target="_blank")
  - st.switch_page raises an error (internal navigation only)
  - st.page_link renders them as external links

  Demo

  https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76a3d917-051c-4e2b-9f1f-fdc33072ddd4

  External links in the sidebar open in a new tab. Internal pages navigate normally.

  Closes #9025
author: t0k0shi

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds support for external web links in `st.Page` for navigation menus, allowing users to include external URLs alongside internal pages. The implementation addresses feature request #9025 by enabling external links that open in new tabs when clicked.

**Changes:**
- Added external URL detection and validation in `st.Page` constructor with proper error handling for missing titles and invalid default page configuration
- Extended the AppPage protobuf message with `is_external` and `external_url` fields for client-server communication
- Updated frontend navigation components (SidebarNavLink, TopNav, TopNavSection, SidebarNav) to render external links with `target="_blank"` and proper security attributes

### Reviewed changes

commits.message:
feat: allow external URLs in st.Page for navigation sidebar

Add support for external web links in st.navigation, allowing users to
include external URLs alongside internal pages in the navigation menu.

Changes:
- Backend (lib/streamlit/navigation/page.py):
  - Add _is_url() helper to detect HTTP/HTTPS URLs
  - Handle external URLs in StreamlitPage.__init__
  - Add is_external and external_url properties
  - Skip code execution for external links in run()

- Protobuf (proto/streamlit/proto/AppPage.proto):
  - Add is_external boolean field
  - Add external_url string field

- Frontend:
  - Update SidebarNavLink to handle external links with target="_blank"
  - Update SidebarNav, TopNav, TopNavSection to pass external link info

- Tests:
  - Add 11 unit tests for external URL support

Example usage:
```python
pages = [
    st.Page(home, title="Home", icon="🏠"),
    st.Page("https://docs.streamlit.io", title="Docs", icon="📚"),
]
pg = st.navigation(pages)
```

Closes #9025

fix: address AI review comments for external URL support

Changes based on Copilot and Cursor Bugbot review feedback:

Backend fixes:
- Replace local _is_url() with url_util.is_url() for DRY compliance
- Add _sanitize_url_path() to handle special characters in URL paths
  (removes &, #, ?, /, \, :, *, ", <, >, |, ' characters)
- Add validation for empty and nested URL paths on external URLs
- Skip external URLs when selecting implicit default page
- Add mock verification to test_external_url_run_does_nothing

Frontend fixes:
- Always call onClick for external links (fixes popover not closing)
- Collapse sidebar on mobile when clicking external links
- Add comprehensive tests for external link functionality
  (target="_blank", rel="noopener noreferrer", externalUrl handling)

Tests added:
- 7 frontend tests for isExternal behavior
- 4 backend tests for URL path sanitization and validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add external link icon for navigation sidebar

Add a small 'open_in_new' material icon to the right side of external
links in the navigation sidebar. This visual indicator helps users
identify links that will open in a new tab.

- Add DynamicIcon with ':material/open_in_new:' for external links
- Add tests for icon rendering when isExternal is true/false
fix: change external link icon from open_in_new to launch

fix: add type casts to satisfy type checker

Add cast() calls to confirm default_page and page_to_return are
StreamlitPage at points where they are used. These are guaranteed by
the code logic but the type checker cannot infer this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove external link launch icon per maintainer feedback

- Remove launch icon rendering from SidebarNavLink
- Remove related tests for launch icon display
- External links now use the same layout as internal pages

Addresses jrieke's feedback to remove the icon and keep
the layout simple.

fix: strip slashes before empty check in external URL path validation

url_path="///" bypassed the empty string check because strip() only
removed whitespace, not slashes. Move strip("/") before the empty
check so slash-only paths are correctly rejected.

test: add tests for external link behavior and validate empty title

- Add frontend tests for external link click behavior (SidebarNav, TopNav)
- Add mobile collapseSidebar test for external links
- Add empty/whitespace title validation for external URL pages
- Add navigation tests for all-external-pages, mixed pages, proto fields
- Organize external link tests into dedicated describe blocks

Fix mypy errors and add tests for external URL pages

Source fixes:
- page.py: fix mypy no-redef errors by using conditional assignments
- navigation.py: remove redundant cast calls (mypy redundant-cast)
- execution_control.py: reject st.switch_page for external URL pages
- button.py: support external StreamlitPage in st.page_link

Test additions:
- execution_control_test.py: add is_external=False to existing mocks,
  add test for switch_page with external page
- page_link_test.py: add tests for external StreamlitPage support
- page_test.py: fix test assertions for updated page behavior
- proto_compatibility_test.py: add new proto fields to known list

Fix MagicMock spec truthy issue in test_page_link_with_streamlit_page

MagicMock(spec=StreamlitPage) creates mock attributes that are truthy
by default. Without explicitly setting is_external=False, the test
enters the external page code path and fails with TypeError.

fix: address review feedback and add E2E tests for external URL navigation

- Fix RUF043 lint error: use raw string with escaped metacharacters in
  pytest.raises match parameter
- Fix url_path="" vs None inconsistency: use explicit None check instead
  of falsy-or to distinguish "not provided" from "explicitly empty"
- Add test for explicit empty url_path on external URL pages
- Add E2E tests for external links in sidebar and top navigation (8 tests)
- Remove {…item} spread in TopNavSection to prevent proto field DOM leakage
- Fix handleClick return type from boolean to void to match prop interface
- Remove Issue #9025 reference comments from 5 files

style: apply ruff format to fix pre-commit check

Reformat long lines in page.py and execution_control_test.py
to satisfy ruff line-length rules.
refactor: restructure default page selection for ty type narrowing

fix: guard external pages from direct URL access and unify url_path normalization

Address maintainer review feedback (sfc-gh-bnisco):
- Prevent external pages from being selected as runnable target on direct URL access
- Unify url_path normalization between external and internal pages
- Add regression tests for both fixes
fix: formatting

refactor: model AppPage destinations with oneof

Replace external navigation flags with an internal/external destination oneof to simplify AppPage semantics and avoid invalid state combinations. Also centralize frontend external-page detection helpers and remove unused open behavior metadata.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: harden external destination detection and clarify hidden external docs

Treat pages as external based on destination oneof presence instead of URL truthiness so malformed payloads do not get misclassified as internal navigation targets. Clarify st.Page and st.switch_page docs to document that hidden external pages can only be opened via st.page_link.

Made-with: Cursor
fix: address PR comments

fix: improve accessibility and robustness of external nav links

- Add visually-hidden "(opens in new tab)" text for screen readers
- Guard isActive so external pages never show as active
- Use nullish coalescing (??) in getExternalPageUrl for precision
- Add tests for external/internal url_path collision detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: simplify external navigation link implementation

Remove redundant comments, consolidate repetitive tests using
parameterized expansion, and simplify conditional prop spreading
in SidebarNavLink. Streamline _set_page_destination with direct
None-check instead of redundant is_external guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add unit tests for TopNavSection external link behavior

Cover external link click handling in the TopNavSection popover dropdown,
verifying that handlePageChange is not called for external links, is
called for internal links, and that external links render with correct
href/target/rel attributes while internal links do not.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add security and visibility tests for external nav links

Verify that dangerous URL schemes (javascript:, vbscript:, data:) are
rejected by st.Page and that hidden external pages are excluded from
both SidebarNav and TopNav rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
merge conflicts with develop

fix: use nullish coalescing for external nav link href

Replace falsy check with nullish coalescing (`??`) so that an
external page with an empty-string URL is not incorrectly routed
to the internal pageUrl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: clarify visibility behavior for external vs internal pages

Update the `visibility` parameter docstring in `StreamlitPage` to
reflect that hidden external pages are not URL-accessible or
switchable via `st.switch_page`, unlike hidden internal pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
refactor: simplify the AppPage.proto per comment
fix(test): update test to match updated proto

title: [fix] Restore layered vconcat width propagation and add #13974 regression tests
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixed a bug where layered Altair charts inside vconcat compositions with `autosize="fit-x"` and `width="stretch"` weren't rendering properly. The issue was in the `useVegaElementPreprocessor` where we were skipping setting width on layer children, causing "infinite extent" errors. This change ensures that layer children still receive width so layered + vconcat charts can stretch consistently.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Fixes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/13974

## Testing Plan

- Added a regression test case in the e2e_playwright/st_altair_chart.py file that demonstrates the issue
- Added a new test in st_altair_chart_test.py to verify the fix works properly
- Updated the unit test in useVegaElementPreprocessor.test.ts to better test the layer + vconcat case

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a regression introduced in PR #13423 where layered Altair charts inside `vconcat` compositions with `autosize="fit-x"` and `width="stretch"` stopped rendering. The issue was that the width propagation logic was skipping all composition operators including `layer`, but `layer` children need to receive width to render correctly within vertical concatenations.

**Changes:**
- Modified the width propagation logic to only skip width on nested concatenation operators (hconcat, vconcat, concat) while allowing layer children to receive width
- Updated unit tests to reflect the corrected behavior
- Added an E2E regression test to guard against this specific issue in the future

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.ts` | Removed "layer" from the composition operator check, allowing layer children to receive width from their vconcat parent |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.test.ts` | Removed incorrect parametrized test case and added new test verifying layer children receive width |
| `e2e_playwright/st_altair_chart.py` | Added regression test case with layered vconcat chart using autosize="fit-x" and width="stretch" |
| `e2e_playwright/st_altair_chart_test.py` | Added dedicated regression test function and updated chart count constants |
LGTM 👍

commits.message: [fix] Restore layered vconcat width propagation and add #13974 regression tests

| .claude/agents/reviewing-local-changes.md | Mirrors the same security-sensitive checklist for local-change reviews. |
LGTM, thanks for making this list.

commits.message:
[chore] Add security-sensitive areas to code review instructions

Enhance the reviewing-local-changes agent and code-review instructions
with a detailed checklist of security-sensitive areas to pay closer
attention to during reviews, including:
- WebSocket/server/auth/session handling
- File upload and path traversal risks
- CORS, XSRF, and security headers
- External dependencies and assets
- Runtime JS execution and code injection
- HTML/Markdown sanitization (XSS)
- iframe embedding and postMessage
- Sensitive data handling

Add st.login()/st.logout() to security-sensitive areas

Address review feedback: add explicit bullet point for OAuth token
handling in st.login()/st.logout() features.

title: [feat] Serve static files with native content types
body:
## Describe your changes

We have a bit overcautious limitation of our static file serving to only allow a couple of selected types to be served with the correct file type. This was put in place for the initial release with the goal reduce this limitation, but this never happened. In its current state, you can already serve any file type with custom components without limitations, and Starlette adds another way for developers to serve arbitrary files with correct file types. Based on that, there doesn't seem to be a reason why we would need this limitation for the static file serving feature. Especially since it's already behind a config option. 

- Remove the `SAFE_APP_STATIC_FILE_EXTENSIONS` allowlist that forced non-allowlisted file types (like `.js`, `.html`, `.css`) to be served as `text/plain`
- Static files are now served with their natural content types using `guess_content_type()`
- Both Tornado and Starlette handlers now use the same `guess_content_type` function for consistent behavior

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python) - Updated existing tests to verify correct content-type headers
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR removes the content-type allowlist for static files served from the `app/static/` directory, allowing files to be served with their native MIME types instead of forcing non-allowlisted types to `text/plain`. The change enables developers to serve HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files with proper content types while maintaining security through the `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` header.

**Changes:**
- Removed `SAFE_APP_STATIC_FILE_EXTENSIONS` allowlist that restricted which file types could be served with native content types
- Both Tornado and Starlette handlers now consistently use `guess_content_type()` for determining content types
- Updated tests to verify correct content-type headers for JavaScript files while maintaining nosniff header checks

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/app_static_file_handler.py | Removed allowlist constant and logic; added `get_content_type()` override to use `guess_content_type()` |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_routes.py | Removed allowlist usage and now directly passes `guess_content_type()` result to FileResponse |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/app_static_file_handler_test.py | Updated tests to verify JavaScript files get appropriate content types instead of text/plain |

commits.message:
feat(static): serve all static files with native content types

Remove the SAFE_APP_STATIC_FILE_EXTENSIONS allowlist that forced
non-allowlisted file types to be served as text/plain. Files are
now served with their natural content type using guess_content_type().

Both Tornado and Starlette handlers now use the same guess_content_type
function for consistent behavior. The X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
header remains in place to prevent MIME-type sniffing attacks.

chore: address PR review comments

- Add comment explaining unused path parameter in set_extra_headers
- Add test coverage for HTML and CSS file content types

title: Improve backend cleanup of stale query param bindings
body:
## Describe your changes
- Add `QueryParams.unbind_and_clear_param()` method that removes a stale widget binding **and** clears the associated query param from the URL (with frontend notification), encapsulating cleanup that previously required reaching into private internals
- Add an `elif` branch in `SessionState.register_widget()` so that when a keyed widget re-registers with `bind=None` (i.e., the developer removed `bind="query-params"`), any stale binding and its URL param are cleaned up immediately
- This fixes a gap where `remove_stale_bindings()` could not catch stale bindings for widgets that are still active but simply dropped their `bind` parameter, because `bind` is not part of the widget ID computation

## Testing Plan
- Python Unit Tests: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR improves Streamlit’s backend handling of stale `bind="query-params"` widget bindings by encapsulating cleanup in `QueryParams` and triggering that cleanup when a keyed widget stops using `bind`, ensuring the URL is updated accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Add `QueryParams.unbind_and_clear_param()` to remove a widget binding and clear its associated query param (including a forward message to update the frontend URL).
- Update `SessionState.register_widget()` to clean up stale query-param bindings when a keyed widget re-registers with `bind=None`.
- Add unit tests covering the new cleanup behavior in both `QueryParams` and `SessionState`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/query_params.py | Adds `unbind_and_clear_param` to unbind widgets and clear the corresponding query param with a URL update. |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Triggers stale binding cleanup during widget registration when `bind` is removed. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/query_params_test.py | Adds tests for `unbind_and_clear_param`. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state_test.py | Adds tests ensuring `register_widget` cleanup occurs when `bind` is removed. |
This would benefit from an e2e test so that we could assert that the URL is actually modified. LGTM after adding that!

commits.message: Fix stale bindings on BE
Address feedback
Add recommended test
Add e2e test

title: [refactor] Move metric font config to theme typography primitives
body:
## Describe your changes

Refactors `metricValueFontSize` and `metricValueFontWeight` from top-level `EmotionTheme` properties to their proper locations within the theme typography primitives:

- **`theme.fontSizes.metricValueFontSize`** (default: `2.25rem`)
- **`theme.fontWeights.metricValueFontWeight`** (default: `400`)

### Changes

- Added `metricValueFontSize` and `metricValueFontWeight` to typography primitives
- Removed optional top-level properties from `EmotionTheme` interface
- Updated `createEmotionTheme` to use conditional overrides pattern (consistent with other font configs)
- Updated `StyledMetricValueText` to read from new locations
- Updated Components V2 theme extraction to use new paths
- Simplified `StreamlitTheme` interface by making these properties non-nullable

### Benefits

- Aligns metric font configuration with existing typography structure
- Simplifies Components V2 API (non-nullable properties with sensible defaults)
- Cleaner code organization using established conditional overrides pattern

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS) - Updated tests for new property locations and default values

author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors `metricValueFontSize` and `metricValueFontWeight` from optional top-level properties on the `EmotionTheme` interface into their proper places within the theme's typography primitives (`theme.fontSizes.metricValueFontSize` and `theme.fontWeights.metricValueFontWeight`). This aligns metric font configuration with how other typography (e.g., heading fonts) is structured and simplifies the Components V2 public `StreamlitTheme` interface by providing non-nullable values with sensible defaults.

**Changes:**
- Added `metricValueFontSize: "2.25rem"` and `metricValueFontWeight: 400` to typography primitives, and removed the old optional top-level fields from `EmotionTheme`
- Updated `createEmotionTheme` to apply metric font overrides via `conditionalOverrides.fontSizes`/`conditionalOverrides.fontWeights` (consistent with other font configs), replacing the IIFE-based spread approach
- Updated all consumers (`StyledMetricValueText`, `extractComponentsV2Theme`, and `StreamlitTheme`) to read from the new paths, with tests updated to reflect always-non-null defaults

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| `frontend/lib/src/theme/utils.test.ts` | Updates tests to assert new `theme.fontSizes.metricValueFontSize` / `theme.fontWeights.metricValueFontWeight` paths and default values |

commits.message:
[refactor] Move metric font config to theme typography primitives

Refactor metricValueFontSize and metricValueFontWeight from top-level
EmotionTheme properties to their proper locations within theme.fontSizes
and theme.fontWeights. This aligns metric font configuration with other
typography primitives and simplifies the Components V2 API by making
these properties non-nullable with sensible defaults (2.25rem, 400).

fix(cache): preserve on_release behavior in TTLCleanupCache.clear() (#14180)

## Describe your changes

cachetools 7.0.2 changed TTLCache.clear() to an O(1) implementation that
bypasses `popitem()`/`expire()`. That skipped `TTLCleanupCache` release
hooks and broke resource cleanup paths that expect on_release to run for
each entry.

Override `TTLCleanupCache.clear()` to evict entries via `popitem()`,
preserving release semantics across `cachetools` versions. Update
related inline comments in `cache_resource_api` to document the
callback/exception behavior and why per-item cleanup is still used.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

title: Fix `AppTest` not rendering content for apps using `st.navigation`
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes #9446.

`AppTest` was not rendering content for pages in apps using `st.navigation` and `st.Page`. The root cause is that `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory` is a class-level attribute that persists across test runs. When set to `True` (from a previous test or because a `pages/` directory exists), the `ScriptRunner` would invoke the legacy MPA v1 path instead of executing the script code directly, causing `st.navigation`-based pages to not render properly.

**Changes:**
- Reset `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory = None` before each `AppTest.run()` to ensure clean state
- Updated `AppTest` docstring to reflect that `st.navigation` is now supported
- Added two regression tests for the fix

## Testing Plan

- [x] Added `test_navigation_with_callable_pages` - tests the user scenario from the issue
- [x] Added `test_navigation_resets_pages_manager_state` - regression test that verifies the fix works even if `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory` was previously set to `True`

**Contributor License Agreement**

- [x] By submitting this pull request I have read and agree to the [Contributor License Agreement](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md).

<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 1 |
| skill | updating-internal-docs | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 2 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 1 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a bug (issue #9446) where `AppTest` was not rendering content for pages in apps using `st.navigation` and `st.Page`. The root cause was that `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory` is a class-level attribute that persists across test runs; when set to `True`, `ScriptRunner` would invoke the legacy MPA v1 code path instead of executing the script directly, bypassing any `st.navigation`-based page content.

**Changes:**
- Reset `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory = None` in `AppTest._run()` before each run to ensure a clean state
- Update the `AppTest` class docstring to reflect that `st.navigation` is now supported
- Add two regression tests covering the basic navigation scenario and the dirty-state regression

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/testing/v1/app_test.py` | Core fix: reset `PagesManager.uses_pages_directory` to `None` before each `AppTest._run()` call; updated docstring to remove the prior limitation note |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/testing/app_test_test.py` | Two new regression tests verifying that `st.navigation` works correctly, and that the fix holds even when `uses_pages_directory` was previously set to `True` |

commits.message:
Fix AppTest not rendering content for apps using st.navigation

Reset PagesManager.uses_pages_directory to None before each AppTest.run()
to ensure clean state. This allows scripts using st.navigation to work
correctly regardless of prior test runs or the presence of a pages/ folder.

Fixes #9446

title: [feat] Extract leading icon from alert body
body:
## Describe your changes

Automatically extract a leading emoji or Material icon from alert body text when no explicit `icon` parameter is provided.

- When calling `st.error()`, `st.warning()`, `st.info()`, or `st.success()` without an `icon` parameter, the function now checks if the body starts with an emoji or Material icon shortcode (e.g., `:material/warning:`)
- If found, the icon is extracted and displayed as the alert's icon, while being removed from the body text
- Explicit `icon` parameter takes precedence over body extraction (backwards compatible)

**Example:**
```python
# Before: emoji displayed inline in body
st.warning("🔔 Important notification")

# After: emoji automatically becomes the icon
st.warning("🔔 Important notification")  # 🔔 shown as icon, body shows "Important notification"
```

## Github

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10892

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit tests for `extract_leading_icon` in `string_util_test.py`
- [x] Unit tests for icon extraction behavior in `alert_test.py`
- [x] E2E tests for emoji and material icon extraction from body
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.



<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_BEGIN -->
<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_END -->

## Pull request overview

This PR adds automatic extraction of a leading emoji or material icon shortcode from the body text of `st.error()`, `st.warning()`, `st.info()`, and `st.success()` alert functions, when no explicit `icon` parameter is provided. Previously, an emoji at the start of the body was rendered inline; now it is promoted to the alert's icon slot and removed from the body text. Explicit `icon` parameters always take precedence over auto-extraction.

**Changes:**
- A new `extract_leading_icon()` function in `string_util.py` that checks for a leading material icon shortcode (validated) or emoji at the start of a string and returns a `(icon, remaining_text)` tuple.
- A new `_process_alert_body_and_icon()` helper in `alert.py` that replaces the direct `validate_icon_or_emoji`/`clean_text` calls in all four alert methods, adding the auto-extraction logic.
- Tests and E2E test cases covering the new extraction behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/string_util.py` | Adds `extract_leading_icon()` function for material icon/emoji detection at start of text |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/alert.py` | Refactors alert body/icon processing into `_process_alert_body_and_icon()` with auto-extraction |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/string_util_test.py` | Unit tests for the new `extract_leading_icon()` function |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/alert_test.py` | Unit tests verifying extraction behavior in all four alert types |
| `e2e_playwright/st_alert.py` | Adds two new test alert cases with leading emoji and material icon |
| `e2e_playwright/st_alert_test.py` | Updates alert count assertions and adds snapshot tests for the new extraction cases |
</details>

commits.message:
[feat] Extract leading icon from alert body when no icon parameter provided

When the `icon` parameter is not explicitly set, alert functions (st.error,
st.warning, st.info, st.success) now automatically extract a leading emoji
or Material icon from the body text and use it as the alert icon.

- Add `extract_leading_icon` function to string_util.py
- Add `_process_alert_body_and_icon` helper to alert.py
- Comprehensive unit tests for icon extraction logic
- E2E tests for emoji and material icon extraction from body
docs: Update alert icon docstrings to describe auto-extraction behavior

refactor: Use extract_leading_icon in button_group

Refactor _transformed_format_func in button_group.py to use the shared
extract_leading_icon utility instead of manual icon detection logic.
This keeps icon extraction consistent across st.alert and st.pills.

title: [fix] Fix flaky e2e tests for time_input, mpa_v2, select_slider, and image
body:
## Describe your changes

Fix multiple flaky e2e tests by addressing timing and synchronization issues.

### Test fixes:

**st_time_input_test.py:**
- `test_time_input_with_custom_theme`: Added `expect(selection_dropdown).to_be_visible()` wait before interacting with dropdown
- `test_handles_callback_on_change_correctly`: Added `wait_for_app_run(app)` after dropdown selection and input
