author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR improves CI reliability by de-flaking several Playwright E2E tests that were failing due to timing/focus issues across browsers (notably WebKit) and due to navigation synchronization in MPAv2 tests.

**Changes:**
- Add explicit synchronization for dropdown visibility and fullscreen state transitions to avoid race conditions.
- Ensure key events target the intended element (select slider) for better cross-browser stability.
- Add appropriate waits after `st.switch_page`-triggered navigations and adjust slider interaction to be more reliable on WebKit.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `e2e_playwright/st_time_input_test.py` | Waits for the BaseWeb popover dropdown to be visible before hover/snapshot. |
| `e2e_playwright/st_select_slider_test.py` | Sends ArrowRight directly to the slider locator to avoid focus issues (WebKit). |
| `e2e_playwright/st_image_test.py` | Replaces a fixed timeout with a CSS-based wait for fullscreen open/close state. |
| `e2e_playwright/multipage_apps_v2/mpa_v2_basics_test.py` | Adds page-load waiting after `st.switch_page` navigation and makes slider click more reliable on WebKit. |

commits.message:
Fix flaky e2e tests for timing and synchronization issues

- st_time_input: Add visibility wait before dropdown interaction
- mpa_v2_basics: Add wait_for_app_loaded after switch_page navigation
- mpa_v2_basics: Use force=True for slider click on webkit
- st_select_slider: Target slider element directly for keypress
- st_image: Replace timeout with CSS position assertion for fullscreen

Fix additional flaky e2e tests for timing issues

- st_time_input: Add wait_for_app_run after dropdown selection and input
- mpa_v2_basics: Add wait_for_app_loaded to test_removes_query_params_with_st_switch_page
- mpa_v2_basics: Reorder assertions in test_switch_page_with_query_params to check markdown first

Fix more flaky e2e tests by addressing timing issues

- page_5.py: Add unique "Page 5 Query Params:" label to avoid collision during page transitions
- mpa_v2_basics_test: Use unique page_5 label for query params assertions
- mpa_v2_basics_test: Wait for Page 5 header before checking query params
- st_select_slider_test: Add wait_for_app_run between click and ArrowRight for second slider interaction

Fix remaining flaky e2e tests

- st_time_input: Add wait_for_app_run before snapshot in test_dynamic_time_input_props
- mpa_v2_basics: Increase URL assertion timeout to 10s for query param tests

Fix flaky test_switch_page_with_query_params and test_time_input_with_custom_theme

- test_switch_page_with_query_params: Use wait_until to poll for URL query
  params before exact URL assertion. Webkit can be slow to update the URL
  even after page content is fully rendered.

- test_time_input_with_custom_theme: Remove hover state from snapshot test
  as it's inherently flaky across browsers (~5% pixel diff). Hover state
  is already tested in the separate test_correct_menu_font_colors test.
  Delete old baselines so they get regenerated without hover.
Add regenerated snapshots for time_input custom theme test (without hover)

Clean up unnecessary waits and unused parameters in e2e tests

- Remove wait_for_app_loaded after click_button (click_button already waits)
- Remove unused _app parameter from set_fullscreen helper
- Remove unnecessary wait_for_app_run and hover state comments

Fix flaky MPA query params tests with proper wait_for_app_loaded

Both tests were missing wait_for_app_loaded after click_button, causing
the URL assertions to run before the page navigation completed.

- test_removes_query_params_with_st_switch_page: Add wait_for_app_loaded
  and use wait_until to poll for URL to not contain old query params
- test_switch_page_with_query_params: Add wait_for_app_loaded before
  checking URL (wait_until was already present)

Use Playwright wait_for_url for more reliable URL navigation detection

Replace wait_for_app_loaded + wait_until polling with Playwright's native
wait_for_url which is optimized for URL navigation detection. Also put
URL wait BEFORE content checks to catch the URL change before anything
can overwrite it.

- test_switch_page_with_query_params: Use wait_for_url with glob pattern
- test_removes_query_params_with_st_switch_page: Same approach, with
  explicit assertion that old query params are not in URL

Fix flaky test_dynamic_select_slider_props_and_options on webkit

Add wait_for_app_run between click and ArrowRight to ensure the click
interaction completes before sending the key press. The second slider
interaction in this test already had this wait, but the first one was
missing it.

title: [docs] add exact matching and element count guidance to E2E docs
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds two best practices to the E2E Playwright testing docs (`AGENTS.md` and mirrors):

- Use `exact=True` with `get_by_text()` and `filter(has_text=...)` to prevent substring matching from causing strict mode violations when the target text is a prefix of other text on the page.
- Check and update existing element count assertions (e.g., `to_have_count(N)`) when adding new elements to an existing app script.

Both patterns caused recurring CI failures across the dynamic containers callback PRs.

## Testing Plan

- Explanation of why no additional tests are needed: Documentation-only changes, no behavior modifications.

title: [fix] Fix spec-validation workflow: install js-yaml and accept tech-spec.md
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes the validate-spec CI job which was failing with Cannot find module 'js-yaml'. Rather than installing js-yaml at runtime (which has a security risk: a malicious PR could add a repo-level .npmrc to redirect the registry or enable lifecycle scripts), this PR replaces it with an inline key: value frontmatter parser.
The inline parser is sufficient for spec frontmatter, which only ever contains two simple string fields (author and created). As a bonus, js-yaml auto-converts bare dates to Date objects, requiring a runtime instanceof Date workaround — the inline parser returns plain strings, so that dead branch is removed.
Also extends the validator to accept tech-spec.md alongside product-spec.md, and derives the missing-file error message from the candidates list so it stays in sync automatically.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- Explanation of why no additional tests are needed: validated by CI on this PR

title: Bump ty from 0.0.18 to 0.0.19
body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.18 to 0.0.19.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.19

Release Notes

Released on 2026-02-26.

Bug fixes

  • Fix panic in diagnostic rendering when attempting to render a code frame pointing to leading whitespace (#23458)
  • Fix panics and incorrect inference stemming from incorrectly considering overloads in another file as being associated with a function in the file being checked (#21977)
  • Fix panic when attempting to narrow the type of a dictionary key that was assigned using a multi-target assignment, e.g. x = y = {"a": 1} (#23523)
  • Fix infinite hang on mutually recursive TypeAliasType definitions (#23397)

LSP server

  • Fix inlay hints for starred unpacking targets (#23454)

Core type checking

  • Fix assignability, subtyping and equivalence checks relating to typing.Generator prior to Python 3.13 (#23386)
  • Understand that a scope's control flow terminates after await foo() if foo returns typing.Awaitable[typing.Never] or similar (#23479)
  • Implement stricter handling of calls to instances of type[T] types (#23472)
  • Support basic type narrowing for case {...}: patterns in match statements (#23462)
  • Fix bugs that could manifest in incorrect overload evaluation, false-positive complaints regarding assert_type calls or false-positive redundant-cast diagnostics by reimplementing the equivalence type relation as mutual subtyping of top and bottom materializations (#23428)
  • Fix equality and __contains__ narrowing with PEP-695 type aliases (#23545)
  • Support _value_ annotations on enum classes (#22228)

Improvements to diagnostics

  • Improve diagnostics when attempting to specialize non-generic types (#23516)
  • Render subdiagnostics when --output-format=github is specified (#23455)

Performance

  • Add a cached method for calculating the intersection of two types (#23547)
  • Add a cached method for calculating the union of two types (#23565)
  • Reduce the threshold above which Literal types in unions are upcasted to nominal-instance types in situations where the union type is recursively defined (#23521)
  • Control flow: isolate the calculation of "loop header reachability" in a dedicated, cached function (#23520)

title: Allow programmatically setting `st.dataframe` selections
body:
## Describe your changes

Allow programmatically setting the `st.dataframe` selection state via session state, and add a new `selection_default` parameter for specifying initial selections.

- Session state writes to the widget key are validated and forwarded to the frontend as a one-shot `selectionState` proto field
- New `selection_default` parameter provides initial selection on first render without overriding subsequent user selections
- Backend validates row indices, column names, and cell positions against actual data dimensions, filtering invalid entries
- Programmatic cell selection is limited to `single-cell` mode (multi-cell ranges require rectangular range info that can't be reconstructed)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10128

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python): `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py` — 27+ new tests for `_validate_selection_state` and integration tests for `selection_default`
- [x] Unit Tests (TypeScript): `useWidgetState.test.ts` — 14+ new tests for `getProgrammaticSelectionState` and selection default loading; `useSelectionHandler.test.ts` — consolidated with `it.each`
- [x] E2E Tests: `e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_selections_test.py` — Tests for programmatic row/column/cell selection, clearing, and selection defaults

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Enables programmatic control of `st.dataframe` selection by allowing users to write a selection-state object into `st.session_state[key]`, which is then validated on the backend and applied on the frontend.

**Changes:**
- Add `selection_state` to the Dataframe protobuf to carry backend-driven selection state to the frontend.
- Add backend validation (`_validate_selection_state`) and send validated selection JSON to the frontend when session state changes.
- Add frontend support to apply programmatic selections, avoid feedback loops, and extend unit + e2e coverage.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Dataframe.proto | Adds `selection_state` field to transport programmatic selection state to the frontend. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py | Validates session-state selection payloads and forwards programmatic selection state to the frontend; allows session-state writes for this widget. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_dataframe_test.py | Removes the “no session_state writes” expectation and adds focused unit tests for selection-state validation. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useWidgetState.ts | Adds parsing helper + programmatic selection retrieval/sync behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useWidgetState.test.ts | Adds unit tests covering programmatic selection parsing + widget manager syncing. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/hooks/useSelectionHandler.ts | Adds an option to skip syncing selection updates to widget state (used to avoid programmatic feedback loops). |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/DataFrame.tsx | Applies programmatic selection state from the element and prevents redundant backend syncs. |
| e2e_playwright/st_dataframe_select

✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

title: Bump ruff from 0.15.2 to 0.15.4
body:
Bumps [ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) from 0.15.2 to 0.15.4.
Release notes

Sourced from ruff's releases.

0.15.4

Release Notes

Released on 2026-02-26.

This is a follow-up release to 0.15.3 that resolves a panic when the new rule PLR1712 was enabled with any rule that analyzes definitions, such as many of the ANN or D rules.

Bug fixes

  • Fix panic on access to definitions after analyzing definitions (#23588)
  • [pyflakes] Suppress false positive in F821 for names used before del in stub files (#23550)

Documentation

  • Clarify first-party import detection in Ruff (#23591)
  • Fix incorrect import-heading example (#23568)

Contributors

  • @​stakeswky
  • @​ntBre
  • @​thejcannon
  • @​GeObts

Install ruff 0.15.4

Install prebuilt binaries via shell script

curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/releases/download/0.15.4/ruff-installer.sh | sh

Install prebuilt binaries via powershell script

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -c "irm https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/releases/download/0.15.4/ruff-installer.ps1 | iex"

Download ruff 0.15.4

File Platform Checksum
ruff-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz Apple Silicon macOS checksum
ruff-x86_64-apple-darwin.tar.gz Intel macOS checksum
ruff-aarch64-pc-windows-msvc.zip ARM64 Windows checksum
ruff-i686-pc-windows-msvc.zip x86 Windows checksum
ruff-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip x64 Windows checksum
ruff-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz ARM64 Linux checksum
ruff-i686-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz x86 Linux checksum
ruff-powerpc64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz PPC64 Linux checksum

... (truncated)

0.15.4

Released on 2026-02-26.

This is a follow-up release to 0.15.3 that resolves a panic when the new rule PLR1712 was enabled with any rule that analyzes definitions, such as many of the ANN or D rules.

Bug fixes

  • Fix panic on access to definitions after analyzing definitions (#23588)
  • [pyflakes] Suppress false positive in F821 for names used before del in stub files (#23550)

Documentation

  • Clarify first-party import detection in Ruff (#23591)

Contributors

Released on 2026-02-26.

Preview features

  • Drop explicit support for .qmd file extension (#23572)

    This can now be enabled instead by setting the extension option:

    # ruff.toml
extension = { qmd = "markdown" }

    pyproject.toml
    

    [tool.ruff]
extension = { qmd = "markdown" }

  • Include configured extensions in file discovery (#23400)

  • [flake8-bandit] Allow suspicious imports in TYPE_CHECKING blocks (S401-S415) (#23441)

  • [flake8-bugbear] Allow B901 in pytest hook wrappers (#21931)

  • [flake8-import-conventions] Add missing conventions from upstream (ICN001, ICN002) (#21373)

## Pull request overview

This PR updates Streamlit’s public-facing docstrings for the 1.55.0 release, primarily to standardize Markdown/label behavior descriptions, unify `key` and query-parameter binding documentation across widgets/elements, and expand docs for dynamic containers (`st.tabs`, `st.expander`, `st.popover`) including their `.open` state.

**Changes:**
- Expanded and standardized Markdown support descriptions (including custom CSS colors in `st.markdown`, and label auto-escaping behavior).
- Unified/expanded `key` and `bind="query-params"` documentation across multiple widgets and chart elements.
- Added/expanded docstrings and examples for dynamic container return types (`TabContainer`, `ExpanderContainer`, `PopoverContainer`) and stateful lazy execution via `.open`.

title: [fix] Remove erroneous expander block ID and align on_change defaults
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes two issues in layout container state persistence:

- Removes erroneous `block_proto.id = element_id` assignment in `st.expander` — the ID should only live on `expandable_proto.id`, matching the pattern used by `st.tabs` and `st.popover`
- Aligns `on_change` default to `"ignore"` across all three stateful layout containers. `st.tabs` previously used `None` as its default and accepted `None` as a value; now it uses `"ignore"` consistent with `st.expander`, `st.popover`, and the [product spec](../specs/2026-01-14-dynamic-tabs-expander/product-spec.md#new-parameter-on_change)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
- Updated existing tests to match corrected behavior (block-level ID not set on expander, `on_change=None` now raises on tabs).


**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes layout container state persistence by correcting where element IDs are stored for `st.expander` and standardizing `on_change` defaults for stateful layout containers.

**Changes:**
- Remove expander `add_block.id` assignment so the element ID lives only in `add_block.expandable.id`.
- Change `st.tabs` `on_change` default from `None` to `"ignore"` and disallow `None` as an input value.
- Update unit tests to reflect the corrected ID behavior and the new `on_change=None` error behavior for tabs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/layouts.py` | Align `tabs(on_change)` default to `"ignore"`, tighten validation, and stop setting block-level IDs for expanders. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py` | Update expander ID assertions and change the tabs test to expect an exception for `on_change=None`. |
LGTM 👍

title: [docs] AI-assisted documentation updates
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to update internal documentation based on AI review.

This PR was created by the `ai-update-docs` workflow using `opus-4.6-thinking`.

## Changes

# Documentation Review Summary

Reviewed all internal documentation files against the current codebase state.

**Scope**: 10 AGENTS.md files, 11 SKILL.md files, 3 subagent definitions, 4 wiki pages, CONTRIBUTING.md, and 10 README files.

## Issues Found and Fixed

### 1. [INCORRECT] AGENTS.md — `frontend-format` tool description

- **File**: `AGENTS.md` (line 61)
- **Issue**: `frontend-format` described as using "(eslint)" but `make frontend-format` runs `yarn format`, which invokes **prettier** in each workspace.
- **Fix**: Changed "(eslint)" to "(prettier)".

### 2. [INCORRECT] frontend/AGENTS.md — Same `frontend-format` error

- **File**: `frontend/AGENTS.md` (line 134)
- **Issue**: Same incorrect "(eslint)" description for `make frontend-format`.
- **Fix**: Changed "(eslint)" to "(prettier)".

### 3. [INCORRECT] .claude/skills/finalizing-pr/SKILL.md — Duplicate step numbering

- **File**: `.claude/skills/finalizing-pr/SKILL.md`
- **Issue**: Two steps numbered "### 10." (Fix CI issues / Post agent metrics), followed by "### 11." (Trigger final AI review). Steps should be sequential 10, 11, 12.
- **Fix**: Renumbered "Post agent metrics" to step 11 and "Trigger final AI review" to step 12.

### 4. [OUTDATED] CONTRIBUTING.md — Removed obsolete submodule instruction

- **File**: `CONTRIBUTING.md` (line 166)
- **Issue**: Included `git submodule update --init` in the clone instructions, but the repository has no `.gitmodules` file and no submodules.
- **Fix**: Removed the `git submodule update --init` line.

## Files Modified

```
AGENTS.md                                  | 1 line changed (eslint -> prettier)
frontend/AGENTS.md                         | 1 line changed (eslint -> prettier)
.claude/skills/finalizing-pr/SKILL.md      | 2 lines changed (step renumbering)
CONTRIBUTING.md                            | 1 line removed (submodule instruction)
```

## Verified As Correct (No Changes Needed)

- All make commands in AGENTS.md match `make help` output
- All folder paths in AGENTS.md exist in the codebase
- All workflow files in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` match actual `.yml` files (41/41)
- All composite actions in `.github/actions/` match documentation (5/5)
- All skills in CONTRIBUTING.md match `.claude/skills/*/` (11/11)
- All subagents in CONTRIBUTING.md match `.claude/agents/` (3/3)
- Tool versions match config files (TypeScript v5, React v18, Vite v7, Vitest v4, ESLint v9, Prettier v3, Yarn v4, Ruff 0.x, mypy 1.x, pytest 9.x, Python 3.10-3.14)
- All file paths referenced in architecture docs exist
- Wiki pages and README files are up to date
- Skill cross-references use current names

---
*This is an automated documentation update by `opus-4.6-thinking`.*

title: [chore] Add basic agent metrics logging for skill/subagent invocations
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds infrastructure to track skill and subagent invocations for development analytics:

- `scripts/log_agent_metrics.py` - NDJSON logger that records invocations by branch
- `SubagentStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` for subagent tracking
- `PreToolUse` hooks for skill invocations in `.claude/settings.json` 
## Testing Plan

- [x] Manual testing - verified hooks fire and metrics are logged correctly
- [ ] No automated tests (internal developer tooling)

<!-- agent-metrics-start -->
<details>
<summary>Agent metrics</summary>

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| skill | checking-changes | 1 |
| skill | discovering-make-commands | 1 |
| skill | finalizing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 2 |
| subagent | reviewing-local-changes | 1 |
| subagent | simplifying-local-changes | 1 |

</details>
<!-- agent-metrics-end -->
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body: Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues. [Bugbot Autofix](https://cursor.com/docs/bugbot#autofix) prepared fixes for both issues found in the latest run. - ✅ Fixed: **Redundant subprocess calls for git branch detection** - Called _get_git_branch() once in main() and passed the result to both _get_log_path() and the entry dict, eliminating the redundant subprocess call and potential inconsistency. - ✅ Fixed: **Metrics hook missing from discovering-make-commands skill** - Added the PreToolUse metrics logging hook to discovering-make-commands/SKILL.md frontmatter, matching the pattern used in all other skills.

## Pull request overview

Adds internal developer-tooling to log Claude skill and subagent invocations into per-branch NDJSON files, and wires up the necessary hooks/templates so invocations are tracked consistently across the repo.

**Changes:**
- Added `scripts/log_agent_metrics.py` to append invocation events, print stats, and optionally post aggregated stats to the PR body.
- Added a `SubagentStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` to track subagent invocations.
- Added `PreToolUse` (with `once: true`) logging hooks to skill templates and existing skills, and updated the synced documentation/rules.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated 11 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `scripts/log_agent_metrics.py` | Implements NDJSON logging, stats aggregation, and PR-body posting. |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Adds a `SubagentStart` command hook to log subagent invocations. |
| `.claude/skills/AGENTS.md` | Updates the canonical skill format docs to require invocation logging hooks. |
| `.github/instructions/skills.instructions.md` | Synced generated instructions reflecting the new hook requirement. |
| `.cursor/rules/skills.mdc` | Synced generated Cursor rules reflecting the new hook requirement. |
| `.claude/skills/implementing-new-features/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` invocation logging hook. |
| `.claude/skills/fixing-streamlit-ci/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` invocation logging hook. |
| `.claude/skills/finalizing-pr/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` logging hook and adds a step to post metrics to the PR. |
| `.claude/skills/debugging-streamlit/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` invocation logging hook. |
| `.claude/skills/creating-pull-requests/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` invocation logging hook. |
| `.claude/skills/checking-changes/SKILL.md` | Adds a `PreToolUse` invocation logging hook. |
| `.claude/skills/add

commits.message: Add agent metrics logging for skill/subagent invocations

Refactor skill metrics logging to use centralized hook

Move from per-skill hooks to a single PreToolUse hook in
settings.json for cleaner, DRY implementation. Extract skill
name from payload when not provided explicitly.

fix: use uv run python for hook script invocation

Aligns with repo convention of using `uv run python scripts/...`
instead of relying on shebang/executable permissions.

chore: remove unused log_agent_metrics.py script

The script was replaced by centralized hook configuration in the
previous refactor.

Revert "chore: remove unused log_agent_metrics.py script"

This reverts commit df92eebd23ce4ed0859bdbdaa156eeaccb9c1350.

Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/skill-hook-log

Resolved conflict in finalizing-pr/SKILL.md by keeping the agent
metrics step.

refactor: simplify subagent source tag check

Remove unused _SUBAGENT_SOURCE_TAGS frozenset and use direct string
comparison since we control the hook configuration.

body:
## Describe your changes

Adds support for category shortcuts and MIME types in the `type` parameter for `st.file_uploader` and the `file_type` parameter for `st.chat_input`:

- **Category shortcuts**: `"image"`, `"audio"`, `"video"`, `"text"` (expand to MIME wildcards like `"image/*"`)
- **MIME types**: `"image/jpeg"`, `"application/pdf"` (passed through as-is)
- **MIME wildcards**: `"image/*"`, `"audio/*"` (passed through as-is)
- **Extensions**: `".jpg"`, `"pdf"` (existing behavior preserved with automatic pairing)

### Changes

**Backend (`lib/streamlit/elements/lib/file_uploader_utils.py`):**
- Add `CATEGORY_SHORTCUTS` constant and `classify_file_type()` function
- Update `normalize_upload_file_type()` to handle shortcuts and MIME types
- Update `enforce_filename_restriction()` to skip validation for MIME-only types (trusts browser filtering)

**Frontend (`frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/`):**
- Update `FileUploader/utils.ts` to handle MIME types as direct keys in react-dropzone
- Update `ChatInput/fileUpload/fileUploadUtils.ts` to validate MIME types with wildcard support
- Update `FileDropzoneInstructions.tsx` to display category names (e.g., "image" instead of "image/*")
- Update `ChatFileUploadButton.tsx` to show file types in upload button tooltip

**Tests:**
- Comprehensive backend unit tests for all type formats
- Frontend unit tests for MIME type handling
- E2E test verifying file type shortcuts display correctly

- [Product spec](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/529a4569802e22c1acc3d16879ee36493c6cc7a0/specs/2026-02-26-file-type-shortcuts/product-spec.md)

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit tests (Python and TypeScript)
- [x] E2E test (`test_file_uploader_type_shortcuts`)
- [x] Manual testing via `make debug`
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds support for file-type *shortcuts* and *MIME-based* specifiers for uploads, extending Streamlit’s existing extension-only behavior for both `st.file_uploader` and `st.chat_input` (file attachments). This aligns accepted types with HTML `<input accept>` semantics while keeping legacy extension normalization/pairing.

**Changes:**
- Backend: classify/normalize type specifiers (shortcuts → MIME wildcards, MIME passthrough, extensions normalized + paired) and skip backend validation when only MIME-based types are provided.
- Frontend: update `react-dropzone` accept mapping and chat file validation to support MIME types/wildcards; update UI text/tooltip formatting to display categories like `image` instead of `image/*`.
- Tests: add/extend Python + TS unit tests and add an e2e test for shortcut display behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| ---- | ----------- |
| `specs/2026-02-26-file-type-shortcuts/product-spec.md` | Product spec describing shortcuts/MIME support and expected behavior. |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/lib/file_uploader_utils.py` | Adds classification/normalization for shortcuts + MIME types; adjusts backend filename enforcement behavior. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/file_uploader_utils_test.py` | Adds unit tests covering new type formats and backend enforcement behavior with MIME-only lists. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/utils.ts` | Updates Dropzone `accept` config builder to support MIME keys directly. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/utils.test.ts` | Adds unit tests for MIME/wildcard handling in `getAccept` and `isMimeType`. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/FileUploader/FileDropzoneInstructions.tsx` | Formats display text for MIME wildcards/types and extensions. |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/ChatI

commits.message:
[feature] Add file type shortcuts and MIME type support for st.file_uploader

Extend the `type` parameter in `st.file_uploader` and `file_type` in
`st.chat_input` to accept category shortcuts (`"image"`, `"audio"`,
`"video"`, `"text"`), full MIME types (`"image/jpeg"`, `"application/pdf"`),
and MIME type wildcards (`"image/*"`, `"audio/*"`).

This aligns with the HTML `<input accept>` attribute while providing
convenient shortcuts for common use cases.

Changes:
- Backend: Update normalize_upload_file_type() to handle shortcuts, MIME
  types, and wildcards; skip backend validation for MIME-only types
- Frontend: Update getAccept() to pass MIME types to react-dropzone;
  update isFileTypeAllowed() for MIME type matching
- UI: Display category names instead of extensions in dropzone instructions

Add chat input file type tooltip and E2E test for file type shortcuts

- Update ChatFileUploadButton to display supported file types in tooltip
- Add formatTypeForDisplay helper for consistent type formatting
- Add E2E test verifying file type shortcuts display correctly in file uploader

Remove product spec from branch

The spec is not needed for this feature PR.

Fix E2E test for chat input file upload tooltip

Update expected tooltip text to include file type (TXT) since the
single_file chat input now has file_type="txt" configured.

Address PR review comments: extract shared utility and improve whitespace handling

- Extract formatTypeForDisplay to shared FileHelper utility to eliminate
  code duplication between ChatFileUploadButton and FileDropzoneInstructions
- Add whitespace handling to classify_file_type in Python backend
- Add unit tests for new shared utility and whitespace handling

Fix MIME type handling for directory uploads and mixed type validation

Bug fixes:
- Fix directory upload filtering to support MIME types (was using extension-only matching)
- Fix backend validation to skip when MIME types are present in mixed types
  (prevents false rejections for files matching MIME patterns)

Improvements:
- Move shared isFileTypeAllowed() to FileHelper.ts for reuse
- Rename acceptedExtensions prop to acceptedTypes (now carries MIME types too)
- Add comprehensive tests for MIME type matching and mixed type scenarios

Address review feedback: fix isMimeType duplication and multi-part extensions

- Import isMimeType from FileHelper.ts instead of redefining in utils.ts
- Fix multi-part extension matching (e.g., .tar.gz) by using endsWith()
- Add tests for multi-part extensions (.tar.gz, .csv.gz)
- Add MIME type rendering tests for FileDropzoneInstructions

Remove re-exports and add formatTypesForDisplay helper

Consolidate utility functions in FileHelper.ts by removing backwards
compatibility re-exports and adding a formatTypesForDisplay helper for
cleaner array formatting.
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/file-type-shortcuts

title: [chore] Add e2e test count comparison and PR comment
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a `check-e2e-test-count` job to the playwright workflow that compares E2E test counts between PRs and develop, posting a comment when changes exceed the threshold.

- Compares test stats artifact from PR against latest successful develop run
- Posts PR comment when test count change exceeds threshold (30 tests)
- Updates existing comments when changes become insignificant
- Handles fork PRs by logging instead of commenting (insufficient permissions)
- Uses `continue-on-error: true` to avoid blocking PRs

Also updates `actions/upload-artifact` from v6 to v7.

## Testing Plan

- [ ] Manual: Workflow changes will be validated by CI on this PR

commits.message:
[ci] Add E2E test count comparison and PR comment

Add a new `check-e2e-test-count` job to the playwright workflow that:

- Downloads test statistics from the current PR and latest develop run
- Compares total test counts between the PR and develop branch
- Posts a PR comment when test count changes exceed the threshold (30)
- Updates existing comments when the change becomes insignificant
- Handles fork PRs gracefully by logging instead of commenting
- Uses `continue-on-error: true` to avoid blocking PRs on comparison failures

Also includes minor improvements to the existing workflow:
- Update actions/upload-artifact from v6 to v7
- Add step to upload test-stats.json artifact for comparison
- Use `uv run pytest` for consistency with other workflows

fix: handle zero test count change case in PR comment

When test count is exactly equal to develop, show "no change"
instead of misleadingly saying "removed 0 test cases".
chore: update actions/download-artifact to v8

fix: add continue-on-error for downloading current test stats

This makes the download step consistent with the develop stats download
step and prevents confusing failed job status when the artifact is
missing (e.g., if playwright-e2e-tests job failed before uploading).

title: fix(cache): preserve on_release behavior in TTLCleanupCache.clear()
body:
## Describe your changes

cachetools 7.0.2 changed TTLCache.clear() to an O(1) implementation that bypasses `popitem()`/`expire()`. That skipped `TTLCleanupCache` release hooks and broke resource cleanup paths that expect on_release to run for each entry.

Override `TTLCleanupCache.clear()` to evict entries via `popitem()`, preserving release semantics across `cachetools` versions. Update related inline comments in `cache_resource_api` to document the callback/exception behavior and why per-item cleanup is still used.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

## Testing Plan

- [x] Covered by existing tests

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR restores resource cleanup semantics for Streamlit’s TTL-based cache by ensuring `TTLCleanupCache.clear()` triggers `on_release` for each entry even after `cachetools` changed `TTLCache.clear()` internals.

**Changes:**
- Override `TTLCleanupCache.clear()` to evict entries via `popitem()` so per-entry release hooks run.
- Update `cache_resource_api` inline comments to clarify exception and cleanup behavior during cache clearing.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/runtime/caching/ttl_cleanup_cache.py` | Adds a `clear()` override to preserve per-entry `on_release` behavior across `cachetools` versions. |
| `lib/streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_resource_api.py` | Refines comments explaining why cache entries are cleared individually and how callback exceptions are handled/logged. |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
LGTM 👍 One potential issue to double check

commits.message:
fix(cache): preserve on_release behavior in TTLCleanupCache.clear()

cachetools 7.0.2 changed TTLCache.clear() to an O(1) implementation that
bypasses popitem()/expire(). That skipped TTLCleanupCache release hooks and
broke resource cleanup paths that expect on_release to run for each entry.

Override TTLCleanupCache.clear() to evict entries via popitem(), preserving
release semantics across cachetools versions.

title: feat(skills): add external-test risk assessment and review integration
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR adds a new skill `assessing-external-test-risk` to help determine when changes require external end-to-end test coverage using `@pytest.mark.external_test`. The skill evaluates 11 risk categories including routing, authentication, websockets, embedding, asset serving, and cross-origin behavior to recommend whether external testing is needed for externally hosted or embedded Streamlit scenarios.

The `reviewing-local-changes` agent now incorporates this skill into its workflow, requiring an explicit external test risk assessment and recommendation in all code reviews.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

N/A

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- **Explanation of why no additional tests are needed**: This change adds tooling for the development workflow rather than runtime functionality. The skill provides structured guidance for risk assessment during code review.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-bnisco

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a new agent skill `assessing-external-test-risk` that provides a structured 11-category risk checklist to determine whether code changes require `@pytest.mark.external_test` E2E coverage for externally hosted or embedded Streamlit deployments. The skill is integrated into the `reviewing-local-changes` subagent and the shared code-review instructions so that every code review now includes a mandatory external test risk assessment section.

**Changes:**
- New skill `assessing-external-test-risk` with YAML frontmatter, an any-hit decision policy, 11 risk categories, a structured output template, and concrete examples.
- `reviewing-local-changes` agent updated to declare the new skill as a dependency and adds an explicit step 5 to run the assessment, plus a corresponding `## External test recommendation` section in the output format.
- Supporting files (`CONTRIBUTING.md`, `.claude/.gitignore`, `scripts/assets/code-review-instructions.md`) updated to register and document the new skill.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| `.claude/agents/reviewing-local-changes.md` | Registers the new skill, adds workflow step 5 to run it, and adds the `## External test recommendation` output section |

LGTM 👍 Maybe you could add a note in the first section that gives slighlty more context on why all of this is need: e.g. something like: we want to support deployment platforms that allow secure, production-ready, and scalable deployments of Streamlit apps involving web proxies, embedded/iframed setups, CSP rules, security measures, and more.

I think its a bit covered in the description, but might be nice to clarify this a bit more in the body itself.

This PR refactors the widget state duplication warning from a UI-displayed warning (`st.warning()`) to a console-logged warning (`_LOGGER.warning()`), reducing visual clutter in apps while still alerting developers to potential configuration issues. The change adds `stack_info=True` for better debugging capabilities, helping developers trace where the warning originates.

**Changes:**
- Changed warning output method from UI display to console logging for widget state duplication
- Added stack trace information to the warning for improved debugging
- Updated all related tests to mock and verify the logger instead of the UI warning function

| lib/streamlit/config.py | Updated documentation to reflect that the warning is now logged to console rather than displayed in the UI |

Add AI documentation update skill and workflow

Add a Claude Code skill (`updating-internal-docs`) for reviewing and updating
internal documentation, and a GitHub Actions workflow (`ai-update-docs.yml`)
to automate weekly documentation reviews using Cursor CLI.

Address PR review feedback

- Use unique heredoc delimiter to prevent parsing issues if AI-generated
  content contains "EOF"

Fix changes.md leak and add shell defaults

- Remove changes.md after copying to /tmp to prevent it from being
  included in the patch and committed to the PR
- Add defaults: run: shell: bash to both jobs for consistency with
  other AI workflows (ai-pr-review.yml, ai-issue-triage.yml)

- skills/creating-pull-requests/ (from develop)
- skills/updating-internal-docs/ (from this branch)

Add CLI snippets for:
- Checking folder path existence (ls -d, test -d, batch verification)

Simplify verification commands to single-line examples
Add internal docs step to finalizing-pr and improve updating-internal-docs skill
Simplify finalizing-pr docs step and change workflow to Tuesdays

Add PR-scoped mode to ai-update-docs workflow

Allow running documentation updates scoped to a specific PR by providing
a pr_number input. In this mode, the workflow:
- Checks out the PR's head branch
- Only fixes docs related to PR changes
- Creates the docs PR targeting the PR's head branch instead of develop

This enables running targeted doc updates as part of PR finalization.
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/docs-update-skill

fix: use base_branch instead of head_branch for docs update PR target

When creating a docs update PR for a specific PR, the target branch should
be the original PR's base branch (e.g., develop), not the head branch.

title: [fix] Allow running streamlit with python optimization
body:
## Describe your changes

Falls back to empty string for config option descriptions when docstrings are stripped (`PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2`) instead of raising a `RuntimeError`.

Fixes #14155

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (Python)
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/config_option_test.py` — Tests missing docstring fallback
  - `lib/tests/streamlit/config_test.py` — Tests complex config option without docstring
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a runtime error when Streamlit is run with `PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2` / `python -OO` by making complex config options tolerate missing function docstrings (which are stripped under `-OO`) and default their descriptions to an empty string.

**Changes:**
- Update `ConfigOption.__call__` to fall back to `""` when the decorated function has no docstring.
- Replace prior tests that expected a `RuntimeError` with assertions that the description becomes an empty string.
- Add coverage for both direct `ConfigOption(key)` usage and `@c` decorator usage when docstrings are missing.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/config_option.py | Makes complex config option descriptions resilient to docstring stripping by defaulting to `""`. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/config_option_test.py | Updates unit tests to validate the missing-docstring fallback behavior. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/config_test.py | Updates config system tests to validate complex option behavior when docstrings are absent. |

commits.message:
Handle PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2 stripping docstrings

Fall back to empty string for config option descriptions when
docstrings are stripped (PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2) instead of crashing.

Fixes #14155

title: [feat] Add `on_click` rerun support to `st.link_button`
body:
## Describe your changes

- Update `st.link_button` so `on_click=\"rerun\"` and callable `on_click` return a `bool` (`False` by default, `True` after click), matching `st.button` trigger semantics.
- Keep `on_click=\"ignore\"` returning `DeltaGenerator`, and add API overloads + doc updates to make return types explicit.
- Add/expand unit, typing, and e2e coverage for callback/rerun return values and keyed CSS class behavior.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/7453

## Testing Plan

- [x] `make check`
- [x] `uv run pytest lib/tests/streamlit/elements/button_test.py -k link_button`
- [x] `make run-e2e-test e2e_playwright/st_link_button_test.py::test_link_button_click_calls_callback`
- [x] `make run-e2e-test e2e_playwright/st_link_button_test.py::test_link_button_click_returns_true_for_rerun`
- [x] `make run-e2e-test e2e_playwright/st_link_button_test.py::test_custom_css_class_via_key`
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

## Pull request overview

This PR adds `on_click`, `args`, and `kwargs` support to `st.link_button`, enabling callback execution and click-triggered reruns while maintaining backward compatibility with the default `on_click="ignore"` behavior. The changes bring link_button closer to the feature parity of other button widgets like `st.button` and `st.download_button`.

**Changes:**
- Adds `on_click`, `args`, `kwargs`, and `key` parameters to `st.link_button` with three modes: "ignore" (default, no rerun), "rerun" (triggers rerun without callback), and callable (triggers rerun with callback)
- Implements widget registration and state management for non-ignore modes while preserving the lightweight behavior of ignore mode
- Ensures key-based identity stability across all modes and validates keys even in ignore mode for consistency

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |

| `lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/button.py` | Implements on_click/args/kwargs support in link_button with conditional widget registration, key validation, and icon_position fix in ID computation |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/link_button_test.py` | Adds tests for key validation in ignore mode, rerun enablement for callback/rerun modes, and ID generation |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/button_test.py` | Validates stable key-based identity across rerun and ignore modes with varying parameters |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/button_types.py` | Adds type assertions for new parameters (key, on_click, args, kwargs) covering all usage patterns |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/LinkButton/LinkButton.tsx` | Implements click handler that conditionally calls `setTriggerValue` based o

commits.message:
Add on_click support to st.link_button.

This adds rerun and callback handling for link buttons while keeping the default ignore behavior, and wires frontend trigger handling plus tests for key/on_click semantics.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Add disabled rerun test for link button

This covers the disabled + rerun-enabled path to ensure disabled link buttons never trigger widget reruns.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Refine link_button on_click internals and tests.

Keep behavior unchanged while simplifying link-button code paths, and enforce key validation in ignore mode to match widget policy expectations.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Stabilize link_button rerun tests with explicit WidgetStateManager spy.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Clarify link_button callback args docs

Document that link_button args and kwargs are only used when on_click is callable, and are ignored for rerun/ignore modes.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Add e2e test case

Return boolean for link_button rerun and callback modes.

Align link_button with button-like trigger semantics for non-ignore on_click paths, and add unit, typing, and e2e coverage for return values and keyed CSS class behavior.

Made-with: Cursor

title: Add `understanding-streamlit-architecture` skill
body:
## Describe your changes

- Add new Claude Code skill `understanding-streamlit-architecture` for explaining Streamlit's internal architecture
- Covers high-level concepts to understand the backend, frontend, and communication. 
- Most of these are core concepts that are not expected to change anytime soon, but if changes happen we will soon have a skill / workflow to keep it up-dated: https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/14001

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A

## Testing Plan

- [x] Documentation-only change, no code tests needed
- [x] Verified skill appears in Claude Code skill list
- [x] Validated all file paths and component references are accurate
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds comprehensive architecture documentation for Streamlit as a Claude Code skill. The new `understanding-streamlit-architecture` skill provides detailed documentation about Streamlit's internal architecture including the Python backend runtime, TypeScript/React frontend rendering system, and Protocol Buffer-based WebSocket communication protocol. This documentation is intended to help AI coding agents understand how Streamlit works end-to-end when debugging cross-layer issues, planning architectural changes, or onboarding to the codebase.

**Changes:**
- Added new Claude skill `understanding-streamlit-architecture` with detailed architectural documentation
- Created four markdown files covering backend runtime, frontend rendering, communication protocol, and overall architecture
- Updated `.claude/.gitignore` to whitelist the new skill directory

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/SKILL.md` | Main skill entry point with overview, core concepts, and quick reference guide |
| `.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/backend.md` | Deep dive into Python backend components (Runtime, AppSession, ScriptRunner, SessionState) |
| `.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/frontend.md` | Detailed frontend architecture covering App.tsx, AppRoot tree, rendering pipeline, and WidgetStateManager |
| `.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/communication.md` | Protocol Buffer communication layer documentation including ForwardMsg/BackMsg and message flow |
| `.claude/.gitignore` | Whitelisted new skill directory for version control |
</details>

✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

LGTM, let's try to get data so we can test what works best. Let's also keep in mind what works best for humans too -- less important at early stages, but it will be nice to have a tidy organization to navigate.

commits.message:
[docs] Add understanding-streamlit-architecture skill

Add a new Claude Code skill that explains Streamlit's internal architecture
for onboarding and debugging cross-layer issues. Includes detailed
documentation on backend runtime, frontend rendering, and WebSocket
communication via Protocol Buffers.

Update mixin count to ~53 for precision

Address Copilot review comments about being more precise
with the DeltaGenerator mixin count.
Expand ForwardMsg types list and clarify it's not exhaustive
Clarify BlockProto contains layout containers
Trim ForwardMsg list to most essential types
Fix TransientNode description - only spinners are transient

[docs] Simplify architecture skill docs to reduce maintenance burden

Remove line counts and overly specific code blocks that could become
outdated. Keep high-level explanations with references to source files.

[docs] Expand architecture skill with glossary, fragments, and host integration

Add comprehensive documentation for:
- Concepts glossary covering all major Streamlit abstractions
- Execution model details (rerun triggers, callbacks, session isolation)
- Fragment system (@st.fragment) with sequence diagrams
- Session reconnection flow and TTL behavior
- Media file management and garbage collection
- Custom components v1/v2 architecture
- Host integration via postMessage protocol
- ForwardMsg metadata semantics for MPA/fragments

Add Rerun concept to architecture glossary

Explains what a rerun is (script re-execution for current page),
what triggers it, and which files are involved.

[docs] Simplify architecture skill by linking to proto sources

- Replace inline proto excerpts with links to actual proto files
- Categorize widget value types more clearly
- Remove debug paths table (redundant with existing skill docs)
- Clarify host config as tamper-proof host platform mechanism

[docs] Simplify PostMessage protocol docs, link to source

List only essential message types and point to types.ts for the
complete definitions of all 20+ host/guest message types.

[docs] Remove widget value types section (rarely needed)

Adding new widget state types is uncommon; refer to WidgetStates.proto
directly when needed.
Refine architecture skill glossary and doc structure
Update architecture skill glossary and host-config wording
Fix grammar in architecture skill quick reference

Expand Runtime section in architecture skill docs

Replace the state machine diagram with more actionable content:
- RuntimeConfig constructor options
- AsyncObjects coordination primitives
- Main loop behavior explanation
- Thread safety guidelines
- Script validation purpose
Remove 'Adding new widget state type' section (rarely needed)
Simplify Runtime section to essential concepts
Link architecture skill from debugging skill
Add maintenance note to architecture skill
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/architecture-skills

Move architecture skill reference docs to references/ folder

Reorganize understanding-streamlit-architecture skill by moving
backend.md, frontend.md, and communication.md into a references/
subfolder and updating all links in SKILL.md.
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/architecture-skills

Update skills documentation and gitignore

- Remove redundant gitignore allowlist for architecture skill subdirectory
- Fix link text in debugging skill's related skills section
- Add understanding-streamlit-architecture skill to CONTRIBUTING.md table

Restore gitignore allowlist and clarify component endpoint

- Re-add !skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/** to allowlist
- Clarify /component/* serves v1 component resources in backend.md
Remove unnecessary gitignore pattern for architecture skill
Add 'and more' to architecture skill description

title: [feat] Allow specifying number of visible items via `expanded` in `st.navigation`
body:
## Describe your changes

- Add support for passing a positive integer to the expanded parameter in st.navigation() to specify exactly how many navigation items should be visible when the sidebar is collapsed
- expanded=True shows all pages, expanded=False/expanded=0 uses the default collapse threshold, and expanded=N (positive int) shows exactly N items
- Add visible_items field to Navigation protobuf and sidebarNavVisibleItems to frontend context

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9646

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds support for passing a positive integer to `expanded` in `st.navigation()` to control how many navigation items are shown when the sidebar navigation is collapsed, wiring the value through protobuf to the frontend sidebar navigation rendering.

**Changes:**
- Extend the `Navigation` protobuf with an optional `visible_items` field to carry the collapsed-visible-items setting.
- Update Python `st.navigation()` to accept `expanded: bool | int` and populate `expanded` vs `visible_items` in the outbound navigation message.
- Plumb the new value through frontend navigation state/context and update `SidebarNav` collapse logic + tests to honor a custom visible-items count.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Navigation.proto | Adds optional `visible_items` to the `Navigation` message. |
| lib/streamlit/commands/navigation.py | Accepts `expanded` as `bool | int` and sets `expanded` / `visible_items` on the navigation proto accordingly. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/commands/navigation_test.py | Adds Python unit tests asserting correct proto fields for `expanded=True`, `expanded=5`, and `expanded=0`. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/SidebarConfigContext.tsx | Extends sidebar config context with `sidebarNavVisibleItems`. |
| frontend/app/src/util/AppNavigation.ts | Maps `navigationMsg.visibleItems` into app state as `sidebarNavVisibleItems`. |
| frontend/app/src/util/AppNavigation.test.ts | Updates expected navigation state objects to include `sidebarNavVisibleItems`. |
| frontend/app/src/components/StreamlitContextProvider.tsx | Threads `sidebarNavVisibleItems` into `SidebarConfigContext` provider values. |
| frontend/app/src/components/Navigation/SidebarNav.tsx | Uses `sidebarNavVisibleItems` (or default) to compute collapse threshold and

commits.message:
[feat] Allow integer value for `expanded` in `st.navigation()` to control visible items

Add support for passing a positive integer to the `expanded` parameter in
`st.navigation()` to specify exactly how many navigation items should be
visible when the sidebar navigation is collapsed. This provides finer-grained
control over the navigation collapse behavior.

- `expanded=True`: Show all pages (existing behavior)
- `expanded=False` or `expanded=0`: Use default collapse threshold (existing behavior)
- `expanded=N` (positive int): Show exactly N items when collapsed

Address PR review comments

- Add validation for invalid expanded values (negative int, wrong type)
- Add typing test for expanded=int
- Add AppNavigation test for visibleItems state mapping
- Add Python tests for negative and invalid type errors

Address PR review comments: add E2E tests and remove redundant type annotation

- Added E2E tests for `st.navigation(pages, expanded=N)` to verify
  the collapse behavior with a custom visible items count
- Removed redundant `: number` type annotation per frontend AGENTS.md
  guideline ("omit trivially inferred types")

Use generic form of reduce instead of type assertion

Address PR review comment from @sfc-gh-bnisco: use the generic form
of reduce<Record<string, boolean>>() instead of casting with `as`.

body:

Release notes

Sourced from fast-xml-parser's releases.

Summary update on all the previous releases from v4.2.4

  • Multiple minor fixes provided in the validator and parser
  • v6 is added for experimental use.
  • ignoreAttributes support function, and array of string or regex
  • Add support for parsing HTML numeric entities
  • v5 of the application is ESM module now. However, JS is also supported

Note: Release section in not updated frequently. Please check CHANGELOG or Tags for latest release information.

title: Bump actions/download-artifact from 7 to 8
body:
Bumps [actions/download-artifact](https://github.com/actions/download-artifact) from 7 to 8.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/download-artifact's releases.

v8.0.0

v8 - What's new

Direct downloads

To support direct uploads in actions/upload-artifact, the action will no longer attempt to unzip all downloaded files. Instead, the action checks the Content-Type header ahead of unzipping and skips non-zipped files. Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter to false.

Enforced checks (breaking)

A previous release introduced digest checks on the download. If a download hash didn't match the expected hash from the server, the action would log a warning. Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter. To be secure by default, we are now defaulting the behavior to error which will fail the workflow run.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Don't attempt to un-zip non-zipped downloads by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#460
  • Add a setting to specify what to do on hash mismatch and default it to error by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#461

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/compare/v7...v8.0.0

title: Bump actions/upload-artifact from 6 to 7
body:
Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 6 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

title: Format date/time slider URL query params as ISO strings
body:
## Describe your changes
- Date/time/datetime sliders with bind="query-params" now format URL values as human-readable ISO strings (e.g., `?date=2024-06-15`, `?time=14:30`, `?dt=2024-06-15T14:30`) instead of raw microsecond timestamps (e.g., ?`date=1718409600000000`).
- The conversion is localized to the URL boundary: the frontend converts micros → ISO when writing to the URL, and the backend parses ISO → micros when reading from the URL. The internal wire format (double_array_value with microsecond floats) is unchanged.
- Old bookmarked URLs with raw microsecond values continue to work (backward compatible).

## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests: ✅ Added
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request enhances the user experience of date/time/datetime sliders with `bind="query-params"` by formatting URL query parameters as human-readable ISO strings instead of raw microsecond timestamps.

**Changes:**
- Date sliders now display `?date=2024-06-15` instead of `?date=1718409600000000`
- Time sliders format as `?time=14:30` and datetime as `?datetime=2024-06-15T14:30`
- Backend parses ISO strings when reading from URLs; frontend formats to ISO when writing to URLs
- Wire protocol remains unchanged (still uses microsecond floats internally)
- Old bookmarked URLs with raw microsecond values continue to work (backward compatible)

title: Fix spinner clear_transient race condition during rapid reruns
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixes a frontend stale-node edge case where the first widget above a `@st.fragment` could disappear and be replaced by `div.stEmpty` after a cache miss.

**Root cause:** During stale-node cleanup, a `TransientNode` that had already been explicitly cleared in the current run (`transientNodes.length === 0`) could still hold a valid anchor element from a previous run. The existing stale filtering path treated that anchor as stale and removed it.

**Fix:** In `ClearStaleNodeVisitor.visitTransientNode`, return the anchor directly when the transient was cleared in the current run and has no transient children. This preserves the anchored widget instead of incorrectly dropping it.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes #13634

## Testing Plan

- [x] Frontend unit test added in `ClearStaleNodeVisitor.test.ts`: `restores anchor for transient cleared in current run`
- [x] Targeted Vitest run:
  - `yarn vitest run lib/src/render-tree/visitors/ClearStaleNodeVisitor.test.ts -t "restores anchor for transient cleared in current run"`
- [x] Manual reproduction validated in debug session: widget no longer disappears

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: kmcgrady

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a race condition in `st.spinner` where `clear_transient` could be sent even when no transient was ever created, which could lead to stale-anchor behavior and incorrect element removal during rapid reruns.

**Changes:**
- Track whether `create_transient()` actually ran and only enqueue `clear_transient()` when a transient was created.
- Add a regression unit test to ensure no transient-related messages are sent when the spinner exits before its delay timer fires.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/spinner.py` | Adds `transient_created` tracking so `clear_transient()` is only sent when needed, preventing unnecessary transient node creation. |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/spinner_test.py` | Adds a regression test asserting that fast-exiting spinners do not enqueue transient messages. |
LGTM 👍

title: [chore] Wire up check_widget_policies correctly in dynamic containers
body:
## Describe your changes

The partial change that landed with callable `on_change` support used `callable(on_change)` inline in the `check_widget_policies` call for all three dynamic containers, but without the `is_callback` intermediate variable or `cast`. This aligns all three with the established pattern from `vega_charts.py`:

- Extracts `is_callback = callable(on_change)` as a named variable for clarity
- Passes `on_change=cast("WidgetCallback", on_change) if is_callback else None` for type correctness
- `enable_check_callback_rules=is_callback` (functionally equivalent, but consistent with the pattern)

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

N/A

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Surfaced in review of #13910.

## Testing Plan

- [x] Python unit tests — added 6 regression tests to `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py`, covering all three containers:
  - `test_callable_on_change_inside_form_raises` — verifies a callable `on_change` inside `st.form` raises `StreamlitInvalidFormCallbackError` (expander, tabs, popover)
  - `test_on_change_rerun_inside_form_does_not_raise` — verifies `on_change="rerun"` inside `st.form` is still allowed (expander, tabs, popover)

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR resolves a deferred follow-up item from the dynamic container callback PRs by correctly wiring up `check_widget_policies` in `st.tabs`, `st.expander`, and `st.popover`. Previously, these functions called `check_widget_policies` with hardcoded `on_change=None` and `enable_check_callback_rules=False`, preventing the proper validation of callback usage inside forms.

**Changes:**
- Updated `st.tabs`, `st.expander`, and `st.popover` to follow the established pattern from chart elements (vega_charts.py, plotly_chart.py) for handling callable vs non-callable `on_change` parameters
- Added comprehensive test coverage to verify that callable `on_change` inside `st.form` raises `StreamlitInvalidFormCallbackError` while `on_change="rerun"` continues to work

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `lib/streamlit/elements/layouts.py` | Updated `tabs`, `expander`, and `popover` functions to evaluate `is_callback = callable(on_change)` and pass it to `enable_check_callback_rules` in `check_widget_policies`, ensuring proper form callback validation |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py` | Added 6 regression tests (2 per container type) verifying that callable `on_change` inside forms raises errors while `on_change="rerun"` does not |
The code LGTM, but it seems like the PR description is referring to TODOs and other items that don't seem to be reflected in the actual code changes.

author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds markdown rendering support to navigation section headers in both sidebar and top navigation modes. Section headers can now display bold, italics, strikethroughs, inline code, Material icons, and images using GitHub-flavored Markdown syntax. The changes refactor CSS styling into a reusable helper function and update documentation to reflect the new capability.

**Changes:**
- Added `StreamlitMarkdown` component to render section headers in `NavSection.tsx` and `TopNavSection.tsx` with appropriate props (`allowHTML={false}`, `isLabel`, `disableLinks`, `truncate`)
- Extracted shared CSS helper function `markdownLineHeightOverride` in `styled-components.ts` to maintain consistent nav item heights and reduce code duplication
- Updated `st.navigation()` docstring to document markdown support for section labels with restrictions on allowed elements
- Added comprehensive E2E and unit tests to verify markdown rendering (bold, italic, Material icons) and confirm links are disabled

### Reviewed changes

| `lib/streamlit/commands/navigation.py` | Updated docstring to document markdown support for section labels with restricted elements |
| `frontend/app/src/components/Navigation/styled-components.ts` | Extracted `markdownLineHeightOverride` helper function and applied it to section header styled components |
| `frontend/app/src/components/Navigation/NavSection.tsx` | Added `StreamlitMarkdown` to render section headers with consistent props |
| `frontend/app/src/components/Navigation/TopNavSection.tsx` | Added `StreamlitMarkdown` to render section titles and dropdown section names |
| `frontend/app/src/components/Navigation/TopNav.test.tsx` | Added unit test to verify markdown rendering in section titles with links disabled |
| `fronte

commits.message:
Add markdown support for navigation section headers

Enable GitHub-flavored Markdown rendering in navigation section headers,
consistent with page titles. Supports bold, italics, strikethroughs,
inline code, Material icons, and images. Links are disabled to prevent
nested links in navigation.

Fix E2E test selector for section header icon

Use .first on the icon locator since section headers now have two
icons: the markdown-rendered icon and the expand/collapse chevron.

[snapshots] Update E2E snapshots for #14053 (#14056)

## Describe your changes

Automated snapshot updates for #14053 created via the snapshot autofix
CI workflow.

This workflow was triggered by adding the `update-snapshots` label to a
PR after Playwright E2E tests failed with snapshot mismatches.

**Updated snapshots:** 39 file(s)

⚠️ **Please review the snapshot changes carefully** - they could mask
visual bugs if accepted blindly.

This PR targets a feature branch and can be merged without review
approval.

Co-authored-by: Streamlit Bot <core+streamlitbot-github@streamlit.io>

fix: Use text filter for section header icon test

The icon selector was incorrectly using .first which picked up
the expand_more chevron instead of the settings icon. Use
.filter(has_text="settings") to reliably select the correct icon.

fix: Use role selector for markdown-rendered material icon

StreamlitMarkdown renders material icons as <span role="img"> with
aria-label, not using the stIconMaterial test id. Update selector
to use get_by_role("img", name="settings icon") for reliable matching.

fix: Preserve semiBold font-weight in nav section headers with markdown

Add fontWeight inheritance to the markdown style override for section
headers so that the semiBold weight from StyledSidebarNavSectionHeader
properly propagates to the inner StreamlitMarkdown div/p elements.

[snapshots] Update E2E snapshots for #14053 (#14062)

## Describe your changes

Automated snapshot updates for #14053 created via the snapshot autofix

title: [feature] Add `link` parameter to `st.image`
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a new `link` parameter to `st.image` that makes images clickable:

```python
st.image("photo.jpg", link="https://example.com")
```

- Only supports single image input (throws exception for multiple images)
- Validates HTTP/HTTPS URLs only (rejects javascript: etc.)
- Opens in new tab with `rel="noreferrer"` for security
- Includes keyboard focus styling for accessibility

Closes #9836

title: Improve version copy accessibility
body:
## Describe your changes
Improves accessibility and layout of the version copy button in the `MainMenu` footer.

- **Move version footer outside `role="menu"`**: The `StyledMenuVersionFooter` (containing the `CopyButton`) is now a sibling of `StyledMenuContainer` rather than a child. This fixes a WAI-ARIA violation where an interactive button was nested inside `role="menu"` without being a `menuitem`. A new `StyledMenuPopoverContent` wrapper owns the outer padding and `minWidth` for both the menu and footer.
- **Keyboard navigation for the footer CopyButton**: Tab from the last menu item moves focus to the `CopyButton` (via focus-lock), then a second Tab closes the popover. Escape from the `CopyButton` also closes the popover. Shift+Tab returns focus to the menu.
- **Remove hover jitter on copy button**: Removed the `transform: scale(0.9)` → `scale(1)` transition that caused visual jitter on hover. The button now fades in with a clean opacity transition only.

## Testing Plan
- JS Unit Tests: ✅ Added 
- E2E Tests: ✅ Updated
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR improves the accessibility and keyboard navigation of the MainMenu's version footer by restructuring the DOM to comply with WAI-ARIA standards and removing a visual jitter effect.

**Changes:**
- Moved the version footer (containing the CopyButton) outside `role="menu"` to fix WAI-ARIA violation
- Added keyboard navigation support for the CopyButton via Tab/Shift+Tab/Escape
- Removed `transform: scale()` animation that caused hover jitter on the copy button

| frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/MainMenu.test.tsx | Added comprehensive keyboard navigation tests for footer interactions |

title: Simplify query-params infra for wire format changes
body:
## Describe your changes
Replace the `optionStrings` infrastructure used for query parameter binding with a simpler `urlDefault` field. Instead of threading option strings through three interfaces (`QueryParamBindingConfig` → `QueryParamBindingOptions` → `QueryParamBinding`) and normalizing index-based defaults inside `WidgetStateManager.registerQueryParamBinding`, the conversion now happens once at the call site in `Slider.tsx` using the existing `indicesToStringValues` helper.

Also removes dead code paths in `WidgetStateManager.convertToUrlValue` and `session_state.py._auto_correct_url_if_needed` that handled index-to-string conversion via `options`/`formatted_options` but were no longer exercised by any bound widget.

## Changes

**Frontend:**
- `useBasicWidgetState.ts`: Replace `optionStrings?: string[]` with `urlDefault?: unknown` on `QueryParamBindingConfig`; simplify `defaultValueForBinding` memo to use `urlDefault` when provided
- `useQueryParamBinding.ts`: Remove `optionStrings` from `QueryParamBindingOptions`
- `WidgetStateManager.ts`: Remove `options` from `QueryParamBinding` interface and `registerQueryParamBinding`; remove normalization block and options-based branches in `convertToUrlValue`
- `Slider.tsx`: Compute `urlDefault` directly via `indicesToStringValues(element.default, element.options)` for select_slider

**Backend:**
- `session_state.py`: Remove dead `formatted_options` index-to-string conversion block in `_auto_correct_url_if_needed`
- `widgets.py`: Update `formatted_options` comment to reflect current usage


## Testing Plan
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python): ✅ Updated
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR simplifies the query parameter binding infrastructure by replacing the `optionStrings` mechanism with a simpler `urlDefault` field. The change eliminates the need to thread option strings through multiple interfaces and removes dead code that performed index-to-string conversion for widgets that no longer use index-based wire formats.

**Changes:**
- Replaced `optionStrings` infrastructure with `urlDefault` field that allows widgets to provide URL-compatible default values directly
- Moved index-to-string conversion for `select_slider` from `WidgetStateManager` to the call site in `Slider.tsx` using the existing `indicesToStringValues` helper
- Removed dead code paths in both frontend (`WidgetStateManager.convertToUrlValue`) and backend (`session_state._auto_correct_url_if_needed`) that handled index-based option conversion

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 23 out of 23 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| `lib/streamlit/runtime/state/widgets.py` | Updated comment to clarify current usage of `formatted_options` parameter |
| `lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state_test.py` | Removed tests for dead code paths |
| `frontend/lib/src/hooks/useBasicWidgetState.ts` | Replaced `optionStrings` with `urlDefault` in interface and implementation |
| `frontend/lib/src/hooks/useQueryParamBinding.ts` | Removed `optionStrings` from interface and parameter list |
| `frontend/lib/src/hooks/useQueryParamBinding.test.ts` | Updated tests to match new signature without `optionStrings` |

LGTM 👍

body:

Run the stale PR workflow on a nightly cron matching `nightly.yml` and keep
