title: Add `delta_description` to `st.metric`
body: ## Describe your changes Implements the `delta_description` parameter for `st.metric()` to display descriptive text next to delta values. The description appears in a muted gray font (similar to `st.caption`) with ellipsis overflow handling for responsive layouts. Includes accessibility improvements with ARIA labels for screen readers and native browser tooltips for mouse hover. ## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes) ## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable) - Closes #13690 ## Testing Plan - **Unit Tests (Python)**: 3 new tests verify delta_description parameter parsing and proto field setting - **Unit Tests (TypeScript)**: 7 new tests including accessibility tests for aria-label and aria-hidden attributes - **E2E Tests (Playwright)**: 3 new tests verify rendering with/without delta and title attribute - **Accessibility**: Tests confirm proper ARIA attributes for screen readers and title attribute for browser tooltips - All existing tests pass (68 Python, 58 Frontend unit tests; 6 E2E tests) --- **Contribution License Agreement** By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license. Co-authored-by: Prashant <186384788+prashcod@users.noreply.github.com>
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new `delta_description` parameter to `st.metric()` so apps can show supplementary context (e.g., “month over month”) alongside the delta, with corresponding backend/proto plumbing, frontend rendering/styling, and automated test coverage.

**Changes:**
- Extend the `Metric` protobuf with a `delta_description` field and plumb it through the Python `st.metric()` API.
- Update the frontend `Metric` component to render the description next to the delta with truncation/tooltip behavior.
- Add/extend Python unit tests, frontend unit tests (incl. a11y assertions), and Playwright E2E tests for the new behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Metric.proto | Adds `delta_description` field to the Metric proto message. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/metric.py | Adds `delta_description` parameter and sets the proto field when provided; refactors delta-arrow mapping. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/metric_test.py | Adds unit tests validating default and explicit `delta_description` proto behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Metric/styled-components.ts | Adds layout/styling for delta+description row and muted description text. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Metric/Metric.tsx | Renders `deltaDescription` next to delta and adds ARIA/title attributes. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Metric/Metric.test.tsx | Adds rendering/styling/a11y unit tests for delta description. |
| e2e_playwright/st_metric.py | Adds app scenarios for delta description (incl. long text truncation). |
| e2e_playwright/st_metric_test.py | Adds E2E checks + snapshot coverage for delta description rendering and tooltip/title. |
</details>

✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

commits.message:
Add delta_description parameter to st.metric

Implement support for configuring descriptive text next to delta values
in metrics (e.g., "month over month", "vs. last quarter"). The description
is displayed in a muted gray font with ellipsis overflow handling. Includes
accessibility improvements with ARIA labels and native browser tooltips.
Fix metric delta description accessibility relation

Fix metric delta text flexbox truncation

Add flexShrink and minWidth to StyledMetricDeltaText to ensure proper
truncation behavior when used alongside StyledDeltaDescription in the
flex container.

Integrate delta_description into existing metric e2e tests

Instead of dedicated tests, add delta_description to existing metrics
that already have screenshot tests. This includes a material icon case.

Use StreamlitMarkdown for delta description rendering

Enables material icons, rainbow colors, and other markdown features
in delta_description text.

Add more delta_description test coverage

- Long ellipsized description on "Apples I've eaten" (with chart)
- Description without border on "Test 9"

Fix delta badge disappearing when description is long

Changed flexShrink from 1 to 0 on StyledMetricDeltaText so the delta
badge doesn't shrink. Only the description should truncate.

Update metric e2e snapshots with delta_description

Updated screenshots now include delta_description in various metrics.

Restore bottom padding on delta container

The switch from inline-flex to flex layout removed implicit line-height
spacing. Add paddingBottom to match the previous visual appearance.
Update metric e2e snapshots
Update e2e snapshots for metric and columns tests

Treat empty string delta_description as None

Empty string "" is now treated the same as None, avoiding setting
an unnecessary proto field. Added test to verify the behavior.
Revert e2e snapshot changes to develop state
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/metric-delta-desc
Revert e2e snapshot changes to develop state
Update e2e snapshots for st_metric tests
Update e2e snapshots for columns and custom colors tests

Simplify delta_description unit tests

Consolidate Python tests into a single parameterized test and
combine redundant TypeScript tests for cleaner, more maintainable
test code while preserving the same coverage.
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/metric-delta-desc

Add truncate prop to StreamlitMarkdown for cleaner text truncation

Replace hacky nested CSS selectors with a dedicated truncate prop on
StreamlitMarkdown. This encapsulates truncation logic in one place,
removes the need for consumers to know the internal DOM structure,
and improves code maintainability.

- Add truncate prop to StreamlitMarkdown component
- Add truncation styles to StyledStreamlitMarkdown
- Remove StyledTruncateText wrapper from Metric component
- Clean up nested CSS selectors in StyledDeltaDescription
- Add unit tests for truncate functionality

Update E2E snapshots for truncate prop changes

Visual snapshots updated after adding truncate prop to StreamlitMarkdown.
The changes are minor visual adjustments from the new truncation styling.

Fix truncate prop adding extra height to metrics

Add lineHeight: "normal" to truncate styles to reset inherited
line heights. The old StyledTruncateText wrapper had this style,
but it was missing from the new truncate prop implementation.

Without this, when inheritFont is true and the parent has a large
line-height (like metric values), the markdown container would
inherit it, adding ~10px extra height.

Update E2E snapshots after lineHeight fix

Snapshots reflect correct metric heights now that lineHeight: normal
is applied in the truncate styles.

Address PR review: explicit None check and typing test

- Change `if delta_description:` to `if delta_description is not None:`
  for consistency with format parameter handling
- Add metric_types.py typing test covering all st.metric parameters
  including delta_description

Remove unused id attribute from StyledMetricDeltaText

The id attribute on the delta element was never used. The aria-describedby
accessibility feature only needs the ID on the description element.

Use isCaption for delta description to fade all content uniformly

Replace color: fadedText60 with isCaption (opacity: 0.6) so that
colored text, badges, and icons in delta_description are faded
consistently with regular text. Simplifies StyledDeltaDescription
by removing redundant styling now handled by StreamlitMarkdown props.
Update E2E snapshots for metric delta description changes

Simplify deltaA11yId usage by removing redundant suffix

Remove the `-delta-description` suffix from the useId() generated ID since
useId() already returns a unique ID, making the additional suffix unnecessary.
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/metric-delta-desc

title: [refactor] Rename internal DocString to Help for consistency
body:
## Describe your changes

Rename the internal `DocString` component/proto/module to `Help` to align with the public `st.help()` API. This improves naming consistency throughout the codebase and consolidates Python tests with better parameterization.

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python) - 103 tests passed
- [x] E2E Tests - covered by existing `st_help.py` script
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors Streamlit’s internal “DocString” element naming to “Help” to align with the public `st.help()` API, updating the protobuf schema, Python backend enqueueing/tests, and the frontend element renderer/component accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Renames the protobuf element/message from `DocString` to `Help` and updates `Element` oneof wiring.
- Updates Python `HelpMixin` to enqueue the renamed element and consolidates/extends unit tests into `help_test.py`.
- Updates the frontend element component and renderer to consume the renamed protobuf oneof case, plus adjusts the related e2e helper import.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Help.proto | Renames the element message from `DocString` to `Help`. |
| proto/streamlit/proto/Element.proto | Switches the `Element` oneof entry from `doc_string` to `help_info` and updates imports. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/help.py | Uses the renamed protobuf + enqueues the renamed oneof field. |
| lib/streamlit/delta_generator.py | Updates the mixin import to the renamed module. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/help_test.py | Migrates/expands tests to validate new behavior and the new element field name. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/doc_string_test.py | Removes the old test file after consolidation. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Help/* | Renames the frontend element component and adds styled-components for the Help element folder. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/ElementNodeRenderer.tsx | Updates rendering switch to the new oneof case and component import. |
| e2e_playwright/st_help.py | Updates test helper import to the renamed backend module. |
</details>



<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (4)</summary>

**lib/streamlit/elements/help.py
LGTM

commits.message:
[refactor] Rename internal DocString to Help for consistency

Align internal component naming with the public st.help() API:
- Rename DocString.proto to Help.proto (message renamed to Help)
- Rename doc_string.py to help.py in Python backend
- Rename frontend DocString component to Help
- Update Element.proto field from doc_string to help_info
- Consolidate tests into help_test.py with improved parameterization

This is an internal naming consistency improvement. The public API
(st.help()) remains unchanged.
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/rename-docstring-help

title: [fix] Add selection support for treemap and sunburst in `st.plotly_chart`
body:
## Describe your changes

- Adds `onClick` handler to capture click events from treemap and sunburst charts
- Hierarchical charts don't emit `plotly_selected` events, but do emit `plotly_click` events
- Selection data sent with consistent format including `id`, `parent`, `label`, `value`, and hierarchy-specific fields

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes #9001

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS)
- [x] E2E Tests (treemap and sunburst selection)

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds click event selection support for hierarchical Plotly charts (treemap and sunburst) to fix issue #9001. These chart types don't emit `plotly_selected` events like other charts, but they do emit `plotly_click` events that can be leveraged to provide the same selection functionality.

**Changes:**
- Added `handleClickEvent` function to process click events from hierarchical charts and convert them to selection state
- Integrated the click handler into the PlotlyChart component with proper activation guards
- Added comprehensive unit tests and E2E tests for both treemap and sunburst chart selection

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/PlotlyChart/utils.ts` | Added `handleClickEvent` function to handle click events from hierarchical charts and emit selection data in a format consistent with other chart types |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/PlotlyChart/PlotlyChart.tsx` | Integrated the click handler with proper conditional activation based on `isSelectionActivated` |
| `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/PlotlyChart/utils.test.ts` | Added comprehensive unit tests covering early returns, treemap/sunburst clicks, undefined pointNumber handling, and deduplication |
| `e2e_playwright/st_plotly_chart_select.py` | Added test app sections for treemap and sunburst charts with selection enabled |
| `e2e_playwright/st_plotly_chart_select_test.py` | Added parametrized E2E test verifying click interaction and selection data emission for both chart types |
</details>

✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

commits.message:
Add selection support for treemap and sunburst charts

Hierarchical charts (treemap, sunburst) don't emit plotly_selected events
but do emit plotly_click events. Add onClick handler that captures these
clicks and sends them as selection state for API consistency.

Refactor hierarchical chart tests and fix click handler condition

Split parameterized treemap/sunburst test into separate functions for
clarity. Use isPointsSelectionActivated instead of isSelectionActivated
for onClick handler to properly scope click events.

Clarify eslint-disable comments for element.id dependency pattern

Reword comments to clearly explain why element.id is used instead of
element in useCallback dependency arrays: the proto object gets a new
reference on each render, but element.id only changes when the element
actually changes.

title: [chore] Fix or ignore deprecation warnings in Python unit tests
body:
## Describe your changes

- Reduced warnings from 126 to 10. 

- Replace deprecated Starlette `@app.route()` decorator with `Route` class in auth route tests
- Add pytest warning suppression for protobuf `label()` deprecation (100 warnings)
- Add pytest warning suppression for AsyncMock unawaited coroutine in fragment tests
- Fix pandas ChainedAssignmentError by using `.loc[]` accessor in dataframe test
- Add global pytest warning filters for external library warnings:
  - Matplotlib converter deprecation (from Plotly)
  - Altair selection deduplication warning
  - pytest asyncio config warning
  - DataFrame.attrs serialization warning

## Testing Plan

- [x] Existing unit tests pass
- [x] `make check` passes with reduced warnings

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR addresses deprecation warnings in Python unit tests to improve test suite maintainability and reduce noise in test output. The changes modernize test code to align with updated library APIs and suppress unavoidable warnings from underlying dependencies.

**Changes:**
- Replaced deprecated Starlette `@app.route()` decorator with the `Route` class pattern
- Added pytest warning filters for protobuf field descriptor deprecations and AsyncMock coroutine warnings
- Fixed pandas `ChainedAssignmentError` warnings by using explicit `.loc[]` accessor

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/starlette/starlette_auth_routes_test.py | Migrated from deprecated `@app.route()` decorator to `Route` class for defining test routes |
| lib/tests/streamlit/runtime/fragment_test.py | Added class-level warning suppression for unawaited coroutine warnings from MagicMock |
| lib/tests/streamlit/proto_compatibility_test.py | Added module-level warning suppression for protobuf `label()` deprecation |
| lib/tests/streamlit/dataframe_util_test.py | Replaced direct column assignment with `.loc[:, column]` to avoid pandas chained assignment warnings |
Nice job reducing the amount of warnings here!

commits.message:

- Replace deprecated Starlette @app.route() with Route class
- Add warning suppression for protobuf label() deprecation
- Add warning suppression for AsyncMock unawaited coroutine
- Fix pandas ChainedAssignmentError using .loc[] accessor

Add pytest warning suppressions for external library warnings

- Matplotlib converter deprecation (from Plotly)
- Altair selection deduplication warning
- pytest asyncio config warning
- DataFrame.attrs serialization warning

title: Bump ty from 0.0.15 to 0.0.16
body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.15 to 0.0.16.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.16

Release Notes

Released on 2026-02-10.

Bug fixes

  • Allow stringified argument in PEP-613 alias to Optional (#23200)
  • Fix fuzzer panic on slice expression in unclosed comprehension (#23146)
  • Fix combinatorial explosion due to fixed-length tuple expansion in overload matching (#23190)
  • Respect @no_type_check when combined with other decorators (#23177)
  • Fix diagnostic location for an incorrect sub-call to a specialized ParamSpec (#23036)

LSP server

  • Assign lower completions ranking to deprecated functions and classes (#23089)
  • Change goto-def for class constructors to always go to class definition (#23071)
  • Ensure diagnostic mode is consistent across projects inside the LSP server (#23121)
  • Don't include the class Foo in autocomplete suggestions when the user is typing out Foo's bases (#23141)
  • Fix parameter references across files via keyword args (#23012)
  • Fix wrong inlay hints for overloaded function arguments (#23179)
  • Support diagnostics in newly created files inside neovim (#23095)
  • Exclude already-included classes when providing completion suggestions for class bases (#23085)

CLI

  • Add support for TY_OUTPUT_FORMAT environment variable (#23123)
  • Fall back to python3 found in $PATH if no environment is found (#22843)

Type checking

  • Add inconsistent-mro autofix to move Generic[] to the end of the bases list (#22998)
  • Add precise return-type inference for struct.unpack (#22562, #23130)
  • Disallow TypeVars within ClassVars (#23184)
  • Emit diagnostic on unbound call to abstract @classmethod or @staticmethod (#23182)
  • Fix false-positive diagnostics when providing the total= keyword to TypedDict classes that had PEP-695 type parameters (#23114)
  • Narrow both left- and right-hand operands where possible (#23084)
  • Narrow chained operators (#23093)
  • Narrow equality subscripts on either operand (#23104)
  • Recognize __dataclass_transform__ to support SQLModel (#23070)
  • Relax the attribute narrowing condition to support deeper-nested attribute type narrowing (#22440)
  • Support constrained TypeVar compatibility across function boundaries (#23103)
  • Support comparison methods (__gt__, etc.) where a parameter is annotated with a Literal type (#23100)
  • Support partially specialized type context (#22748)
  • Use type context when inferring constructor argument types (#23139)
  • Validate TypedDict constructor calls for generic aliases and type[...] targets (#23113)

Performance

  • Conservative narrowing places optimization (#22734)

title: Add `addressing-pr-review-comments` skill
body:
## Describe your changes

Add a new skill to help address review comments on PRs in the streamlit/streamlit repository. This skill provides a structured workflow for analyzing, presenting, and applying fixes to PR review comments.

## Testing Plan

- No additional tests needed (configuration/skill addition)

Adds a new “skill” document under `.claude/skills/` that defines a structured workflow (using `gh` CLI) for collecting, triaging, and applying fixes for PR review comments in the `streamlit/streamlit` repo.

**Changes:**
- Added a new skill guide: `.claude/skills/addressing-pr-review-comments/SKILL.md` with a step-by-step checklist and example outputs.
- Updated `.claude/.gitignore` to allow the new skill directory to be committed.

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.claude/skills/addressing-pr-review-comments/SKILL.md` | New workflow documentation for fetching/analyzing PR review comments and applying selected fixes. |

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commits.message:
Add addressing-pr-review-comments skill

Add a new skill to help address review comments on PRs in the streamlit/streamlit repository.

Address PR review comments

- Replace REST API workaround with GraphQL query that properly filters by isResolved
- Fix example summary count (3 of 4 → 3 of 5)
- Clarify exclusion criteria for questions
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/add-address-comment-skill

Fix pagination and normalize skill formatting

- Add --paginate flag to gh api calls to fetch all comment pages
- Normalize placeholder syntax to {PR_NUMBER} in GraphQL query
- Apply sentence casing to section headers

Add critical analysis and response type guidance

- Add critical analysis section to validate all comments (not just bots)
- Add response type categories for better comment classification
- Add tip to save outputs to work-tmp/reviews/ for context preservation

Align action types to conventional comments standard

Replace custom categories (CODE, STYLE, DOCS, TEST, QUESTION) and
verbose response types with conventional comments labels (issue, todo,
chore, suggestion, nitpick, question, praise, thought, note).

Updates presentation format and example summary to use new labels.

title: Add theme switcher to `MainMenu`
body: ## Describe your changes Adds a theme toggle (System / Light / Dark) directly into the main menu as `menuitemradio` elements, building on the accessibility refactor in #13878. **Note**: the Settings dialog's theme selection mechanism will be removed in a separate PR when the full `SettingsDialog` & menu option is removed. **Changes:** - Three radio items (System, Light, Dark) appear at the top of the menu, inside a `role="group"` container with `aria-label="Theme"`. - Each item uses `role="menuitemradio"` with `aria-checked` to reflect the active theme. - Selecting a theme calls `setTheme` via `ThemeContext` without closing the menu, so users can preview different themes. - Keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down, Home/End, Enter/Space) flows seamlessly between radio items and action items in a single roving tabindex. ## Testing Plan - JS Unit Tests: ✅ Added - E2E Tests: ✅ Added - Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a theme switcher directly to the MainMenu, allowing users to toggle between System, Light, and Dark themes using `menuitemradio` elements. This builds on the accessibility refactor from #13878 and provides a more convenient way to change themes without opening the Settings dialog (which will be deprecated in a future PR).

**Changes:**
- Added theme selection UI as three radio buttons (System, Light, Dark) at the top of the main menu
- Integrated theme switching with existing ThemeContext to maintain theme state
- Updated keyboard navigation to seamlessly flow between radio items and action items
- Radio items do not close the menu when selected, allowing users to preview themes

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/themeSection.ts` | Pure logic for building theme section data structure from ThemeContext state |
| `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/themeSection.test.ts` | Comprehensive unit tests for theme section builder logic |
| `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/styled-components.ts` | Styled components for theme radio group and individual radio items |
| `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/MainMenu.tsx` | Integration of theme section into menu with discriminated unions and roving tabindex |
| `frontend/app/src/components/MainMenu/MainMenu.test.tsx` | Comprehensive unit tests for theme switcher integration in MainMenu |
| `e2e_playwright/main_menu_test.py` | E2E tests for keyboard navigation, theme persistence, and OS preference changes |
| `e2e_playwright/__snapshots__/*` | Updated visual snapshots for all browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) in both light and dark themes |
</details>

title: Optimize agent rules
body:
## Describe your changes

Applies a couple of tweaks to the agent rules to 1) prevent agents to handle e2e test like unit tests 2) add runtime estimates to `make` commands to simulate the intuition we get when using these commands 3) hint about covering new features with e2e and unit tests. 4) hint about logging usage in frontend.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

## Pull request overview

This PR enhances E2E testing documentation to guide AI agents and developers toward more efficient testing practices. The changes emphasize cost-awareness and strategic test organization to optimize CI/CD performance without sacrificing coverage.

**Changes:**
- Added guidance emphasizing that E2E tests should optimize for confidence per browser run rather than creating many tiny test functions
- Introduced recommendation to aggregate related assertions into single scenario tests that reuse one app load
- Added caution about using `@pytest.mark.parametrize` sparingly in E2E contexts to avoid multiplying expensive browser setup/execution cycles

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `e2e_playwright/AGENTS.md` | Added three new best practice bullets for efficient E2E testing patterns |
| `.github/instructions/e2e_playwright.instructions.md` | Applied identical E2E efficiency guidance as AGENTS.md for consistency |
| `.cursor/rules/e2e_playwright.mdc` | Updated with same E2E best practices to ensure all documentation sources align |

commits.message:
Optimize E2E test guidance for AI agents

Add best practices for efficient E2E test design:
- Emphasize E2E tests are expensive; optimize confidence per browser run
- Prefer aggregated scenario tests over many micro-tests with shared setup
- Recommend using parametrize sparingly, iterating variants in single test

Improve AI agent documentation clarity and consistency

- Consolidate redundant E2E test cost guidance into single bullet point
- Change runtime estimates from parentheses to square brackets
- Update time descriptions: (~2-3min) → (~3min), (persistent) → (until stopped)
- Remove debug-e2e-test command from e2e_playwright docs
- Add guidance that user-facing features need both unit and E2E tests

Add guidance to not use parametrize for E2E test simplification

E2E tests are expensive and parameterization multiplies browser runs.
Prefer iterating variants within a single test function instead.

title: [chore] Fix and suppress frontend test warnings
body:

- Fixed a couple of test related warnings, e.g. related to `act()`

author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR aims to reduce/resolve noisy frontend unit test warnings (act(), validateDOMNesting, deprecated `findDOMNode`, jsdom “Not implemented”, etc.) by adjusting specific tests/components and centralizing some suppression/mocking in the Vitest setup.

**Changes:**
- Adds/extends warning suppression and environment mocks in the Vitest setup.
- Updates several widget/menu tests to await BaseWeb/BaseUI Popover async mounting to avoid act() warnings.
- Makes small component/test fixes to remove DOM nesting and `findDOMNode` deprecation warnings.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 11 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/vitest.setup.ts | Adds process warning filtering, console warn/error filtering, jsdom virtualConsole suppression, and wavesurfer/audio mocks. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/menus/FormattingMenu.test.tsx | Adds async render helper to wait for popover mount before assertions/interactions. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/menus/ColumnVisibilityMenu.test.tsx | Adds async render helper to wait for popover mount before assertions/interactions. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/menus/ColumnMenu.test.tsx | Adds async render helper to wait for popover mount before assertions/interactions. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/DataFrame.test.tsx | Updates DataEditor mock to avoid spreading non-DOM props into a div. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Markdown/Markdown.test.tsx | Adjusts ElementNode construction for Markdown auto-width integration tests. |
| frontend/eslint.config.mjs | Ignores vitest config/setup files from ESLint. |
| frontend/connection/src/StaticConnection.test.tsx | Uses `vi.restoreAllMocks()` to fully reset spies/mocks between tests. |
| frontend/app/src/hocs/withScreencast/components/Scree

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commits.message:
[chore] Fix and suppress frontend test warnings - Fix act() warnings in DataFrame menu tests by adding renderAndWaitForPopover helper - Wrap state updates in act() for StreamlitLib.test.tsx - Fix validateDOMNesting warning by changing StyledInstruction from

to

- Suppress third-party warnings in vitest.setup.ts: - Node.js 22+ --localstorage-file warning - baseui defaultProps deprecation warnings - baseui Popover/Transition act() warnings (internal async updates) - jsdom HTMLMediaElement not implemented errors (wavesurfer.js) - popper.js preventOverflow modifier warning - Add vitest config files to ESLint ignores

[chore] Additional test warning fixes and infrastructure improvements

- Add nodeRef to StatusWidget's CSSTransition to avoid findDOMNode deprecation
- Make openMenu helper async with proper waitFor for baseui state updates
- Fix Markdown test to correctly construct ElementNode with Element wrapper
- Fix DataFrame mock to avoid spreading non-DOM props to div element
- Add messageIncludes helper for cleaner warning suppression
- Add wavesurfer.js and HTMLMediaElement mocks for audio-related tests
- Add additional warning suppressions: findDOMNode, validateDOMNesting, sprintf, Emotion SSR

[chore] Add localStorage mock for Node.js 22+ compatibility

Node.js 22+ has a built-in localStorage that requires --localstorage-file flag.
Without it, localStorage exists but its methods don't work, causing test failures
like "window.localStorage.clear is not a function". This mock ensures tests have
a working localStorage regardless of Node version.

[chore] Wrap async operations in act() and fix test warnings

- Wrap timer advances, focus/blur, and event dispatches in act()
- Add waitFor for async Popover state updates in Selectbox and Tooltip tests
- Convert localStorage mock to class-based approach for better spy compatibility
- Add beforeEach to reset mock config in Snow tests
[chore] Add waitFor for async Popover state updates in additional tests

[chore] Fail tests on act() warnings in our own code

Enhances act() warning handling to catch missing act() wrappers in our
components while still suppressing known third-party library issues.

[chore] Fix act() warning detection for third-party libs and DownloadButton tests

- Fix vitest.setup.ts to check all console.error args for component names
  (React passes component name as separate arg, not in format string)
- Wrap vi.advanceTimersByTime in act() for timer-triggered state updates
- Use renderWithContexts for consistent async context behavior in tests

[chore] Fix DataFrame mock with forwardRef and suppress test warnings

- Use forwardRef for DataEditor mock to properly handle refs from DataFrame
- Add separate mock function for prop assertions instead of asserting on component
- Suppress ComponentRegistry "unregistered ComponentInstance" warnings in parallel tests
- Suppress WindowDimensionsProvider error from intentional constraint tests

[chore] Suppress accept-attribute library warning in tests

The accept-attribute library (used by react-dropzone) warns about
invalid MIME types like "application/streamlit" used as test placeholders
in ChatInput tests. This warning is expected and caused by an upstream
library issue, so suppress it to reduce test log noise.

[chore] Address PR review comments for test warning fixes

- Fix virtualConsole.emit to use rest args for proper argument forwarding
- Add missing ReactElement type imports in DataFrame menu tests
- Make App.test.tsx await async openMenu() helper
- Make openMenu helper work with or without fake timers via vi.isFakeTimers()
- Use process.emitWarning override instead of removeAllListeners for safer warning suppression
- Fix comment to match implementation (any -> all substrings)

[chore] Enforce Node.js version from .nvmrc in frontend commands

Add Node.js major version check to frontend-init that verifies the
running Node version matches .nvmrc. This prevents subtle test failures
caused by version mismatches (e.g., Node 25+ localStorage issues).

Remove localStorage mock and warning suppression from vitest.setup.ts
since enforcing the correct Node version makes these workarounds
unnecessary.
[chore] Simplify Node version note in frontend AGENTS.md

[chore] Stabilize frontend tests and tighten warning handling

Improve frontend test reliability by fixing event handling, DOM structure, and timer interactions while removing broad warning suppression so actionable issues surface during local runs.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

[chore] Refine frontend Node hint and popover visibility assertion

Tighten the frontend-init Node version guidance message and strengthen the DataFrame column visibility menu test to assert the popover is visible when opened.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/fix-local-test-fail

[chore] Stabilize Popover test with waitFor and suppress third-party act warnings

- Wrap skeleton assertion in waitFor to handle async Popper.js updates
- Suppress act() warnings from BaseUI Popover's internal Popper.js scheduling

title: New styling for remaining dropdowns (select all 5)
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR unifies dropdown/popover styling across multiple components to achieve a consistent visual design language. The key changes are:

- Light/dark mode border behavior: In light mode, borders are hidden (or match the background) and a `boxShadow` popover effect is shown instead. In dark mode, a visible border is used with no shadow.
- Border radius updates: Several components switch from `theme.radii.default` or `theme.radii.md` to `theme.radii.md2` for inner elements, creating more consistent rounding.
- Spacing adjustments: Menu items, dividers, and header rows have updated padding/margins for tighter, more consistent spacing.
- `popoverMargin`: Added to `Popover`, `BaseColorPicker`, and `TopNavSection` for consistent gap between trigger and popover.
- Scrollbar gutter handling: `ColumnVisibilityMenu` now uses a CSS custom property (`--scrollbar-gutter-size`) to adjust divider padding when a scrollbar is present.
- Background color simplification: Dark mode background for Inner elements changed from `secondaryBg` to `bgColor` for consistency.

## Screenshot or video (only for visual changes)

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes #12956 

## Testing Plan

- Explanation of why no additional tests are needed
- Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
- E2E Tests
- Any manual testing needed?

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-dmatthews

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR unifies dropdown and popover styling across multiple Streamlit components to achieve a consistent, modern visual design. The changes implement the design patterns established for top navigation dropdowns (issue #12956) and apply them to all dropdown menus, data frame menus, color pickers, and popovers throughout the application.

**Changes:**
- Introduced `getPopoverContainerStyle` utility function for consistent light/dark mode border and shadow behavior across all popovers
- Updated border radius from `theme.radii.default` to `theme.radii.md2` (0.375rem) for dropdown item highlights
- Adjusted spacing (padding/margins) across menu components for tighter, more consistent spacing
- Added `popoverMargin` prop to `Popover`, `BaseColorPicker`, and `TopNavSection` for consistent gap between trigger and content
- Implemented CSS custom property pattern for dynamic scrollbar gutter handling in `ColumnVisibilityMenu`
- Simplified background colors from `secondaryBg` to `bgColor` in dark mode for consistency

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 69 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/lib/src/index.ts | Exports new `getPopoverContainerStyle` utility |
| frontend/lib/src/components/shared/Base/styled-components.ts | Not changed in diff, but contains the `getPopoverContainerStyle` implementation |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/styled-components.ts | Applies `getPopoverContainerStyle` to search bar, removes hardcoded border radius |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/menus/styled-components.ts | Updates padding/margins, adds md2 radius, implements scrollbar gutter handling via CSS custom property |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/DataFrame/menus/FormattingMenu.tsx | Applies `getPopoverContainerStyle`, simplifies background color logic |

## Describe your changes
The PR validates the `max_selections` parameter in `st.multiselect` to ensure that it is a positive integer.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)
Closes #13965

title: [DynamicContainers] Dynamic Popovers
body:
## Describe your changes

Test App: https://dynamic-popover.streamlit.app/

**Stacked on #13867.**

Adds `open`, `key`, and `on_change` parameters to `st.popover`, enabling stateful, lazily-executed popovers. When `on_change="rerun"`, opening or closing a popover triggers a rerun and the `.open` property returns `True` when open and `False` when closed. This lets apps skip expensive work inside closed popovers:

```python
pop = st.popover("Details", on_change="rerun")

if pop.open:
    with pop:
        run_expensive_query()
```

Programmatic control is supported via `st.session_state`:

```python
def show_settings():
    st.session_state.settings_pop = True

st.button("Settings", on_click=show_settings)
pop = st.popover("Settings", key="settings_pop", on_change="rerun")

if pop.open:
    with pop:
        st.write("Settings panel")
```

### Backend

- Adds `open`, `key`, and `on_change` parameters to `st.popover()`. When `on_change="rerun"`, registers the popover as a widget via `register_widget`, tracking its open/closed state as a boolean. Sets `.open` on the returned `PopoverContainer` to the current state. Without `on_change="rerun"`, `.open` remains `None`.
- `open` sets the initial open/closed state (default `False`); `st.session_state[key]` allows programmatic control.
- **Note:** `st-key-` CSS class support for popovers is deferred to a follow-up PR. A `key` without `on_change="rerun"` currently has no effect.

### Frontend

- On toggle in widget mode, sends the new state to the backend via `widgetMgr.setBoolValue` with optimistic UI updates for instant feedback.
- Syncs with backend state changes via `useEffect` for programmatic control through `session_state`.
- Shows a loading skeleton when opening an empty dynamic popover while waiting for content.
- Dynamic popovers (with `widget_id`) are not disabled when empty, unlike regular popovers, to allow lazy content loading.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

N/A — part of the DynamicContainers feature work.

## Testing Plan

- [x] Unit Tests (JS and/or Python)
- [x] E2E Tests
- [x] Any manual testing needed? Test manually with this app: https://dynamic-popover.streamlit.app/

**Python unit tests** (`lib/tests/streamlit/elements/layouts_test.py`): 10 tests covering invalid `on_change` values, `.open` state for both `open=False` and `open=True`, widget ID presence with and without `on_change`, session state accessibility, proto `open` field, and key-only behavior.

**Frontend unit tests** (`frontend/lib/src/components/elements/Popover/Popover.test.tsx`): 4 tests for dynamic popover behavior including widget state updates on toggle/close, non-widget popover passthrough, and programmatic state sync from backend.

**E2E tests** (`e2e_playwright/st_popover_test.py`): `test_dynamic_popover_lazy_execution` verifies content only executes when the popover is open; `test_dynamic_popover_programmatic_control` verifies opening/closing via session state buttons; `test_popover_key_only_does_not_trigger_rerun` verifies a key-only popover does not cause reruns on toggle.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds dynamic popover functionality to Streamlit by introducing `open`, `key`, and `on_change` parameters to `st.popover`. When `on_change="rerun"` is set, the popover becomes a stateful widget that triggers reruns on open/close events, enabling lazy content execution and programmatic control via `st.session_state`. This builds on PR #13867 which added the foundational `.open` property infrastructure.

**Changes:**
- Adds widget registration for popovers with `on_change="rerun"`, tracking open/closed state as a boolean value
- Implements lazy execution pattern where expensive operations inside the popover only run when `.open` is `True`
- Enables programmatic control through `st.session_state[key]` for opening/closing popovers from other widgets
- Adds loading skeleton UI feedback when opening empty dynamic popovers while waiting for content

### Reviewed changes

title: Bind widgets to query params - `st.multiselect`
body:
## Describe your changes
Adds the bind parameter to `st.multiselect` to enable two-way sync between widget values and URL query parameters.

**Key changes:**
- Added `bind="query-params"` support to `st.multiselect` using repeated params (`?tags=Red&tags=Blue`)
- Implement robust URL sanitization for array-valued widgets: invalid option filtering, duplicate deduplication, and max_selections truncation — all with automatic URL correction
- When `accept_new_options=True`, any URL string accepted (no filtering)
- Multiselect is always clearable (with `?foo=`)

## Testing Plan
- JS & Python Unit Tests: ✅ Added
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds query parameter binding support to `st.multiselect`, enabling two-way synchronization between multiselect widget values and URL query parameters. The implementation follows established patterns from other bound widgets (checkbox, radio, selectbox, text_input) and introduces robust URL sanitization for array-valued widgets.

**Changes:**
- Added `bind="query-params"` parameter to `st.multiselect` that uses repeated URL params (e.g., `?tags=Red&tags=Blue`)
- Implemented URL sanitization pipeline for array-valued widgets: filters invalid options, deduplicates values, and truncates to `max_selections` limit
- When `accept_new_options=True`, any URL string is accepted without validation
- Multiselect is always clearable (empty URL param `?key=` clears to `[]`)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/streamlit/proto/MultiSelect.proto | Added optional `query_param_key` field (15) to enable query param binding |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/common.py | Added `max_array_length` field to `WidgetMetadata` for array truncation support |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Implemented `_sanitize_url_array` helper and integrated sanitization into `_seed_widget_from_url` |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/widgets.py | Added `max_array_length` parameter to `register_widget` function |
| lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/multiselect.py | Added `bind` parameter to multiselect, set `query_param_key` in proto when bound, pass metadata for URL validation |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/Multiselect/Multiselect.tsx | Created `queryParamBinding` config with `urlFormat: "repeated"` and passed to `useBasicWidgetState` |
| lib/tests/streamlit/typing/multiselect_types.py | Added type tests for `bind` parameter with various configurations |
| lib/test

LGTM 👍 

> Consider unbinding on the backend when a keyed widget stops using bind="query-params" so URL params are not left permanently marked as bound for active widgets.

Is this valid? If so, might be a good to add to potential follow-ups.

title: Bind widgets to query params - `st.number_input`
body:
## Describe your changes
Adds the bind parameter to `st.number_input` to enable two-way sync between widget values and URL query parameters.

**Key changes:**
- Added `bind="query-params"` support to `st.number_input`
- Invalid (out of bounds, non-numeric) URL values are caught during deserialization and the query param is removed, falling back to the widget default.
- Clearability is tied to `value=None`: when the widget is registered with no default value, an empty URL param (`?key=`) clears the widget to `None`. When `value` is set, empty URL params are ignored.


## Testing Plan
- JS & Python Unit Tests: ✅ Added
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds query parameter binding support to `st.number_input`, enabling two-way synchronization between the widget's value and URL query parameters. When `bind="query-params"` is specified with a key, the widget's value is synced with the URL—changing the widget updates the URL, and loading the page with a query parameter initializes the widget from it. Values from the URL are automatically clamped to the specified `min_value` and `max_value` constraints.

**Changes:**
- Added `bind` parameter to `st.number_input` API with query parameter support
- Implemented URL value clamping in `NumberInputSerde.deserialize` for out-of-range values
- Added comprehensive test coverage (Python unit tests, TypeScript unit tests, and E2E tests)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/number_input_test.py | Added unit tests for bind parameter validation and serde clamping logic |
| lib/tests/streamlit/typing/number_input_types.py | Added type tests for bind parameter |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/NumberInput/NumberInput.tsx | Configured query param binding in `useBasicWidgetState` hook |

</details>
LGTM 👍

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

## Pull request overview

Adds an E2E-focused pytest plugin to collect Playwright/pytest execution statistics (counts, timings, reruns, xdist worker info, and memory) and publishes the output as a CI artifact to support tracking trends over time.

**Changes:**
- Introduce `e2e_playwright/shared/stats_reporter.py` pytest plugin that writes `test-results/test-stats.json`.
- Register the plugin for the e2e suite via `e2e_playwright/conftest.py`.
- Add CI artifact upload for the stats JSON and add `psutil` (+ stubs) for memory tracking.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `pyproject.toml` | Adds `psutil` + `types-psutil` to test dependencies for memory reporting. |
| `e2e_playwright/shared/stats_reporter.py` | New pytest plugin that collects and writes E2E statistics JSON. |
| `e2e_playwright/conftest.py` | Registers the new stats reporter plugin for the e2e suite. |
| `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` | Uploads the produced stats JSON as a CI artifact. |
LGTM, looking forward to seeing this! As a follow-up, are you planning on integrating the output of this into https://issues.streamlit.app/ ?

commits.message:
[chore] Add pytest plugin for E2E test statistics collection

Add a pytest plugin that collects and reports E2E test execution statistics
(pass/fail counts, durations, browser breakdowns, rerun details, worker stats,
memory usage) and outputs them to a JSON artifact for CI tracking.

Fix ty type error by using sys.platform instead of platform module

The ty type checker lacks stubs for the platform module, causing false
positive errors. sys.platform provides similar information and is well-typed.

fix: address PR review comments for stats reporter

- Fix rerun counting: only count reruns for "call" phase to avoid
  false positives from setup/teardown failures on the same test run
- Fix outcome mapping: setup/teardown failures now map to "error"
  to match pytest semantics
- Use time.perf_counter() for monotonic elapsed time measurement
- Rename peak_memory_mb to memory_mb (it's a snapshot, not peak)
- Improve docstring with CLI usage documentation

Rename slowest_modules to test_modules and include all modules

- Rename field from "slowest_modules" to "test_modules" for clarity
- Remove [:10] limit to include all test modules in stats output

Improve stats reporter robustness and worker_id accuracy

- Add try/except in _write_stats to prevent file-write failures from
  affecting pytest session exit status
- Extract worker_id from report.node.gateway.id for forwarded xdist
  reports on primary node, ensuring accurate worker attribution

Experiment with 45 pytest-xdist workers in CI

- Set PYTEST_XDIST_AUTO_NUM_WORKERS=45 to override psutil's physical
  core detection (32) as a middle ground with logical cores (64)
- Fix type: ensure worker_id is returned as str from gateway.id

Document pytest-xdist physical core behavior in CI workflow

Add comment explaining that with psutil installed, `-n auto` uses
physical CPU cores (32 on 64-core runner) since additional workers
would share the same cores while increasing memory usage.

Add fixture statistics (setup/teardown durations) to stats reporter

Track fixture setup and teardown durations separately from test execution
time. This helps identify slow fixtures that impact test performance.

Changes:
- Capture setup/teardown phase durations in pytest_runtest_logreport hook
- Add total and mean setup/teardown stats to duration section
- Add slowest_setup and slowest_teardown lists (top 10 each)
- Include setup/teardown durations in slowest_tests entries
- Update schema version to 1.1

Simplify stats reporter and workflow for clarity

- Replace verbose dict initialization with setdefault()
- Add _BYTES_PER_MB constant for magic number
- Remove redundant comments that restate code behavior
- Simplify workflow step names and combine commands

Fix memory stats to use xdist worker memory instead of dead children

The previous implementation tried to measure child process memory via
psutil.Process().children(), but with pytest-xdist all workers have
already exited by the time _get_memory_stats() is called at session
finish. This resulted in children_rss_mb always being 0.

Now we sum the memory values that each worker reported via workeroutput
right before exiting, giving accurate total memory usage.

Remove unused WorkerStats.tests field

The tests list (nodeid, duration tuples) was collected but never
included in the final JSON output. This removes the dead code and
reduces memory usage during test runs.
Update toast snapshot for dark theme webkit

Fix teardown failure handling to preserve original test result

When teardown fails after a successful test, update only the outcome
to "error" instead of creating a new TestResult. This preserves:
- The correct call-phase duration (not teardown duration)
- Avoids duplicate entries in collector.results
Add hovering reset
Update toast snapshot for dark theme webkit

title: Make st.logout use end_session_endpoint if provided in OIDC config (V2)
body:
## Describe your changes

Currently, st.logout ignores end_session_endpoint (ESE) in the OIDC configuration. 
This can cause issues, as some OIDC providers keep their own cookies and skip the login screen if they have an active session. 

This change:
a) Writes the name of provider into the session cookie (so it would be clear which provider the user is currently logged into)
b) Makes st.logout redirect to ESE if one is provided

This is a revised version of https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/11901 to adress the issues raised in https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12144#issuecomment-3172828767
Most notably:
1) client_id is added to ESE request (original issue, with attempted fix at https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/12182)
2) id_token_hint is also added (addressing https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12144#issuecomment-3172828767)
3) ESE request redirect target it changed to /oauth2callback (adressing https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12144#issuecomment-3164241186)

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/12044 

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/11900

## Testing Plan

- unit tests added both for a case where ESE is provided as well as when not

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: velochy

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

Adds support for OIDC provider logout by redirecting to the end_session_endpoint when available, and introduces infrastructure to expose selected OAuth tokens to app code via a new st.user.tokens API. Key changes include splitting user auth data and tokens into separate signed cookies, updating logout / callback flows, and adding token exposure and access utilities with accompanying tests.

- Implement provider-aware logout (uses end_session_endpoint with client_id, post_logout_redirect_uri, optional id_token_hint).
- Add tokens cookie, exposure configuration (expose_tokens), TokensProxy, and st.user.tokens accessor.
- Expand test coverage for logout flows, callback behavior, token exposure, and configuration parsing.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/server_util.py | Adds constant for separate tokens cookie. |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/oauth_authlib_routes.py | Implements provider logout logic, splits auth vs token cookies, and augments callback handling. |
| lib/streamlit/web/server/browser_websocket_handler.py | Reads tokens cookie and filters exposed tokens per configuration. |
| lib/streamlit/user_info.py | Adds TokensProxy and st.user.tokens API; updates logout docstring. |
| lib/streamlit/auth_util.py | Adds get_expose_tokens_config helper. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/web/server/oauth_authlib_routes_test.py | Adds tests for logout behavior with/without end_session_endpoint and id_token_hint. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/user_info_test.py | Adds tests for TokensProxy and st.user.tokens behavior. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/auth_util_test.py | Adds tests for expose_tokens config parsing. |
</details>
Copilot encountered an error and was unable to review this pull request. You can try again by re-requesting a review.
Overall the changes look good @velochy just one question from me before I merge. I rebased off the latest and pushed a recommended test.

Move imports to top of test file
Add client_id to OIDC logout request
Add id_token_hint to OIDC logout request
Make OIDC logout flow redirect to oauth2callback instead of root
Add unit test for malformed JSON
Remove dead code
Use convenience function for get_redirect_uri

title: Bind widgets to query params - `st.radio` & `st.selectbox`
body:
## Describe your changes
Adds the bind parameter to `st.radio` & `st.selectbox` to enable two-way sync between widget values and URL query parameters.

**Key changes:**
- Added `bind="query-params"` support to `st.radio` and `st.selectbox`
- Added centralized validation in `_seed_widget_from_url` to reject invalid URL option strings by checking against `formatted_options`. This ensures that invalid query params (e.g., `?size=Random` for a radio with options `["Small", "Medium", "Large"]`) are removed from the URL and the widget falls back to its default. This replaces the previous TODO on `formatted_options` in `WidgetMetadata`
- Clearability is tied to `index=None`: when the widget is registered with no default selection, an empty URL param (`?key=`) clears the widget to `None`. When `index` is set, empty URL params are ignored.
- For `st.selectbox` with `accept_new_options=True`, `formatted_options` is intentionally not passed, so any string value from the URL is accepted.
- `format_func` is handled naturally: the URL contains formatted option strings (what the user sees), and validation checks against the same formatted list.


## Testing Plan
- JS & Python Unit Tests: ✅ Added
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds query parameter binding functionality to `st.radio` and `st.selectbox` widgets, enabling two-way synchronization between widget values and URL query parameters. This feature was already available for other widgets like `st.checkbox`, `st.text_input`, and `st.color_picker`, and this PR extends it to selection widgets.

**Changes:**
- Added `bind` parameter to `st.radio` and `st.selectbox` with support for `"query-params"` value
- Implemented URL seeding, validation, and auto-clearing for invalid options
- Added comprehensive test coverage at all levels (Python unit tests, TypeScript unit tests, and E2E tests)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| proto/streamlit/proto/Selectbox.proto | Added `query_param_key` protobuf field (field 14) for selectbox query param binding |
| lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/radio.py | Added `bind` parameter, set `query_param_key` in proto when bound, and pass validation options to `register_widget` |
| lib/streamlit/elements/widgets/selectbox.py | Added `bind` parameter with proper handling of `accept_new_options` edge case for validation |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/session_state.py | Enhanced `_seed_widget_from_url` with validation logic for selection widgets using `formatted_options` |
| lib/streamlit/runtime/state/common.py | Updated `formatted_options` docstring to clarify its role in URL validation |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/radio_test.py | Added comprehensive unit tests for `bind` parameter functionality |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/selectbox_test.py | Added unit tests covering bind parameter with format_func, index=None, and accept_
LGTM 👍 with one aspect that might allow some simplification.

title: Add `width` and `height` configuration to `st.table`
body:
## Describe your changes

Implement configurable width and height support for `st.table` with proper scrolling behavior and sticky headers/index columns. The table element now accepts:
- `width`: `"content"` | `"stretch"` | `int` (pixels)
- `height`: `"content"` | `"stretch"` | `int` (pixels)

When a fixed pixel dimension is specified, the table enables scrolling with sticky headers (top) and index columns (left) for easy navigation through large datasets.

## Github Issues

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10775
- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10820
- Related https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/13185

## Testing Plan

- Frontend unit tests validate width/height configuration, sticky header/index positioning, and content truncation behavior
- Backend unit tests verify parameter validation and LayoutConfig serialization
- Default behavior (`width="stretch"`, `height="content"`) maintains backward compatibility

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds width/height sizing support for `st.table` across the Python API and frontend rendering so tables can be constrained (pixel, stretch, content) and support scrolling with sticky headers/index.

**Changes:**
- Backend: add `width`/`height` parameters to `st.table`, validate them, and serialize via `LayoutConfig`.
- Frontend: update `ArrowTable` styling/logic for scroll containers, sticky header/index behavior, and cell wrapping/truncation.
- Tests: add Python unit tests for layout config serialization + frontend unit tests for truncation/sticky offsets.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| table-width-height.md | Adds an implementation plan document for the feature. |
| table-sizing.md | Adds implementation notes/learned behavior for CSS sizing + sticky/scroll details. |
| lib/streamlit/elements/arrow.py | Extends `st.table` API with `width`/`height`, validates inputs, and enqueues with `LayoutConfig`. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/elements/arrow_table_test.py | Adds backend tests for width/height validation + layout config serialization. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowTable/ArrowTable.tsx | Adds width/height config props + sticky/scroll logic. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowTable/styled-components.ts | Implements scroll container changes, sticky cell styles, and wrapping/truncation rules. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowTable/ArrowTable.test.tsx | Adds frontend tests for truncation defaults and sticky offset behavior. |
| frontend/lib/src/components/core/Block/StyledElementContainerLayoutWrapper.tsx | Adds `table` to stretch sizing behavior and documents overflow handling intent. |
</details>

✅ Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no new issues!

_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

commits.message:
Add width and height configuration support to st._arrow_table

Implement configurable width and height for arrow tables with proper overflow handling and scrolling behavior. Includes styled-components updates, frontend component changes, and backend protobuf field additions with corresponding tests.

Simplify ArrowTable width/height implementation

Remove unnecessary useMemo wrappers for simple boolean coercions and
replace nested ternary with clearer getStickyType helper function.

Remove temporary planning files

Delete table-sizing.md and table-width-height.md files that are no longer needed.

Merge origin/develop into lukasmasuch/table-width-height

Resolved conflict in StyledElementContainerLayoutWrapper.tsx by adopting the new
ElementContainerConfig pattern from develop. Updated the table case in
ElementNodeRenderer.tsx to use ElementContainerConfig.LARGE_ELEMENT to preserve
the large stretch behavior for st._arrow_table.
Update st_table E2E snapshots for width/height support
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/develop' into lukasmasuch/table-width-height

Add E2E tests for st.table width/height and document fallback constants

- Add E2E tests for fixed height, width, and combined scrolling behavior
- Add test for multi-index tables with sticky index columns
- Document fallback offset constants in ArrowTable.tsx explaining their purpose
Add Linux E2E snapshots for st.table width/height tests

Increase table column max width from 20rem to 25rem

Add dedicated tableColumnMaxWidth constant to avoid affecting
the status widget which shares appStatusMaxWidth.

Fix multi-index sticky column overlap by enforcing min-width

Sticky index columns now have a min-width matching the fallback
offset (120px), ensuring consistent column widths and preventing
gaps or overlaps between sticky columns.

docs: clarify sticky index behavior in st.table height parameter

Update the height parameter documentation to accurately describe that
sticky index columns only work when horizontal scrolling is enabled
via a fixed pixel width, not just with vertical scrolling.

Update Linux E2E snapshots for st.table column width changes

Update snapshots to reflect the increased table column max width
(20rem -> 25rem) and multi-index sticky column fixes.

Simplify st.table width/height E2E tests

- Consolidate 4 separate width/height tests into 2 focused tests:
  1. Fixed dimensions with multi-index (tests scrolling in both directions
     with sticky headers and index columns)
  2. Content width sizing (tests width="content")
- Remove redundant snapshots for height-only and width-only tests
- CI will regenerate Linux snapshots for the new tests

Add Linux E2E snapshots for simplified st.table width/height tests

Add snapshots for:
- st_table-35/36: Numbered snapshots for the two new test tables
- st_table-fixed_dimensions_scrolled: Multi-index table scrolled in both directions
- st_table-content_width: Table with width="content" sizing

Fix code blocks in st.table to fill cell width

The fit-content styling on markdown containers was preventing code
blocks from expanding to fill the available cell width. Add a specific
CSS rule for pre elements to use 100% width while keeping the
fit-content behavior for regular text content.
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/table-width-height

Remove redundant test_table_content_width test

The content_width table is already covered by test_table_rendering
which iterates through all table indices including index 36.

Refactor: Use StyledStreamlitMarkdown component selector

Replace data-testid selector with direct Emotion component selector
for better type safety and maintainability.

Fix table overflow and add brief markdown test row

- Add overflow-y: hidden to markdown CSS in ArrowTable to prevent
  vertical overflow affecting layout
- Add width constraints and overflow: clip to StyledTableContainer
- Add brief markdown test row to st_table.py with key features
- Add code block to st_markdown.py test
Remove Python code block from st_markdown e2e test
Update st_table e2e test snapshots

Refactor: Use rem-based fallback offsets for sticky table positioning

Convert hardcoded pixel fallbacks to rem-based values so they scale
properly with user font size changes. The header row offset (2rem) is
now derived from theme tokens, and the index column offset (7.5rem)
scales proportionally instead of being fixed at 120px.

Fix table cell height regression from baseline shift

Add verticalAlign: top to markdown elements in table cells to prevent
the baseline shift caused by overflowY: hidden on inline-block elements.
Update st_table e2e test snapshots for cell height fix
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/table-width-height

Optimize ArrowTable perf and add a11y for scrollable tables

Precompute fallbackIndexColumnWidthPx once via useMemo to avoid redundant
DOM-based rem-to-px conversions per cell. Add tabindex, role, and aria-label
to scrollable table wrapper for keyboard navigation accessibility.

Simplify ArrowTable code and tests

- Remove redundant conditional in getStickyOffset function
- Replace if-else chain with STICKY_Z_INDEX lookup map
- Remove unnecessary toBeTruthy assertions in frontend tests
- Consolidate repetitive Python tests with parameterized.expand

Disable sticky index for multi-index tables

Sticky index columns now only work for single-index DataFrames. This
simplifies the code by removing the fallback width approximation that
was needed for positioning multiple sticky index columns.
Update st_table e2e snapshots for single-index sticky change

title: Add page visibility parameter to `st.Page`
body:
## Describe your changes

Implements the `visibility` parameter for `st.Page` to allow pages to be hidden from navigation menus while remaining accessible via direct URL, `st.page_link`, and `st.switch_page`. This addresses issues #10738 and #9195.

- [Product spec](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/5d331ef2a46f5f2053f909ac73f974f31ab3e10b/specs/0009-page-visibility/product-spec.md)
- [Tech spec](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/5d331ef2a46f5f2053f909ac73f974f31ab3e10b/specs/0009-page-visibility/tech-spec.md)

**Key changes:**
- Backend: Added `visibility` parameter with validation (visible/hidden)
- Frontend: Filtering of hidden pages in SidebarNav and TopNav components
- Tests: Comprehensive E2E and unit tests for the new feature

title: `MainMenu` accessibility improvements
body:
## Describe your changes
Adds full WAI-ARIA menu pattern support to the `MainMenu`, including keyboard navigation, proper ARIA semantics, and focus management.

**Keyboard navigation**
- **Open menu**: `Enter` / `Space` on the menu button (handles legacy `Spacebar` / `Space` key values)
- **Navigate items**: `ArrowDown` / `ArrowUp` with wrapping at boundaries
- **Jump to ends**: `Home` / `End`
- **Activate item**: `Enter` / `Space` on focused menu item
- **Close menu**: `Escape` returns focus to the trigger; `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` close the menu and advance focus to the next/previous tabbable element
- **Disabled items**: All items are focusable and navigable, including disabled ones (per [WAI-ARIA menuitem role guidance](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/menuitem_role)). Disabled items use `aria-disabled` instead of the native `disabled` attribute; clicks are blocked via the `onClick` handler.

**ARIA attributes**
- Menu button: `aria-haspopup="menu"`, `aria-expanded` (tracks open/close state), `aria-label="Main menu"`
- Menu container: `role="menu"`, `aria-label="Main menu"`
- Menu items: `role="menuitem"`, `aria-disabled` for disabled items (native `disabled` removed to keep items focusable)
- Dividers: `role="separator"`, `aria-hidden="true"`
- Roving tabindex: focused item gets `tabIndex={0}`, all others get `tabIndex={-1}`

**Focus management**
- Focus moves to the first menu item when the menu opens
- Focus returns to the menu button after `Escape` or item click, via react-focus-lock's `returnFocus` callback
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` close the menu and programmatically advance focus to the next/previous tabbable element using `focusNextElement`/`focusPrevElement` from `focus-lock`
- **WebKit limitation**: On Safari, focus restoration after menu close may not work because react-focus-lock invokes the `returnFocus` callback after BaseWeb's close animation timer, which puts the `.focus()` call outside the user-activation context

**Clear cache dialog (WAI-ARIA best practice)**
- Moves `autoFocus` from the destructive "Clear caches" button to the non-destructive "Cancel" button, per WAI-ARIA guidance for confirmation dialogs
- Removes the now-unnecessary `defaultAction` prop from `ClearCacheProps`

**Theme fix**
- `focusRingMuted` shadow now uses `gray10` on dark backgrounds (was using `gray90`, which was invisible)

## Testing Plan
- JS Unit Tests: ✅  Added
- E2E Tests: ✅ Added
- Manual Testing: ✅

title: Initialize all columns when appending rows to `data_editor`
body:
## Describe your changes

When a user adds a row to a `data_editor` with `num_rows='dynamic'` and then shows a previously hidden column (like the index column) via the "show/hide columns" menu, the app crashes with "No cell found for an added row" error.

**Root cause:** Cells were only initialized for currently visible columns when a row was added. When hidden columns were later made visible, the cells for those columns didn't exist, causing the error.

**Fix:** Initialize cells for all columns (including hidden ones) when a new row is appended. This is achieved by using `allColumns` instead of `columns` in the `appendEmptyRow` function.

## GitHub Issue Link

Fixes #13915

## Testing Plan

- **Unit tests**: Added a new test "creates cells for hidden columns when row is appended (issue #13915)" that simulates the exact bug scenario with hidden columns and verifies cells are created for all columns.
- **Existing tests**: Updated all 16 existing useDataEditor tests to include the new `allColumns` parameter. All tests pass.
- **Manual testing**: Verified the fix with Playwright script that reproduces the bug scenario (add row → show hidden index column → no error).

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

commits.message:
Fix issue #13915: Initialize all columns when appending rows to data_editor

When a user adds a row to a data_editor with num_rows='dynamic', cells were only
created for currently visible columns. If the index column was hidden when adding
the row, no cell was created for it. Later, when showing the hidden column via the
"show/hide columns" menu, the code tried to access the cell but it didn't exist,
causing the error "No cell found for an added row".

The fix ensures cells are initialized for all columns (including hidden ones) when
a new row is added. This allows columns to be shown/hidden without errors.

Changes:
- useDataEditor: Use allColumns instead of columns when initializing new rows
- DataFrame: Pass allColumns parameter to useDataEditor
- Tests: Updated all test cases and added a new test for this specific scenario

Address PR review comments: add baseline assertion and issue reference

- Add baseline assertion in test to verify row count before onRowAppended()
- Add reference to issue #13915 in code comment for better context
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/fix-issue-13915

title: Add spec PR validation workflow and simplify process
body:
## Describe your changes

Added a GitHub Actions workflow to validate product spec PRs marked with the `change:spec` label. The workflow enforces three validation requirements: 1) specs use the next available number (4-digit NNNN format), 2) frontmatter status is set to "Approved", and 3) frontmatter contains valid author and created date fields (YYYY-MM-DD format).

The workflow posts validation results as a PR comment and blocks merging if any errors are found. Updated specs (reusing existing numbers) are allowed with warnings.

## Testing Plan

- The workflow will run automatically on PRs with `specs/**` changes and the `change:spec` label
- Manual testing: add test specs with various validation states to confirm the workflow behaves correctly
- No additional tests needed as this is a new CI/CD workflow

---

title: [feat] Add external URL runner in e2e playwright
body:
## Describe your changes

Added support for running Playwright E2E tests against **externally hosted** Streamlit apps (instead of starting a local app server).

- **External app / host modes**
  - `--external-app-url` (or `STREAMLIT_E2E_EXTERNAL_APP_URL`) to target an already-running app URL
  - Optional `--external-host-url` (or `STREAMLIT_E2E_EXTERNAL_HOST_URL`) for cases where the app is embedded in a host page
  - Optional `--external-iframe-selector` (or `STREAMLIT_E2E_EXTERNAL_IFRAME_SELECTOR`, default `iframe`) to locate the embedded app iframe
- **Authenticated browser sessions**
  - `--browser-state-path` (or `STREAMLIT_E2E_BROWSER_STATE_PATH`) to load a Playwright storage state JSON file
- Introduced a new `external_test` marker (`@pytest.mark.external_test`) for tests that are compatible with external mode.
- When external mode is enabled, tests **not** marked `external_test` are skipped; if no tests are marked, pytest fails early with a clear usage error to avoid silently skipping the entire suite.
- Added shared helpers to read configuration from either CLI options or environment variables.
- Updated app navigation URL construction to preserve existing trailing-slash behavior while handling optional URL fragments cleanly.

## Testing Plan

- The usual CI pipeline validates the existing tests.
- The changes have been manually tested by running the mega tester app test with the external app URL option.

---

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
