title: Fix material icon font download script
body:
## Describe your changes

Fixed the material icon font download script that was failing to extract the font URL. Google Fonts changed the URL structure from `/s/materialsymbolsrounded/` to `/l/font?kit=` format. Also updated the User-Agent from Safari to Chrome to ensure `.woff2` format is returned instead of `.woff`.

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

Related to the issue where `update_material_icon_font_and_names.py` was failing with "URL not found".

## Testing Plan

- Script has been tested to successfully extract the font URL and download the `.woff2` file.
- No additional tests required as this is a maintenance script that's run manually.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a broken maintenance script that downloads Material Icons font files from Google Fonts. Google changed their URL structure from `/s/materialsymbolsrounded/` to `/l/font?kit=` format, requiring updates to the regex pattern used to extract font URLs. Additionally, the User-Agent was changed from Safari to Chrome to ensure `.woff2` format is returned.

**Changes:**
- Updated regex pattern to be more general and match the new Google Fonts URL structure
- Changed User-Agent header from Safari to Chrome to ensure `.woff2` format is downloaded

title: Extract `st.feedback` into its own proto and component
body:
## Describe your changes

Extracted `st.feedback` from `st.button_group` into a dedicated Feedback widget. The feedback type (thumbs, faces, stars) is now sent via a new Feedback.proto instead of sending individual icon strings. The frontend constructs the correct options based on the feedback type, eliminating the dependency on baseui's ButtonGroup component. Widget state continues to return an integer rating value. Removed the BORDERLESS style from ButtonGroup which was only used by feedback.

Reasons for extraction:
- Feedback is sufficiently different from pills and segmented control to get its own frontend component. 
- Made it difficult to unlock `options` of pills and segmented control for dynamic changes.
- Proper accessibility support.
- No more dependency on Baseweb.

## Testing Plan

- Added unit and e2e tests.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extracts `st.feedback` into its own widget and protobuf message, decoupling it from `ButtonGroup` and BaseWeb while preserving the Python API and integer rating semantics.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a new `Feedback` protobuf message and wires it into the `Element` union and backend `FeedbackMixin`, with a dedicated serde and layout behavior.
- Adds a standalone frontend `Feedback` React component (with styling, keyboard navigation, and test IDs) and routes the new `feedback` element type through `ElementNodeRenderer`.
- Removes `st.feedback`-specific logic and the `BORDERLESS` style from `ButtonGroup` (backend and frontend), updates button group tests accordingly, and updates E2E tests plus Playwright helpers to target the new `stFeedback` widget.

Notable issues to address:
- `lib/tests/streamlit/elements/button_group_test.py` and `lib/tests/streamlit/testing/element_tree_test.py` still assert that `st.feedback` produces a `ButtonGroup` with `Style.BORDERLESS`; these tests need to be updated to the new `Feedback` element and enum layout.
- The new `Feedback` widget lacks a dedicated frontend unit test, and the accessible naming for individual feedback options is currently non-descriptive (“Rating 1/2/…”) rather than expressing the meaning of each choice (e.g. thumbs up/down or star count).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/Feedback.proto` | Adds a new `Feedback` message with a `FeedbackType` enum (`THUMBS`, `FACES`, `STARS`), default/value fields, and wiring for widget state. |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/Element.proto` | Imports `Feedback.proto` and adds `feedback = 63` to the `Element` oneof, updating the next-ID comment. |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/ButtonGroup.proto` | Removes the `BORDERLESS` style (now reserved value 2) with

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

commits.message:
Extract st.feedback into its own proto and component

Separate st.feedback from st.button_group into a dedicated Feedback widget. The feedback type (thumbs, faces, stars) is now sent via proto instead of individual icon strings. The frontend constructs the correct options based on the feedback type without relying on baseui. Widget state continues to send back an integer rating value. Removed BORDERLESS style from ButtonGroup which was only used by feedback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI failures for st.feedback extraction

- Fix mypy unreachable statement error in feedback.py by removing
  unnecessary default return after all Literal cases handled
- Update typing/feedback.py to import FeedbackMixin instead of
  ButtonGroupMixin
- Update button_group_test.py:
  - Remove imports of deleted constants
  - Remove TestGetMappedOptions class (tests removed function)
  - Update feedback tests to use feedback proto instead of button_group
  - Split shared tests to have separate feedback-specific tests
  - Update test_invalid_style error message

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI failures for st.feedback testing framework support

- Remove st.feedback from test_widget_id_computation_options_widgets
  since it now uses its own module instead of button_group
- Add Feedback element class to testing framework (element_tree.py)
- Add feedback property to ElementTree and AppTest classes
- Update test_button_group_feedback to test_feedback_element
- Fix lint errors in Feedback.tsx:
  - Use optional chain expression for button focus
  - Use option.value as key instead of array index

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ButtonGroup tests after BORDERLESS style removal

- Update expectHighlightStyle to check for primary color instead of
  bodyText color for selected buttons
- Remove "shows bigger icons for borderless ButtonGroup" test since
  BORDERLESS style is no longer used
- Update "shows selection content..." test to expect segmented_control
  kind instead of borderlessIcon
- Rename "shows smaller icons for non-borderless ButtonGroup" to
  "shows icons with correct size for segmented control ButtonGroup"
- Update comment for materialIconOnlyOptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address PR review comments for st.feedback

- Use _NUM_STARS_OPTIONS constant in _get_num_options
- Replace ellipsis with pass in overload definitions
- Remove unneeded null check in Feedback.tsx handleKeyDown
- Add descriptive aria-labels for better accessibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mypy unreachable code error in feedback widget

Remove unreachable fallback return statement in _get_num_options function.
Since the parameter is typed as Literal["thumbs", "faces", "stars"], mypy
correctly identifies that all cases are handled by the preceding if statements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tests and cleanup for st.feedback component

- Add comprehensive frontend unit tests for Feedback component (35 tests)
  covering rendering, selection behavior, stars visualization, disabled
  state, keyboard navigation, and accessibility attributes
- Add separate Python test file for st.feedback with tests for serde,
  command options, width configuration, and widget ID stability
- Remove feedback-migration.md planning document from PR
- Change pass to ... in @overload method bodies per PEP 484

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ESLint errors in Feedback tests

Remove unused async/await and userEvent variable in test that checks
default selection state (no user interaction needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Update
Update
Fix
Update
Fix
Update
Fix initial sate with disabled elements
Remove high-level min width carve out
Update
Merge branch 'develop' into lukasmasuch/feedback-component

Add Final annotations and remove Java proto options

- Add Final type annotations to feedback constants
- Remove unused java_package and java_outer_classname options from Feedback.proto

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix misleading test name for width configuration test

Rename test from "renders with content width by default" to
"renders with content width when configured" to accurately reflect
that the test explicitly sets useContent: true, not testing defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address PR review comments for feedback tests

- Fix incorrect comment in widgets_test.py that wrongly stated
  st.segmented_control does not take a label
- Remove duplicate feedback tests from button_group_test.py that
  already exist in feedback_test.py
- Clean up unused imports (FeedbackProto, Literal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-5) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Bump ty from 0.0.13 to 0.0.14
body:
Bumps [ty](https://github.com/astral-sh/ty) from 0.0.13 to 0.0.14.
Release notes

Sourced from ty's releases.

0.0.14

Release Notes

Released on 2026-01-26.

Bug fixes

  • Consider keyword arguments when unpacking a variadic argument (#22796)
  • Fix binary operator false-positive for constrained TypeVars (#22782)
  • Fix docstring rendering for literal blocks after doctests (#22676)
  • Fix false-positive unsupported-operator for "symmetric" TypeVars (#22756)
  • Fix panic when overriding a final method using an assignment (#22831)
  • Fix unary operator false-positive for constrained TypeVars (#22783)
  • Fix generic functions with a generic (ParamSpec) decorator (#22544)
  • Fix memo.changed_at assertion panics (#22498)

LSP server

  • Look up attributes on metaclasses for Go to Definition (#22758)
  • Suppress type inlay hints for leading-underscore assignments (#22855)

Configuration

  • Add allowed-unresolved-imports setting (#22800)

Other changes

  • Add assert-type-unspellable-subtype diagnostic, for failed assert_type() where the actual type is a subtype of the named type that can't be spelled in a type expression (#22815)
  • Add a new empty-body return code for functions with stub bodies that have non-None return annotations (#22846)
  • Add diagnostic disambiguation for different type aliases with the same name (#22852)
  • Add support for dict literals and dict() calls as default values for parameters with TypedDict types (#22161)
  • Add support for subscripts on intersections (#22654)
  • Avoid duplicate syntax errors for await outside functions (#22826)
  • Emit an error if the same type parameter appears more than once in a Generic[] subscript (#22738)
  • Emit diagnostic for unimplemented abstract method on @​final class (#22753)
  • Fix GitLab Code Quality output format for empty diagnostics (#22833)
  • Fix assignment in decorated method causing Unknown fallback (#22778)
  • Fix false negative when using a non-runtime-checkable protocol in a match class pattern (#22836)
  • Improve completion rankings for raise-from/except contexts (#22775)
  • Improve invalid assignment diagnostics with type context (#22643)
  • Improve support for kwarg splats in dictionary literals (#22781)
  • Infer TypedDict types with >=1 required key as being always truthy (#22808)
  • Point to an overload with an invalid @final decoator when emitting invalid-overload errors for invalid @final decorators (#22812)
  • Require both *args and **kwargs when calling a ParamSpec callable (#22820)
  • Stricter validation of TypedDict definitions (#22811)
  • Support recursive and stringified annotations in functional typing.NamedTuples (#22718)
  • Support solving generics involving PEP 695 type aliases (#22678)
  • Use a more lenient fallback type for failed namedtuple() and NamedTuple calls (#22765)
  • Use type context from augmented assignment dunder calls (#22540)
  • Check that starred arguments in function calls are iterable (#22805)

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from ty's changelog.

0.0.14

Released on 2026-01-26.

Bug fixes

  • Consider keyword arguments when unpacking a variadic argument (#22796)
  • Fix binary operator false-positive for constrained TypeVars (#22782)
  • Fix docstring rendering for literal blocks after doctests (#22676)
  • Fix false-positive unsupported-operator for "symmetric" TypeVars (#22756)
  • Fix panic when overriding a final method using an assignment (#22831)
  • Fix unary operator false-positive for constrained TypeVars (#22783)
  • Fix generic functions with a generic (ParamSpec) decorator (#22544)
  • Fix memo.changed_at assertion panics (#22498)

LSP server

  • Look up attributes on metaclasses for Go to Definition (#22758)
  • Suppress type inlay hints for leading-underscore assignments (#22855)

Configuration

  • Add allowed-unresolved-imports setting (#22800)

Other changes

  • Add assert-type-unspellable-subtype diagnostic, for failed assert_type() where the actual type is a subtype of the named type that can't be spelled in a type expression (#22815)
  • Add a new empty-body return code for functions with stub bodies that have non-None return annotations (#22846)
  • Add diagnostic disambiguation for different type aliases with the same name (#22852)
  • Add support for dict literals and dict() calls as default values for parameters with TypedDict types (#22161)
  • Add support for subscripts on intersections (#22654)
  • Avoid duplicate syntax errors for await outside functions (#22826)
  • Emit an error if the same type parameter appears more than once in a Generic[] subscript (#22738)
  • Emit diagnostic for unimplemented abstract method on @​final class (#22753)
  • Fix GitLab Code Quality output format for empty diagnostics (#22833)
  • Fix assignment in decorated method causing Unknown fallback (#22778)
  • Fix false negative when using a non-runtime-checkable protocol in a match class pattern (#22836)
  • Improve completion rankings for raise-from/except contexts (#22775)
  • Improve invalid assignment diagnostics with type context (#22643)
  • Improve support for kwarg splats in dictionary literals (#22781)
  • Infer TypedDict types with >=1 required key as being always truthy (#22808)
  • Point to an overload with an invalid @final decoator when emitting invalid-overload errors for invalid @final decorators (#22812)
  • Require both *args and **kwargs when calling a ParamSpec callable (#22820)
  • Stricter validation of TypedDict definitions (#22811)
  • Support recursive and stringified annotations in functional typing.NamedTuples (#22718)
  • Support solving generics involving PEP 695 type aliases (#22678)
  • Use a more lenient fallback type for failed namedtuple() and NamedTuple calls (#22765)
  • Use type context from augmented assignment dunder calls (#22540)
  • Check that starred arguments in function calls are iterable (#22805)

... (truncated)

  • 342e6b3 Update actions/checkout action to v6.0.2 (#2624)
  • a390952 Update prek dependencies (#2625)
  • 1d3d416 Update actions/cache action to v5.0.2 (#2623)

Release notes

Sourced from @​typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin's releases.

v8.54.0

🚀 Features

  • eslint-plugin-internal: add prefer-tsutils-methods rule (#11974, #11625)
  • scope-manager: support ScopeManager#addGlobals (#11914)
  • typescript-estree: add shortcut methods to ParserServicesWithTypeInformation (#11965, #11955)

  • eslint-plugin: [no-unused-private-class-members] private destructured class member is defined but used (#11785)

  • scope-manager: fix catch clause scopes def.name (#11982)

title: Extend sprintf to support thousand separator
body:
## Describe your changes

Vendors in the [sprintf-js](https://github.com/alexei/sprintf.js) library (only a single file) and modifies it slightly to support setting the thousand separator in the [python printf format](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_string_formatting.asp):

```
1234.56 -> %,.2f -> 1,234.56
1234.56 -> %_.2f -> 1_234.56
```

## GitHub Issue Link (if applicable)

- Closes https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/1301

## Testing Plan

- Added unit tests.

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR vendors the sprintf-js library (converting it to TypeScript) and extends it with thousand separator support using `,` and `_` flags, mirroring Python's format mini-language (e.g., `f"{x:,}"` and `f"{x:_}"`).

**Key changes:**
- Vendored sprintf-js as TypeScript with thousand separator support (`%,d` for comma, `%_d` for underscore)
- Removed npm dependency on sprintf-js package
- Updated all import statements to use vendored version
- Added comprehensive test coverage for new formatting features

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 15 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| frontend/yarn.lock | Removed sprintf-js and @types/sprintf-js dependencies from lockfile |
| frontend/lib/src/vendor/sprintf.js/sprintfjs.ts | New vendored sprintf implementation with thousand separator support |
| frontend/lib/src/vendor/sprintf.js/sprintfjs.test.ts | Comprehensive tests for sprintf functionality including new thousand separator formats |
| frontend/lib/src/vendor/sprintf.js/sprintfjs-LICENSE.txt | BSD-3-Clause license file for vendored sprintf library |
| frontend/lib/src/vendor/fzy.js/fuzzySearch.ts | Removed Streamlit copyright header and reordered exports (cosmetic changes to vendored file) |
| frontend/lib/src/vendor/fzy.js/fuzzySearch.test.ts | Removed Streamlit copyright header (cosmetic change to vendored file) |
| frontend/lib/src/util/formatNumber.ts | Updated import to use vendored sprintf; added documentation for thousand separator flags |
| frontend/lib/src/util/formatNumber.test.ts | Added test cases for thousand separator formatting, removed now-valid test cases from invalid list |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/Slider/Slider.tsx | Updated import to use vendored sprintf |
| frontend/lib/src/components/widgets/NumberInput/utils.ts | Updated import to use vendored sprintf |
| front

title: [spec] Dynamic Expanders, Tabs and Popovers
body:
This PR adds a product specification for implementing dynamic (lazy-loading) tabs, expanders, and popovers in Streamlit. The spec proposes adding an on_change parameter and .open attribute to enable conditional execution of content only when tabs/expanders/popovers are active/open, addressing performance issues when multiple tabs contain expensive operations.

Changes:

Adds comprehensive product specification documenting API design, behavior, parameters, and implementation rationale for the API design.

Includes detailed examples, comparison with alternative approaches, and references to related GitHub issues and PRs

Rendered spec: https://issues.streamlit.app/spec_renderer?pr=13587

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.
author: sfc-gh-lwilby

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a product specification for implementing dynamic (lazy-loading) tabs, expanders, and popovers in Streamlit. The spec proposes adding an `on_change` parameter and `.open` attribute to enable conditional execution of content only when tabs/expanders/popovers are active/open, addressing performance issues when multiple tabs contain expensive operations.

**Changes:**
- Adds comprehensive product specification documenting API design, behavior, parameters, and implementation rationale for Option 1b approach
- Includes detailed examples, comparison with alternative approaches, and references to related GitHub issues and PRs
Looks good to me overall, but one concern about adding `open` to the shared DeltaGenerator. I think we should follow the existing pattern of using a dedicated class for every element.
Generally looks good, let's figure out the details from the comments, and then this should be ready to merge :)
LGTM 👍 great spec for a very impactful feature!
LGTM. Only important comment above is on when we ship callbacks, but we can discuss that outside of the spec. Great work!

title: Improve `streamlit config show` output for theme sourcing
body:
## Describe your changes
This improves the theme `where_defined` labels that surface when running `streamlit config show`, so users can distinguish between values coming from a base theme file, config.toml overrides (with scope), and CLI/env overrides.

**Note**: config.toml (script) only appears when a main script path is set (e.g., via streamlit run); streamlit config show typically includes only global + project config scopes.

**Before Examples:**
- Base theme file value: `theme file: /path/to/theme.toml`
- Config.toml override (global): `theme file: /path/to/theme.toml`
- Config.toml override (project): `theme file: /path/to/theme.toml`
- CLI/env override: `command-line argument or environment variable` (this was already accurate)

**After**
- Base theme file value: `base theme file: /path/to/theme.toml`
- Config.toml override (global): `config.toml (global): /path/to/global/config.toml`
- Config.toml override (project): `config.toml (project): /path/to/project/config.toml`
- CLI/env override: `command-line argument or environment variable`
- Defaulted base (no base in theme file): `default light theme`

## Testing Plan
- Python Unit Tests: ✅ 
- Manual Testing: ✅
author: mayagbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Improves `streamlit config show` provenance strings for theme-related config values so users can distinguish values coming from a base theme file vs config.toml overrides (with scope) vs CLI/env overrides.

**Changes:**
- Add scope-aware labeling for theme overrides sourced from config.toml during theme inheritance processing.
- Update theme inheritance to label base theme file sources distinctly and to surface a “default light theme” provenance when no base is provided in the theme file.
- Extend and adjust unit tests to assert the new `where_defined` outputs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| lib/streamlit/config_util.py | Adds scope-label formatting for config.toml paths and updates theme inheritance to emit improved `where_defined` strings. |
| lib/streamlit/config.py | Passes the ordered config.toml path list into theme inheritance so scope labeling is possible. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/config_util_test.py | Adds unit tests for config scope labeling and updates theme inheritance unit tests to validate new provenance strings. |
| lib/tests/streamlit/config_test.py | Updates integration tests to assert the new `where_defined` outputs for theme inheritance scenarios. |

Consolidates duplicated named color mapping logic into a single source of truth (`namedColors.ts`), improving maintainability and type safety.

This PR consolidates duplicated named color mapping logic across the codebase into a single source of truth (`namedColors.ts`), improving maintainability and type safety. title: Fix nightly build package name mismatch with uv
body:
## Describe your changes

After the uv migration (PR #13622), the nightly build fails when it attempts to rename the package to `streamlit-nightly`. The error occurs because uv validates that the package name in the source matches the reference name. This change fixes that by updating the root `pyproject.toml` alongside the package name change.

The fix updates `update_name.py` to modify `[tool.uv.sources]` and dependency-groups in the root `pyproject.toml` when the package name changes. The nightly workflow is also updated to include the root `pyproject.toml` in the git commit.

## Testing Plan

- This fixes the nightly build process
- No new tests needed; the fix is tested by the existing nightly CI workflow
- The script passes ruff lint and mypy type checks

---

**Contribution License Agreement**

By submitting this pull request you agree that all contributions to this project are made under the Apache 2.0 license.

**Changes:**
- Extend `scripts/update_name.py` to update `[tool.uv.sources]` and dependency-groups in the root `pyproject.toml` when renaming the package.
- Update the nightly workflow to include the root `pyproject.toml` in the commit created for the nightly tag.

Fix nightly build package name mismatch with uv migration

After the uv migration (PR #13622), the nightly build fails with
"Package metadata name `streamlit-nightly` does not match given name `streamlit`".

This occurs because update_name.py changes lib/pyproject.toml to use
streamlit-nightly, but the root pyproject.toml still references it as
"streamlit" in [tool.uv.sources] and dependency-groups.

Update update_name.py to also update the root pyproject.toml when
changing the package name, and include it in the nightly workflow's
git add command.

title: Remove deprecated and unused proto messages and fields
body:
## Describe your changes

This PR removes deprecated protobuf definitions and unused fields that have been replaced by newer implementations:

- Deletes 4 unused proto files: `DataFrame.proto`, `VegaLiteChart.proto`, `NamedDataSet.proto`, and `PagesChanged.proto`
- Removes 2 unused messages: `Figure` and `ElementDimensionSpec`
- Removes 24 unused fields from proto definitions across multiple proto files
- Updates frontend components to remove fallback code for deprecated proto fields (ImageList, DataFrame hooks)
- Adds comprehensive proto usage analysis documentation

All deprecated field numbers are reserved for backward protocol compatibility.

## Testing Plan

- Proto cleanup is backward compatible via reserved field numbers
- Frontend components already use the new proto structure (Element.widthConfig/heightConfig)
- Existing unit tests in proto_compatibility_test.py verify backward compatibility
- No additional tests needed as this is a cleanup of deprecated code
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

## Pull request overview

This PR cleans up deprecated and unused protobuf definitions and removes frontend fallbacks that depended on those legacy fields, consolidating everything onto the Arrow-based and layout-config-based protocols. It also adds a human-readable inventory of removed and reserved fields/messages.

**Changes:**
- Deletes fully deprecated proto files (`DataFrame.proto`, `VegaLiteChart.proto`, `NamedDataSet.proto`, `PagesChanged.proto`) and removes unused messages/fields from remaining protos, reserving their field numbers for wire compatibility in most cases.
- Updates core proto messages (`Element`, `ForwardMsg`, `Delta`, `Arrow`, chart and widget protos, etc.) to drop legacy fields in favor of `WidthConfig`/`HeightConfig` and Arrow-based dataset messages, and adds `reserved` entries where appropriate.
- Simplifies DataFrame and ImageList frontend logic and tests to rely solely on `widthConfig`/`heightConfig`, removing legacy fallbacks to deprecated proto fields and behavior enums.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 29 out of 29 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `unused-deprecated-protos.md` | Documents which proto files/messages/fields were removed and which field numbers are reserved; currently overstates reservation of `value` fields for select widgets, which do not yet have `reserved` entries in the `.proto` files. |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/VegaLiteChart.proto` | Removes deprecated, unused VegaLiteChart message in favor of Arrow-based `ArrowVegaLiteChart`. |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/TextArea.proto` | Drops deprecated `height` field and reserves field number/name, relying on `HeightConfig`/layout config for sizing. |
| `proto/streamlit/proto/Selectbox.proto` | Removes deprecated int32 `value` field after migration to string `raw_value`, but does not reserve field number/name 7, creating a wire-compatibilit
Looks good to me mostly, but just a small concern about double checking the expected behaviour.

- Delete 4 unused proto files: DataFrame.proto, VegaLiteChart.proto, NamedDataSet.proto, PagesChanged.proto
- Remove 2 unused messages: Figure, ElementDimensionSpec
- Remove 24 unused fields from proto definitions
- Update frontend components to remove fallback code for deprecated proto fields
- Add proto usage analysis documentation

These changes are backward compatible via reserved field numbers.

title: Bump axios from 1.13.6 to 1.14.0 in /frontend
body:
Bumps [axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) from 1.13.6 to 1.14.0.
Release notes

Sourced from axios's releases.

v1.14.0

This release focuses on compatibility fixes, adapter stability improvements, and test/tooling modernisation.

⚠️ Important Changes

  • Breaking Changes: None identified in this release.
  • Action Required: If you rely on env-based proxy behaviour or CJS resolution edge-cases, validate your integration after upgrade (notably proxy-from-env v2 alignment and main entry compatibility fix).

🚀 New Features

  • Runtime Features: No new end-user features were introduced in this release.
  • Test Coverage Expansion: Added broader smoke/module test coverage for CJS and ESM package usage. (#7510)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Headers: Trim trailing CRLF in normalised header values. (#7456)
  • HTTP/2: Close detached HTTP/2 sessions on timeout to avoid lingering sessions. (#7457)
  • Fetch Adapter: Cancel ReadableStream created during request-stream capability probing to prevent async resource leaks. (#7515)
  • Proxy Handling: Fixed env proxy behavior with proxy-from-env v2 usage. (#7499)
  • CommonJS Compatibility: Fixed package main entry regression affecting CJS consumers. (#7532)

🔧 Maintenance & Chores

  • Security/Dependencies: Updated formidable and refreshed package set to newer versions. (#7533, #10556)
  • Tooling: Continued migration to Vitest and modernised CI/test harnesses. (#7484, #7489, #7498)
  • Build/Lint Stack: Rollup, ESLint, TypeScript, and related dev-dependency updates. (#7508, #7509, #7522)
  • Documentation: Clarified JSON parsing and adapter-related docs/comments. (#7398, #7460, #7478)

🌟 New Contributors

We are thrilled to welcome our new contributors. Thank you for helping improve Axios:

title: [chore] Add dependency update cooldown for uv and yarn
body:
## Describe your changes

Adds a 24-hour cooldown period for newly published packages to mitigate supply chain attacks:

- **uv (Python)**: Adds `exclude-newer = "24 hours"` to `pyproject.toml`
- **yarn (frontend)**: Adds `npmMinimalAgeGate: 1440` (24 hours in minutes) to `.yarnrc.yml`

Also updates yarn from 4.5.3 to 4.13.0 across all workspaces for `npmMinimalAgeGate` support.

## Testing Plan

- No code changes, only package manager configuration
- Verified `make autofix` and `make check` pass
author: lukasmasuch

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds “cooldown”/age-gating for newly published dependencies to reduce supply-chain risk across Python (uv) and frontend (Yarn), alongside a Yarn version bump needed to support the new Yarn setting.

**Changes:**
- Add `exclude-newer = "3 days"` to `[tool.uv]` in `pyproject.toml`.
- Add `npmMinimalAgeGate: 4320` to `frontend/.yarnrc.yml`.
- Bump Yarn `packageManager` from `4.5.3` to `4.13.0` in the frontend root and component library packages; update `NOTICES` accordingly.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| pyproject.toml | Configure uv to exclude packages published within the last 3 days. |
| frontend/.yarnrc.yml | Configure Yarn to enforce a 3-day minimal publish age gate for npm packages. |
| frontend/package.json | Bump declared Yarn version to 4.13.0 (Corepack-driven). |
| frontend/component-lib/package.json | Align declared Yarn version to 4.13.0. |
| frontend/component-v2-lib/package.json | Align declared Yarn version to 4.13.0. |
| NOTICES | Update third-party notices to reflect dependency graph changes (but currently appears inconsistent with the lockfile). |
</details>

## Summary

This PR adds supply-chain hardening by introducing a 3-day publication cooldown for dependency resolution in both package managers:

- **uv (Python):** Adds `exclude-newer = "3 days"` to `pyproject.toml`, preventing uv from resolving packages published within the last 3 days.
- **yarn (frontend):** Adds `npmMinimalAgeGate: 4320` (3 days in minutes) to `frontend/.yarnrc.yml`, blocking recently published npm packages.

The yarn version is bumped from 4.5.3 to 4.13.0 across all three workspace `package.json` files that declare `packageManager` (`frontend/package.json`, `frontend/component-lib/package.json`, `frontend/component-v2-lib/package.json`), since `npmMinimalAgeGate` requires yarn >= 4.10.0. The `NOTICES` file is regenerated to reflect license metadata changes under the new yarn version.

**Reviewer consensus:** 2 of 3 reviewers completed their reviews; both unanimously approved. 1 reviewer (`gemini-3.1-pro`) failed to complete its review.

## Code Quality

Both reviewers agreed the changes are clean, minimal, and correctly placed. The consolidated assessment:

- `pyproject.toml`: The `exclude-newer = "3 days"` setting uses a documented "friendly duration" format. The pre-existing `required-version = ">=0.9.0"` ensures all developers have a uv version supporting this syntax.
- `frontend/.yarnrc.yml`: The `npmMinimalAgeGate: 4320` value correctly converts to 72 hours (3 days). Placed in the root `.yarnrc.yml` where it applies to all workspaces.
- `packageManager` fields are consistently updated to `yarn@4.13.0` in all three `package.json` files that declare it. No files were missed.
- `NOTICES` changes are auto-generated by the license plugin under the new yarn version. Minor differences in grouped entries are expected and not a correctness issue.
- Comments on both configuration entries are clear and explain the security rationale.

## Test Coverage

Both reviewers agreed: no tests are needed. This PR modifies only package manager configuration file

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).

## Summary

This PR adds a 3-day cooldown period for newly published packages to mitigate supply chain attacks, applying to both package managers used in the project:

- **uv (Python):** Adds `exclude-newer = "3 days"` to `pyproject.toml`, preventing uv from resolving packages published within the last 3 days.
- **yarn (frontend):** Adds `npmMinimalAgeGate: 4320` (3 days in minutes) to `frontend/.yarnrc.yml`, preventing installation of npm packages published within the last 3 days.

To support `npmMinimalAgeGate` (introduced in Yarn 4.10), Yarn is upgraded from 4.5.3 to 4.13.0 across all workspace `package.json` files. The `NOTICES` file is regenerated to reflect the updated dependency tree.

**Reviewer consensus:** All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) approved unanimously with no disagreements on any aspect of the PR.

## Code Quality

The changes are minimal, cohesive, and follow existing repo conventions:

- **`pyproject.toml`**: The `exclude-newer = "3 days"` setting uses uv's documented "friendly duration" format, placed logically next to the existing `required-version` setting with a clear explanatory comment.
- **`frontend/.yarnrc.yml`**: The `npmMinimalAgeGate: 4320` value correctly represents 3 days in minutes (3 × 24 × 60 = 4320), with a matching comment.
- **`packageManager` updates**: All three `package.json` files (`frontend/`, `frontend/component-lib/`, `frontend/component-v2-lib/`) are consistently updated from `yarn@4.5.3` to `yarn@4.13.0`.
- **`NOTICES`**: License file regeneration reflecting minor dependency tree changes from the updated Yarn version — no functional impact.

No structural or maintainability issues were identified. All reviewers agreed the changes are clean and well-scoped.

## Test Coverage

No tests are needed — these are purely package manager configuration changes with no impact on application code or behavior. The PR description confirms that `make autofix` and `make check` pas

commits.message:
Add supply chain security cooldown for uv and yarn

Configure both package managers to exclude newly published packages:
- uv: exclude-newer = 3 days in pyproject.toml
- yarn: npmMinimalAgeGate = 4320 (3 days in minutes)

Also update yarn from 4.5.3 to 4.13.0 for npmMinimalAgeGate support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Change cooldown period from 3 days to 2 days

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Change cooldown period to 24 hours

title: [docs] AI-assisted documentation updates
body:
## Summary

Automated PR to update internal documentation based on AI review.

This PR was created by the `ai-update-docs` workflow using `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.

## Changes

# Documentation Review Summary

Reviewed 44 internal documentation files across the repository against the current codebase state.

## Files Reviewed

- 11 AGENTS.md files (root, frontend, lib, lib/streamlit, lib/tests, proto, e2e_playwright, scripts, specs, .github/workflows, .claude/skills)
- 10 README.md files (root, wiki, specs, frontend, frontend/component-lib, frontend/component-v2-lib, frontend/eslint-plugin-streamlit-custom, e2e_playwright, e2e_playwright/load_testing, lib/tests/streamlit/typing)
- 15 SKILL.md files (all skills in .claude/skills/)
- 3 agent .md files (.claude/agents/)
- 5 wiki .md files (code-style-guide, new-feature-guide, running-e2e-tests, pull-requests, README)
- CONTRIBUTING.md

## Changes Made

### 1. [OUTDATED] `frontend/README.md`
- **Issue**: Missing `component-lib` from the packages list. The `frontend/package.json` workspaces includes `component-lib` but the README omitted it.
- **Fix**: Added `component-lib` entry to the packages list.

### 2. [INCORRECT] `e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md` — Test Scenarios table
- **Issue**: Missing `many_messages_app` scenario which exists in `_SCENARIOS` in `test_load.py` and as `scenarios/many_messages_app.py`.
- **Fix**: Added `many_messages_app` row to the Test Scenarios table.

### 3. [INCORRECT] `e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md` — Results Format JSON
- **Issue**: The example JSON showed a flat structure with `scenario` and `concurrent_users` inside `metadata` and `server_metrics`/`session_metrics` at the top level. The actual output from `ResultsCollector.to_dict()` uses a `scenarios` array with per-scenario objects, and `metadata` only contains `timestamp`, `git_sha`, `git_branch`, and `runner`.
- **Fix**: Updated the JSON example to match the actual combined output format with `metadata` + `scenarios` array structure, including all server metric fields (`memory_rss_mb_avg`, `thread_count_max`, `sample_count`) and `duration_seconds`.

### 4. [INCORRECT] `e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md` — generate_report.py invocation
- **Issue**: The command `uv run python generate_report.py --results-dir=results` assumed the working directory was `e2e_playwright/load_testing/`, but no directory context was given.
- **Fix**: Changed to use full repo-root relative paths: `uv run python e2e_playwright/load_testing/generate_report.py --results-dir=e2e_playwright/load_testing/results`.

### 5. [MISSING] `e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md` — Directory Structure
- **Issue**: Missing `worker.py` and `scenarios/many_messages_app.py` from the directory tree.
- **Fix**: Added both files to the directory structure listing.

### 6. [MISSING] `e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md` — Server Metrics table
- **Issue**: Missing `memory_rss_mb_avg` metric which is present in the actual output.
- **Fix**: Added `memory_rss_mb_avg` row to the Server Metrics table.

### 7. [OUTDATED] `lib/tests/streamlit/typing/README.md`
- **Issue**: Only mentioned "mypy" as the type checker, but `ty` is also used (as documented in `lib/AGENTS.md` and the `make python-types` command description).
- **Fix**: Changed "checked by our mypy script" to "checked by mypy and ty".

### 8. [OUTDATED] `README.md` — Contributing link
- **Issue**: Line 136 linked to `https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/wiki/Contributing` (returns 302 redirect). The canonical contributing guide is `CONTRIBUTING.md` at the repository root.
- **Fix**: Updated link to `https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md`.

### 9. [INCORRECT] `.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/SKILL.md` — Custom Components path
- **Issue**: Referenced `component-lib/` as a top-level path, but the actual path is `frontend/component-lib/`. Also missing `frontend/component-v2-lib/`.
- **Fix**: Updated to `frontend/component-lib/`, `frontend/component-v2-lib/`.

## Files Modified

```
frontend/README.md                                              | 1 +
e2e_playwright/load_testing/README.md                           | 30 +++++++++-----
lib/tests/streamlit/typing/README.md                            | 2 +-
README.md                                                       | 2 +-
.claude/skills/understanding-streamlit-architecture/SKILL.md    | 2 +-
```

## Verified (No Changes Needed)

The following were verified and found to be accurate:
- All `make` commands referenced in documentation exist in `make help` output
- All file/directory paths referenced in AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and wiki files exist
- Python version range (3.10-3.14) matches CI config in `.github/actions/build_info/action.yml`
- Frontend tool versions (TypeScript v6, React v18, Vite v8, Vitest v4, etc.) match `frontend/package.json`
- Python tool versions (Ruff 0.x, mypy 1.x, pytest 9.x) match `pyproject.toml`
- Skill/subagent lists in CONTRIBUTING.md match actual `.claude/skills/` and `.claude/agents/` directories
- Workflow list in `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md` matches actual workflow files (43 total)
- All internal cross-references between documentation files resolve correctly

---
*This is an automated documentation update by `claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking`.*

title: Pin 6 actions to commit SHA
body:
## Summary

This PR pins all GitHub Actions to immutable commit SHAs instead of mutable version tags.

- Pin 6 unpinned actions to full 40-character SHAs
- Add version comments for readability (e.g., `@abc123 # v6`)

## Changes by file

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| cherry-pick-to-release-branch.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| patch-release-branch-creation.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| release-branch-creation.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| release-tag-and-pr-creation.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| semgrep.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |
| update-emojis-material-icons.yml | Pinned actions to SHA |

## A note on internal action pinning

This PR pins all actions including org-owned ones. Best practice is to pin everything — the tj-actions/changed-files attack was an internally maintained action that was compromised, and every repo referencing it by tag silently executed attacker code. That said, it's your codebase. If you'd prefer to leave org-owned actions unpinned, let us know and we'll adjust the PR.

## How to verify

Review the diff — each change is mechanical and preserves workflow behavior:
- **SHA pinning**: `action@v3` becomes `action@abc123 # v3` — original version preserved as comment
- No workflow logic, triggers, or permissions are modified

I wrote a scanner called Runner Guard and open sourced it [here](https://github.com/Vigilant-LLC/runner-guard).

If you have any questions, reach out. I'll be monitoring comms.

\- Chris Nyhuis (dagecko)
author: dagecko

title: [docs] Merge overlapping st.table examples
body:
## Describe your changes

- Replace `st.table` docstring example 4 (simple key-value data with auto-hidden headers) with the more feature-rich badges example
- The new example showcases material icons, markdown links, and color badges
- Reduces total examples from 6 to 5 by removing the redundant original example 6

## Testing Plan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the `st.table` docstring examples to showcase a more feature-rich key-value table example that demonstrates Streamlit’s Markdown capabilities (material icons, links, and badges), while removing a redundant older example.

**Changes:**
- Replaced the prior “auto-hidden headers” key-value example with a badges/material-icons example.
- Reduced the total number of `st.table` docstring examples from 6 to 5 by removing the redundant badges example that previously appeared later.

## Summary

This PR is a docs-only change to the `st.table` docstring in `lib/streamlit/elements/table.py`. It consolidates two examples by promoting the more feature-rich "badges" example (previously Example 6) into the Example 4 slot, replacing the simpler key-value dict example. The old Example 6 is then removed as redundant, reducing the total example count from 6 to 5. The example format is also converted from doctest (`>>>`) to `.. code-block:: python`, consistent with project docstring guidelines. No runtime code, frontend, protobuf, or test changes are included.

All three reviewers (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, gemini-3.1-pro, gpt-5.3-codex-high) agreed on the scope and nature of the change. The sole point of contention was the `.. output::` URL for Example 4 (see Recommendations).

## Code Quality

The docstring edit is clean and well-structured. The new Example 4 uses the recommended `.. code-block:: python` format with `:filename:` directive and `.. output::` reference, consistent with the other examples in the file and with the project's docstring guidelines in `lib/streamlit/AGENTS.md`. The auto-hidden-header behavior is still implicitly demonstrated because the example data remains a flat dict.

All three reviewers agreed the code quality is good with no structural or maintainability issues.

## Test Coverage

No test changes needed. This is a pure documentation change with no behavioral impact. All three reviewers concurred.

## Backwards Compatibility

No concerns. Only docstring content was modified — no API signatures, defaults, or runtime behavior changed. All three reviewers agreed.

## Security & Risk

No security concerns. No runtime code is modified. All three reviewers agreed this is zero-risk from a security perspective.

## External test recommendation

- Recommend external_test: No
- Triggered categories: None
- Evidence:
  - `lib/streamlit/elements/table.py`: Only docstring text was changed; no runtime, network, auth, embedding, or ass

## Summary

This PR consolidates the `st.table` docstring examples by replacing the simple key-value Example 4 ("Display key-value data with auto-hidden headers") with the more feature-rich badges example (previously Example 6, "Display key-value data with badges"), and removing the now-redundant Example 6. This reduces the total examples from 6 to 5 while showcasing more of `st.table`'s capabilities (material icons, Markdown links, color badges, `border="horizontal"`, `width="content"`) in a single example.

All three reviewers agreed on the nature and scope of the change.

## Code Quality

The change is clean and well-structured. All three reviewers agreed on this point:

- The new Example 4 uses the `.. code-block:: python` format with `:filename:` directive, consistent with the other examples. The old Example 6 used the `>>>` doctest format, so this change also improves format consistency.
- The example title accurately describes the content.
- Indentation and docstring structure follow existing patterns.

**Resolved disagreement — output URL:** One reviewer (gemini-3.1-pro) flagged that the `.. output::` URL (`doc-table-auto-header.streamlit.app/`) no longer matches the new example content and requested it be updated. However, the commit history explicitly shows this was intentionally preserved: the URL was updated in a second commit and then reverted in a third commit titled *"Revert URL to doc-table-auto-header as requested"*. The deployed example app at that URL will be updated separately to reflect the new content. This is not a valid concern.

## Test Coverage

No test changes are needed. All three reviewers agreed this is a pure docstring edit with no functional code, API surface, or runtime behavior changes.

## Backwards Compatibility

No backwards compatibility concerns. All three reviewers agreed: no API signatures, behavior, or defaults are modified.

## Security & Risk

No security concerns. All three reviewers agreed: this is a documentation-only c

commits.message:
[docs] Replace st.table example 4 with badges example

Replace the simple key-value example with the more feature-rich badges
example that showcases material icons, markdown links, and color badges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[docs] Update output URL for st.table badges example

Update the .. output:: URL to match the new badges example content.
The previous URL (doc-table-auto-header) referenced the old key-value
example that has been replaced with the badges example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[docs] Revert URL to doc-table-auto-header as requested

Keep the original URL per user request.

[docs] Simplify example title to "Display key-value data"
